Genesis1:1 IN THE beginning God (prepared
Genesis1:2 The earth was without form and an empty waste
Genesis1:3 And God said
Genesis1:4 And God saw that the light was good (suitable
Genesis1:5 And God called the light Day
Genesis1:6 And God said
Genesis1:7 And God made the firmament [the expanse] and separated the waters which were under the expanse from the waters which were above the expanse. And it was so.
Genesis1:8 And God called the firmament Heavens. And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:9 And God said
Genesis1:10 God called the dry land Earth
Genesis1:11 And God said
Genesis1:12 The earth brought forth vegetation: plants yielding seed according to their own kinds and trees bearing fruit in which was their seed
Genesis1:13 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:14 And God said
Genesis1:15 And let them be lights in the expanse of the sky to give light upon the earth. And it was so.
Genesis1:16 And God made the two great lights–the greater light (the sun) to rule the day and the lesser light (the moon) to rule the night. He also made the stars.
Genesis1:17 And God set them in the expanse of the heavens to give light upon the earth
Genesis1:18 To rule over the day and over the night
Genesis1:19 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:20 And God said
Genesis1:21 God created the great sea monsters and every living creature that moves
Genesis1:22 And God blessed them
Genesis1:23 And there was evening and there was morning
Genesis1:24 And God said
Genesis1:25 And God made the [wild] beasts of the earth according to their kinds
Genesis1:26 God said
Genesis1:27 So God created man in His own image
Genesis1:28 And God blessed them and said to them
Genesis1:29 And God said
Genesis1:30 And to all the animals on the earth and to every bird of the air and to everything that creeps on the ground–to everything in which there is the breath of life–I have given every green plant for food. And it was so.
Genesis1:31 And God saw everything that He had made
Genesis2:1 THUS THE heavens and the earth were finished
Genesis2:2 And on the seventh day God ended His work which He had done; and He rested on the seventh day from all His work which He had done. [Heb. 4:9
Genesis2:3 And God blessed (spoke good of) the seventh day
Genesis2:4 This is the history of the heavens and of the earth when they were created. In the day that the Lord God made the earth and the heavens–
Genesis2:5 When no plant of the field was yet in the earth and no herb of the field had yet sprung up
Genesis2:6 But there went up a mist (fog
Genesis2:7 Then the Lord God formed man from the dust of the ground and breathed into his nostrils the breath or spirit of life
Genesis2:8 And the Lord God planted a garden toward the east
Genesis2:9 And out of the ground the Lord God made to grow every tree that is pleasant to the sight or to be desired–good (suitable
Genesis2:10 Now a river went out of Eden to water the garden; and from there it divided and became four [river] heads.
Genesis2:11 The first is named Pishon; it is the one flowing around the whole land of Havilah
Genesis2:12 The gold of that land is of high quality; bdellium (pearl?) and onyx stone are there.
Genesis2:13 The second river is named Gihon; it is the one flowing around the whole land of Cush.
Genesis2:14 The third river is named Hiddekel [the Tigris]; it is the one flowing east of Assyria. And the fourth river is the Euphrates.
Genesis2:15 And the Lord God took the man and put him in the Garden of Eden to tend and guard and keep it.
Genesis2:16 And the Lord God commanded the man
Genesis2:17 But of the tree of the knowledge of good and evil and blessing and calamity you shall not eat
Genesis2:18 Now the Lord God said
Genesis2:19 And out of the ground the Lord God formed every [wild] beast and living creature of the field and every bird of the air and brought them to Adam to see what he would call them; and whatever Adam called every living creature
Genesis2:20 And Adam gave names to all the livestock and to the birds of the air and to every [wild] beast of the field; but for Adam there was not found a helper meet (suitable
Genesis2:21 And the Lord God caused a deep sleep to fall upon Adam; and while he slept
Genesis2:22 And the rib or part of his side which the Lord God had taken from the man He built up and made into a woman
Genesis2:23 Then Adam said
Genesis2:24 Therefore a man shall leave his father and his mother and shall become united and cleave to his wife
Genesis2:25 And the man and his wife were both naked and were not embarrassed or ashamed in each other's presence.

Genesis4:1 AND ADAM knew Eve as his wife
Genesis4:2 And [next] she gave birth to his brother Abel. Now Abel was a keeper of sheep
Genesis4:3 And in the course of time Cain brought to the Lord an offering of the fruit of the ground.
Genesis4:4 And Abel brought of the firstborn of his flock and of the fat portions. And the Lord had respect and regard for Abel and for his offering
Genesis4:5 But for Cain and his offering He had no respect or regard. So Cain was exceedingly angry and indignant
Genesis4:6 And the Lord said to Cain
Genesis4:7 If you do well
Genesis4:8 And Cain said to his brother
Genesis4:9 And the Lord said to Cain
Genesis4:10 And [the Lord] said
Genesis4:11 And now you are cursed by reason of the earth
Genesis4:12 When you till the ground
Genesis4:13 Then Cain said to the Lord
Genesis4:14 Behold
Genesis4:15 And the Lord said to him
Genesis4:16 So Cain went away from the presence of the Lord and dwelt in the land of Nod [wandering]
Genesis4:17 And Cain's wife [one of Adam's offspring] became pregnant and bore Enoch; and Cain built a city and named it after his son Enoch.
Genesis4:18 To Enoch was born Irad
Genesis4:19 And Lamech took two wives; the name of the one was Adah and of the other was Zillah.
Genesis4:20 Adah bore Jabal; he was the father of those who dwell in tents and have cattle and purchase possessions.
Genesis4:21 His brother's name was Jubal; he was the father of all those who play the lyre and pipe.
Genesis4:22 Zillah bore Tubal-cain; he was the forger of all [cutting] instruments of bronze and iron. The sister of Tubal-cain was Naamah.
Genesis4:23 Lamech said to his wives
Genesis4:24 If Cain is avenged sevenfold
Genesis4:25 And Adam's wife again became pregnant
Genesis4:26 And to Seth also a son was born
Genesis5:1 THIS IS the book (the written record
Genesis5:2 He created them male and female and blessed them and named them [both] Adam [Man] at the time they were created.
Genesis5:3 When Adam had lived 130 years
Genesis5:4 After he had Seth
Genesis5:5 So altogether Adam lived 930 years
Genesis5:6 When Seth was 105 years old
Genesis5:7 Seth lived after the birth of Enosh 807 years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:8 So Seth lived 912 years
Genesis5:9 When Enosh was 90 years old

Genesis5:11 So Enosh lived 905 years
Genesis5:12 When Kenan was 70 years old
Genesis5:13 Kenan lived after the birth of Mahalalel 840 years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:14 So Kenan lived 910 years
Genesis5:15 When Mahalalel was 65 years old
Genesis5:16 Mahalalel lived after the birth of Jared 830 years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:17 So Mahalalel lived 895 years
Genesis5:18 When Jared was 162 years old
Genesis5:19 Jared lived after the birth of Enoch 800 years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:20 So Jared lived 962 years
Genesis5:21 When Enoch was 65 years old
Genesis5:22 Enoch walked [in habitual fellowship] with God after the birth of Methuselah 300 years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:23 So all the days of Enoch were 365 years.
Genesis5:24 And Enoch walked [in habitual fellowship] with God; and he was not
Genesis5:25 When Methuselah was 187 years old
Genesis5:26 Methuselah lived after the birth of Lamech 782 years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:27 So Methuselah lived 969 years
Genesis5:28 When Lamech was 182 years old
Genesis5:29 He named him Noah
Genesis5:30 Lamech lived after the birth of Noah 595 years and had other sons and daughters.
Genesis5:31 So all the days of Lamech were 777 years
Genesis5:32 After Noah was 500 years old

Genesis6:2 The sons of God saw that the daughters of men were fair

Genesis6:6 And the Lord regretted that He had made man on the earth
Genesis6:7 So the Lord said
Genesis6:8 But Noah found grace (favor) in the eyes of the Lord.
Genesis6:9 This is the history of the generations of Noah. Noah was a just and righteous man
Genesis6:10 And Noah became the father of three sons: Shem
Genesis6:11 The earth was depraved and putrid in God's sight
Genesis6:12 And God looked upon the world and saw how degenerate
Genesis6:13 God said to Noah
Genesis6:14 Make yourself an ark of gopher or cypress wood; make in it rooms (stalls
Genesis6:15 And this is the way you are to make it: the length of the ark shall be 300 cubits
Genesis6:16 You shall make a roof or window [a place for light] for the ark and finish it to a cubit [at least 18 inches] above–and the door of the ark you shall put in the side of it; and you shall make it with lower
Genesis6:17 For behold
Genesis6:18 But I will establish My covenant (promise

Genesis6:20 Of fowls and birds according to their kinds

Genesis6:22 Noah did this; he did all that God commanded him.

Genesis7:5 And Noah did all that the Lord commanded him. [Heb. 11:7.]
Genesis7:6 Noah was 600 years old when the flood of waters came upon the earth or land.
Genesis7:7 And Noah and his sons and his wife and his sons' wives with him went into the ark because of the waters of the flood. [Matt. 24:38; Luke 17:27.]
Genesis7:8 Of clean animals and of animals that are not clean

Genesis7:12 And it rained upon the earth forty days and forty nights.
Genesis7:13 On the very same day Noah and Shem
Genesis7:14 They and every [wild] beast according to its kind
Genesis7:15 And they went into the ark with Noah
Genesis7:16 And they that entered
Genesis7:17 The flood [that is
Genesis7:18 And the waters became mighty and increased greatly upon the land
Genesis7:19 And the waters prevailed so exceedingly and were so mighty upon the earth that all the high hills under the whole sky were covered.
Genesis7:20 [In fact] the waters became fifteen cubits higher
Genesis7:21 And all flesh ceased to breathe that moved upon the earth–fowls and birds
Genesis7:22 Everything on the dry land in whose nostrils were the breath and spirit of life died.
Genesis7:23 God destroyed (blotted out) every living thing that was upon the face of the earth; man and animals and the creeping things and the birds of the heavens were destroyed (blotted out) from the land. Only Noah remained alive
Genesis7:24 And the waters prevailed [mightily] upon the earth or land 150 days (five months).
Genesis8:1 AND GOD [earnestly] remembered Noah and every living thing and all the animals that were with him in the ark; and God made a wind blow over the land
Genesis8:2 Also the fountains of the deep and the windows of the heavens were closed
Genesis8:3 And the waters receded from the land continually. At the end of 150 days the waters had diminished.
Genesis8:4 On the seventeenth day of the seventh month the ark came to rest on the mountains of Ararat [in Armenia].
Genesis8:5 And the waters continued to diminish until the tenth month; on the first day of the tenth month the tops of the high hills were seen.
Genesis8:6 At the end of [another] forty days Noah opened a window of the ark which he had made
Genesis8:7 And sent forth a raven
Genesis8:8 Then he sent forth a dove to see if the waters had decreased from the surface of the ground.
Genesis8:9 But the dove found no resting-place on which to roost
Genesis8:10 He waited another seven days and again sent forth the dove out of the ark.
Genesis8:11 And the dove came back to him in the evening
Genesis8:12 Then he waited another seven days and sent forth the dove
Genesis8:13 In the year 601 [of Noah's life]
Genesis8:14 And on the twenty-seventh day of the second month the land was entirely dry.
Genesis8:15 And God spoke to Noah
Genesis8:16 Go forth from the ark
Genesis8:17 Bring forth every living thing that is with you of all flesh–birds and beasts and every creeping thing that creeps on the ground–that they may breed abundantly on the land and be fruitful and multiply upon the earth.
Genesis8:18 And Noah went forth
Genesis8:19 Every beast
Genesis8:20 And Noah built an altar to the Lord and took of every clean [four-footed] animal and of every clean fowl or bird and offered burnt offerings on the altar.
Genesis8:21 When the Lord smelled the pleasing odor [a scent of satisfaction to His heart]
Genesis8:22 While the earth remains

Exodus21:3 If he came [to you] by himself

Exodus21:5 But if the servant shall plainly say

Exodus21:10 If he marries again
Exodus21:11 And if he does not do these three things for her
Exodus21:12 Whoever strikes a man so that he dies shall surely be put to death.
Exodus21:13 But if he did not lie in wait for him
Exodus21:14 But if a man comes willfully upon another to slay him craftily
Exodus21:15 Whoever strikes his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
Exodus21:16 Whoever kidnaps a man
Exodus21:17 Whoever curses his father or his mother shall surely be put to death.
Exodus21:18 If men quarrel and one strikes another with a stone or with his fist and he does not die but keeps his bed
Exodus21:19 If he rises again and walks about leaning upon his staff
Exodus21:20 And if a man strikes his servant or his maid with a rod and he [or she] dies under his hand
Exodus21:21 But if the servant lives on for a day or two
Exodus21:22 If men contend with each other
Exodus21:23 But if any damage follows
Exodus21:24 Eye for eye
Exodus21:25 Burn for burn
Exodus21:26 And if a man hits the eye of his servant or the eye of his maid so that it is destroyed
Exodus21:27 And if he knocks out his manservant's tooth or his maidservant's tooth
Exodus21:28 If an ox gores a man or a woman to death
Exodus21:29 But if the ox has tried to gore before
Exodus21:30 If a ransom is put on [the man's] life
Exodus21:31 If the [man's ox] has gored another's son or daughter
Exodus21:32 If the ox gores a manservant or a maidservant
Exodus21:33 If a man leaves a pit open or digs a pit and does not cover it and an ox or a donkey falls into it
Exodus21:34 The owner of the pit shall make it good; he shall give money to the animal's owner
Exodus21:35 If one man's ox hurts another's so that it dies
Exodus21:36 Or if it is known that the ox has gored in the past
Exodus22:1 IF A man steals an ox or sheep and kills or sells it
Exodus22:2 If a thief is found breaking in and is struck so that he dies
Exodus22:3 But if the sun has risen [so he can be seen]
Exodus22:4 If the beast which he stole is found in his possession alive
Exodus22:5 If a man causes a field or vineyard to be grazed over or lets his beast loose and it feeds in another man's field
Exodus22:6 If fire breaks out and catches so that the stacked grain or standing grain or the field be consumed
Exodus22:7 If a man delivers to his neighbor money or goods to keep and it is stolen out of the neighbor's house
Exodus22:8 But if the thief is not found
Exodus22:9 For every unlawful deed
Exodus22:10 If a man delivers to his neighbor a donkey or an ox or a sheep or any beast to keep and it dies or is hurt or driven away
Exodus22:11 Then an oath before the Lord shall be required between the two that the man has not taken his neighbor's property; and the owner of it shall accept his word and not require him to make good the loss.
Exodus22:12 But if it is stolen when in his care
Exodus22:13 If it be torn in pieces [by some wild beast or by accident]
Exodus22:14 And if a man borrows anything of his neighbor and it gets hurt or dies without its owner being with it
Exodus22:15 But if the owner is with it [when the damage is done]
Exodus22:16 If a man seduces a virgin not betrothed and lies with her
Exodus22:17 If her father utterly refuses to give her to him
Exodus22:18 You shall not allow a woman to live who practices sorcery.
Exodus22:19 Whoever lies carnally with a beast shall surely be put to death.
Exodus22:20 He who sacrifices to any god but the Lord only shall be utterly destroyed.
Exodus22:21 You shall not wrong a stranger or oppress him; for you were strangers in the land of Egypt.
Exodus22:22 You shall not afflict any widow or fatherless child.
Exodus22:23 If you afflict them in any way and they cry at all to Me
Exodus22:24 And My wrath shall burn; I will kill you with the sword
Exodus22:25 If you lend money to any of My people with you who is poor
Exodus22:26 If you ever take your neighbor's garment in pledge
Exodus22:27 For that is his only covering
Exodus22:28 You shall not revile God [the judges as His agents] or esteem lightly or curse a ruler of your people.
Exodus22:29 You shall not delay to bring to Me from the fullness [of your harvested grain] and the outflow [of your grape juice and olive oil]; give Me the firstborn of your sons [or redeem them]. [Exod. 34:19
Exodus22:30 Likewise shall you do with your oxen and your sheep. Seven days the firstborn [beast] shall be with its mother; on the eighth day you shall give it to Me.
Exodus22:31 And you shall be holy men [consecrated] to Me; therefore you shall not eat any flesh that is torn by beasts in the field; you shall throw it to the dogs.
Exodus23:1 YOU SHALL not repeat or raise a false report; you shall not join with the wicked to be an unrighteous witness.
Exodus23:2 You shall not follow a crowd to do evil; nor shall you bear witness at a trial so as to side with a multitude to pervert justice.
Exodus23:3 Neither shall you be partial to a poor man in his trial [just because he is poor].
Exodus23:4 If you meet your enemy's ox or his donkey going astray
Exodus23:5 If you see the donkey of one who hates you lying [helpless] under his load
Exodus23:6 You shall not pervert the justice due to your poor in his cause.
Exodus23:7 Keep far from a false matter and [be very careful] not to condemn to death the innocent and the righteous
Exodus23:8 You shall take no bribe
Exodus23:9 Also you shall not oppress a temporary resident
Exodus23:10 Six years you shall sow your land and reap its yield.
Exodus23:11 But the seventh year you shall release it and let it rest and lie fallow
Exodus23:12 Six days you shall do your work
Exodus23:13 In all I have said to you take heed; do not mention the name of other gods [either in blessing or cursing]; do not let such speech be heard from your mouth.
Exodus23:14 Three times in the year you shall keep a feast to Me.
Exodus23:15 You shall keep the Feast of Unleavened Bread; seven days you shall eat unleavened bread as I commanded you
Exodus23:16 Also you shall keep the Feast of Harvest [Pentecost]
Exodus23:17 Three times in the year all your males shall appear before the Lord God.
Exodus23:18 You shall not offer the blood of My sacrifice with leavened bread [but keep it unmixed]
Exodus23:19 The first of the firstfruits of your ground you shall bring into the house of the Lord your God. You shall not boil a kid in its mother's milk.
Exodus23:20 Behold
Exodus23:21 Give heed to Him
Exodus23:22 But if you will indeed listen to and obey His voice and all that I speak
Exodus23:23 When My Angel goes before you and brings you to the Amorites
Exodus23:24 You shall not bow down to their gods or serve them or do after their works; but you shall utterly overthrow them and break down their pillars and images.
Exodus23:25 You shall serve the Lord your God; He shall bless your bread and water
Exodus23:26 None shall lose her young by miscarriage or be barren in your land; I will fulfill the number of your days.
Exodus23:27 I will send My terror before you and will throw into confusion all the people to whom you shall come
Exodus23:28 And I will send hornets before you which shall drive out the Hivite
Exodus23:29 I will not drive them out from before you in one year
Exodus23:30 Little by little I will drive them out from before you
Exodus23:31 I will set your borders from the Red Sea to the Sea of the Philistines
Exodus23:32 You shall make no covenant with them or with their gods.
Exodus23:33 They shall not dwell in your land

Exodus24:4 Moses wrote all the words of the Lord. He rose up early in the morning and built an altar at the foot of the mountain and set up twelve pillars representing Israel's twelve tribes.

Exodus24:8 And Moses took the [remaining half of the] blood and sprinkled it on the people

Exodus25:8 Let them make Me a sanctuary
Exodus25:9 And you shall make it according to all that I show you
Exodus25:10 They shall make an ark of acacia wood: two and a half cubits long
Exodus25:11 You shall overlay the ark with pure gold
Exodus25:12 You shall cast four gold rings and attach them to the four lower corners of it
Exodus25:13 You shall make poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold
Exodus25:14 And put the poles through the rings on the ark's sides
Exodus25:15 The poles shall remain in the rings of the ark; they shall not be removed from it [that the ark be not touched].
Exodus25:16 And you shall put inside the ark the Testimony [the Ten Commandments] which I will give you.

Exodus25:20 And the cherubim shall spread out their wings above
Exodus25:21 You shall put the mercy seat on the top of the ark
Exodus25:22 There I will meet with you and

Exodus25:27 Close against the frame shall the rings be as places for the poles to pass to carry the table [of showbread].
Exodus25:28 You shall make the poles of acacia wood and overlay them with gold
Exodus25:29 And you shall make its plates [for showbread] and cups [for incense]
Exodus25:30 And you shall set the showbread (the bread of the Presence) on the table before Me always. [John 6:58.]

Exodus25:36 Their knobs and their branches shall be of one piece with it; the whole of it one beaten work of pure gold.

Exodus25:39 Use a talent of pure gold for it

Exodus26:1 MOREOVER
Exodus26:2 The length of one curtain shall be twenty-eight cubits and the breadth of one curtain four cubits; each of the curtains shall measure the same.
Exodus26:3 The five curtains shall be coupled to one another
Exodus26:4 And you shall make loops of blue on the edge of the last curtain in the first set
Exodus26:5 Fifty loops you shall make on the one curtain and fifty loops on the edge of the last curtain that is in the second coupling or set
Exodus26:6 And you shall make fifty clasps of gold and fasten the curtains together with the clasps; then the tabernacle shall be one whole.
Exodus26:7 And make curtains of goats' hair to be a [second] covering over the tabernacle; eleven curtains shall you make.
Exodus26:8 One curtain shall be thirty cubits long and four cubits wide; and the eleven curtains shall all measure the same.
Exodus26:9 You shall join together five curtains by themselves and six curtains by themselves
Exodus26:10 And make fifty loops on the edge of the outmost curtain in the one set and fifty loops on the edge of the outmost curtain in the second set.
Exodus26:11 You shall make fifty clasps of bronze and put the clasps into the loops and couple the tent together
Exodus26:12 The surplus that remains of the tent curtains
Exodus26:13 And the cubit on the one side and the cubit on the other side of what remains in the length of the curtains of the tent shall hang over the sides of the tabernacle
Exodus26:14 You shall make a [third] covering for the tent of rams' skins tanned red
Exodus26:15 And you shall make the upright frame for the tabernacle of boards of acacia wood.
Exodus26:16 Ten cubits shall be the length of a board and a cubit and a half shall be the breadth of one board.
Exodus26:17 Make two tenons in each board for dovetailing and fitting together; so shall you do for all the tabernacle boards.
Exodus26:18 And make the boards for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side;
Exodus26:19 And you shall make forty silver sockets under the twenty boards
Exodus26:20 And for the north side of the tabernacle there shall be twenty boards
Exodus26:21 And their forty silver sockets
Exodus26:22 For the back or west side of the tabernacle you shall make six boards.
Exodus26:23 Make two boards for the corners of the tabernacle in the rear on both sides.
Exodus26:24 They shall be coupled down below and coupled together on top with one ring. Thus shall it be for both of them; they shall form the two corners.
Exodus26:25 And that will be eight boards and their sockets of silver
Exodus26:26 And you shall make bars of acacia wood: five for the boards of one side
Exodus26:27 And five bars for the boards of the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus26:28 And the middle bar halfway up the boards shall pass through from end to end.
Exodus26:29 You shall overlay the boards with gold and make their rings of gold to hold the bars and overlay the bars with gold.
Exodus26:30 You shall erect the tabernacle after the plan of it shown you on the mountain.
Exodus26:31 And make a veil of blue
Exodus26:32 You shall hang it on four pillars of acacia wood overlaid with gold
Exodus26:33 And you shall hang the veil from the clasps and bring the ark of the Testimony into place within the veil; and the veil shall separate for you the Holy Place from the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:34 And you shall put the mercy seat on the ark of the Testimony in the Most Holy Place.
Exodus26:35 And you shall set the table [for the showbread] outside the veil [in the Holy Place] on the north side and the lampstand opposite the table on the south side of the tabernacle.
Exodus26:36 You shall make a hanging [to form a screen] for the door of the tent of blue
Exodus26:37 You shall make five pillars of acacia wood to support the hanging curtain and overlay them with gold; their hooks shall be of gold

Exodus28:1 FROM AMONG the Israelites take your brother Aaron and his sons with him
Exodus28:2 And you shall make for Aaron your brother sacred garments [appointed official dress set apart for special holy services] for honor and for beauty.
Exodus28:3 Tell all who are expert
Exodus28:4 They shall make these garments: a breastplate
Exodus28:5 They shall receive [from the people] and use gold
Exodus28:6 And they shall make the ephod of gold
Exodus28:7 It shall have two shoulder straps to join the two [back and front] edges
Exodus28:8 The skillfully woven girding band which is on the ephod shall be made of the same
Exodus28:9 And you shall take two onyx or beryl stones and engrave on them the names of the twelve sons of Israel;
Exodus28:10 Six of their names on one stone and the six names of the rest on the other stone
Exodus28:11 With the work of a stone engraver
Exodus28:12 And you shall put the two stones upon the [two] shoulder straps of the ephod [of the high priest] as memorial stones for Israel; and Aaron shall bear their names upon his two shoulders as a memorial before the Lord.
Exodus28:13 And you shall make sockets or rosettes of gold for settings
Exodus28:14 And two chains of pure gold
Exodus28:15 You shall make a breastplate of judgment
Exodus28:16 The breastplate shall be square and doubled; a span [nine inches] shall be its length and a span shall be its breadth.
Exodus28:17 You shall set in it four rows of stones: a sardius
Exodus28:18 The second row an emerald
Exodus28:19 The third row a jacinth
Exodus28:20 And the fourth row a beryl
Exodus28:21 And the stones shall be twelve
Exodus28:22 You shall make for the breastplate chains of pure gold twisted like cords.
Exodus28:23 You shall make on the breastplate two rings of gold and put [them] on the two edges of the breastplate.
Exodus28:24 And you shall put the two twisted
Exodus28:25 The other two ends of the two twisted
Exodus28:26 And make two rings of gold and put them at the two ends of the breastplate on its inside edge next to the ephod.
Exodus28:27 Two gold rings you shall make and attach them to the lower part of the two shoulder pieces of the ephod in front
Exodus28:28 And they shall bind the breastplate by its rings to the rings of the ephod with a lace of blue
Exodus28:29 So Aaron shall bear the names of the sons of Israel in the breastplate of judgment upon his heart when he goes into the Holy Place
Exodus28:30 In the breastplate of judgment you shall put the Urim and the Thummim [unspecified articles used when the high priest asked God's counsel for all Israel]; they shall be upon Aaron's heart when he goes in before the Lord
Exodus28:31 Make the robe [to be worn beneath] the ephod all of blue.
Exodus28:32 There shall be a hole in the center of it [to slip over the head]
Exodus28:33 And you shall make pomegranates of blue
Exodus28:34 A gold bell and a pomegranate
Exodus28:35 Aaron shall wear the robe when he ministers
Exodus28:36 And you shall make a plate of pure gold and engrave on it
Exodus28:37 You shall fasten it on the front of the turban with a blue cord.
Exodus28:38 It shall be upon Aaron's forehead
Exodus28:39 And you shall weave the long and sleeved tunic of checkerwork of fine linen or silk and make a turban of fine linen or silk; and you shall make a girdle
Exodus28:40 For Aaron's sons you shall make long and sleeved tunics and belts or sashes and caps
Exodus28:41 And you shall put them on Aaron your brother and his sons with him
Exodus28:42 You shall make for them [white] linen trunks to cover their naked flesh
Exodus28:43 And they shall be on Aaron and his sons when they go into the Tent of Meeting or when they come near to the altar to minister in the Holy Place
Exodus29:1 THIS IS what you shall do to consecrate (set them apart) that they may serve Me as priests. Take one young bull and two rams
Exodus29:2 And unleavened bread and unleavened cakes mixed with oil and unleavened wafers spread with oil; of fine flour shall you make them.
Exodus29:3 You shall put them in one basket and bring them in [it]
Exodus29:4 And bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the Tent of Meeting [out where the laver is] and wash them with water.
Exodus29:5 Then take the garments and put on Aaron the long and sleeved tunic and the robe of the ephod and the ephod and the breastplate
Exodus29:6 And you shall put the turban or miter upon his head and put the holy crown upon the turban.
Exodus29:7 Then take the anointing oil and pour it on his head and anoint him.
Exodus29:8 And bring his sons and put long and sleeved tunics on them.
Exodus29:9 And you shall gird them with sashes or belts
Exodus29:10 Then bring the bull before the Tent of Meeting
Exodus29:11 And you shall kill the bull before the Lord by the door of the Tent of Meeting.
Exodus29:12 And you shall take of the blood of the bull and put it on the horns of the altar with your finger
Exodus29:13 And take all the fat that covers the entrails
Exodus29:14 But the flesh of the bull
Exodus29:15 You shall also take one of the rams
Exodus29:16 And you shall kill the ram and you shall take its blood and throw it against the altar round about.
Exodus29:17 And you shall cut the ram in pieces and wash its entrails and legs and put them with its pieces and its head
Exodus29:18 And you shall burn the whole ram upon the altar. It is a burnt offering to the Lord; it is a sweet and satisfying fragrance
Exodus29:19 And you shall take the other ram
Exodus29:20 Then you shall kill the ram and take part of its blood and put it on the tip of the right ears of Aaron and his sons and on the thumb of their right hands and on the great toe of their right feet
Exodus29:21 Then you shall take part of the blood that is on the altar
Exodus29:22 Also you shall take the fat of the ram
Exodus29:23 Take also one loaf of bread
Exodus29:24 And put all these in the hands of Aaron and his sons and they shall wave them for a wave offering before the Lord.
Exodus29:25 Then you shall take them from their hands
Exodus29:26 And take the breast of the ram of Aaron's consecration and ordination and wave it for a wave offering before the Lord; and it shall be your portion [Moses].
Exodus29:27 And you shall sanctify (set apart for God) the waved breast of the ram used in the ordination and the waved thigh of the priests' portion
Exodus29:28 It shall be for Aaron and his sons as their due portion from the Israelites perpetually
Exodus29:29 The holy garments of Aaron shall pass to his descendants who succeed him
Exodus29:30 And that son who is [high] priest in his stead shall put them on [each day for] seven days when he comes into the Tent of Meeting to minister in the Holy Place.
Exodus29:31 You shall take the ram of the consecration and ordination and boil its flesh in a holy and set-apart place.
Exodus29:32 Aaron and his sons shall eat the flesh of the ram and the bread in the basket
Exodus29:33 They shall eat those things with which atonement was made
Exodus29:34 And if any of the flesh or bread for the ordination remains until morning
Exodus29:35 Thus shall you do to Aaron and to his sons according to all I have commanded you; during seven days shall you ordain them.
Exodus29:36 You shall offer every day a bull as a sin offering for atonement. And you shall cleanse the altar by making atonement for it
Exodus29:37 Seven days you shall make atonement for the altar and sanctify it [set it apart for God]; and the altar shall be most holy; whoever or whatever touches the altar must be holy (set apart for God's service).
Exodus29:38 Now this is what you shall offer on the altar: two lambs a year old shall be offered day by day continually.
Exodus29:39 One lamb you shall offer in the morning and the other lamb in the evening;
Exodus29:40 And with the one lamb a tenth measure of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of beaten oil
Exodus29:41 And the other lamb you shall offer at evening
Exodus29:42 This shall be a continual burnt offering throughout your generations at the door of the Tent of Meeting before the Lord
Exodus29:43 There I will meet with the Israelites
Exodus29:44 And I will sanctify the Tent of Meeting and the altar; I will sanctify also both Aaron and his sons to minister to Me in the priest's office.
Exodus29:45 And I will dwell among the Israelites and be their God.
Exodus29:46 And they shall know [from personal experience] that I am the Lord their God
Exodus30:1 AND YOU shall make an altar to burn incense upon; of acacia wood you shall make it.

Exodus30:7 And Aaron shall burn on it incense of sweet spices; every morning when he trims and fills the lamps he shall burn it. [Ps. 141:2; Rev. 5:8; 8:3
Exodus30:8 And when Aaron lights the lamps in the evening
Exodus30:9 You shall offer no unholy incense on the altar nor burnt sacrifice nor cereal offering; and you shall pour no libation (drink offering) on it.
Exodus30:10 Aaron shall make atonement upon the horns of it once a year; with the blood of the sin offering of atonement once in the year shall he make atonement upon and for it throughout your generations. It is most holy to the Lord.
Exodus30:11 And the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:12 When you take the census of the Israelites
Exodus30:13 This is what everyone shall give as he joins those already numbered: a half shekel
Exodus30:14 Everyone from twenty years old and upward
Exodus30:15 The rich shall not give more and the poor shall not give less than half a shekel when [you] give this offering to the Lord to make atonement for yourselves.
Exodus30:16 And you shall take the atonement money of the Israelites and use it [exclusively] for the service of the Tent of Meeting
Exodus30:17 And the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:18 You shall also make a laver or large basin of bronze
Exodus30:19 There Aaron and his sons shall wash their hands and their feet. [Tit. 3:5.]

Exodus30:21 So they shall wash their hands and their feet
Exodus30:22 Moreover
Exodus30:23 Take the best spices: of liquid myrrh 500 shekels
Exodus30:24 And of cassia 500 shekels
Exodus30:25 And you shall make of these a holy anointing oil
Exodus30:26 And you shall anoint the Tent of Meeting with it
Exodus30:27 And the [showbread] table and all its utensils
Exodus30:28 And the altar of burnt offering with all its utensils
Exodus30:29 You shall sanctify (separate) them
Exodus30:30 And you shall anoint Aaron and his sons and sanctify (separate) them
Exodus30:31 And say to the Israelites
Exodus30:32 It shall not be poured upon a layman's body
Exodus30:33 Whoever compounds any like it or puts any of it upon an outsider shall be cut off from his people.
Exodus30:34 Then the Lord said to Moses
Exodus30:35 And make of them incense
Exodus30:36 You shall beat some of it very small and put some of it before the Testimony in the Tent of Meeting
Exodus30:37 And the incense which you shall make according to its composition you shall not make for yourselves; it shall be to you holy to the Lord.
Exodus30:38 Whoever makes any like it for perfume shall be cut off from his people.

Exodus31:2 See
Exodus31:3 And I have filled him with the Spirit of God
Exodus31:4 To devise skillful works
Exodus31:5 And in cutting of stones for setting

Exodus36:1 BEZALEL AND Aholiab and every wisehearted man in whom the Lord has put wisdom and understanding to know how to do all the work for the service of the sanctuary shall work according to all that the Lord has commanded.
Exodus36:2 And Moses called Bezalel and Aholiab and every able and wisehearted man in whose mind the Lord had put wisdom and ability
Exodus36:3 And they received from Moses all the freewill offerings which the Israelites had brought for doing the work of the sanctuary

Exodus36:5 And they said to Moses
Exodus36:6 So Moses commanded and it was proclaimed in all the camp
Exodus36:7 For the stuff they had was sufficient to do all the work and more.
Exodus36:8 And all the able and wisehearted men among them who did the work on the tabernacle made ten curtains of fine twined linen and blue
Exodus36:9 The length of each curtain was twenty-eight cubits and its breadth four cubits; all the curtains were one size.
Exodus36:10 [Bezalel] coupled five curtains one to another and the other five curtains he coupled one to another.
Exodus36:11 And he made loops of blue on the outer edge of the last curtain in the first set; this he did also on the inner edge of the first curtain in the second set.
Exodus36:12 Fifty loops he made in the one curtain and fifty loops in the edge of the curtain which was the second set; the loops were opposite one another.
Exodus36:13 And he made fifty clasps of gold and coupled the curtains together with the clasps so that the tabernacle became one unit.
Exodus36:14 And he made eleven curtains of goats' hair for a tent over the tabernacle.
Exodus36:15 The length of one curtain was thirty cubits and four cubits was the breadth; the eleven curtains were of equal size.
Exodus36:16 And he coupled five curtains by themselves and the other six curtains by themselves.
Exodus36:17 And he made fifty loops on the outmost edge of the curtain to be coupled and fifty loops he made on the inner edge of the second curtain to be coupled.
Exodus36:18 He made fifty clasps of bronze to couple the tent together into one whole.
Exodus36:19 He made a covering for the tent of rams' skins tanned red
Exodus36:20 He made boards of acacia wood for the upright framework of the tabernacle.
Exodus36:21 The length of a board was ten cubits and the breadth one cubit and a half.
Exodus36:22 Each board had two tenons (projections) to fit into a mortise to form a clutch; he did this for all the boards of the tabernacle.
Exodus36:23 And he made thus the boards [for frames] for the tabernacle: twenty boards for the south side
Exodus36:24 And he made under the twenty boards forty sockets or bases of silver
Exodus36:25 For the other side of the tabernacle
Exodus36:26 And their forty sockets or bases of silver
Exodus36:27 And for the rear or west side of the tabernacle he made six [frame] boards.
Exodus36:28 And two boards he made for each corner of the tabernacle in the rear.
Exodus36:29 They were separate below but linked together at the top with one ring; thus he made both of them in both corners.
Exodus36:30 There were eight boards with sixteen sockets or bases of silver
Exodus36:31 He made bars of acacia wood
Exodus36:32 And five bars for the boards of its other side
Exodus36:33 And he made the middle bar pass through halfway up the boards from one end to the other.
Exodus36:34 He overlaid the boards and the bars with gold and made their rings of gold as places for the bars.
Exodus36:35 And he made the veil of blue
Exodus36:36 For [the veil] he made four pillars of acacia [wood] and overlaid them with gold; their hooks were of gold
Exodus36:37 And he made a screen for the tent door of blue
Exodus36:38 And he made the five pillars of it with their hooks

Exodus39:12 The third row a jacinth

Exodus39:23 And there was an opening [for the head] in the middle of the robe like the hole in a coat of mail

Exodus40:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Exodus40:2 On the first day of the first month you shall set up the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting [of God with you].
Exodus40:3 And you shall put in it the ark of the Testimony and screen the ark [of God's Presence] with the veil. [Heb. 10:19-23.]
Exodus40:4 You shall bring in the [showbread] table and set in order the things that are to be upon it; and you shall bring in the lampstand and set up and light its lamps. [Rev. 21:23-25.]
Exodus40:5 You shall set the golden altar for the incense before the ark of the Testimony [outside the veil] and put the hanging or screen at the tabernacle door.
Exodus40:6 You shall set the altar of the burnt offering before the door of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting.
Exodus40:7 And you shall set the laver between the Tent of Meeting and the altar and put water in it.
Exodus40:8 And you shall set up the court [curtains] round about and hang up the hanging or screen at the court gate.
Exodus40:9 You shall take the anointing oil and anoint the tabernacle and all that is in it
Exodus40:10 You shall anoint the altar of burnt offering and all its utensils; and consecrate (set apart for God) the altar
Exodus40:11 And you shall anoint the laver and its base and consecrate it.
Exodus40:12 You shall bring Aaron and his sons to the door of the Tent of Meeting and wash them with water. [John 17:17-19.]
Exodus40:13 You shall put on Aaron the holy garments
Exodus40:14 And you shall bring his sons and put long and sleeved tunics on them
Exodus40:15 And you shall anoint them as you anointed their father
Exodus40:16 Thus did Moses; according to all that the Lord commanded him
Exodus40:17 And on the first day of the first month in the second year the tabernacle was erected.
Exodus40:18 Moses set up the tabernacle
Exodus40:19 [Moses] spread the tent over the tabernacle and put the covering of the tent over it
Exodus40:20 He took the Testimony [the Ten Commandments] and put it into the ark
Exodus40:21 [Moses] brought the ark into the tabernacle and set up the veil of the screen and screened the ark of the Testimony
Exodus40:22 Moses put the table [of showbread] in the Tent of Meeting on the north side of the tabernacle outside the veil;
Exodus40:23 He set the bread [of the Presence] in order on it before the Lord
Exodus40:24 And he put the lampstand in the Tent of Meeting opposite the table on the south side of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:25 Moses set up and lighted the lamps before the Lord
Exodus40:26 He put the golden altar [of incense] in the Tent of Meeting before the veil;
Exodus40:27 He burned sweet incense [symbol of prayer] upon it
Exodus40:28 And he set up the hanging or screen at the door of the tabernacle.
Exodus40:29 [Moses] put the altar of burnt offering at the door of the tabernacle of the Tent of Meeting and offered on it the burnt offering and the cereal offering
Exodus40:30 And Moses set the laver between the Tent of Meeting and the altar and put water in it for washing.
Exodus40:31 And Moses and Aaron and his sons washed their hands and their feet there.
Exodus40:32 When they went into the Tent of Meeting or came near the altar
Exodus40:33 And he erected the court round about the tabernacle and the altar and set up the hanging or screen at the court gate. So Moses finished the work.
Exodus40:34 Then the cloud [the Shekinah
Exodus40:35 And Moses was not able to enter the Tent of Meeting because the cloud remained upon it
Exodus40:36 In all their journeys
Exodus40:37 But if the cloud was not taken up
Exodus40:38 For throughout all their journeys the cloud of the Lord was upon the tabernacle by day
Leviticus1:1 THE
Leviticus1:2 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus1:3 If his offering is a burnt offering from the herd
Leviticus1:4 And he shall lay [both] his hands upon the head of the burnt offering [transferring symbolically his guilt to the victim]
Leviticus1:5 The man shall kill the young bull before the Lord
Leviticus1:6 And he shall skin the burnt offering and cut it into pieces.
Leviticus1:7 And the sons of Aaron the priest shall put fire on the altar and lay wood in order on the fire;
Leviticus1:8 And Aaron's sons the priests shall lay the pieces
Leviticus1:9 But its entrails and its legs he shall wash with water. And the priest shall burn all of it on the altar for a burnt offering
Leviticus1:10 And if the man's offering is of the flock
Leviticus1:11 And he shall kill it on the north side of the altar before the Lord
Leviticus1:12 And [the man] shall cut it into pieces
Leviticus1:13 But he shall wash the entrails and legs with water. The priest shall offer all of it and burn it on the altar; it is a burnt offering
Leviticus1:14 And if the offering to the Lord is a burnt offering of birds
Leviticus1:15 And the priest shall bring it to the altar
Leviticus1:16 And he shall take away its crop with its feathers and cast it beside the altar on the east side
Leviticus1:17 And he shall split it open [holding it] by its wings
Leviticus2:1 WHEN ANYONE offers a cereal offering to the Lord
Leviticus2:2 And he shall bring it to Aaron's sons the priests. Out of it he shall take a handful of the fine flour and oil
Leviticus2:3 What is left of the cereal offering shall be Aaron's and his sons'; it is a most holy part of the offerings made to the Lord by fire.
Leviticus2:4 When you bring as an offering cereal baked in the oven
Leviticus2:5 If your offering is cereal baked on a griddle
Leviticus2:6 You shall break it in pieces and pour oil on it; it is a cereal offering.
Leviticus2:7 And if your offering is cereal cooked in the frying pan
Leviticus2:8 And you shall bring the cereal offering that is made of these things to the Lord; it shall be presented to the priest
Leviticus2:9 The priest shall take from the cereal offering its memorial portion and burn it on the altar
Leviticus2:10 What is left of the cereal offering shall be Aaron's and his sons'; it is a most holy part of the offerings made to the Lord by fire.
Leviticus2:11 No cereal offering that you bring to the Lord shall be made with leaven
Leviticus2:12 As an offering of firstfruits you may offer leaven and honey to the Lord
Leviticus2:13 Every cereal offering you shall season with salt [symbol of preservation]; neither shall you allow the salt of the covenant of your God to be lacking from your cereal offering; with all your offerings you shall offer salt. [Mark 9:49
Leviticus2:14 If you offer a cereal offering of your firstfruits to the Lord
Leviticus2:15 And you shall put oil on it and lay frankincense on it; it is a cereal offering.
Leviticus2:16 The priest shall burn as its memorial portion part of the bruised and crushed grain of it and part of the oil of it
Leviticus3:1 IF A man's offering is a sacrifice of peace offering
Leviticus3:2 He shall lay [both] his hands upon the head of his offering and kill it at the door of the Tent of Meeting; and Aaron's sons the priests shall throw the blood against the altar round about.
Leviticus3:3 And from the sacrifice of the peace offering
Leviticus3:4 And the two kidneys with the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:5 Aaron's sons shall burn it all on the altar upon the burnt offering which is on the wood on the fire
Leviticus3:6 If his peace offering to the Lord is an animal from the flock
Leviticus3:7 If he offers a lamb
Leviticus3:8 He shall lay [both] his hands on the head of his offering and kill it before the Tent of Meeting; and Aaron's sons shall throw its blood around against the altar.
Leviticus3:9 And he shall offer from the peace offering as an offering made by fire to the Lord: the fat of it
Leviticus3:10 And the two kidneys
Leviticus3:11 The priest shall burn it upon the altar
Leviticus3:12 If [a man's] offering is a goat
Leviticus3:13 And lay his hands upon its head
Leviticus3:14 Then he shall offer from it as his offering made by fire to the Lord: the fat that covers and is on the entrails
Leviticus3:15 And the two kidneys and the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus3:16 The priest shall burn them on the altar as food
Leviticus3:17 It shall be a perpetual statute for your generations in all your dwelling places
Leviticus4:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus4:2 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus4:3 If it is the anointed priest who sins
Leviticus4:4 He shall bring the bull to the door of the Tent of Meeting before the Lord
Leviticus4:5 And the anointed priest shall take some of the bull's blood and bring it into the Tent of Meeting;
Leviticus4:6 And the priest shall dip his finger in the blood and sprinkle some of [it] seven times before the Lord before the veil of the sanctuary.
Leviticus4:7 And the priest shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar of sweet incense before the Lord which is in the Tent of Meeting; and all the rest of the blood of the bull shall he pour out at the base of the altar of the burnt offering at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus4:8 And all the fat of the bull for the sin offering he shall take off of it–the fat that covers and is on the entrails
Leviticus4:9 And the two kidneys and the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus4:10 Just as these are taken off of the bull of the sacrifice of the peace offerings; and the priest shall burn them on the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:11 But the hide of the bull and all its flesh
Leviticus4:12 Even the whole bull shall he carry forth without the camp to a clean place
Leviticus4:13 If the whole congregation of Israel sins unintentionally
Leviticus4:14 When the sin which they have committed becomes known
Leviticus4:15 The elders of the congregation shall lay their hands upon the head of the bull before the Lord
Leviticus4:16 The anointed priest shall bring some of the bull's blood to the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus4:17 And shall dip his finger in the blood
Leviticus4:18 He shall put some of the blood on the horns of the altar [of incense] which is before the Lord in the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus4:19 And he shall take all its fat from the bull and burn it on the altar.
Leviticus4:20 Thus shall he do with the bull; as he did with the bull for a sin offering
Leviticus4:21 And he shall carry forth the bull outside the camp and burn it as he burned the first bull; it is the sin offering for the congregation.
Leviticus4:22 When a ruler or leader sins and unwittingly does any one of the things the Lord his God has forbidden
Leviticus4:23 If his sin which he has committed be known to him
Leviticus4:24 He shall lay his hand on the head of the goat and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering before the Lord; it is a sin offering.
Leviticus4:25 The priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering and pour the rest of its blood at the base of the altar of burnt offering.
Leviticus4:26 And he shall burn all its fat upon the altar like the fat of the sacrifice of peace offerings; so the priest shall make atonement for him for his sin
Leviticus4:27 If any one of the common people sins unwittingly in doing anything the Lord has commanded not to be done
Leviticus4:28 When the sin which he has committed is made known to him
Leviticus4:29 The offender shall lay his hand on the head of the sin offering and kill [it] at the place of the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:30 And the priest shall take some of its blood with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering and shall pour out the rest of its blood at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:31 And all the fat of it he shall take away
Leviticus4:32 If he brings a lamb as his sin offering
Leviticus4:33 He shall lay his hand upon the head of the sin offering and kill it in the place where they kill the burnt offering.
Leviticus4:34 And the priest shall take some of the blood of the sin offering with his finger and put it on the horns of the altar of burnt offering and all the rest of the blood of the lamb he shall pour out at the base of the altar.
Leviticus4:35 And he shall take away all the fat of it
Leviticus5:1 IF ANYONE sins in that he is sworn to testify and has knowledge of the matter
Leviticus5:2 Or if anyone touches an unclean thing
Leviticus5:3 Or if he touches human uncleanness
Leviticus5:4 Or if anyone unthinkingly swears he will do something
Leviticus5:5 When a man is guilty in one of these
Leviticus5:6 He shall bring his guilt or trespass offering to the Lord for the sin which he has committed
Leviticus5:7 But if he cannot afford a lamb
Leviticus5:8 He shall bring them to the priest
Leviticus5:9 And he shall sprinkle some of the blood of the sin offering on the side of the altar
Leviticus5:10 And he shall prepare the second bird for a burnt offering
Leviticus5:11 But if the offender cannot afford to bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus5:12 He shall bring it to the priest
Leviticus5:13 Thus the priest shall make atonement for him for the sin that he has committed in any of these things
Leviticus5:14 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus5:15 If anyone commits a breach of faith and sins unwittingly in the holy things of the Lord
Leviticus5:16 And he shall make restitution for what he has done amiss in the holy thing
Leviticus5:17 If anyone sins and does any of the things the Lord has forbidden
Leviticus5:18 He shall bring [to the priest] a ram without blemish out of the flock
Leviticus5:19 It is a trespass or guilt offering; he is certainly guilty before the Lord.
Leviticus6:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:2 If anyone sins and commits a trespass against the Lord and deals falsely with his neighbor in a matter of deposit given him to keep
Leviticus6:3 Or has found what was lost and lied about it
Leviticus6:4 Then if he has sinned and is guilty
Leviticus6:5 Or anything about which he has sworn falsely; he shall not only restore it in full
Leviticus6:6 And he shall bring to the priest his trespass or guilt offering to the Lord
Leviticus6:7 And the priest shall make atonement for him before the Lord
Leviticus6:8 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:9 Command Aaron and his sons
Leviticus6:10 And the priest shall put on his linen garment and put his linen breeches on his body
Leviticus6:11 And he shall put off his garments and put on other garments
Leviticus6:12 And the fire upon the altar shall be kept burning on it; it shall not be allowed to go out. The priest shall burn wood on it every morning and lay the burnt offering in order upon it and he shall burn on it the fat of the peace offerings.
Leviticus6:13 The fire shall be burning continually upon the altar; it shall not go out.
Leviticus6:14 And this is the law of the cereal offering: The sons of Aaron shall offer it before the Lord
Leviticus6:15 One of them shall take his handful of the fine flour of the cereal offering
Leviticus6:16 And the remainder of it shall Aaron and his sons eat
Leviticus6:17 It shall not be baked with leaven. I have given it as their portion of My offerings made by fire; it is most holy
Leviticus6:18 Every male among the children of Aaron may eat of it
Leviticus6:19 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:20 This is the offering which Aaron and his sons shall offer to the Lord on the day when one is anointed (and consecrated): the tenth of an ephah of fine flour for a regular cereal offering
Leviticus6:21 On a griddle or baking pan it shall be made with oil; and when it is fried you shall bring it in; in broken and fried pieces shall you offer the cereal offering as a sweet and satisfying odor to the Lord.
Leviticus6:22 And the priest among Aaron's sons who is consecrated and anointed in his stead shall offer it; by a statute forever it shall be entirely burned to the Lord.
Leviticus6:23 For every cereal offering of the priest shall be wholly burned
Leviticus6:24 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus6:25 Say to Aaron and his sons: This is the law of the sin offering: In the place where the burnt offering is killed shall the sin offering be killed before the Lord; it is most holy.
Leviticus6:26 The priest who offers it for sin shall eat it; in a sacred place shall it be eaten
Leviticus6:27 Whoever or whatever touches its flesh shall [first] be dedicated and made clean
Leviticus6:28 But the earthen vessel in which it is boiled shall be broken
Leviticus6:29 Every male among the priests may eat of this offering; it is most holy.
Leviticus6:30 But no sin offering shall be eaten of which any of the blood is brought into the Tent of Meeting to make atonement in the Holy Place; it shall be [wholly] burned with fire. [Heb. 13:11-13.]
Leviticus7:1 THIS IS the law of the guilt or trespass offering; it is most holy or sacred:
Leviticus7:2 In the place where they kill the burnt offering shall they kill the guilt or trespass offering; the blood of it shall the priest dash against the altar round about.
Leviticus7:3 And he shall offer all its fat
Leviticus7:4 And the two kidneys and the fat that is on them at the loins
Leviticus7:5 And the priest shall burn them on the altar for an offering made by fire to the Lord; it is a guilt or trespass offering.
Leviticus7:6 Every male among the priests may eat of it; it shall be eaten in a sacred place; it is most holy.
Leviticus7:7 As is the sin offering
Leviticus7:8 And the priest who offers any man's burnt offering
Leviticus7:9 And every cereal offering that is baked in the oven and all that is prepared in a pan or on a griddle shall belong to the priest who offered it.
Leviticus7:10 And every cereal offering
Leviticus7:11 And this is the law of the sacrifice of peace offerings which shall be offered to the Lord:
Leviticus7:12 If one offers it for a thanksgiving
Leviticus7:13 With cakes of leavened bread he shall offer his sacrifice of thanksgiving with the sacrifice of his peace offerings.
Leviticus7:14 And of it he shall offer one cake from each offering as an offering to the Lord; it shall belong to the priest who dashes the blood of the peace offerings.
Leviticus7:15 The flesh of the sacrifice of thanksgiving presented as a peace offering shall be eaten on the day that it is offered; none of it shall be left until morning.
Leviticus7:16 But if the sacrifice of the worshiper's offering is a vow or a freewill offering
Leviticus7:17 But the remainder of the flesh of the sacrifice on the third day shall be [wholly] burned with fire.
Leviticus7:18 If any of the flesh of the sacrifice of his peace offerings be eaten at all on the third day
Leviticus7:19 The flesh that comes in contact with anything that is not clean shall not be eaten; it shall be burned with fire. As for the meat
Leviticus7:20 But the one who eats of the flesh of the sacrifice of peace offerings that belong to the Lord when he is [ceremonially] unclean
Leviticus7:21 And if anyone touches any unclean thing–the uncleanness of man or an unclean beast or any unclean abomination–and then eats of the flesh of the sacrifice of the Lord's peace offerings
Leviticus7:22 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:23 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus7:24 The fat of the beast that dies of itself and the fat of one that is torn with beasts may be put to any other use
Leviticus7:25 For whoever eats the fat of the beast from which men offer an offering made by fire to the Lord
Leviticus7:26 Moreover
Leviticus7:27 Whoever eats any kind of blood
Leviticus7:28 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus7:29 Tell the Israelites
Leviticus7:30 He shall bring with his own hands the offerings made by fire to the Lord; he shall bring the fat with the breast
Leviticus7:31 The priest shall burn the fat on the altar
Leviticus7:32 And the right thigh you shall give to the priest for an offering from the sacrifices of your peace offerings.
Leviticus7:33 The son of Aaron who offers the blood of the peace offerings and the fat shall have the right thigh for his portion.
Leviticus7:34 For I have taken the breast that was waved and the thigh that was offered from the Israelites
Leviticus7:35 This is the anointing portion of Aaron and his sons out of the offerings to the Lord made by fire on the day when they were presented to minister to the Lord in the priest's office.
Leviticus7:36 The Lord commanded this to be given them of the Israelites on the day when they were anointed. It is their portion perpetually throughout their generations.
Leviticus7:37 This is the law of the burnt offering
Leviticus7:38 Which the Lord ordered Moses on Mount Sinai on the day He commanded the Israelites to offer their sacrifices to the Lord
Leviticus8:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus8:2 Take Aaron and his sons with him
Leviticus8:3 And assemble all the congregation at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus8:4 Moses did as the Lord commanded him
Leviticus8:5 Moses told the congregation
Leviticus8:6 Moses brought Aaron and his sons and washed them with water.
Leviticus8:7 He put on Aaron the long undertunic
Leviticus8:8 And Moses put upon Aaron the breastplate; also he put in the breastplate the Urim and the Thummim [articles upon which the high priest put his hand when seeking the divine will concerning the nation].
Leviticus8:9 And he put the turban or miter on his head; on it
Leviticus8:10 And Moses took the anointing oil and anointed the tabernacle and all that was in it
Leviticus8:11 And he sprinkled some of the oil on the altar seven times and anointed the altar and all its utensils
Leviticus8:12 And he poured some of the anointing oil upon Aaron's head and anointed him to consecrate him.
Leviticus8:13 And Moses brought Aaron's sons and put undertunics on them and girded them with sashes and wound turbans on them
Leviticus8:14 Then he brought the bull of the sin offering
Leviticus8:15 Moses killed it and took the blood and put it on the horns of the altar round about with his finger and poured the blood at the base of the altar and purified and consecrated the altar to make atonement for it.
Leviticus8:16 He took all the fat that was on the entrails
Leviticus8:17 But the bull [the sin offering] and its hide
Leviticus8:18 He brought the ram for the burnt offering
Leviticus8:19 And Moses killed it and dashed the blood upon the altar round about.
Leviticus8:20 He cut the ram into pieces and Moses burned the head
Leviticus8:21 And he washed the entrails and the legs in water; then Moses burned the whole ram on the altar; it was a burnt sacrifice
Leviticus8:22 And he brought the other ram
Leviticus8:23 And Moses killed it and took some of its blood and put it on the tip of Aaron's right ear
Leviticus8:24 And he brought Aaron's sons and Moses put some of the blood on the tips of their right ears
Leviticus8:25 And he took the fat
Leviticus8:26 And out of the basket of unleavened bread
Leviticus8:27 And he put all these in Aaron's hands and his sons' hands and waved them for a wave offering before the Lord.
Leviticus8:28 Then Moses took these things from their hands and burned them on the altar with the burnt offering as an ordination offering
Leviticus8:29 And Moses took the breast and waved it for a wave offering before the Lord; for of the ram of consecration and ordination it was Moses' portion
Leviticus8:30 And Moses took some of the anointing oil and some of the blood which was on the altar and sprinkled it on Aaron and his garments
Leviticus8:31 And Moses said to Aaron and his sons
Leviticus8:32 And what remains of the flesh and of the bread you shall burn with fire.
Leviticus8:33 And you shall not go out of the door of the Tent of Meeting for seven days
Leviticus8:34 As has been done this day
Leviticus8:35 At the door of the Tent of Meeting you shall remain day and night for seven days
Leviticus8:36 So Aaron and his sons did all the things which the Lord commanded through Moses.
Leviticus9:1 ON THE eighth day Moses called Aaron and his sons and the elders of Israel;
Leviticus9:2 And he said to Aaron
Leviticus9:3 And say to the Israelites
Leviticus9:4 Also a bull and a ram for peace offerings to sacrifice before the Lord
Leviticus9:5 They brought before the Tent of Meeting what Moses [had] commanded; all the congregation drew near and stood before the Lord.
Leviticus9:6 And Moses said
Leviticus9:7 And Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus9:8 So Aaron drew near to the altar and killed the calf of the sin offering
Leviticus9:9 The sons of Aaron presented the blood to him; he dipped his finger in the blood and put it on the horns of the altar and poured out the blood at the altar's base;
Leviticus9:10 But the fat
Leviticus9:11 And the flesh and the hide Aaron burned with fire outside the camp.
Leviticus9:12 He killed the burnt offering
Leviticus9:13 And they brought the burnt offering to him piece by piece
Leviticus9:14 And he washed the entrails and the legs and burned them with the burnt offering on the altar.
Leviticus9:15 Then Aaron presented the people's offering
Leviticus9:16 And he presented the burnt offering and offered it according to the ordinance.
Leviticus9:17 And Aaron presented the cereal offering and took a handful of it and burned it on the altar in addition to the burnt offering of the morning.
Leviticus9:18 He also killed the bull and the ram
Leviticus9:19 And the fat of the bull and of the ram
Leviticus9:20 And they put the fat upon the breasts
Leviticus9:21 But the breasts and the right thigh Aaron waved for a wave offering before the Lord
Leviticus9:22 Then Aaron lifted his hands toward the people and blessed them
Leviticus9:23 Moses and Aaron went into the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus9:24 Then there came a fire out from before the Lord and consumed the burnt offering and the fat on the altar; and when all the people saw it
Leviticus10:1 AND NADAB and Abihu
Leviticus10:2 And there came forth fire from before the Lord and killed them
Leviticus10:3 Then Moses said to Aaron
Leviticus10:4 Moses called Mishael and Elzaphan
Leviticus10:5 So they drew near and carried them in their undertunics [stripped of their priestly vestments] out of the camp
Leviticus10:6 And Moses said to Aaron and Eleazar and Ithamar
Leviticus10:7 And you shall not go out from the door of the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus10:8 And the Lord said to Aaron
Leviticus10:9 Do not drink wine or strong drink
Leviticus10:10 You shall make a distinction and recognize a difference between the holy and the common or unholy
Leviticus10:11 And you are to teach the Israelites all the statutes which the Lord has spoken to them by Moses.
Leviticus10:12 And Moses said to Aaron and to Eleazar and Ithamar
Leviticus10:13 You shall eat it in a sacred place
Leviticus10:14 But the breast that is waved and the thigh that is offered you shall eat in a clean place
Leviticus10:15 The thigh that is offered and the breast that is waved they shall bring with the offerings made by fire of the fat
Leviticus10:16 And Moses diligently tried to find [what had become of] the goat [that had been offered] for the sin offering
Leviticus10:17 Why have you not eaten the sin offering in the Holy Place? It is most holy; and God has given it to you to bear and take away the iniquity of the congregation
Leviticus10:18 Behold
Leviticus10:19 But Aaron said to Moses
Leviticus10:20 And when Moses heard that
Leviticus11:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus11:2 Say to the Israelites: These are the animals which you may eat among all the beasts that are on the earth. [Mark 7:15-19.]
Leviticus11:3 Whatever parts the hoof and is cloven-footed and chews the cud
Leviticus11:4 Nevertheless these you shall not eat of those that chew the cud or divide the hoof: the camel
Leviticus11:5 And the coney or rock badger
Leviticus11:6 And the hare
Leviticus11:7 And the swine
Leviticus11:8 Of their flesh you shall not eat
Leviticus11:9 These you may eat of all that are in the waters: whatever has fins and scales in the waters
Leviticus11:10 But all that have not fins and scales in the seas and in the rivers
Leviticus11:11 They shall continue to be an abomination to you; you shall not eat of their flesh
Leviticus11:12 Everything in the waters that has not fins or scales shall be abhorrent and detestable to you.
Leviticus11:13 These you shall have in abomination among the birds; they shall not be eaten
Leviticus11:14 The kite
Leviticus11:15 Every kind of raven
Leviticus11:16 The ostrich
Leviticus11:17 The owl
Leviticus11:18 The swan
Leviticus11:19 The stork
Leviticus11:20 All winged insects that go upon all fours are to be an abomination to you;
Leviticus11:21 Yet of all winged insects that go upon all fours you may eat those which have legs above their feet with which to leap on the ground.
Leviticus11:22 Of these you may eat: the whole species of locust
Leviticus11:23 But all other winged insects which have four feet shall be detestable to you.
Leviticus11:24 And by [contact with] these you shall become unclean; whoever touches the carcass of them shall be unclean until the evening
Leviticus11:25 And whoever carries any part of their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:26 Every beast which parts the hoof but is not cloven-footed or does not chew the cud is unclean to you; everyone who touches them shall be unclean.
Leviticus11:27 And all that go on their paws
Leviticus11:28 And he who carries their carcass shall wash his clothes and be unclean until the evening; they are unclean to you.
Leviticus11:29 These also are unclean to you among the creeping things [that multiply greatly] and creep upon the ground: the weasel
Leviticus11:30 The gecko
Leviticus11:31 These are unclean to you among all that creep; whoever touches them when they are dead shall be unclean until the evening.
Leviticus11:32 And upon whatever they may fall when they are dead
Leviticus11:33 And every earthen vessel into which any of these [creeping things] falls
Leviticus11:34 Of all the food [in one of these unclean vessels] which may be eaten
Leviticus11:35 And everything upon which any part of their carcass falls shall be unclean; whether an oven
Leviticus11:36 Yet a spring or a cistern or reservoir of water shall be clean; but whoever touches their carcass shall be unclean.
Leviticus11:37 If a part of their carcass falls on seed which is to be sown
Leviticus11:38 But if any water be put on the seed and any part of their carcass falls on it
Leviticus11:39 If any animal of which you may eat dies [unslaughtered]
Leviticus11:40 And he who eats of its carcass [ignorantly] shall wash his clothes
Leviticus11:41 And everything that creeps on the ground and [multiplies in] swarms shall be an abomination; it shall not be eaten.
Leviticus11:42 Whatever goes on its belly
Leviticus11:43 You shall not make yourselves loathsome and abominable [by eating] any swarming thing that [multiplies by] swarms
Leviticus11:44 For I am the Lord your God; so consecrate yourselves and be holy
Leviticus11:45 For I am the Lord Who brought you up out of the land of Egypt to be your God; therefore you shall be holy
Leviticus11:46 This is the law of the beast
Leviticus11:47 To make a difference (a distinction) between the unclean and the clean
Leviticus12:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus12:2 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus12:3 And on the eighth day the child shall be circumcised.
Leviticus12:4 Then she shall remain [separated] thirty-three days to be purified [from her loss] of blood; she shall touch no hallowed thing nor come into the [court of the] sanctuary until the days of her purifying are over.
Leviticus12:5 But if the child she bears is a girl
Leviticus12:6 When the days of her purifying are completed
Leviticus12:7 And he shall offer it before the Lord and make atonement for her
Leviticus12:8 If she is unable to bring a lamb [for lack of means] then she shall bring two turtledoves or young pigeons
Leviticus13:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus13:2 When a man has a swelling on his skin
Leviticus13:3 The priest shall look at the diseased spot on his skin
Leviticus13:4 If the bright spot is white on his skin
Leviticus13:5 And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day
Leviticus13:6 And the priest shall examine him again the seventh day
Leviticus13:7 But if the eruption or scab spreads farther in the skin after he has shown himself to the priest for his cleansing
Leviticus13:8 If the priest sees that the eruption or scab is spreading in the skin
Leviticus13:9 When the disease of leprosy is in a man
Leviticus13:10 And the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:11 It is a chronic leprosy in the skin of his body
Leviticus13:12 But if [supposed] leprosy breaks out in the skin
Leviticus13:13 The priest shall examine him; if the [supposed] leprosy covers all his body
Leviticus13:14 But when the raw flesh appears on him
Leviticus13:15 And the priest shall examine the raw flesh and pronounce him unclean; for the raw flesh is unclean; it is leprosy.
Leviticus13:16 But if the raw flesh turns again and becomes white
Leviticus13:17 And the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:18 And when there is in the skin of the body [the scar of] a boil that is healed
Leviticus13:19 And in the place of the boil there is a white swelling or a bright spot
Leviticus13:20 And if when the priest examines it it looks lower than the skin and the hair on it is turned white
Leviticus13:21 But if the priest examines it and finds no white hair in it and it is not lower than the skin but appears darker
Leviticus13:22 If it spreads in the skin
Leviticus13:23 But if the bright spot does not spread
Leviticus13:24 Or if there is any flesh in the skin of which there is a burn by fire and the quick flesh of the burn becomes a bright spot
Leviticus13:25 Then the priest shall examine it
Leviticus13:26 But if the priest examines it and there is no white hair in the bright spot and it is not lower than the rest of the skin but is darker
Leviticus13:27 And the priest shall examine him on the seventh day; if it is spreading in the skin
Leviticus13:28 But if the bright spot has not spread but is darker
Leviticus13:29 When a man or woman has a disease upon the head or in the beard
Leviticus13:30 The priest shall examine the diseased place; if it appears to be deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:31 If the priest examines the spot infected by the mangelike disease
Leviticus13:32 On the seventh day the priest shall examine the diseased spot; if the mange has not spread and has no yellow hair in it and does not look deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:33 Then the patient shall be shaved
Leviticus13:34 On the seventh day the priest shall look at the mangelike spot; if the mange has not spread and looks no deeper than the skin
Leviticus13:35 But if the mangelike spot spreads in the skin after his cleansing
Leviticus13:36 Then the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:37 But if in his estimation the mange is at a standstill and has black hair in it
Leviticus13:38 When a man or a woman has on the skin bright spots
Leviticus13:39 Then the priest shall look
Leviticus13:40 If a man's hair has fallen from his head
Leviticus13:41 And if his hair has fallen out from the front of his head
Leviticus13:42 But if there is on the bald head or forehead a reddish white diseased spot
Leviticus13:43 Then the priest shall examine him
Leviticus13:44 He is a leprous man; he is unclean; the priest shall surely pronounce him unclean; his disease is on his head.
Leviticus13:45 And the leper's clothes shall be rent
Leviticus13:46 He shall remain unclean as long as the disease is in him; he is unclean; he shall live alone [and] his dwelling shall be outside the camp.
Leviticus13:47 The garment also that the disease of leprosy [symbolic of sin] is in
Leviticus13:48 Whether it be in woven or knitted stuff or in the warp or woof of linen or of wool
Leviticus13:49 If the disease is greenish or reddish in the garment
Leviticus13:50 The priest shall examine the diseased article and shut it up for seven days.
Leviticus13:51 He shall examine the disease on the seventh day; if [it] is spread in the garment
Leviticus13:52 He shall burn the garment
Leviticus13:53 But if the priest finds the disease has not spread in the garment
Leviticus13:54 Then the priest shall command that they wash the thing in which the plague is
Leviticus13:55 And the priest shall examine the diseased article after it has been washed
Leviticus13:56 If the priest looks and the diseased portion is less noticeable after it is washed
Leviticus13:57 If it appears still in the garment
Leviticus13:58 But the garment
Leviticus13:59 This is the law for a leprous disease in a garment of wool or linen
Leviticus14:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus14:2 This shall be the law of the leper on the day when he is to be pronounced clean: he shall be brought to the priest [at a meeting place outside the camp];
Leviticus14:3 The priest shall go out of the camp [to meet him]; and [he] shall examine him
Leviticus14:4 Then the priest shall command to take for him who is to be cleansed two living clean birds and cedar wood and scarlet [material] and hyssop. [Heb. 9:19-22.]
Leviticus14:5 And the priest shall command to kill one of the birds in an earthen vessel over fresh
Leviticus14:6 As for the living bird
Leviticus14:7 And he shall sprinkle [the blood] on him who is to be cleansed from the leprosy seven times and shall pronounce him clean
Leviticus14:8 He who is to be cleansed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus14:9 But on the seventh day he shall shave all his hair off his head
Leviticus14:10 On the eighth day he shall take two he-lambs without blemish and one ewe lamb a year old without blemish
Leviticus14:11 And the priest who cleanses him shall set the man who is to be cleansed and these things before the Lord at the door of the Tent of Meeting;
Leviticus14:12 The priest shall take one of the male lambs and offer it for a guilt or trespass offering
Leviticus14:13 He shall kill the lamb in the place where they kill the sin offering and the burnt offering
Leviticus14:14 And the priest shall take some of the blood of the guilt or trespass offering and put it on the tip of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:15 And the priest shall take some of the log of oil and pour it into the palm of his own left hand;
Leviticus14:16 And the priest shall dip his right finger in the oil that is in his left hand and shall sprinkle some of the oil with his finger seven times before the Lord;
Leviticus14:17 And of the rest of the oil that is in his hand shall the priest put some on the tip of the right ear of him who is to be cleansed
Leviticus14:18 And the rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall pour upon the head of him who is to be cleansed and make atonement for him before the Lord.
Leviticus14:19 And the priest shall offer the sin offering and make atonement for him who is to be cleansed from his uncleanness
Leviticus14:20 And the priest shall offer the burnt offering and the cereal offering on the altar; and he shall make atonement for him
Leviticus14:21 If the cleansed leper is poor and cannot afford so much
Leviticus14:22 And two turtledoves or two young pigeons
Leviticus14:23 He shall bring them on the eighth day for his cleansing to the priest at the door of the Tent of Meeting
