Leviticus14:24 And the priest shall take the lamb of the guilt or trespass offering
Leviticus14:25 And he shall kill the lamb of the guilt or trespass offering
Leviticus14:26 And the priest shall pour some of the oil into the palm of his own left hand
Leviticus14:27 And shall sprinkle with his right finger some of the oil that is in his left hand seven times before the Lord.
Leviticus14:28 The priest shall put some of the oil in his hand on the tip of the right ear of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:29 The rest of the oil that is in the priest's hand he shall put on the head of the one to be cleansed
Leviticus14:30 And he shall offer one of the turtledoves or of the young pigeons
Leviticus14:31 As he can afford
Leviticus14:32 This is the law of him in whom is the plague of leprosy
Leviticus14:33 And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus14:34 When you have come into the land of Canaan
Leviticus14:35 Then he who owns the house shall come and tell the priest
Leviticus14:36 Then the priest shall command that they empty the house before [he] goes in to examine the disease
Leviticus14:37 He shall examine the disease
Leviticus14:38 Then the priest shall go out of the door and shut up the house seven days.
Leviticus14:39 The priest shall come again on the seventh day and shall look; and if the disease has spread in the walls of the house
Leviticus14:40 He shall command that they take out the diseased stones and cast them into an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:41 He shall cause the house to be scraped within round about and the plaster or mortar that is scraped off to be emptied out in an unclean place outside the city.
Leviticus14:42 And they shall put other stones in the place of those stones
Leviticus14:43 If the disease returns
Leviticus14:44 Then the priest shall come and look
Leviticus14:45 He shall tear down the house–its stones and its timber and all the plaster or mortar of the house–and shall carry them forth out of the city to an unclean place.
Leviticus14:46 Moreover
Leviticus14:47 And he who lies down or eats in the house shall wash his clothes.
Leviticus14:48 But if the priest inspects it and the disease has not spread after the house was plastered
Leviticus14:49 He shall take to cleanse the house two birds
Leviticus14:50 And he shall kill one of the birds in an earthen vessel over running water
Leviticus14:51 And he shall take the cedar wood
Leviticus14:52 And he shall cleanse the house with the blood of the bird
Leviticus14:53 But he shall let the living bird go out of the city into the open field; so he shall make atonement for the house
Leviticus14:54 This is the law for all kinds of leprous diseases
Leviticus14:55 For the leprosy of a garment or of a house
Leviticus14:56 And for a swelling or an eruption or a scab or a bright spot
Leviticus14:57 To teach when it is unclean and when it is clean. This is the law of leprosy.
Leviticus15:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Leviticus15:2 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus15:3 This shall be [the law concerning] his uncleanness in his discharge: whether his body runs with his discharge or has stopped [running]
Leviticus15:4 Every bed on which the one who has the discharge lies is unclean
Leviticus15:5 Whoever touches that person's bed shall wash his clothes
Leviticus15:6 And whoever sits on anything on which he who has the discharge has sat shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Leviticus15:7 And he who touches the flesh of him who has the discharge shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Leviticus15:8 And if he who has the discharge spits on him who is clean
Leviticus15:9 And any saddle on which he who has the discharge rides shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:10 Whoever touches anything that has been under him shall be unclean until evening; and he who carries those things shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Leviticus15:11 Whomever he who has the discharge touches without rinsing his hands in water shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Leviticus15:12 The earthen vessel that he with the discharge touches shall be broken
Leviticus15:13 When he who has a discharge is cleansed of it
Leviticus15:14 On the eighth day he shall take two turtledoves or two young pigeons and come before the Lord to the door of the Tent of Meeting and give them to the priest;
Leviticus15:15 And the priest shall offer them
Leviticus15:16 And if any man has a discharge of semen
Leviticus15:17 And every garment and every skin on which the sperm comes shall be washed with water
Leviticus15:18 The woman also with whom a man with emission of semen shall lie
Leviticus15:19 And if a woman has a discharge
Leviticus15:20 And everything that she lies on in her separation shall be unclean; everything also that she sits on shall be unclean.
Leviticus15:21 And whoever touches her bed shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Leviticus15:22 Whoever touches anything she sat on shall wash his clothes and bathe himself in water
Leviticus15:23 And if her flow has stained her bed or anything on which she sat
Leviticus15:24 And if any man lie with her and her impurity be upon him
Leviticus15:25 And if a woman has an issue of blood for many days
Leviticus15:26 Every bed on which she lies all the days of her discharge shall be as the bed of her impurity
Leviticus15:27 And whoever touches those things shall be unclean
Leviticus15:28 But if she is cleansed of her discharge
Leviticus15:29 And on the eighth day she shall take two turtledoves or two young pigeons and bring them to the priest at the door of the Tent of Meeting;
Leviticus15:30 He shall offer one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering; and he shall make atonement for her before the Lord for her unclean discharge.
Leviticus15:31 Thus you shall separate the Israelites from their uncleanness
Leviticus15:32 This is the law for him who has a discharge and for him who has emissions of sperm
Leviticus15:33 And for her who is sick with her impurity
Leviticus16:1 AFTER THE death of Aaron's two sons
Leviticus16:2 The Lord said to Moses
Leviticus16:3 But Aaron shall come into the holy enclosure in this way: with a young bull for a sin offering and a ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:4 He shall put on the holy linen undergarment
Leviticus16:5 He shall take [at the expense] of the congregation of the Israelites two male goats for a sin offering and one ram for a burnt offering.
Leviticus16:6 And Aaron shall present the bull as the sin offering for himself and make atonement for himself and for his house [the other priests].
Leviticus16:7 He shall take the two goats and present them before the Lord at the door of the Tent of Meeting.
Leviticus16:8 Aaron shall cast lots on the two goats–one lot for the Lord
Leviticus16:9 And Aaron shall bring the goat on which the Lord's lot fell and offer him as a sin offering.
Leviticus16:10 But the goat on which the lot fell for Azazel or removal shall be presented alive before the Lord to make atonement over him
Leviticus16:11 Aaron shall present the bull as the sin offering for his own sins and shall make atonement for himself and for his house [the other priests]
Leviticus16:12 He shall take a censer full of burning coals of fire from off the [bronze] altar before the Lord
Leviticus16:13 And put the incense on the fire [in the censer] before the Lord
Leviticus16:14 He shall take of the bull's blood and sprinkle it with his finger on the front [the east side] of the mercy seat
Leviticus16:15 Then shall he kill the goat of the sin offering that is for [the sins of] the people and bring its blood within the veil [into the Holy of Holies] and do with that blood as he did with the blood of the bull
Leviticus16:16 Thus he shall make atonement for the Holy Place because of the uncleanness of the Israelites and because of their transgressions
Leviticus16:17 There shall be no man in the Tent of Meeting when the high priest goes in to make atonement in the Holy of Holies [within the veil] until he comes out and has made atonement for his own sins and those of his house [the other priests] and of all the congregation of Israel.
Leviticus16:18 And he shall go out to the altar [of burnt offering in the court] which is before the Lord and make atonement for it
Leviticus16:19 And he shall sprinkle some of the blood on it with his fingers seven times and cleanse it and hallow it from the uncleanness of the Israelites.
Leviticus16:20 And when he has finished atoning for the Holy of Holies and the Tent of Meeting and the altar [of burnt offering]
Leviticus16:21 And Aaron shall lay both his hands upon the head of the live goat and confess over him all the iniquities of the Israelites and all their transgressions
Leviticus16:22 The goat shall bear upon himself all their iniquities
Leviticus16:23 Aaron shall come into the Tent of Meeting and put off the linen garments which he put on when he went into the Holy of Holies
Leviticus16:24 And he shall bathe his body with water in a sacred place and put on his garments
Leviticus16:25 And the fat of the sin offering he shall burn upon the altar.
Leviticus16:26 The man who led the sin-bearing goat out and let him go for Azazel or removal shall wash his clothes and bathe his body
Leviticus16:27 The bull and the goat for the sin offering
Leviticus16:28 And he who burns them shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Leviticus16:29 It shall be a statute to you forever that in the seventh month [nearly October] on the tenth day of the month you shall afflict yourselves [by fasting with penitence and humiliation] and do no work at all
Leviticus16:30 For on this day atonement shall be made for you
Leviticus16:31 It is a sabbath of [solemn] rest to you
Leviticus16:32 And the priest who shall be anointed and consecrated to minister in the priest's office in his father's stead shall make atonement
Leviticus16:33 He shall make atonement for the Holy Sanctuary
Leviticus16:34 This shall be an everlasting statute for you
Leviticus17:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus17:2 Tell Aaron
Leviticus17:3 If any man of the house of Israel kills an ox or lamb or goat in the camp or kills it outside the camp
Leviticus17:4 And does not bring it to the door of the Tent of Meeting to offer it as an offering to the Lord before the Lord's tabernacle
Leviticus17:5 This is so that the Israelites
Leviticus17:6 And the priest shall dash the blood on the altar of the Lord at the door of the Tent of Meeting and burn the fat for a sweet and satisfying fragrance to the Lord.
Leviticus17:7 So they shall no more offer their sacrifices to goatlike gods or demons or field spirits after which they have played the harlot. This shall be a statute forever to them throughout their generations.
Leviticus17:8 And you shall say to them
Leviticus17:9 And does not bring it to the door of the Tent of Meeting to offer it to the Lord shall be cut off from among his people.
Leviticus17:10 Any one of the house of Israel or of the strangers who dwell temporarily among them who eats any kind of blood
Leviticus17:11 For the life (the animal soul) is in the blood
Leviticus17:12 Therefore I have said to the Israelites
Leviticus17:13 And any of the Israelites or of the strangers who sojourn among them who takes in hunting any clean beast or bird shall pour out its blood and cover it with dust.
Leviticus17:14 As for the life of all flesh
Leviticus17:15 And every person who eats what dies of itself or was torn by beasts
Leviticus17:16 But if he does not wash his clothes or bathe his body
Leviticus18:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus18:2 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus18:3 You shall not do as was done in the land of Egypt in which you dwelt
Leviticus18:4 You shall do My ordinances and keep My statutes and walk in them. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus18:5 You shall therefore keep My statutes and My ordinances which
Leviticus18:6 None of you shall approach anyone close of kin to him to have sexual relations. I am the Lord.
Leviticus18:7 The nakedness of your father
Leviticus18:8 The nakedness of your father's wife you shall not uncover; it is your father's nakedness.
Leviticus18:9 You shall not have intercourse with or uncover the nakedness of your sister
Leviticus18:10 You must not have sexual relations with your son's daughter or your daughter's daughter; their nakedness you shall not uncover
Leviticus18:11 You must not have intercourse with your father's wife's daughter; begotten by your father
Leviticus18:12 You shall not have intercourse with your father's sister; she is your father's near kinswoman.
Leviticus18:13 You shall not have sexual relations with your mother's sister
Leviticus18:14 You shall not have intercourse with your father's brother's wife; you shall not approach his wife; she is your aunt.
Leviticus18:15 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your daughter-in-law; she is your son's wife; you shall not have intercourse with her.
Leviticus18:16 You shall not have intercourse with your brother's wife; she belongs to your brother.
Leviticus18:17 You shall not marry a woman and her daughter
Leviticus18:18 You must not marry a woman in addition to her sister
Leviticus18:19 Also you shall not have intercourse with a woman during her [menstrual period or similar] uncleanness.
Leviticus18:20 Moreover
Leviticus18:21 You shall not give any of your children to pass through the fire and sacrifice them to Molech [the fire god]
Leviticus18:22 You shall not lie with a man as with a woman; it is an abomination. [I Cor. 6:9
Leviticus18:23 Neither shall you lie with any beast and defile yourself with it; neither shall any woman yield herself to a beast to lie with it; it is confusion
Leviticus18:24 Do not defile yourselves in any of these ways
Leviticus18:25 And the land is defiled; therefore I visit the iniquity of it upon it
Leviticus18:26 So you shall keep My statutes and My ordinances and shall not commit any of these abominations
Leviticus18:27 For all these abominations have the men of the land done who were before you
Leviticus18:28 [Do none of these things] lest the land spew you out when you defile it as it spewed out the nation that was before you.
Leviticus18:29 Whoever commits any of these abominations shall be cut off from among [his] people.
Leviticus18:30 So keep My charge: do not practice any of these abominable customs which were practiced before you and defile yourselves by them. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus19:2 Say to all the assembly of the Israelites
Leviticus19:3 Each of you shall give due respect to his mother and his father
Leviticus19:4 Do not turn to idols and things of nought or make for yourselves molten gods. I the Lord am your God.
Leviticus19:5 And when you offer a sacrifice of peace offering to the Lord
Leviticus19:6 It shall be eaten the same day you offer it and on the day following; and if anything remains until the third day
Leviticus19:7 If it is eaten at all the third day
Leviticus19:8 But everyone who eats it shall bear his iniquity
Leviticus19:9 And when you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus19:10 And you shall not glean your vineyard bare
Leviticus19:11 You shall not steal
Leviticus19:12 And you shall not swear by My name falsely
Leviticus19:13 You shall not defraud or oppress your neighbor or rob him; the wages of a hired servant shall not remain with you all night until morning.
Leviticus19:14 You shall not curse the deaf or put a stumbling block before the blind
Leviticus19:15 You shall do no injustice in judging a case; you shall not be partial to the poor or show a preference for the mighty
Leviticus19:16 You shall not go up and down as a dispenser of gossip and scandal among your people
Leviticus19:17 You shall not hate your brother in your heart; but you shall surely rebuke your neighbor
Leviticus19:18 You shall not take revenge or bear any grudge against the sons of your people
Leviticus19:19 You shall keep My statutes. You shall not let your domestic animals breed with a different kind [of animal]; you shall not sow your field with mixed seed
Leviticus19:20 And if a man lies carnally with a woman who is a slave betrothed to a husband and not yet ransomed or given her freedom
Leviticus19:21 But he shall bring his guilt or trespass offering to the Lord to the door of the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus19:22 The priest shall make atonement for him with the ram of the guilt or trespass offering before the Lord for his sin
Leviticus19:23 And when you come into the land and have planted all kinds of trees for food
Leviticus19:24 In the fourth year all their fruit shall be holy for giving praise to the Lord.
Leviticus19:25 But in the fifth year you may eat of the fruit [of the trees]
Leviticus19:26 You shall not eat anything with the blood; neither shall you use magic
Leviticus19:27 You shall not round the corners of the hair of your heads nor trim the corners of your beard [as some idolaters do].
Leviticus19:28 You shall not make any cuttings in your flesh for the dead nor print or tattoo any marks upon you; I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:29 Do not profane your daughter by causing her to be a harlot
Leviticus19:30 You shall keep My Sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:31 Turn not to those [mediums] who have familiar spirits or to wizards; do not seek them out to be defiled by them. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus19:32 You shall rise up before the hoary head and honor the face of the old man and [reverently] fear your God. I am the Lord.
Leviticus19:33 And if a stranger dwells temporarily with you in your land
Leviticus19:34 But the stranger who dwells with you shall be to you as one born among you; and you shall love him as yourself
Leviticus19:35 You shall do no unrighteousness in judgment
Leviticus19:36 You shall have accurate and just balances
Leviticus19:37 You shall observe all My statutes and ordinances and do them. I am the Lord.
Leviticus20:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus20:2 Moreover
Leviticus20:3 I also will set My face against that man [opposing him
Leviticus20:4 And if the people of the land do at all hide their eyes from the man when he gives one of his children [as a burnt offering] to Molech [the fire god] and they overlook it or neglect to take legal action to punish him
Leviticus20:5 Then I will set My face against that man and against his family and will cut him off from among their people
Leviticus20:6 The person who turns to those who have familiar spirits and to wizards
Leviticus20:7 Consecrate yourselves therefore
Leviticus20:8 And you shall keep My statutes and do them. I am the Lord Who sanctifies you.
Leviticus20:9 Everyone who curses his father or mother shall surely be put to death; he has cursed his father or mother; his bloodguilt is upon him.
Leviticus20:10 The man who commits adultery with another's wife
Leviticus20:11 And the man who lies carnally with his father's wife has uncovered his father's nakedness; both of the guilty ones shall surely be put to death; their blood shall be upon their own heads.
Leviticus20:12 And if a man lies carnally with his daughter-in-law
Leviticus20:13 If a man lies with a male as if he were a woman
Leviticus20:14 And if a man takes a wife and her mother
Leviticus20:15 And if a man lies carnally with a beast
Leviticus20:16 If a woman approaches any beast and lies carnally with it
Leviticus20:17 If a man takes his sister
Leviticus20:18 And if a man shall lie with a woman having her menstrual pains and shall uncover her nakedness
Leviticus20:19 You shall not uncover the nakedness of your mother's sister or of your father's sister
Leviticus20:20 And if a man shall lie carnally with his uncle's wife
Leviticus20:21 And if a man shall take his brother's wife
Leviticus20:22 You shall therefore keep all My statutes and all My ordinances and do them
Leviticus20:23 You shall not walk in the customs of the nation which I am casting out before you; for they did all these things
Leviticus20:24 But I have said to you
Leviticus20:25 You shall therefore make a distinction between the clean beast and the unclean
Leviticus20:26 And you shall be holy to Me; for I the Lord am holy
Leviticus20:27 A man or woman who is a medium and has a familiar spirit or is a wizard shall surely be put to death
Leviticus21:1 THE
Leviticus21:2 Except for his near [blood] kin
Leviticus21:3 And for his sister
Leviticus21:4 He shall not even defile himself
Leviticus21:5 The priests [like the other Israelite men] shall not shave the crown of their heads or clip off the corners of their beard or make any cuttings in their flesh.
Leviticus21:6 They shall be holy to their God and not profane the name of their God; for they offer the offerings made by fire to the Lord
Leviticus21:7 They shall not take a wife who is a harlot or polluted or profane or divorced
Leviticus21:8 You shall consecrate him therefore
Leviticus21:9 The daughter of any priest who profanes herself by playing the harlot profanes her father; she shall be burned with fire [after being stoned]. [Josh. 7:15
Leviticus21:10 But he who is the high priest among his brethren
Leviticus21:11 Neither shall he go in where any dead body lies nor defile himself [by doing so
Leviticus21:12 Neither shall he go out of the sanctuary nor desecrate or make ceremonially unclean the sanctuary of his God
Leviticus21:13 He shall take a wife in her virginity.
Leviticus21:14 A widow or a divorced woman or a woman who is polluted or profane or a harlot
Leviticus21:15 That he may not profane or dishonor his children among his people; for I the Lord do sanctify the high priest.
Leviticus21:16 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus21:17 Say to Aaron
Leviticus21:18 For no man who has a blemish shall approach [God's altar to serve as priest]
Leviticus21:19 Or who has a fractured foot or hand
Leviticus21:20 Or is a hunchback
Leviticus21:21 No man of the offspring of Aaron the priest who has a blemish and is disfigured or deformed shall come near [the altar] to offer the offerings of the Lord made by fire. He has a blemish; he shall not come near to offer the bread of his God.
Leviticus21:22 He may eat the bread of his God
Leviticus21:23 But he shall not come within the veil or come near the altar [of incense]
Leviticus21:24 And Moses told it to Aaron and to his sons and to all the Israelites.
Leviticus22:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:2 Say to Aaron and his sons that they shall stay away from the holy things which the Israelites dedicate to Me
Leviticus22:3 Tell them
Leviticus22:4 No man of the offspring of Aaron who is a leper or has a discharge shall eat of the holy things [the offerings and the showbread] until he is clean. And whoever touches any person or thing made unclean by contact with a corpse or a man who has had a discharge of semen
Leviticus22:5 Or whoever touches any dead creeping thing by which he may be made unclean
Leviticus22:6 The priest who has touched any such thing shall be unclean until evening and shall not eat of the holy things unless he has bathed with water. [Heb. 10:22.]
Leviticus22:7 When the sun is down
Leviticus22:8 That which dies of itself or is torn by beasts he shall not eat
Leviticus22:9 The priests therefore shall observe My ordinance
Leviticus22:10 No outsider [not of the family of Aaron] shall eat of the holy thing [which has been offered to God]; a sojourner with the priest or a hired servant shall not eat of the holy thing.
Leviticus22:11 But if a priest buys a slave with his money
Leviticus22:12 If a priest's daughter is married to an outsider [not of the priestly tribe]
Leviticus22:13 But if a priest's daughter is a widow or divorced
Leviticus22:14 And if a man eats unknowingly of the holy thing [which has been offered to God]
Leviticus22:15 The priests shall not profane the holy things the Israelites offer to the Lord
Leviticus22:16 And so cause them [by neglect of any essential observance] to bear the iniquity when they eat their holy things; for I the Lord sanctify them.
Leviticus22:17 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:18 Say to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites
Leviticus22:19 That you may be accepted
Leviticus22:20 But you shall not offer anything which has a blemish
Leviticus22:21 And whoever offers a sacrifice of peace offering to the Lord to make a special vow to the Lord or for a freewill offering from the herd or from the flock must bring what is perfect to be accepted; there shall be no blemish in it.
Leviticus22:22 Animals blind or made infirm and weak or maimed
Leviticus22:23 For a freewill offering you may offer either a bull or a lamb which has some part too long or too short
Leviticus22:24 You shall not offer to the Lord any animal which has its testicles bruised or crushed or broken or cut
Leviticus22:25 Neither shall you offer as the bread of your God any such animals obtained from a foreigner [who may wish to pay respect to the true God]
Leviticus22:26 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus22:27 When a bull or a sheep or a goat is born
Leviticus22:28 And whether [the mother] is a cow or a ewe
Leviticus22:29 And when you sacrifice an offering of thanksgiving to the Lord
Leviticus22:30 It shall be eaten on the same day; you shall leave none of it until the next day. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:31 So shall you heartily accept My commandments and conform your life and conduct to them. I am the Lord.
Leviticus22:32 Neither shall you profane My holy name [applying it to an idol
Leviticus22:33 Who brought you out of the land of Egypt to be your God. I am the Lord.
Leviticus23:1 THE
Leviticus23:2 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus23:3 Six days shall work be done
Leviticus23:4 These are the set feasts or appointed seasons of the Lord
Leviticus23:5 On the fourteenth day of the first month at twilight is the Lord's Passover.
Leviticus23:6 On the fifteenth day of the same month is the Feast of Unleavened Bread to the Lord; for seven days you shall eat unleavened bread. [I Cor. 5:7
Leviticus23:7 On the first day you shall have a holy calling together; you shall do no servile or laborious work on that day.
Leviticus23:8 But you shall offer an offering made by fire to the Lord for seven days; on the seventh day is a holy convocation; you shall do no servile or laborious work on that day.
Leviticus23:9 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:10 Tell the Israelites
Leviticus23:11 And he shall wave the sheaf before the Lord
Leviticus23:12 You shall offer on the day when you wave the sheaf a male lamb a year old without blemish for a burnt offering to the Lord.
Leviticus23:13 Its cereal offering shall be two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil
Leviticus23:14 And you shall eat neither bread nor parched grain nor green ears
Leviticus23:15 And you shall count from the day after the Sabbath
Leviticus23:16 Count fifty days to the day after the seventh Sabbath; then you shall present a cereal offering of new grain to the Lord.
Leviticus23:17 You shall bring from your dwellings two loaves of bread to be waved
Leviticus23:18 And you shall offer with the bread seven lambs
Leviticus23:19 Then you shall sacrifice one he-goat for a sin offering and two he-lambs
Leviticus23:20 The priest shall wave the two lambs
Leviticus23:21 You shall make proclamation the same day
Leviticus23:22 And when you reap the harvest of your land
Leviticus23:23 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:24 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus23:25 You shall do no servile work on it
Leviticus23:26 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:27 Also the tenth day of this seventh month is the Day of Atonement; it shall be a holy [called] assembly
Leviticus23:28 And you shall do no work on this day
Leviticus23:29 For whoever is not afflicted [by fasting in penitence and humility] on this day shall be cut off from among his people [that he may not be included in the atonement made for them].
Leviticus23:30 And whoever does any work on that same day I will destroy from among his people.
Leviticus23:31 You shall do no kind of work [on that day]. It is a statute forever throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Leviticus23:32 It shall be to you a sabbath of rest
Leviticus23:33 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus23:34 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus23:35 On the first day shall be a holy convocation; you shall do no servile work on that day.
Leviticus23:36 For seven days you shall offer an offering made by fire to the Lord; on the eighth day shall be a holy convocation and you shall present an offering made by fire to the Lord. It is a solemn assembly; you shall do no laborious work on that day.
Leviticus23:37 These are the set feasts or appointed seasons of the Lord
Leviticus23:38 This is in addition to the Sabbaths of the Lord and besides your gifts and all your vowed offerings and all your freewill offerings which you give to the Lord.
Leviticus23:39 Also on the fifteenth day of the seventh month [nearly October]
Leviticus23:40 And on the first day you shall take the fruit of pleasing trees [and make booths of them]
Leviticus23:41 You shall keep it as a feast to the Lord for seven days in the year
Leviticus23:42 You shall dwell in booths (shelters) for seven days: All native Israelites shall dwell in booths
Leviticus23:43 That your generations may know that I made the Israelites dwell in booths when I brought them out of the land of Egypt. I am the Lord your God.
Leviticus23:44 Thus Moses declared to the Israelites the set or appointed feasts of the Lord.
Leviticus24:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:2 Command the Israelites that they bring to you pure oil from beaten olives for the light [of the golden lampstand] to cause a lamp to burn continually.
Leviticus24:3 Outside the veil of the Testimony [between the Holy and the Most Holy Places] in the Tent of Meeting
Leviticus24:4 He shall keep the lamps in order upon the lampstand of pure gold before the Lord continually. [Rev. 1:12-18.]
Leviticus24:5 And you shall take fine flour and bake twelve cakes with it; two-tenths of an ephah shall be in each cake [of the showbread or bread of the Presence].
Leviticus24:6 And you shall set them in two rows
Leviticus24:7 You shall put pure frankincense [in a bowl or spoon] beside each row
Leviticus24:8 Every Sabbath day Aaron shall set the showbread in order before the Lord continually; it is on behalf of the Israelites
Leviticus24:9 And the bread shall be for Aaron and his sons
Leviticus24:10 Now the son of an Israelite woman
Leviticus24:11 The Israelite woman's son blasphemed the Name [of the Lord] and cursed. They brought him to Moses–his mother was Shelomith
Leviticus24:12 And they put him in custody until the will of the Lord might be declared to them.
Leviticus24:13 And the Lord said to Moses
Leviticus24:14 Bring him who has cursed out of the camp
Leviticus24:15 And you shall say to the Israelites
Leviticus24:16 And he who blasphemes the Name of the Lord
Leviticus24:17 And he who kills any man shall surely be put to death.
Leviticus24:18 And he who kills a beast shall make it good
Leviticus24:19 And if a man causes a blemish or disfigurement on his neighbor
Leviticus24:20 Fracture for fracture
Leviticus24:21 He who kills a beast shall replace it; he who kills a man shall be put to death.
Leviticus24:22 You shall have the same law for the sojourner among you as for one of your own nationality
Leviticus24:23 Moses spoke to the Israelites
Leviticus25:1 THE
Leviticus25:2 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus25:3 For six years you shall sow your field
Leviticus25:4 But in the seventh year there shall be a sabbath of solemn rest for the land
Leviticus25:5 What grows of itself in your harvest you shall not reap and the grapes on your uncultivated vine you shall not gather
Leviticus25:6 And the sabbath rest of the [untilled] land shall [in its increase] furnish food for you
Leviticus25:7 For your domestic animals also and for the [wild] beasts in your land; all its yield shall be for food.
Leviticus25:8 And you shall number seven sabbaths or weeks of years for you
Leviticus25:9 Then you shall sound abroad the loud trumpet on the tenth day of the seventh month [almost October]; on the Day of Atonement blow the trumpet in all your land.
Leviticus25:10 And you shall hallow the fiftieth year and proclaim liberty throughout all the land to all its inhabitants. It shall be a jubilee for you; and each of you shall return to his ancestral possession [which through poverty he was compelled to sell]
Leviticus25:11 That fiftieth year shall be a jubilee for you; in it you shall not sow
Leviticus25:12 For it is a jubilee; it shall be holy to you; you shall eat the [sufficient] increase of it out of the field.
Leviticus25:13 In this Year of Jubilee each of you shall return to his ancestral property.
Leviticus25:14 And if you sell anything to your neighbor or buy from your neighbor
Leviticus25:15 According to the number of years after the Jubilee
Leviticus25:16 If the years [to the next Jubilee] are many
Leviticus25:17 You shall not oppress and wrong one another
Leviticus25:18 Therefore you shall do and give effect to My statutes and keep My ordinances and perform them
Leviticus25:19 The land shall yield its fruit; you shall eat your fill and dwell there in safety.
Leviticus25:20 And if you say
Leviticus25:21 Then [this is My answer:] I will command My [special] blessings on you in the sixth year
Leviticus25:22 And you shall sow in the eighth year
Leviticus25:23 The land shall not be sold into perpetual ownership
Leviticus25:24 And in all the country you possess you shall grant a redemption for the land [in the Year of Jubilee].
Leviticus25:25 If your brother has become poor and has sold some of his property
Leviticus25:26 And if the man has no one to redeem his property
Leviticus25:27 Then let him count the years since he sold it and restore the overpayment to the man to whom he sold it
Leviticus25:28 But if he is unable to redeem it
Leviticus25:29 If a man sells a dwelling house in a fortified city
Leviticus25:30 And if it is not redeemed within a full year
Leviticus25:31 But the houses of the unwalled villages shall be counted with the fields of the country. They may be redeemed
Leviticus25:32 Nevertheless
Leviticus25:33 But if a house is not redeemed by a Levite
Leviticus25:34 But the field of unenclosed or pasture lands of their cities may not be sold; it is their perpetual possession.
Leviticus25:35 And if your [Israelite] brother has become poor and his hand wavers [from poverty
Leviticus25:36 Charge him no interest or [portion of] increase
Leviticus25:37 You shall not give him your money at interest nor lend him food at a profit.
Leviticus25:38 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus25:39 And if your brother becomes poor beside you and sells himself to you
Leviticus25:40 But as a hired servant and as a temporary resident he shall be with you; he shall serve you till the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:41 And then he shall depart from you
Leviticus25:42 For the Israelites are My servants; I brought them out of the land of Egypt; they shall not be sold as bondmen. [I Cor. 7:23.]
Leviticus25:43 You shall not rule over him with harshness (severity
Leviticus25:44 As for your bondmen and your bondmaids whom you may have
Leviticus25:45 Moreover
Leviticus25:46 And you shall make them an inheritance for your children after you
Leviticus25:47 And if a sojourner or stranger with you becomes rich and your [Israelite] brother becomes poor beside him and sells himself to the stranger or sojourner with you or to a member of the stranger's family
Leviticus25:48 After he is sold he may be redeemed. One of his brethren may redeem him:
Leviticus25:49 Either his uncle or his uncle's son may redeem him
Leviticus25:50 And [the redeemer] shall reckon with the purchaser of the servant from the year when he sold himself to the purchaser to the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:51 If there remain many years [before the Year of Jubilee]
Leviticus25:52 And if little time remains until the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus25:53 And as a servant hired year by year shall he deal with him; he shall not rule over him with harshness (severity
Leviticus25:54 And if he is not redeemed during these years and by these means
Leviticus25:55 For to Me the Israelites are servants
Leviticus26:1 YOU SHALL make for yourselves no idols nor shall you erect a graven image
Leviticus26:2 You shall keep My Sabbaths and reverence My sanctuary. I am the Lord.
Leviticus26:3 If you walk in My statutes and keep My commandments and do them
Leviticus26:4 I will give you rain in due season
Leviticus26:5 And your threshing [time] shall reach to the vintage and the vintage [time] shall reach to the sowing time
Leviticus26:6 I will give peace in the land; you shall lie down and none shall fill you with dread or make you afraid; and I will clear ferocious (wild) beasts out of the land
Leviticus26:7 And you shall chase your enemies
Leviticus26:8 Five of you shall chase a hundred
Leviticus26:9 For I will be leaning toward you with favor and regard for you
Leviticus26:10 And you shall eat the [abundant] old store of produce long kept
Leviticus26:11 I will set My dwelling in and among you
Leviticus26:12 And I will walk in and with and among you and will be your God
Leviticus26:13 I am the Lord your God
Leviticus26:14 But if you will not hearken to Me and will not do all these commandments
Leviticus26:15 And if you spurn and despise My statutes
Leviticus26:16 I will do this: I will appoint over you [sudden] terror (trembling
Leviticus26:17 I [the Lord] will set My face against you and you shall be defeated and slain before your enemies; they who hate you shall rule over you; you shall flee when no one pursues you. [I Sam. 4:10; 31:1.]
Leviticus26:18 And if in spite of all this you still will not listen and be obedient to Me
Leviticus26:19 And I will break and humble your pride in your power
Leviticus26:20 And your strength shall be spent in vain
Leviticus26:21 If you walk contrary to Me and will not heed Me
Leviticus26:22 I will loose the wild beasts of the field among you
Leviticus26:23 If by these means you are not turned to Me but determine to walk contrary to Me
Leviticus26:24 I also will walk contrary to you
Leviticus26:25 And I will bring a sword upon you that shall execute the vengeance [for the breaking] of My covenant; and you shall be gathered together within your cities
Leviticus26:26 When I break your staff of bread and cut off your supply of food
Leviticus26:27 And if in spite of all this you will not listen and give heed to Me but walk contrary to Me
Leviticus26:28 Then I will walk contrary to you in wrath
Leviticus26:29 You shall eat the flesh of your sons and of your daughters. [II Kings 6:28
Leviticus26:30 And I will destroy your high places [devoted to idolatrous worship]
Leviticus26:31 I will lay your cities waste
Leviticus26:32 And I will bring the land into desolation
Leviticus26:33 I will scatter you among the nations and draw out [your enemies'] sword after you; and your land shall be desolate and your cities a waste. [Ps. 44:11-14.]
Leviticus26:34 Then shall the land [of Israel have the opportunity to] enjoy its sabbaths as long as it lies desolate and you are in your enemies' land; then shall the land rest
Leviticus26:35 As long as it lies desolate and waste
Leviticus26:36 As for those who are left of you
Leviticus26:37 They shall stumble over one another as if to escape a sword when no one pursues them; and you shall have no power to stand before your enemies.
Leviticus26:38 You shall perish among the nations; the land of your enemies shall eat you up.
Leviticus26:39 And those of you who are left shall pine away in their iniquity in your enemies' lands; also in the iniquities of their fathers shall they pine away like them.
Leviticus26:40 But if they confess their own and their fathers' iniquity in their treachery which they committed against Me–and also that because they walked contrary to Me
Leviticus26:41 I also walked contrary to them and brought them into the land of their enemies–if then their uncircumcised hearts are humbled and they then accept the punishment for their iniquity
Leviticus26:42 Then will I [earnestly] remember My covenant with Jacob
Leviticus26:43 But the land shall be left behind them and shall enjoy its sabbaths while it lies desolate without them; and they shall accept the punishment for their sins and make amends because they despised and rejected My ordinances and their soul scorned and rejected My statutes.
Leviticus26:44 And yet for all that
Leviticus26:45 But I will for their sake [earnestly] remember the covenant with their forefathers whom I brought forth out of the land of Egypt in the sight of the nations
Leviticus26:46 These are the statutes
Leviticus27:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Leviticus27:2 Say to the Israelites
Leviticus27:3 Then your valuation of a male from twenty years old to sixty years old shall be fifty shekels of silver
Leviticus27:4 And if the person is a female
Leviticus27:5 And if the person is from five years old up to twenty years old
Leviticus27:6 And if a child is from a month up to five years old
Leviticus27:7 And if the person is from sixty years old and above
Leviticus27:8 But if the man is too poor to pay your valuation
Leviticus27:9 If it is a beast of which men offer an offering to the Lord
Leviticus27:10 He shall not replace it or exchange it
Leviticus27:11 If it is an unclean animal
Leviticus27:12 And the priest shall value it
Leviticus27:13 But if he wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:14 If a man dedicates his house to be sacred to the Lord
Leviticus27:15 If he who dedicates his house wants to redeem it
Leviticus27:16 And if a man shall dedicate to the Lord some part of a field of his possession
Leviticus27:17 If he dedicates his field during the Year of Jubilee
Leviticus27:18 But if he dedicates his field after the Jubilee
Leviticus27:19 If he who dedicates the field wishes to redeem it
Leviticus27:20 But if he does not want to redeem the field
Leviticus27:21 But the field
Leviticus27:22 And if a man dedicates to the Lord a field he has bought
Leviticus27:23 The priest shall compute the amount of your valuation for it up to the Year of Jubilee; the man shall give that amount on that day as a holy thing to the Lord.
Leviticus27:24 In the Year of Jubilee the field shall return to him of whom it was bought
Leviticus27:25 And all your valuations shall be according to the sanctuary shekel; twenty gerahs shall make a shekel.
Leviticus27:26 But the firstling of the animals
Leviticus27:27 If it be of an unclean animal
Leviticus27:28 But nothing that a man shall devote to the Lord of all that he has
Leviticus27:29 No one doomed to death [under the claim of divine justice]
Leviticus27:30 And all the tithe of the land
Leviticus27:31 And if a man wants to redeem any of his tithe
Leviticus27:32 And all the tithe of the herd or of the flock
Leviticus27:33 The man shall not examine whether the animal is good or bad nor shall he exchange it. If he does exchange it
Leviticus27:34 These are the commandments which the Lord commanded Moses on Mount Sinai for the Israelites. [Rom. 10:4; Heb. 4:2; 12:18-29.]
Numbers1:1 THE
Numbers1:2 Take a census of all the males of the congregation of the Israelites by families
Numbers1:3 From twenty years old and upward
Numbers1:4 And with you there shall be a man [to assist you] from each tribe
Numbers1:5 And these are the names of the men who shall attend you: Of Reuben
Numbers1:6 Of Simeon
Numbers1:7 Of Judah
Numbers1:8 Of Issachar
Numbers1:9 Of Zebulun
Numbers1:10 Of the sons of Joseph: of Ephraim
Numbers1:11 Of Benjamin
Numbers1:12 Of Dan
Numbers1:13 Of Asher
Numbers1:14 Of Gad
Numbers1:15 Of Naphtali
Numbers1:16 These were those chosen from the congregation
Numbers1:17 And Moses and Aaron took these men who have been named
Numbers1:18 And assembled all the congregation on the first day of the second month
Numbers1:19 As the Lord commanded Moses. So he numbered them in the Wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers1:20 The sons of Reuben
Numbers1:21 Those of the tribe of Reuben numbered 46
Numbers1:22 Of the sons of Simeon
Numbers1:23 Those of the tribe of Simeon numbered 59
Numbers1:24 Of the sons of Gad
Numbers1:25 Those of the tribe of Gad numbered 45
Numbers1:26 Of the sons of Judah
Numbers1:27 Those of the tribe of Judah numbered 74
Numbers1:28 Of the sons of Issachar
Numbers1:29 Those of the tribe of Issachar numbered 54
Numbers1:30 Of the sons of Zebulun
Numbers1:31 Those of the tribe of Zebulun numbered 57
Numbers1:32 Of the sons of Joseph: the sons of Ephraim
Numbers1:33 Those of the tribe of Ephraim numbered 40
Numbers1:34 Of the sons of Manasseh
Numbers1:35 Those of the tribe of Manasseh numbered 32
Numbers1:36 Of the sons of Benjamin
Numbers1:37 Those of the tribe of Benjamin numbered 35
Numbers1:38 Of the sons of Dan
Numbers1:39 Those of the tribe of Dan numbered 62
Numbers1:40 Of the sons of Asher
Numbers1:41 Those of the tribe of Asher numbered 41
Numbers1:42 Of the sons of Naphtali
Numbers1:43 Those of the tribe of Naphtali numbered 53
Numbers1:44 These were numbered by Moses and Aaron
Numbers1:45 So all those numbered of the Israelites
Numbers1:46 All who were numbered were 603
Numbers1:47 But the Levites by their fathers' tribe were not numbered with them.
Numbers1:48 For the Lord had said to Moses
Numbers1:49 Only the tribe of Levi you shall not number in the census of the Israelites.
Numbers1:50 But appoint the Levites over the tabernacle of the Testimony
Numbers1:51 When the tabernacle is to go forward
Numbers1:52 The Israelites shall pitch their tents by their companies
Numbers1:53 But the Levites shall encamp around the tabernacle of the Testimony
Numbers1:54 Thus did the Israelites; according to all that the Lord commanded Moses
Numbers2:1 THE
Numbers2:2 The Israelites shall encamp
Numbers2:3 On the east side toward the sunrise shall they of the standard of the camp of Judah encamp by their companies; Nahshon son of Amminadab being the leader of the sons of Judah.
Numbers2:4 Judah's host as numbered totaled 74
Numbers2:5 Next to Judah the tribe of Issachar shall encamp
Numbers2:6 Issachar's host as numbered totaled 54
Numbers2:7 Then the tribe of Zebulun
Numbers2:8 Zebulun's host as numbered totaled 57
Numbers2:9 All these [three tribes] numbered in the camp of Judah totaled 186
Numbers2:10 On the south side shall be the standard of the camp of Reuben by their companies
Numbers2:11 Reuben's host as numbered totaled 46
Numbers2:12 Those who encamp next to Reuben shall be the tribe of Simeon
Numbers2:13 Simeon's host as numbered totaled 59
Numbers2:14 Then the tribe of Gad
Numbers2:15 Gad's host as numbered totaled 45
Numbers2:16 The whole number in [the three tribes of] the camp of Reuben was 151
Numbers2:17 Then the Tent of Meeting shall set out
Numbers2:18 On the west side shall be the standard of the camp of Ephraim by their companies
Numbers2:19 Ephraim's host as numbered totaled 40
Numbers2:20 Beside Ephraim shall be the tribe of Manasseh
Numbers2:21 Manasseh's host as numbered totaled 32
Numbers2:22 Then the tribe of Benjamin
Numbers2:23 Benjamin's host as numbered totaled 35
Numbers2:24 The whole number [of the three tribes] in the camp of Ephraim totaled 108
Numbers2:25 The standard of the camp of Dan shall be on the north side [of the tabernacle] by their companies
Numbers2:26 Dan's host as numbered totaled 62
Numbers2:27 Encamped next to Dan shall be the tribe of Asher
Numbers2:28 Asher's host as numbered totaled 41
Numbers2:29 Then the tribe of Naphtali
Numbers2:30 Naphtali's host as numbered totaled 53
Numbers2:31 The whole number [of the three tribes] in the camp of Dan totaled 157
Numbers2:32 These are the Israelites as numbered by their fathers' houses. All in the camps who were numbered by their companies were 603
Numbers2:33 But the Levites were not numbered with the Israelites
Numbers2:34 Thus the Israelites did according to all the Lord commanded Moses; so they encamped by their standards
Numbers3:1 NOW THESE are the generations of Aaron and Moses when the Lord spoke with Moses on Mount Sinai.
Numbers3:2 These are the names of the sons of Aaron: Nadab the firstborn
Numbers3:3 These are the names of the sons of Aaron
Numbers3:4 But Nadab and Abihu died before the Lord when they offered strange fire before the Lord in the Wilderness of Sinai; and they had no children. So Eleazar and Ithamar ministered in the priest's office in the presence and under the supervision of Aaron their father. [Lev. 10:1-4.]
Numbers3:5 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:6 Bring the tribe of Levi near and set them before Aaron the priest
Numbers3:7 And they shall carry out his instructions and the duties connected with the whole assembly before the Tent of Meeting
Numbers3:8 And they shall keep all the instruments and furnishings of the Tent of Meeting and take charge of [attending] the Israelites
Numbers3:9 And you shall give the Levites [as servants and helpers] to Aaron and his sons; they are wholly given to him from among the Israelites.
Numbers3:10 And you shall appoint Aaron and his sons
Numbers3:11 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:12 Behold
Numbers3:13 For all the firstborn are Mine. On the day that I slew all the firstborn in the land of Egypt
Numbers3:14 And the Lord said to Moses in the Wilderness of Sinai
Numbers3:15 Number the sons of Levi by their fathers' houses and by families. Every male from a month old and upward you shall number.
Numbers3:16 So Moses numbered them as he was commanded by the word of the Lord.
Numbers3:17 These were the sons of Levi by their names: Gershon
Numbers3:18 And these are the names of the sons of Gershon by their families: Libni and Shimei.
Numbers3:19 The sons of Kohath by their families: Amram
Numbers3:20 The sons of Merari by their families: Mahli and Mushi. These are the families of the Levites by their fathers' houses.
Numbers3:21 Of Gershon were the families of the Libnites and of the Shimeites. These are the families of the Gershonites.
Numbers3:22 The males who were numbered of them from a month old and upward totaled 7
Numbers3:23 The families of the Gershonites were to encamp behind the tabernacle on the west
Numbers3:24 The leader of the fathers' houses of the Gershonites being Eliasaph son of Lael.
Numbers3:25 And the responsibility of the sons of Gershon in the Tent of Meeting was to be the tabernacle
Numbers3:26 And the hangings of the court
Numbers3:27 Of Kohath were the families of the Amramites
Numbers3:28 The number of all the males from a month old and upward totaled 8
Numbers3:29 The families of the sons of Kohath were to encamp on the south side of the tabernacle
Numbers3:30 The chief of the fathers' houses of the families of the Kohathites being Elizaphan son of Uzziel.
Numbers3:31 Their charge was to be the ark
Numbers3:32 Eleazar son of Aaron the priest was to be chief over the leaders of the Levites
Numbers3:33 Of Merari were the families of the Mahlites and the Mushites; these are the families of Merari.
Numbers3:34 Their number of all the males from a month old and upward totaled 6
Numbers3:35 And the head of the fathers' houses of the families of Merari was Zuriel son of Abihail; the Merarites were to encamp on the north side of the tabernacle.
Numbers3:36 And the appointed charge of the sons of Merari was the boards or frames of the tabernacle
Numbers3:37 And the pillars of the surrounding court and their sockets or bases
Numbers3:38 But those to encamp before the tabernacle toward the east
Numbers3:39 All the Levites whom Moses and Aaron numbered at the command of the Lord
Numbers3:40 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:41 You shall take the Levites for Me instead of all the firstborn among the Israelites. I am the Lord; and you shall take the cattle of the Levites for Me instead of all the firstlings among the cattle of the Israelites.
Numbers3:42 So Moses numbered
Numbers3:43 But all the firstborn males from a month old and upward as numbered were 22
Numbers3:44 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers3:45 Take the Levites [for Me] instead of all the firstborn Israelites
Numbers3:46 And for those 273 who are to be redeemed of the firstborn of the Israelites who outnumber the Levites
Numbers3:47 You shall take five shekels apiece
Numbers3:48 And you shall give the ransom silver from the excess number [over the Levites] to be redeemed to Aaron and his sons.
Numbers3:49 So Moses took the redemption money from those who were left over from the number who were redeemed by the Levites.
Numbers3:50 From the firstborn of the Israelites he took the money
Numbers3:51 And Moses gave the money from those who were ransomed to Aaron and his sons
Numbers4:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:2 Take a census of the Kohathite division among the sons of Levi
Numbers4:3 From thirty years old and up to fifty years old
Numbers4:4 This shall be the responsibility of the sons of Kohath in the Tent of Meeting: the most holy things.
Numbers4:5 When the camp prepares to set forward
Numbers4:6 And shall put on it the covering of dolphin or porpoise skin
Numbers4:7 And upon the table of showbread they shall spread a cloth of blue and put on it the plates
Numbers4:8 And they shall spread over them a cloth of scarlet
Numbers4:9 And they shall take a cloth of blue and cover the lampstand for the light and its lamps
Numbers4:10 And they shall put the lampstand and all its utensils within a covering of dolphin or porpoise skin and shall put it upon the frame [for carrying].
Numbers4:11 And upon the golden [incense] altar they shall spread a cloth of blue
Numbers4:12 And they shall take all the utensils of the service with which they minister in the sanctuary
Numbers4:13 And they shall take away the ashes from the altar [of burnt offering] and spread a purple cloth over it.
Numbers4:14 And they shall put upon it all its vessels and utensils with which they minister there
Numbers4:15 When Aaron and his sons have finished covering the sanctuary and all its furniture
Numbers4:16 And Eleazar son of Aaron the priest shall have charge of the oil for the light
Numbers4:17 And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers4:18 [Since] the tribe of the families of the Kohathites [are only Levites and not priests]
Numbers4:19 But deal thus with them
Numbers4:20 But [the Kohathites] shall not go in to see the sanctuary [the Holy Place and the Holy of Holies] or its holy things
Numbers4:21 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers4:22 Take a census of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:23 From thirty years old and up to fifty years old you shall number them
Numbers4:24 This is the service of the families of the Gershonites
Numbers4:25 And they shall carry the curtains of the tabernacle
Numbers4:26 And the hangings of the court
Numbers4:27 Under the direction of Aaron and his sons shall be all the service of the sons of the Gershonites
Numbers4:28 This is the service of the families of the sons of Gershon in the Tent of Meeting; and their work shall be under the direction of Ithamar son of Aaron
Numbers4:29 As for the sons of Merari
Numbers4:30 From thirty years old up to fifty years old you shall number them
Numbers4:31 And this is what they are assigned to carry and to guard [on the march]
Numbers4:32 And the pillars of the court round about with their sockets or bases
Numbers4:33 This is the work of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:34 And Moses and Aaron and the leaders of the congregation numbered the sons of the Kohathites by their families and their fathers' houses
Numbers4:35 From thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:36 And those who were numbered of them by their families were 2
Numbers4:37 These were numbered of the families of the Kohathites
Numbers4:38 And those that were numbered of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:39 From thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:40 Those who were enrolled of them
Numbers4:41 These were numbered of the families of the sons of Gershon
Numbers4:42 And those numbered of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:43 From thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:44 Even those who were numbered of them by their families
Numbers4:45 These are those who were numbered of the families of the sons of Merari
Numbers4:46 All those who were numbered of the Levites
Numbers4:47 From thirty years old up to fifty years old
Numbers4:48 Those that were numbered of them were 8
Numbers4:49 According to the command of the Lord through Moses
Numbers5:1 THE
Numbers5:2 Command the Israelites that they put outside the camp every leper and everyone who has a discharge
Numbers5:3 Both male and female you shall put out; without the camp you shall put them
Numbers5:4 The Israelites did so
Numbers5:5 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:6 Say to the Israelites
Numbers5:7 Then he shall confess the sin which he has committed
Numbers5:8 But if the man [wronged] has no kinsman to whom the restitution may be made
Numbers5:9 And every offering of all the holy things of the Israelites which they bring to the priest shall be his.
Numbers5:10 And every man's hallowed things shall be the priest's; whatever any man gives the priest shall be his.
Numbers5:11 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers5:12 Say to the Israelites
Numbers5:13 And a man lies with her carnally
Numbers5:14 And if the spirit of jealousy comes upon him and he is jealous and suspicious of his wife who has defiled herself–or if the spirit of jealousy comes upon him and he is jealous and suspicious of his wife though she has not defiled herself–
Numbers5:15 Then shall the man bring his wife to the priest
Numbers5:16 And the priest shall bring her near and set her before the Lord.
Numbers5:17 And the priest shall take holy water [probably from the sacred laver] in an earthen vessel and take some of the dust that is on the floor of the tabernacle and put it in the water.
Numbers5:18 And the priest shall set the woman before the Lord
Numbers5:19 Then the priest shall make her take an oath
Numbers5:20 But if you have gone astray and you are defiled
Numbers5:21 Then the priest shall make the woman take the oath of the curse
Numbers5:22 May this water that brings the curse go into your bowels and make your body swell and your thigh fall away. And the woman shall say
Numbers5:23 The priest shall then write these curses in a book and shall wash them off into the water of bitterness;
Numbers5:24 And he shall cause the woman to drink the water of bitterness that brings the curse
Numbers5:25 Then the priest shall take the cereal offering of jealousy and suspicion out of the woman's hand and shall wave the offering before the Lord and offer it upon the altar.
Numbers5:26 And the priest shall take a handful of the cereal offering as the memorial portion of it and burn it on the altar
Numbers5:27 And when he has made her drink the water
Numbers5:28 But if the woman is not defiled and is clean
Numbers5:29 This is the law of jealousy and suspicion when a wife goes aside to another instead of her husband and is defiled
Numbers5:30 Or when the spirit of jealousy and suspicion comes upon a man and he is jealous and suspicious of his wife; then shall he set the woman before the Lord
Numbers5:31 The [husband] shall be free from iniquity and guilt
Numbers6:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:2 Say to the Israelites
Numbers6:3 He shall separate himself from wine and strong drink; he shall drink no vinegar of wine or of strong drink
Numbers6:4 All the days of his separation he shall eat nothing produced from the grapevine
Numbers6:5 All the days of the vow of his separation and abstinence there shall no razor come upon his head. Until the time is completed for which he separates himself to the Lord
Numbers6:6 All the days that he separates himself to the Lord he shall not go near a dead body.
Numbers6:7 He shall not make himself unclean for his father
Numbers6:8 All the days of his separation and abstinence he is holy to the Lord.
Numbers6:9 And if any man dies very suddenly beside him
Numbers6:10 On the eighth day he shall bring two turtledoves or two young pigeons to the priest to the door of the Tent of Meeting
Numbers6:11 And the priest shall offer the one for a sin offering and the other for a burnt offering and make atonement for him because he sinned by reason of the dead body. He shall consecrate his head the same day
Numbers6:12 And he shall consecrate and separate himself to the Lord for the days of his separation and shall bring a male lamb a year old for a trespass or guilt offering; but the previous days shall be void and lost
Numbers6:13 And this is the law of the Nazirite when the days of his separation and abstinence are fulfilled. He shall be brought to the door of the Tent of Meeting
Numbers6:14 And he shall offer his gift to the Lord
Numbers6:15 And a basket of unleavened bread
Numbers6:16 And the priest shall present them before the Lord and shall offer the person's sin offering and his burnt offering.
Numbers6:17 And he shall offer the ram for a sacrifice of peace offering to the Lord
Numbers6:18 And the Nazirite shall shave his consecrated head at the door of the Tent of Meeting
Numbers6:19 And the priest shall take the boiled shoulder of the ram
Numbers6:20 And the priest shall wave them for a wave offering before the Lord; they are a holy portion for the priest
Numbers6:21 This is the law for the Nazirite who has made a vow. His offering to the Lord
Numbers6:22 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers6:23 Say to Aaron and his sons
Numbers6:24 The Lord bless you and watch
Numbers6:25 The Lord make His face to shine upon and enlighten you and be gracious (kind
Numbers6:26 The Lord lift up His [approving] countenance upon you and give you peace (tranquility of heart and life continually).

Numbers8:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:2 Say to Aaron
Numbers8:3 And Aaron did so; he lighted the lamps of the lampstand to give light in front of it
Numbers8:4 And this was the workmanship of the candlestick: beaten or turned gold
Numbers8:5 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:6 Take the Levites from among the Israelites and cleanse them.
Numbers8:7 And thus you shall do to them to cleanse them: sprinkle the water of purification [water to be used in case of sin] upon them
Numbers8:8 Then let them take a young bull and its cereal offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers8:9 You shall present the Levites before the Tent of Meeting
Numbers8:10 And you shall present the Levites before the Lord
Numbers8:11 And Aaron shall offer the Levites before the Lord as a wave offering from the Israelites and on their behalf
Numbers8:12 Then the Levites shall lay their hands upon the heads of the bulls
Numbers8:13 And you shall present the Levites before Aaron and his sons and offer them as a wave offering to the Lord.
Numbers8:14 Thus you shall separate the Levites from among the Israelites
Numbers8:15 And after that the Levites shall go in to do service at the Tent of Meeting
Numbers8:16 For they are wholly given to Me from among the Israelites; instead of all who open the womb
Numbers8:17 For all the firstborn of the Israelites are Mine
Numbers8:18 And I have taken the Levites instead of all the firstborn of the Israelites.
Numbers8:19 And I have given the Levites as a gift to Aaron and to his sons from among the Israelites to do the service of the Israelites at the Tent of Meeting and to make atonement for them
Numbers8:20 So Moses and Aaron and all the congregation of the Israelites did thus to the Levites; according to all that the Lord commanded Moses concerning [them]
Numbers8:21 The Levites cleansed and purified themselves and they washed their clothes; and Aaron offered them as a wave offering before the Lord and Aaron made atonement for them to cleanse them.
Numbers8:22 And after that the Levites went in to do their service in the Tent of Meeting with the attendance of Aaron and his sons; as the Lord had commanded Moses concerning the Levites
Numbers8:23 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers8:24 This is what applies to the Levites: from twenty-five years old and upward they shall go in to perform the work of the service of the Tent of Meeting
Numbers8:25 And at the age of fifty years
Numbers8:26 But shall help their brethren in the Tent of Meeting [attend to protecting the sacred things from being profaned]

Numbers15:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:2 Say to the Israelites
Numbers15:3 And will make an offering by fire to the Lord from the herd or from the flock
Numbers15:4 Then shall he who brings his offering to the Lord bring a cereal offering of a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a fourth of a hin of oil.
Numbers15:5 And a fourth of a hin of wine for the drink offering you shall prepare with the burnt offering or for the sacrifice
Numbers15:6 Or for a ram you shall prepare for a cereal offering two tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with a third of a hin of oil.
Numbers15:7 And for the drink offering you shall offer a third of a hin of wine
Numbers15:8 And when you prepare a bull for a burnt offering or for a sacrifice
Numbers15:9 Then shall one offer with the bull a cereal offering of three tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with half a hin of oil.
Numbers15:10 And you shall bring for the drink offering half a hin of wine for an offering made by fire
Numbers15:11 Thus shall it be done for each bull or for each ram
Numbers15:12 According to the number that you shall prepare
Numbers15:13 All who are native-born shall do these things in this way in bringing an offering made by fire of a sweet and pleasant odor to the Lord.
Numbers15:14 And if a stranger sojourns with you or whoever may be among you throughout your generations
Numbers15:15 There shall be one [and the same] statute [both] for you [of the congregation] and for the stranger who is a temporary resident with you
Numbers15:16 One law and one ordinance shall be for you and for the stranger who sojourns with you.
Numbers15:17 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers15:18 Say to the Israelites
Numbers15:19 Then
Numbers15:20 You shall set apart a cake made of the first of your coarse meal as a gift [to the Lord]; as an offering set apart from the threshing floor
Numbers15:21 Of the first of your coarse meal you shall give to the Lord a portion for a gift throughout your generations [your heave or lifted-out offering].
Numbers15:22 When you have erred and have not observed all these commandments which the Lord has spoken to Moses
Numbers15:23 Even all that the Lord has commanded you through Moses
Numbers15:24 Then it shall be
Numbers15:25 And the priest shall make atonement for all the congregation of the Israelites
Numbers15:26 And all the congregation of the Israelites shall be forgiven and the stranger who lives temporarily among them
Numbers15:27 And if any person sins unknowingly or unintentionally
Numbers15:28 And the priest shall make atonement before the Lord for the person who commits an error when he sins unknowingly or unintentionally
Numbers15:29 You shall have one law for him who sins unknowingly or unintentionally
Numbers15:30 But the person who does anything [wrong] willfully and openly
Numbers15:31 Because he has despised and rejected the word of the Lord
Numbers15:32 While the Israelites were in the wilderness
Numbers15:33 Those who found him gathering sticks brought him to Moses and Aaron and to all the congregation.
Numbers15:34 They put him in custody

Numbers15:36 And all the congregation brought him without the camp and stoned him to death with stones

Numbers15:39 And it shall be to you a fringe or tassel that you may look upon and remember all the commandments of the Lord and do them
Numbers15:40 That you may remember and do all My commandments and be holy to your God.
Numbers15:41 I am the Lord your God

Numbers16:10 And that He has brought you near to Him
Numbers16:11 Therefore you and all your company are gathered together against the Lord. And Aaron
Numbers16:12 And Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram
Numbers16:13 Is it a small thing that you have brought us up out of a land flowing with milk and honey to kill us in the wilderness
Numbers16:14 Moreover
Numbers16:15 And Moses was very angry and said to the Lord
Numbers16:16 And Moses said to Korah
Numbers16:17 And let every man take his censer and put incense upon it and bring before the Lord every man his censer
Numbers16:18 So they took every man his censer
Numbers16:19 Then Korah assembled all the congregation against Moses and Aaron before the entrance of the Tent of Meeting
Numbers16:20 And the Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers16:21 Separate yourselves from among this congregation
Numbers16:22 And they fell upon their faces
Numbers16:23 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers16:24 Say to the congregation
Numbers16:25 Then Moses rose up and went to Dathan and Abiram
Numbers16:26 And he said to the congregation
Numbers16:27 So they got away from around the tents of Korah
Numbers16:28 And Moses said
Numbers16:29 If these men die the common death of all men or if [only] what happens to everyone happens to them
Numbers16:30 But if the Lord causes a new thing [to happen]
Numbers16:31 As soon as he stopped speaking
Numbers16:32 And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them and their households and [Korah and] all [his] men and all their possessions. [Num. 26:10
Numbers16:33 They and all that belonged to them went down alive into Sheol (the place of the dead); and the earth closed upon them
Numbers16:34 And all Israel who were round about them fled at their cry
Numbers16:35 And fire came forth from the Lord and devoured the 250 men who offered the incense.
Numbers16:36 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers16:37 Speak to Eleazar son of Aaron
Numbers16:38 The censers of these men who have sinned against themselves and at the cost of their own lives. Let the censers be made into hammered plates for a covering of the altar [of burnt offering]
Numbers16:39 Eleazar the priest took the bronze censers with which the Levites who were burned had offered incense
Numbers16:40 To be a memorial [a warning forever] to the Israelites
Numbers16:41 But on the morrow all the congregation of the Israelites murmured against Moses and Aaron
Numbers16:42 When the congregation was gathered against Moses and Aaron
Numbers16:43 And Moses and Aaron came to the front of the Tent of Meeting.

Numbers16:45 Get away from among this congregation
Numbers16:46 And Moses said to Aaron
Numbers16:47 So Aaron took the burning censer as Moses commanded
Numbers16:48 And he stood between the dead and the living
Numbers16:49 Now those who died in the plague were 14

Numbers18:1 AND THE Lord said to Aaron
Numbers18:2 And your brethren also of the tribe of Levi
Numbers18:3 And the Levites shall attend you [as servants] and attend to all the duties of the Tent; only they shall not come near the sacred vessels of the sanctuary or to the brazen altar
Numbers18:4 And they shall be joined to you and attend to the duties of the Tent of Meeting–all the [menial] service of the Tent–and no stranger [no layman
Numbers18:5 And you shall attend to the duties of the sanctuary and attend to the altar [of burnt offering and the altar of incense]
Numbers18:6 And I
Numbers18:7 Therefore you and your sons with you shall attend to your priesthood for everything of the altar [of burnt offering and the altar of incense] and [of the Holy of Holies] within the veil
Numbers18:8 And the Lord said to Aaron
Numbers18:9 This shall be yours of the most holy things
Numbers18:10 As the most holy thing and in a sacred place shall you eat of it; every male [of your house] shall eat of it. It shall be holy to you. [Lev. 22:10-16.]
Numbers18:11 And this also is yours: the heave offering of their gift
Numbers18:12 All the best of the oil
Numbers18:13 Whatever is first ripe in the land
Numbers18:14 Every devoted thing in Israel [everything that has been vowed to the Lord] shall be yours.
Numbers18:15 Everything that first opens the womb in all flesh
Numbers18:16 And those that are to be redeemed of them
Numbers18:17 But the firstling of a cow or of a sheep or of a goat you shall not redeem. They [as the firstborn of clean beasts belong to God and] are holy. You shall sprinkle their blood upon the altar and shall burn their fat for an offering made by fire
Numbers18:18 And the flesh of them shall be yours
Numbers18:19 All the heave offerings [the lifted-out and kept portions] of the holy things which the Israelites give to the Lord I give to you and to your sons and your daughters with you
Numbers18:20 And the Lord said to Aaron
Numbers18:21 And
Numbers18:22 Henceforth the Israelites shall not come near the Tent of Meeting [the covered sanctuary
Numbers18:23 But the Levites shall do the [menial] service of the Tent of Meeting
Numbers18:24 But the tithes of the Israelites
Numbers18:25 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers18:26 Moreover
Numbers18:27 And what you lift out and keep [your heave offering] shall be credited to you as though it were the grain of the threshing floor or as the fully ripe produce of the vine.
Numbers18:28 Likewise you shall also present an offering to the Lord of all your tithes which you receive from the Israelites; and therefore you shall give this heave offering [lifted out and kept] for the Lord to Aaron the priest.
Numbers18:29 Out of all the gifts to you
Numbers18:30 Therefore you shall say to them
Numbers18:31 And you may eat it in every place
Numbers18:32 And you shall be guilty of no sin by reason of it when you have lifted out and held back the best of it; neither shall you have polluted the holy things of the Israelites
Numbers19:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses and Aaron
Numbers19:2 This is the ritual of the law which the Lord has commanded: Tell the Israelites to bring you a red heifer without spot
Numbers19:3 And you shall give her to Eleazar the priest
Numbers19:4 Eleazar the priest shall take some of her blood with his finger and sprinkle it toward the front of the Tent of Meeting seven times.
Numbers19:5 The heifer shall be burned in his sight
Numbers19:6 And the priest shall take cedar wood
Numbers19:7 Then the priest shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water; afterward he shall come into the camp
Numbers19:8 He who burns the heifer shall wash his clothes and bathe his body in water
Numbers19:9 And a man who is clean shall collect the ashes of the heifer and put them outside the camp in a clean place
Numbers19:10 And he who gathers the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:11 He who touches the dead body of any person shall be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:12 He shall purify himself with the water for impurity [made with the ashes of the burned heifer] on the third day
Numbers19:13 Whoever touches the corpse of any who has died and does not purify himself defiles the tabernacle of the Lord
Numbers19:14 This is the law when a man dies in a tent: all who come into the tent and all who are in the tent shall be unclean for seven days.
Numbers19:15 And every open vessel
Numbers19:16 And whoever in the open field touches one who is slain with a sword
Numbers19:17 And for the unclean
Numbers19:18 And a clean person shall take hyssop and dip it in the water and sprinkle it upon the tent
Numbers19:19 And the clean person shall sprinkle [the water for purification] upon the unclean person on the third day and on the seventh day
Numbers19:20 But the man who is unclean and does not purify himself
Numbers19:21 And it shall be a perpetual statute to them. He who sprinkles the water for impurity [upon another] shall wash his clothes
Numbers19:22 And whatever the unclean person touches shall be unclean
