Numbers28:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses
Numbers28:2 Command the Israelites
Numbers28:3 And you shall say to the people
Numbers28:4 One lamb you shall offer in the morning and the other in the evening
Numbers28:5 Also a tenth of an ephah of flour for a cereal offering
Numbers28:6 It is a continual burnt offering which was ordained in Mount Sinai for a sweet and soothing odor
Numbers28:7 Its drink offering shall be a fourth of a hin for each lamb; in the Holy Place you shall pour out a fermented drink offering to the Lord.
Numbers28:8 And the other lamb you shall offer in the evening; like the cereal offering of the morning and like its drink offering
Numbers28:9 And on the Sabbath day two male lambs a year old without spot or blemish
Numbers28:10 This is the burnt offering of every Sabbath
Numbers28:11 And at the beginning of your months you shall offer a burnt offering to the Lord: two young bulls
Numbers28:12 And three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour for a cereal offering
Numbers28:13 And a tenth part of fine flour mixed with oil as a cereal offering
Numbers28:14 And their drink offerings shall be half a hin of wine for a bull
Numbers28:15 And one male goat for a sin offering to the Lord–it shall be offered in addition to the continual burnt offering and its drink offering.
Numbers28:16 On the fourteenth day of the first month is the Lord's Passover.
Numbers28:17 On the fifteenth day of this month is a feast; for seven days shall unleavened bread be eaten.
Numbers28:18 On the first day there shall be a holy [summoned] assembly; you shall do no servile work that day.
Numbers28:19 But you shall offer an offering made by fire
Numbers28:20 And their cereal offering shall be of fine flour mixed with oil; three-tenths of an ephah shall you offer for a bull
Numbers28:21 A tenth shall you offer for each of the seven male lambs
Numbers28:22 Also one male goat for a sin offering to make atonement for you.
Numbers28:23 You shall offer these in addition to the burnt offering of the morning
Numbers28:24 In this way you shall offer daily for seven days the food of an offering made by fire
Numbers28:25 And on the seventh day you shall have a holy [summoned] assembly; you shall do no work befitting a slave or a servant.
Numbers28:26 Also in the day of the firstfruits
Numbers28:27 But you shall offer the burnt offering for a sweet
Numbers28:28 And their cereal offering of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers28:29 A tenth for each of the seven male lambs
Numbers28:30 And one male goat to make atonement for you.
Numbers28:31 You shall offer them in addition to the continual burnt offering and its cereal offering and their drink offerings. See that they are without blemish.
Numbers29:1 ON THE first day of the seventh month [on New Year's Day of the civil year]
Numbers29:2 And you shall offer a burnt offering for a sweet and pleasing odor to the Lord: one young bull
Numbers29:3 Their cereal offering shall be of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers29:4 And one-tenth of an ephah for each of the seven lambs
Numbers29:5 And one male goat for a sin offering to make atonement for you.
Numbers29:6 These are in addition to the burnt offering of the new moon and its cereal offering
Numbers29:7 And you shall have on the tenth day of this seventh month a holy [summoned] assembly; [it is the great Day of Atonement
Numbers29:8 But you shall offer a burnt offering to the Lord for a sweet and soothing fragrance: one young bull
Numbers29:9 And their cereal offering shall be of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers29:10 A tenth for each of the seven male lambs
Numbers29:11 One male goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:12 And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month you shall have a holy [summoned] assembly; you shall do no servile work
Numbers29:13 And you shall offer a burnt offering
Numbers29:14 And their cereal offering shall be of fine flour mixed with oil
Numbers29:15 And a tenth part for each of the fourteen male lambs
Numbers29:16 Also one male goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:17 And on the second day you shall offer twelve young bulls
Numbers29:18 With their cereal offering and the drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:19 Also one male goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:20 And on the third day eleven bulls
Numbers29:21 With their cereal offering and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:22 And one male goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:23 On the fourth day ten bulls
Numbers29:24 Their cereal offering and their drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:25 And one male goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:26 And on the fifth day nine bulls
Numbers29:27 And their cereal offering and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:28 And one goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:29 And on the sixth day eight bulls
Numbers29:30 And their cereal offering and their drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:31 And one goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:32 And on the seventh day seven bulls
Numbers29:33 And their cereal and drink offerings for the bulls
Numbers29:34 And one male goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:35 On the eighth day you shall have a solemn assembly; you shall do no servile work.
Numbers29:36 You shall offer a burnt offering
Numbers29:37 Their cereal offering and drink offerings for the bull
Numbers29:38 And one male goat for a sin offering
Numbers29:39 These you shall offer to the Lord at your appointed feasts
Numbers29:40 And Moses told the Israelites all that the Lord commanded him.
Numbers30:1 AND MOSES said to the heads or leaders of the tribes of Israel
Numbers30:2 If a man vows a vow to the Lord or swears an oath to bind himself by a pledge
Numbers30:3 Also when a woman vows a vow to the Lord and binds herself by a pledge
Numbers30:4 And her father hears her vow and her pledge with which she has bound herself and he offers no objection
Numbers30:5 But if her father refuses to allow her [to carry out her vow] on the day that he hears about it
Numbers30:6 And if she is married to a husband while her vows are upon her or she has bound herself by a rash utterance
Numbers30:7 And her husband hears of it and holds his peace concerning it on the day that he hears it
Numbers30:8 But if her husband refuses to allow her [to keep her vow or pledge] on the day that he hears of it
Numbers30:9 But the vow of a widow or of a divorced woman
Numbers30:10 And if she vowed in her husband's house or bound herself by a pledge with an oath
Numbers30:11 And her husband heard it and did not oppose or prohibit her
Numbers30:12 But if her husband positively made them void on the day he heard them
Numbers30:13 Every vow and every binding oath to humble or afflict herself
Numbers30:14 But if her husband altogether holds his peace [concerning the matter] with her from day to day
Numbers30:15 But if he shall nullify them after he hears of them
Numbers30:16 These are the statutes which the Lord commanded Moses

Numbers33:1 THESE ARE the stages of the journeys of the Israelites by which they went out of the land of Egypt by their hosts under the leadership of Moses and Aaron.
Numbers33:2 Moses recorded their starting places
Numbers33:3 They set out from Rameses on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the day after the Passover the Israelites went out [of Egypt] with a high hand and triumphantly in the sight of all the Egyptians
Numbers33:4 While the Egyptians were burying all their firstborn whom the Lord had struck down among them; upon their gods also the Lord executed judgments.
Numbers33:5 The Israelites set out from Rameses and encamped in Succoth.
Numbers33:6 And they departed from Succoth and encamped in Etham
Numbers33:7 They set out from Etham and turned back to Pi-hahiroth
Numbers33:8 And they journeyed from before Pi-hahiroth and passed through the midst of the [Red] Sea into the wilderness; and they went a three days' journey in the Wilderness of Etham and encamped at Marah.
Numbers33:9 They journeyed from Marah and came to Elim; at Elim there were twelve springs of water and seventy palm trees
Numbers33:10 They set out from Elim and encamped by the Red Sea.
Numbers33:11 They journeyed from the Red Sea and encamped in the Wilderness of Sin.
Numbers33:12 And they traveled on from the Wilderness of Sin and encamped at Dophkah.
Numbers33:13 And they departed from Dophkah and encamped at Alush.
Numbers33:14 And they set out from Alush and encamped at Rephidim
Numbers33:15 And they departed from Rephidim and encamped in the Wilderness of Sinai.
Numbers33:16 And they journeyed from the Wilderness of Sinai and encamped at Kibroth-hattaavah.
Numbers33:17 And they traveled on from Kibroth-hattaavah and encamped at Hazeroth.
Numbers33:18 And they journeyed from Hazeroth and encamped at Rithmah.
Numbers33:19 And they departed from Rithmah and encamped at Rimmon-perez.
Numbers33:20 And they departed from Rimmon-perez and encamped at Libnah.
Numbers33:21 And they removed from Libnah and encamped at Rissah.
Numbers33:22 And they journeyed from Rissah and encamped at Kehelathah.
Numbers33:23 And they went from Kehelathah and encamped at Mount Shepher.
Numbers33:24 And they removed from Mount Shepher and encamped at Haradah.
Numbers33:25 And they set out from Haradah and encamped at Makheloth.
Numbers33:26 And they removed from Makheloth and encamped at Tahath.
Numbers33:27 And they departed from Tahath and encamped at Terah.
Numbers33:28 And they removed from Terah and encamped at Mithkah.
Numbers33:29 And they set out from Mithkah and encamped at Hashmonah.
Numbers33:30 And they traveled on from Hashmonah and encamped at Moseroth.
Numbers33:31 And they journeyed from Moseroth and pitched in Bene-jaakan.
Numbers33:32 And they set out from Bene-jaakan and encamped at Hor-haggidgad.
Numbers33:33 And they set out from Hor-haggidgad and encamped at Jotbathah.
Numbers33:34 And they journeyed from Jotbathah and encamped at Abronah.
Numbers33:35 And they traveled on from Abronah and encamped at Ezion-geber.
Numbers33:36 And they removed from Ezion-geber and encamped in the Wilderness of Zin
Numbers33:37 And they removed from Kadesh and encamped at Mount Hor
Numbers33:38 Aaron the priest went up on Mount Hor at the command of the Lord
Numbers33:39 Aaron was 123 years old when he died on Mount Hor.
Numbers33:40 The Canaanite king of Arad
Numbers33:41 They set out from Mount Hor and encamped at Zalmonah.
Numbers33:42 And they set out from Zalmonah and encamped at Punon.
Numbers33:43 And they set out from Punon and encamped at Oboth.
Numbers33:44 And they traveled on from Oboth and encamped at Iye-abarim
Numbers33:45 And they departed from Iyim and encamped at Dibon-gad.
Numbers33:46 And they set out from Dibon-gad and encamped in Almon-diblathaim.
Numbers33:47 And they traveled on from Almon-diblathaim and encamped in the mountains of Abarim
Numbers33:48 And they departed from the mountains of Abarim and encamped in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho.
Numbers33:49 And they encamped by the Jordan from Beth-jeshimoth as far as Abel-shittim in the plains of Moab.
Numbers33:50 And the Lord said to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho
Numbers33:51 Tell the Israelites
Numbers33:52 Then you shall drive out all the inhabitants of the land before you and destroy all their figured stones and all their molten images and completely demolish all their [idolatrous] high places
Numbers33:53 And you shall take possession of the land and dwell in it
Numbers33:54 You shall inherit the land by lot according to your families; to the large tribe you shall give a larger inheritance
Numbers33:55 But if you will not drive out the inhabitants of the land from before you
Numbers33:56 And as I thought to do to them

Numbers35:1 AND THE Lord said to Moses in the plains of Moab by the Jordan at Jericho
Numbers35:2 Command the Israelites that they give to the Levites from the inheritance of their possession cities to dwell in; and [suburb] pasturelands round about the cities' walls you shall give to the Levites also.
Numbers35:3 They shall have the cities to dwell in and their [suburb] pasturelands shall be for their cattle
Numbers35:4 And the pasturelands of the cities which you shall give to the Levites shall reach from the wall of the city and outward 1
Numbers35:5 You shall measure from the wall of the city outward on the east
Numbers35:6 Of the cities which you shall give to the Levites there shall be the six cities of refuge
Numbers35:7 So all the cities which you shall give to the Levites shall be forty-eight; you shall give them with their adjacent [suburb] pasturelands.
Numbers35:8 As for the cities
Numbers35:9 And the Lord said to Moses
Numbers35:10 Say to the Israelites
Numbers35:11 Then you shall select cities to be cities of refuge for you
Numbers35:12 And the cities shall be to you for refuge from the avenger
Numbers35:13 And of the cities which you give there shall be your six cities for refuge.
Numbers35:14 You shall give three cities on this [east] side of the Jordan and three cities in the land of Canaan
Numbers35:15 These six cities shall be a refuge for the Israelites and for the stranger and the temporary resident among them; that anyone who kills any person unintentionally and unawares may flee there.
Numbers35:16 But if he struck him down with an instrument of iron so that he died
Numbers35:17 And if he struck him down by throwing a stone
Numbers35:18 Or if he struck him down with a weapon of wood in his hand
Numbers35:19 The avenger of blood shall himself slay the murderer; when he meets him
Numbers35:20 But if he stabbed him through hatred or hurled at him by lying in wait so that he died
Numbers35:21 Or in enmity struck him down with his hand so that he died
Numbers35:22 But if he stabbed him suddenly without enmity or threw anything at or upon him without lying in wait
Numbers35:23 Or with any stone with which a man may be killed
Numbers35:24 Then the congregation shall judge between the slayer and the avenger of blood according to these ordinances.
Numbers35:25 And the congregation shall rescue the manslayer from the hand of the avenger of blood and restore him to his city of refuge to which he had fled; and he shall live in it until the high priest dies
Numbers35:26 But if the slayer shall at any time come outside the limits of his city of refuge to which he had fled
Numbers35:27 And the avenger of blood finds him outside the limits of his city of refuge and kills the manslayer
Numbers35:28 Because the manslayer should have remained in his city of refuge until the death of the high priest. But after the high priest's death the manslayer shall return to the land of his possession.
Numbers35:29 And these things shall be for a statute and ordinance to you throughout your generations in all your dwellings.
Numbers35:30 Whoever kills any person [intentionally]
Numbers35:31 Moreover
Numbers35:32 And you shall accept no ransom for him who has fled to his city of refuge
Numbers35:33 So you shall not pollute the land in which you live; for blood pollutes the land
Numbers35:34 And you shall not defile the land in which you live

Deuteronomy14:3 You shall not eat anything that is abominable [to the Lord and so forbidden by Him].
Deuteronomy14:4 These are the beasts which you may eat: the ox
Deuteronomy14:5 The hart
Deuteronomy14:6 And every beast that parts the hoof and has it divided into two and brings up and chews the cud among the beasts you may eat.

Deuteronomy14:8 And the swine
Deuteronomy14:9 These you may eat of all that are in the waters: whatever has fins and scales you may eat
Deuteronomy14:10 And whatever has not fins and scales you may not eat; it is unclean for you.

Deuteronomy14:12 But these are the ones which you shall not eat: the eagle

Deuteronomy14:16 The little owl
Deuteronomy14:17 The pelican
Deuteronomy14:18 The stork
Deuteronomy14:19 And all flying insects are unclean for you; they shall not be eaten.
Deuteronomy14:20 But of all clean winged things you may eat.
Deuteronomy14:21 You shall not eat of anything that dies of itself. You may give it to the stranger or the foreigner who is within your towns
Deuteronomy14:22 You shall surely tithe all the yield of your seed produced by your field each year.

Deuteronomy14:24 And if the distance is too long for you to carry your tithe
Deuteronomy14:25 Then you shall turn it into money
Deuteronomy14:26 And you may spend that money for whatever your appetite craves

Deuteronomy15:2 And this is the manner of the release: every creditor shall release that which he has lent to his neighbor; he shall not exact it of his neighbor
Deuteronomy15:3 Of a foreigner you may exact it
Deuteronomy15:4 But there will be no poor among you
Deuteronomy15:5 If only you carefully listen to the voice of the Lord your God

Deuteronomy15:11 For the poor will never cease out of the land; therefore I command you

Deuteronomy15:17 Then take an awl and pierce his ear through to the door
Deuteronomy15:18 It shall not seem hard to you when you let him go free from you

Deuteronomy15:21 But if it has any blemish
Deuteronomy15:22 You shall eat it within your towns; the [ceremonially] unclean and the clean alike may eat it

Deuteronomy16:1 OBSERVE THE month of Abib and keep the Passover to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:2 You shall offer the Passover sacrifice to the Lord your God from the flock or the herd in the place where the Lord will choose to make His Name [and His Presence] dwell.
Deuteronomy16:3 You shall eat no leavened bread with it; for seven days you shall eat it with unleavened bread
Deuteronomy16:4 No leaven shall be seen with you in all your territory for seven days; nor shall any of the flesh which you sacrificed the first day at evening be left all night until the morning.
Deuteronomy16:5 You may not offer the Passover sacrifice within any of your towns which the Lord your God gives you
Deuteronomy16:6 But at the place which the Lord your God will choose in which to make His Name [and His Presence] dwell
Deuteronomy16:7 And you shall roast or boil and eat it in the place which the Lord your God will choose. And in the morning you shall turn and go to your tents.
Deuteronomy16:8 For six days you shall eat unleavened bread
Deuteronomy16:9 You shall count seven weeks; begin to number the seven weeks from the time you begin to put the sickle to the standing grain.
Deuteronomy16:10 Then you shall keep the Feast of Weeks to the Lord your God with a tribute of a freewill offering from your hand
Deuteronomy16:11 And you shall rejoice before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy16:12 And you shall [earnestly] remember that you were a slave in Egypt
Deuteronomy16:13 You shall observe the Feast of Tabernacles or Booths for seven days after you have gathered in from your threshing floor and wine vat.
Deuteronomy16:14 You shall rejoice in your Feast
Deuteronomy16:15 For seven days you shall keep a solemn Feast to the Lord your God in the place which the Lord chooses; because the Lord your God will bless you in all your produce and in all the works of your hands
Deuteronomy16:16 Three times a year shall all your males appear before the Lord your God in the place which He chooses: at the Feast of Unleavened Bread
Deuteronomy16:17 Every man shall give as he is able
Deuteronomy16:18 You shall appoint judges and officers in all your towns which the Lord your God gives you
Deuteronomy16:19 You shall not misinterpret or misapply judgment; you shall not be partial
Deuteronomy16:20 Follow what is altogether just (uncompromisingly righteous)
Deuteronomy16:21 You shall not plant for yourselves any kind of tree dedicated to [the goddess] Asherah beside the altar of the Lord your God which you shall make.
Deuteronomy16:22 Neither shall you set up an idolatrous stone or image
Deuteronomy17:1 YOU SHALL not sacrifice to the Lord your God an ox or sheep with a blemish or any defect whatsoever
Deuteronomy17:2 If there is found among you within any of your towns which the Lord your God gives you a man or woman who does what is wicked in the sight of the Lord your God by transgressing His covenant
Deuteronomy17:3 Who has gone and served other gods and worshiped them
Deuteronomy17:4 And it is told and you hear of it
Deuteronomy17:5 Then you shall bring forth to your town's gates that man or woman who has done that wicked thing and you shall stone that man or woman to death.
Deuteronomy17:6 On the evidence of two or three witnesses he who is worthy of death shall be put to death; he shall not be put to death on the evidence of one witness.
Deuteronomy17:7 The hands of the witnesses shall be the first against him to put him to death
Deuteronomy17:8 If there arises a matter too hard for you in judgment–between one kind of bloodshed and another
Deuteronomy17:9 And you shall come to the Levitical priests and to the judge who is in office in those days
Deuteronomy17:10 And you shall do according to the decision which they declare to you from that place which the Lord chooses; and you shall be watchful to do according to all that they tell you;
Deuteronomy17:11 According to the decision of the law which they shall teach you and the judgment which they shall announce to you
Deuteronomy17:12 The man who does presumptuously and will not listen to the priest who stands to minister there before the Lord your God or to the judge
Deuteronomy17:13 And all the people shall hear and [reverently] fear
Deuteronomy17:14 When you come to the land which the Lord your God gives you and you possess it and live there
Deuteronomy17:15 You shall surely set as king over you him whom the Lord your God will choose. One from among your brethren you shall set as king over you; you may not set a foreigner
Deuteronomy17:16 But he shall not multiply horses to himself or cause the people to return to Egypt in order to multiply horses
Deuteronomy17:17 And he shall not multiply wives to himself
Deuteronomy17:18 And when he sits on his royal throne
Deuteronomy17:19 And he shall keep it with him
Deuteronomy17:20 That his [mind and] heart may not be lifted up above his brethren and that he may not turn aside from the commandment to the right hand or to the left; so that he may continue long
Deuteronomy18:1 THE LEVITICAL priests and all the tribe of Levi shall have no part or inheritance with Israel; they shall eat the offerings made by fire to the Lord
Deuteronomy18:2 They shall have no inheritance among their brethren; the Lord is their inheritance
Deuteronomy18:3 And this shall be the priest's due from the people
Deuteronomy18:4 The firstfruits of your grain
Deuteronomy18:5 For the Lord your God has chosen him out of all your tribes to stand to minister in the name [and presence] of the Lord
Deuteronomy18:6 And if a Levite comes from any of your towns out of all Israel where he is a temporary resident
Deuteronomy18:7 Then he may minister in the name [and presence of] the Lord his God like all his brethren the Levites who stand to minister there before the Lord.
Deuteronomy18:8 They shall have equal portions to eat
Deuteronomy18:9 When you come into the land which the Lord your God gives you
Deuteronomy18:10 There shall not be found among you anyone who makes his son or daughter pass through the fire
Deuteronomy18:11 Or a charmer
Deuteronomy18:12 For all who do these things are an abomination to the Lord
Deuteronomy18:13 You shall be blameless [and absolutely true] to the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy18:14 For these nations whom you shall dispossess listen to soothsayers and diviners. But as for you
Deuteronomy18:15 The Lord your God will raise up for you a prophet (Prophet) from the midst of your brethren like me [Moses]; to him you shall listen. [Matt. 21:11; John 1:21.]
Deuteronomy18:16 This is what you desired [and asked] of the Lord your God at Horeb on the day of the assembly when you said
Deuteronomy18:17 And the Lord said to me
Deuteronomy18:18 I will raise up for them a prophet (Prophet) from among their brethren like you
Deuteronomy18:19 And whoever will not hearken to My words which he shall speak in My name
Deuteronomy18:20 But the prophet who presumes to speak a word in My name which I have not commanded him to speak
Deuteronomy18:21 And if you say in your [minds and] hearts
Deuteronomy18:22 When a prophet speaks in the name of the Lord
Deuteronomy19:1 WHEN THE Lord your God has cut off the nations whose land the Lord your God gives you
Deuteronomy19:2 You shall set apart three cities for yourselves in the land which the Lord your God gives you to possess.
Deuteronomy19:3 You shall prepare the road and divide into three parts the territory of your land which the Lord your God gives you to possess
Deuteronomy19:4 And this is the case of the slayer who shall flee there in order that he may live. Whoever kills his neighbor unintentionally
Deuteronomy19:5 As when a man goes into the wood with his neighbor to hew wood
Deuteronomy19:6 Lest the avenger of the blood pursue the slayer while his [mind and] heart are hot with anger and overtake him
Deuteronomy19:7 Therefore I command you
Deuteronomy19:8 And if the Lord your God enlarges your territory
Deuteronomy19:9 If you keep all these commandments to do them
Deuteronomy19:10 Lest innocent blood be shed in your land
Deuteronomy19:11 But if any man hates his neighbor and lies in wait for him
Deuteronomy19:12 Then the elders of his own city shall send for him and fetch him from there and give him over to the avenger of blood
Deuteronomy19:13 Your eyes shall not pity him
Deuteronomy19:14 You shall not remove your neighbor's landmark in the land which the Lord your God gives you to possess
Deuteronomy19:15 One witness shall not prevail against a man for any crime or any wrong in connection with any sin he commits; only on the testimony of two or three witnesses shall a charge be established.
Deuteronomy19:16 If a false witness rises up against any man to accuse him of wrongdoing
Deuteronomy19:17 Then both parties to the controversy shall stand before the Lord
Deuteronomy19:18 The judges shall inquire diligently
Deuteronomy19:19 Then you shall do to him as he had intended to do to his brother. So you shall put away the evil from among you.
Deuteronomy19:20 And those who remain shall hear and [reverently] fear
Deuteronomy19:21 Your eyes shall not pity: it shall be life for life
Deuteronomy20:1 WHEN YOU go forth to battle against your enemies and see horses and chariots and an army greater than your own
Deuteronomy20:2 And when you come near to the battle
Deuteronomy20:3 And shall say to them
Deuteronomy20:4 For the Lord your God is He Who goes with you to fight for you against your enemies to save you. [I Sam. 17:45.]
Deuteronomy20:5 And the officers shall speak to the people
Deuteronomy20:6 And what man has planted a vineyard and has not used the fruit of it? Let him also return to his house
Deuteronomy20:7 And what man has betrothed a wife and has not taken her? Let him return to his house
Deuteronomy20:8 And the officers shall speak further to the people
Deuteronomy20:9 And when the officers finish speaking to the people
Deuteronomy20:10 When you draw near to a city to fight against it
Deuteronomy20:11 And if that city makes an answer of peace to you and opens to you
Deuteronomy20:12 But if it refuses to make peace with you and fights against you
Deuteronomy20:13 And when the Lord your God has given it into your hands
Deuteronomy20:14 But the women
Deuteronomy20:15 So shall you treat all the cities that are very far off from you
Deuteronomy20:16 But in the cities of these people which the Lord your God gives you for an inheritance
Deuteronomy20:17 But you shall utterly exterminate them
Deuteronomy20:18 So that they may not teach you all the abominable practices they have carried on for their gods
Deuteronomy20:19 When you besiege a city for a long time
Deuteronomy20:20 Only the trees which you know are not trees for food you may destroy and cut down
Deuteronomy21:1 IF ONE is found slain in the land which the Lord your God gives you to possess
Deuteronomy21:2 Then your elders and judges shall come forth and measure the distance to the cities around him who is slain.
Deuteronomy21:3 And the city which is nearest to the slain man
Deuteronomy21:4 And the elders of that city shall bring the heifer down to a valley with running water which is neither plowed nor sown
Deuteronomy21:5 And the priests
Deuteronomy21:6 And all the elders of that city nearest to the slain man shall wash their hands over the heifer whose neck was broken in the valley
Deuteronomy21:7 And they shall testify
Deuteronomy21:8 Forgive
Deuteronomy21:9 So shall you purge the guilt of innocent blood from among you
Deuteronomy21:10 When you go forth to battle against your enemies and the Lord your God has given them into your hands and you carry them away captive
Deuteronomy21:11 And you see among the captives a beautiful woman and desire her
Deuteronomy21:12 Then you shall bring her home to your house
Deuteronomy21:13 And put off her prisoner's garb
Deuteronomy21:14 And if you have no delight in her
Deuteronomy21:15 If a man has two wives
Deuteronomy21:16 Then on the day when he wills his possessions to his sons
Deuteronomy21:17 But he shall acknowledge the son of the disliked as the firstborn by giving him a double portion of all that he has
Deuteronomy21:18 If a man has a stubborn and rebellious son who will not obey the voice of his father or his mother and though they chasten him will not listen to them
Deuteronomy21:19 Then his father and mother shall take hold of him and bring him out to the elders of his city at the gate of the place where he lives
Deuteronomy21:20 And they shall say to the elders of his city
Deuteronomy21:21 Then all the men of his city shall stone him to death; so you shall cleanse out the evil from your midst
Deuteronomy21:22 And if a man has committed a sin worthy of death and he is put to death and [afterward] you hang him on a tree
Deuteronomy21:23 His body shall not remain all night upon the tree
Deuteronomy22:1 YOU SHALL not see your brother's ox or his sheep being driven away or stolen
Deuteronomy22:2 And if your brother [the owner] is not near you or if you do not know who he is
Deuteronomy22:3 And so shall you do with his donkey or his garment or with anything which your brother has lost and you have found. You shall not hide from [your duty concerning] them.
Deuteronomy22:4 You shall not see your brother's donkey or his ox fall down by the way
Deuteronomy22:5 The woman shall not wear that which pertains to a man
Deuteronomy22:6 If a bird's nest should chance to be before you in the way
Deuteronomy22:7 You shall surely let the mother bird go
Deuteronomy22:8 When you build a new house
Deuteronomy22:9 You shall not plant your vineyard with two kinds of seed
Deuteronomy22:10 You shall not plow with an ox [a clean animal] and a donkey [unclean] together. [II Cor. 6:14-16.]
Deuteronomy22:11 You shall not wear a garment of mingled stuff
Deuteronomy22:12 You shall make yourself tassels on the four corners of your cloak with which you cover yourself. [Num. 15:37-40.]
Deuteronomy22:13 If any man takes a wife and goes in to her
Deuteronomy22:14 And charges her with shameful things and gives her an evil reputation
Deuteronomy22:15 Then the father of the young woman
Deuteronomy22:16 And her father shall say to the elders
Deuteronomy22:17 And behold
Deuteronomy22:18 And the elders of that city shall take the man and rebuke and whip him.
Deuteronomy22:19 And they shall fine him 100 shekels of silver and give them to the father of the young woman
Deuteronomy22:20 But if it is true that the evidences of virginity were not found in the young woman
Deuteronomy22:21 Then they shall bring her to the door of her father's house and the men of her city shall stone her to death
Deuteronomy22:22 If a man is found lying with another man's wife
Deuteronomy22:23 If a maiden who is a virgin is engaged to be married
Deuteronomy22:24 Then you shall bring them both out to the gate of that city and shall stone them to death–the young woman because she did not cry for help though she was in the city
Deuteronomy22:25 But if a man finds the betrothed maiden in the open country and the man seizes her and lies with her
Deuteronomy22:26 But you shall do nothing to the young woman; she has committed no sin punishable by death
Deuteronomy22:27 For he came upon her in the open country
Deuteronomy22:28 If a man finds a girl who is a virgin
Deuteronomy22:29 Then the man who lay with her shall give to the girl's father fifty shekels of silver
Deuteronomy22:30 A man shall not take his father's former wife
Deuteronomy23:1 HE WHO is wounded in the testicles
Deuteronomy23:2 A person begotten out of wedlock shall not enter into the assembly of the Lord; even to his tenth generation shall his descendants not enter into the congregation of the Lord.
Deuteronomy23:3 An Ammonite or Moabite shall not enter into the congregation of the Lord; even to their tenth generation their descendants shall not enter into the assembly of the Lord forever
Deuteronomy23:4 Because they did not meet you with food and water on the way when you came forth out of Egypt
Deuteronomy23:5 Nevertheless
Deuteronomy23:6 You shall not seek their peace or their prosperity all your days forever.
Deuteronomy23:7 You shall not abhor an Edomite
Deuteronomy23:8 Their children may enter into the congregation of the Lord in their third generation.
Deuteronomy23:9 When you go forth against your enemies and are in camp
Deuteronomy23:10 If there is among you any man who is not clean by reason of what happens to him at night
Deuteronomy23:11 But when evening comes he shall bathe himself in water
Deuteronomy23:12 You shall have a place also outside the camp to which you shall go [as a comfort station];
Deuteronomy23:13 And you shall have a paddle or shovel among your weapons
Deuteronomy23:14 For the Lord your God walks in the midst of your camp to deliver you and to give up your enemies before you. Therefore shall your camp be holy
Deuteronomy23:15 You shall not give up to his master a servant who has escaped from his master to you.
Deuteronomy23:16 He shall dwell with you in your midst wherever he chooses in one of your towns where it pleases him best. You shall not defraud or oppress him.
Deuteronomy23:17 There shall be no cult prostitute among the daughters of Israel
Deuteronomy23:18 You shall not bring the hire of a harlot or the price of a dog (a sodomite) into the house of the Lord your God as payment of a vow
Deuteronomy23:19 You shall not lend on interest to your brother–interest on money
Deuteronomy23:20 You may lend on interest to a foreigner
Deuteronomy23:21 When you make a vow to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy23:22 But if you refrain from vowing
Deuteronomy23:23 The vow which has passed your lips you shall be watchful to perform
Deuteronomy23:24 When you come into your neighbor's vineyard
Deuteronomy23:25 When you come into the standing grain of your neighbor
Deuteronomy24:1 WHEN A man takes a wife and marries her
Deuteronomy24:2 And when she departs out of his house she goes and marries another man
Deuteronomy24:3 And if the latter husband dislikes her and writes her a bill of divorce and puts it in her hand and sends her out of his house
Deuteronomy24:4 Then her former husband
Deuteronomy24:5 When a man is newly married
Deuteronomy24:6 No man shall take a mill or an upper millstone in pledge
Deuteronomy24:7 If a man is found kidnapping any of his brethren of the Israelites and treats him as a slave or a servant or sells him
Deuteronomy24:8 Take heed in the plague of leprosy
Deuteronomy24:9 Remember [earnestly] what the Lord your God did to Miriam on the way after you had come out of Egypt. [Num. 12:10.]
Deuteronomy24:10 When you lend your brother anything
Deuteronomy24:11 You shall stand outside and the man to whom you lend shall bring the pledge out to you.
Deuteronomy24:12 And if the man is poor
Deuteronomy24:13 You shall surely restore to him the pledge at sunset
Deuteronomy24:14 You shall not oppress or extort from a hired servant who is poor and needy
Deuteronomy24:15 You shall give him his hire on the day he earns it before the sun goes down
Deuteronomy24:16 The fathers shall not be put to death for the children
Deuteronomy24:17 You shall not pervert the justice due the stranger or the sojourner or the fatherless
Deuteronomy24:18 But you shall [earnestly] remember that you were a slave in Egypt and the Lord your God redeemed you from there; therefore I command you to do this.

Deuteronomy24:20 When you beat your olive tree
Deuteronomy24:21 When you gather the grapes of your vineyard
Deuteronomy24:22 You shall [earnestly] remember that you were a slave in the land of Egypt; therefore I command you to do this.

Deuteronomy25:2 Then if the guilty man deserves to be beaten
Deuteronomy25:3 Forty stripes may be given him but not more

Deuteronomy26:1 WHEN YOU have come into the land which the Lord your God gives you as an inheritance and possess it and live in it
Deuteronomy26:2 You shall take some of the first of all the produce of the soil which you harvest from the land the Lord your God gives you and put it in a basket
Deuteronomy26:3 And you shall go to the priest who is in office in those days
Deuteronomy26:4 And the priest shall take the basket from your hand and set it down before the altar of the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy26:5 And you shall say before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy26:6 And the Egyptians treated us very badly and afflicted us and laid upon us hard bondage.
Deuteronomy26:7 And when we cried to the Lord
Deuteronomy26:8 And the Lord brought us forth out of Egypt with a mighty hand and with an outstretched arm
Deuteronomy26:9 And He brought us into this place and gave us this land
Deuteronomy26:10 And now
Deuteronomy26:11 And you and the Levite and the stranger and the sojourner among you shall rejoice in all the good which the Lord your God has given you and your household.
Deuteronomy26:12 When you have finished paying all the tithe of your produce the third year
Deuteronomy26:13 Then you shall say before the Lord your God
Deuteronomy26:14 I have not eaten of the tithe in my mourning [making the tithe unclean]
Deuteronomy26:15 Look down from Your holy habitation
Deuteronomy26:16 This day the Lord your God has commanded you to do these statutes and ordinances. Therefore you shall keep and do them with all your [mind and] heart and with all your being.
Deuteronomy26:17 You have [openly] declared the Lord this day to be your God
Deuteronomy26:18 And the Lord has declared this day that you are His peculiar people as He promised you
Deuteronomy26:19 And He will make you high above all nations which He has made
Deuteronomy27:1 AND MOSES with the elders of Israel commanded the people
Deuteronomy27:2 And on the day when you pass over the Jordan to the land which the Lord your God gives you
Deuteronomy27:3 And you shall write on them all the words of this law when you have passed over
Deuteronomy27:4 And when you have gone over the Jordan
Deuteronomy27:5 And there you shall build an altar to the Lord your God
Deuteronomy27:6 You shall build the altar of the Lord your God of whole stones and offer burnt offerings on it to Him;
Deuteronomy27:7 And you shall offer peace offerings
Deuteronomy27:8 And you shall write upon the stones all the words of this law very plainly.
Deuteronomy27:9 And Moses and the Levitical priests said to all Israel
Deuteronomy27:10 So you shall obey the voice of the Lord your God and do His commandments and statutes which I command you today.
Deuteronomy27:11 And Moses charged the people the same day
Deuteronomy27:12 These [tribes] shall stand on Mount Gerizim to bless the people
Deuteronomy27:13 And these [tribes] shall stand on Mount Ebal to pronounce the curse [for disobedience]: Reuben
Deuteronomy27:14 And the Levites shall declare with a loud voice to all the men of Israel:
Deuteronomy27:15 Cursed is the man who makes a graven or molten image

Deuteronomy27:17 Cursed is he who moves [back] his neighbor's landmark. All the people shall say
Deuteronomy27:18 Cursed is he who misleads a blind man on his way. All the people shall say
Deuteronomy27:19 Cursed is he who perverts the justice due to the sojourner or the stranger
Deuteronomy27:20 Cursed is he who lies with his father's wife
Deuteronomy27:21 Cursed is he who lies with any beast. All the people shall say
Deuteronomy27:22 Cursed is he who lies with his half sister
Deuteronomy27:23 Cursed is he who lies with his mother-in-law. All the people shall say
Deuteronomy27:24 Cursed is he who slays his neighbor secretly. All the people shall say
Deuteronomy27:25 Cursed is he who takes a bribe to slay an innocent person. All the people shall say
Deuteronomy27:26 Cursed is he who does not support and give assent to the words of this law to do them [as the rule of his life]. All the people shall say
Deuteronomy28:1 IF YOU will listen diligently to the voice of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy28:2 And all these blessings shall come upon you and overtake you if you heed the voice of the Lord your God.
Deuteronomy28:3 Blessed shall you be in the city and blessed shall you be in the field.
Deuteronomy28:4 Blessed shall be the fruit of your body and the fruit of your ground and the fruit of your beasts
Deuteronomy28:5 Blessed shall be your basket and your kneading trough.
Deuteronomy28:6 Blessed shall you be when you come in and blessed shall you be when you go out.
Deuteronomy28:7 The Lord shall cause your enemies who rise up against you to be defeated before your face; they shall come out against you one way and flee before you seven ways.
Deuteronomy28:8 The Lord shall command the blessing upon you in your storehouse and in all that you undertake. And He will bless you in the land which the Lord your God gives you.
Deuteronomy28:9 The Lord will establish you as a people holy to Himself
Deuteronomy28:10 And all people of the earth shall see that you are called by the name [and in the presence of] the Lord
Deuteronomy28:11 And the Lord shall make you have a surplus of prosperity
Deuteronomy28:12 The Lord shall open to you His good treasury
Deuteronomy28:13 And the Lord shall make you the head
Deuteronomy28:14 And you shall not turn aside from any of the words which I command you this day
Deuteronomy28:15 But if you will not obey the voice of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy28:16 Cursed shall you be in the city and cursed shall you be in the field.
Deuteronomy28:17 Cursed shall be your basket and your kneading trough.
Deuteronomy28:18 Cursed shall be the fruit of your body
Deuteronomy28:19 Cursed shall you be when you come in and cursed shall you be when you go out.
Deuteronomy28:20 The Lord shall send you curses
Deuteronomy28:21 The Lord will make the pestilence cling to you until He has consumed you from the land into which you go to possess.
Deuteronomy28:22 The Lord will smite you with consumption
Deuteronomy28:23 The heavens over your head shall be brass and the earth under you shall be iron.
Deuteronomy28:24 The Lord shall make the rain of your land powdered soil and dust; from the heavens it shall come down upon you until you are destroyed.
Deuteronomy28:25 The Lord shall cause you to be struck down before your enemies; you shall go out one way against them and flee seven ways before them
Deuteronomy28:26 And your dead body shall be food for all the birds of the air and the beasts of the earth
Deuteronomy28:27 The Lord will smite you with the boils of Egypt and the tumors
Deuteronomy28:28 The Lord will smite you with madness and blindness and dismay of [mind and] heart.
Deuteronomy28:29 And you shall grope at noonday as the blind grope in darkness. And you shall not prosper in your ways; and you shall be only oppressed and robbed continually
Deuteronomy28:30 You shall betroth a wife
Deuteronomy28:31 Your ox shall be slain before your eyes
Deuteronomy28:32 Your sons and daughters shall be given to another people
Deuteronomy28:33 A nation which you have not known shall eat up the fruit of your land and of all your labors
Deuteronomy28:34 So that you shall be driven mad by the sights which your eyes shall see.
Deuteronomy28:35 The Lord will smite you on the knees and on the legs with a sore boil that cannot be healed
Deuteronomy28:36 The Lord shall bring you and your king whom you have set over you to a nation which neither you nor your fathers have known
Deuteronomy28:37 And you shall become an amazement
Deuteronomy28:38 You shall carry much seed out into the field and shall gather little in
Deuteronomy28:39 You shall plant vineyards and dress them but shall neither drink of the wine nor gather the grapes
Deuteronomy28:40 You shall have olive trees throughout all your territory but you shall not anoint yourselves with the oil
Deuteronomy28:41 You shall beget sons and daughters but shall not enjoy them
Deuteronomy28:42 All your trees and the fruit of your ground shall the locust possess. [Fulfilled in Joel 1:4.]
Deuteronomy28:43 The transient (stranger) among you shall mount up higher and higher above you
Deuteronomy28:44 He shall lend to you
Deuteronomy28:45 All these curses shall come upon you and shall pursue you and overtake you till you are destroyed
Deuteronomy28:46 They shall be upon you for a sign [of warning to other nations] and for a wonder
Deuteronomy28:47 Because you did not serve the Lord your God with joyfulness of [mind and] heart [in gratitude] for the abundance of all [with which He had blessed you]
Deuteronomy28:48 Therefore you shall serve your enemies whom the Lord shall send against you
Deuteronomy28:49 The Lord will bring a nation against you from afar
Deuteronomy28:50 A nation of unyielding countenance who will not regard the person of the old or show favor to the young
Deuteronomy28:51 And shall eat the fruit of your cattle and the fruit of your ground until you are destroyed
Deuteronomy28:52 They shall besiege you in all your towns until your high and fortified walls in which you trusted come down throughout all your land; and they shall besiege you in all your towns throughout all your land which the Lord your God has given you.
Deuteronomy28:53 And you shall eat the fruit of your own body
Deuteronomy28:54 The man who is most tender among you and extremely particular and well-bred
Deuteronomy28:55 So that he will not give to any of them any of the flesh of his children which he is eating
Deuteronomy28:56 The most tender and daintily bred woman among you
Deuteronomy28:57 Her afterbirth that comes out from her body and the children whom she shall bear. For she will eat them secretly for want of anything else in the siege and distress with which your enemies shall distress you in your towns.
Deuteronomy28:58 If you will not be watchful to do all the words of this law that are written in this book
Deuteronomy28:59 Then the Lord will bring upon you and your descendants extraordinary strokes and blows
Deuteronomy28:60 Moreover
Deuteronomy28:61 Also every sickness and every affliction which is not written in this Book of the Law the Lord will bring upon you until you are destroyed.
Deuteronomy28:62 And you shall be left few in number
Deuteronomy28:63 And as the Lord rejoiced over you to do you good and to multiply you
Deuteronomy28:64 And the Lord shall scatter you among all peoples from one end of the earth to the other; and there you shall [be forced to] serve other gods
Deuteronomy28:65 And among these nations you shall find no ease and there shall be no rest for the sole of your foot; but the Lord will give you there a trembling heart
Deuteronomy28:66 Your life shall hang in doubt before you; day and night you shall be worried
Deuteronomy28:67 In the morning you shall say
Deuteronomy28:68 And the Lord shall bring you into Egypt again with ships by the way about which I said to you
Deuteronomy29:1 THESE ARE the words of the covenant which the Lord commanded Moses to make with the Israelites in the land of Moab
Deuteronomy29:2 Moses called to all Israel and said to them
Deuteronomy29:3 The great trials which your eyes saw
Deuteronomy29:4 Yet the Lord has not given you a [mind and] heart to understand and eyes to see and ears to hear
Deuteronomy29:5 I have led you forty years in the wilderness; your clothes have not worn out upon you
Deuteronomy29:6 You have not eaten [grain] bread
Deuteronomy29:7 And when you came to this place
Deuteronomy29:8 We took their land and gave it as an inheritance to the Reubenites
Deuteronomy29:9 Therefore keep the words of this covenant and do them
Deuteronomy29:10 All of you stand today before the Lord your God–your heads
Deuteronomy29:11 Your little ones
Deuteronomy29:12 That you may enter into the covenant of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy29:13 That He may establish you this day as a people for Himself
Deuteronomy29:14 It is not with you only that I make this sworn covenant
Deuteronomy29:15 But with future Israelites who do not stand here with us today before the Lord our God
Deuteronomy29:16 You know how we lived in the land of Egypt and how we came through the midst of the nations you crossed.
Deuteronomy29:17 And you have seen their abominations and their idols of wood and stone
Deuteronomy29:18 Beware lest there should be among you a man or woman
Deuteronomy29:19 And lest
Deuteronomy29:20 The Lord will not pardon him
Deuteronomy29:21 And the Lord will single him out for ruin and destruction from all the tribes of Israel
Deuteronomy29:22 So that the next generation
Deuteronomy29:23 The whole land is brimstone and salt and a burned waste
Deuteronomy29:24 Even all the nations shall say
Deuteronomy29:25 Then men shall say
Deuteronomy29:26 For they went and served other gods and worshiped them
Deuteronomy29:27 So the anger of the Lord was kindled against this land
Deuteronomy29:28 And the Lord rooted them out of their land in anger and in wrath and in great indignation and cast them into another land
Deuteronomy29:29 The secret things belong unto the Lord our God
Deuteronomy30:1 AND WHEN all these things have come upon you
Deuteronomy30:2 And shall return to the Lord your God and obey His voice according to all that I command you today
Deuteronomy30:3 Then the Lord your God will restore your fortunes and have compassion upon you and will gather you again from all the nations where He has scattered you.
Deuteronomy30:4 Even if any of your dispersed are in the uttermost parts of the heavens
Deuteronomy30:5 And the Lord your God will bring you into the land which your fathers possessed
Deuteronomy30:6 And the Lord your God will circumcise your hearts and the hearts of your descendants
Deuteronomy30:7 And the Lord your God will put all these curses upon your enemies and on those who hate you
Deuteronomy30:8 And you shall return and obey the voice of the Lord and do all His commandments which I command you today.
Deuteronomy30:9 And the Lord your God will make you abundantly prosperous in every work of your hand
Deuteronomy30:10 If you obey the voice of the Lord your God
Deuteronomy30:11 For this commandment which I command you this day is not too difficult for you
Deuteronomy30:12 It is not [a secret laid up] in heaven
Deuteronomy30:13 Neither is it beyond the sea
Deuteronomy30:14 But the word is very near you
Deuteronomy30:15 See
Deuteronomy30:16 [If you obey the commandments of the Lord your God which] I command you today
Deuteronomy30:17 But if your [mind and] heart turn away and you will not hear
Deuteronomy30:18 I declare to you today that you shall surely perish
Deuteronomy30:19 I call heaven and earth to witness this day against you that I have set before you life and death
Deuteronomy30:20 And may love the Lord your God

Deuteronomy32:1 GIVE EAR
Deuteronomy32:2 My message shall drop as the rain
Deuteronomy32:3 For I will proclaim the name [and presence] of the Lord. Concede and ascribe greatness to our God.
Deuteronomy32:4 He is the Rock
Deuteronomy32:5 They [Israel] have spoiled themselves. They are not sons to Him
Deuteronomy32:6 Do you thus repay the Lord
Deuteronomy32:7 Remember the days of old; consider the years of many generations. Ask your father and he will show you
Deuteronomy32:8 When the Most High gave to the nations their inheritance
Deuteronomy32:9 For the Lord's portion is His people; Jacob (Israel) is the lot of His inheritance.
Deuteronomy32:10 He found him in a desert land
Deuteronomy32:11 As an eagle that stirs up her nest
Deuteronomy32:12 So the Lord alone led him; there was no foreign god with Him.
Deuteronomy32:13 He made Israel ride on the high places of the earth
Deuteronomy32:14 Butter and curds of the herd and milk of the flock
Deuteronomy32:15 But Jeshurun (Israel) grew fat and kicked. You became fat
Deuteronomy32:16 They provoked Him to jealousy with strange gods
Deuteronomy32:17 They sacrificed to demons
Deuteronomy32:18 Of the Rock Who bore you you were unmindful; you forgot the God Who travailed in your birth.
Deuteronomy32:19 And the Lord saw it and He spurned and rejected them
Deuteronomy32:20 And He said
Deuteronomy32:21 They have moved Me to jealousy with what is not God; they have angered Me with their idols. So I will move them to jealousy with those who are not a people; I will anger them with a foolish nation.
Deuteronomy32:22 For a fire is kindled by My anger
Deuteronomy32:23 And I will heap evils upon them; I will spend My arrows upon them.
Deuteronomy32:24 They shall be wasted with hunger and devoured with burning heat and poisonous pestilence; and the teeth of beasts will I send against them
Deuteronomy32:25 From without the sword shall bereave
Deuteronomy32:26 I said
Deuteronomy32:27 Had I not feared the provocation of the foe
Deuteronomy32:28 For they are a nation void of counsel
Deuteronomy32:29 O that they were wise and would see through this [present triumph] to their ultimate fate!
Deuteronomy32:30 How could one have chased a thousand
Deuteronomy32:31 For their rock is not like our Rock
Deuteronomy32:32 For their vine comes from the vine of Sodom and from the fields of Gomorrah; their grapes are grapes of [poisonous] gall
Deuteronomy32:33 Their wine is the [furious] venom of serpents
Deuteronomy32:34 Is not this laid up in store with Me
Deuteronomy32:35 Vengeance is Mine
Deuteronomy32:36 For the Lord will revoke sentence for His people and relent for His servants' sake when He sees that their power is gone and none remains
Deuteronomy32:37 And He will say
Deuteronomy32:38 Who ate the fat of their sacrifices and drank the wine of their drink offering? Let them rise up and help you
Deuteronomy32:39 See now that I
Deuteronomy32:40 For I lift up My hand to heaven and swear
Deuteronomy32:41 If I whet My lightning sword and My hand takes hold on judgment
Deuteronomy32:42 I will make My arrows drunk with blood
Deuteronomy32:43 Rejoice [with] His people
Deuteronomy32:44 And Moses came and spoke all the words of this song in the ears of the people
Deuteronomy32:45 And when Moses had finished speaking all these words to all Israel
Deuteronomy32:46 He said to them
Deuteronomy32:47 For it is not an empty and worthless trifle for you; it is your [very] life. By it you shall live long in the land which you are going over the Jordan to possess.
Deuteronomy32:48 And the Lord said to Moses that same day
Deuteronomy32:49 Get up into this mountain of the Abarim
Deuteronomy32:50 And die on the mountain which you ascend and be gathered to your people
Deuteronomy32:51 Because you broke faith with Me in the midst of the Israelites at the waters of Meribah-kadesh in the Wilderness of Zin and because you did not set Me apart as holy in the midst of the Israelites.
Deuteronomy32:52 For you shall see the land opposite you at a distance

Joshua1:6 Be strong (confident) and of good courage
Joshua1:7 Only you be strong and very courageous

Joshua1:9 Have not I commanded you? Be strong

Joshua2:9 And she said to the men

Joshua2:12 Now then

Joshua6:19 But all the silver and gold and vessels of bronze and iron are consecrated to the Lord; they shall come into the treasury of the Lord.

Joshua7:8 O Lord

Joshua8:15 And Joshua and all Israel pretended to be beaten by them

Joshua8:20 When the men of Ai looked back
Joshua8:21 When Joshua and all Israel saw that the ambush had taken the city and that the smoke of the city went up
Joshua8:22 And the others came forth out of the city against them [of Ai]
Joshua8:23 But they took the king of Ai alive and brought him to Joshua.
Joshua8:24 When Israel had finished slaying all the inhabitants of Ai in the field and in the wilderness into which they pursued them
Joshua8:25 And all that fell that day
Joshua8:26 For Joshua drew not back his hand with which he stretched out the javelin until he had utterly destroyed all the inhabitants of Ai.
Joshua8:27 Only the livestock and the spoil of that city Israel took as booty for themselves
Joshua8:28 So Joshua burned Ai and made it a heap of ruins for ever

Joshua8:33 And all Israel
Joshua8:34 Afterward

Joshua9:10 And all that He did to the two kings of the Amorites who were beyond the Jordan
Joshua9:11 So our elders and all the residents of our country said to us
Joshua9:12 This our bread we took hot for our provision out of our houses on the day we set out to go to you; but now behold
Joshua9:13 These wineskins (bottles) which we filled were new
Joshua9:14 So the [Israelite] men partook of their food and did not consult the Lord.
Joshua9:15 Joshua made peace with them
Joshua9:16 Then three days after they had made a covenant with [the strangers
Joshua9:17 And the Israelites set out and came to their cities on the third day. Now their cities were Gibeon
Joshua9:18 But the Israelites did not slay them
Joshua9:19 But all the leaders said to all the assembly
Joshua9:20 This we will do to them: we will let them live
Joshua9:21 And the leaders said to them
Joshua9:22 Joshua called the men and said
Joshua9:23 Now therefore you are cursed
Joshua9:24 They answered Joshua
Joshua9:25 And now
Joshua9:26 So he did to them
Joshua9:27 But Joshua then made them hewers of wood and drawers of water for the congregation and for the altar of the Lord
Joshua10:1 WHEN ADONI-ZEDEK king of Jerusalem heard how Joshua had taken Ai and had utterly destroyed it
Joshua10:2 He feared greatly
Joshua10:3 So Adoni-zedek king of Jerusalem sent to Hoham king of Hebron
Joshua10:4 Come up to me and help me
Joshua10:5 Then the five kings of the Amorites–the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:6 And the men of Gibeon sent to Joshua at the camp in Gilgal
Joshua10:7 So Joshua went up from Gilgal
Joshua10:8 And the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua10:9 So Joshua came upon them suddenly
Joshua10:10 And the Lord caused [the enemies] to panic before Israel
Joshua10:11 As they fled before Israel
Joshua10:12 Then Joshua spoke to the Lord on the day when the Lord gave the Amorites over to the Israelites
Joshua10:13 And the sun stood still
Joshua10:14 There was no day like it before or since
Joshua10:15 Then Joshua returned
Joshua10:16 Those five kings fled and hid themselves in the cave of Makkedah.
Joshua10:17 And it was told Joshua
Joshua10:18 Joshua said
Joshua10:19 But do not stay. Pursue your enemies and fall upon their rear; do not allow them to enter their cities
Joshua10:20 When Joshua and the Israelites had ended slaying them until they were wiped out and the remnant remaining of them had entered into fortified cities
Joshua10:21 All the people returned to the camp to Joshua at Makkedah in peace; none moved his tongue against any of the Israelites.
Joshua10:22 Then said Joshua
Joshua10:23 They brought the five kings out of the cave to him–the kings of Jerusalem
Joshua10:24 When they brought out those kings to Joshua
Joshua10:25 Joshua said to them
Joshua10:26 Afterward Joshua smote and slew them and hanged their bodies on five trees
Joshua10:27 At sunset Joshua ordered and they took the bodies down from the trees and cast them into the cave where the kings had hidden and laid great stones on the cave's mouth
Joshua10:28 Joshua took Makkedah that day and smote it and its king with the sword and utterly destroyed everyone in it. He left none remaining. And he did to the king of Makkedah as he had done to the king of Jericho. [Josh. 6:21.]
Joshua10:29 Then Joshua and all Israel went from Makkedah to Libnah and attacked Libnah.
Joshua10:30 And the Lord gave it also and its king into Israel's hands
Joshua10:31 And Joshua passed from Libnah
Joshua10:32 And the Lord delivered Lachish into the hands of Israel
Joshua10:33 Then Horam king of Gezer came up to help Lachish
Joshua10:34 From Lachish Joshua and all Israel went on to Eglon
Joshua10:35 And they took it that day and smote it with the sword and utterly destroyed all who were in it that day
Joshua10:36 Then Joshua with all Israel went up from Eglon to Hebron
Joshua10:37 And took it and smote it with the sword
Joshua10:38 And Joshua and all Israel with him returned to Debir and attacked it.
Joshua10:39 And he took it
Joshua10:40 So Joshua smote all the land
Joshua10:41 And Joshua smote them from Kadesh-barnea even to Gaza
Joshua10:42 Joshua took all these kings and their land at one time
Joshua10:43 And Joshua returned
Joshua11:1 WHEN JABIN king of Hazor heard of this
Joshua11:2 And to the kings who were in the north in the hill country and in the Arabah south of Chinneroth and in the lowland and in the heights of Dor on the west;
Joshua11:3 To the Canaanites in the east and west; to the Amorites
Joshua11:4 And they went out with all their hosts
Joshua11:5 And all these kings met and came and encamped together at the Waters of Merom
Joshua11:6 But the Lord said to Joshua
Joshua11:7 So Joshua and all the people of war with him came against them suddenly by the Waters of Merom and fell upon them.
Joshua11:8 And the Lord gave them into the hand of Israel
Joshua11:9 And Joshua did to them as the Lord had commanded him: he hamstrung their horses and burned their chariots with fire.
Joshua11:10 And Joshua at that time turned back and took Hazor and smote its king with the sword; for Hazor previously was the head of all those kingdoms.
Joshua11:11 They smote all the people in it with the sword
Joshua11:12 And Joshua took all the cities of those kings and all the kings and smote them with the sword
Joshua11:13 But Israel burned none of the cities that stood [fortified] on their mounds–except Hazor only
Joshua11:14 And all the spoil of these cities and the livestock the Israelites took for their booty; but every man they smote with the sword until they had destroyed them
Joshua11:15 As the Lord had commanded Moses His servant
Joshua11:16 So Joshua took all that land: the hill country
Joshua11:17 From Mount Halak
Joshua11:18 Joshua had waged war a long time [at least five years] with all those kings.
Joshua11:19 Not a city made peace with the Israelites except the Hivites
Joshua11:20 For it was of the Lord to harden their hearts that they should come against Israel in battle
Joshua11:21 Joshua came at that time and cut off the Anakim [large in stature] from the hill country: from Hebron
Joshua11:22 None of the Anakim were left in the land of the Israelites; only in Gaza
Joshua11:23 So Joshua took the whole land

Joshua14:10 And now

Joshua18:7 But the Levites have no portion among you

Joshua20:1 THE
Joshua20:2 Say to the Israelites
Joshua20:3 That the slayer who kills anyone accidentally and unintentionally may flee there; and they shall be your refuge from the avenger of blood. [Num. 35:10ff.]
Joshua20:4 He who flees to one of those cities shall stand at the entrance of the gate of the city and explain his case to the elders of that city; they shall receive him to [the protection of] that city and give him a place to dwell among them.
Joshua20:5 If the avenger of blood pursues him
Joshua20:6 And he shall dwell in that city until he has been tried before the congregation and until the death of him who is the high priest in those days. Then the slayer shall return to his own city from which he fled and to his own house.

Joshua22:1 THEN JOSHUA called the Reubenites
Joshua22:2 And said to them
Joshua22:3 You have not deserted your brethren [the other tribes] these many days to this day but have carefully kept the charge of the Lord your God.
Joshua22:4 But now the Lord your God has given rest to your brethren
Joshua22:5 But take diligent heed to do the commandment and the law which Moses the servant of the Lord charged you: to love the Lord your God and to walk in all His ways and to keep His commandments and to cling to and unite with Him and to serve Him with all your heart and soul [your very life].

1Samuel2:1 HANNAH PRAYED
1Samuel2:2 There is none holy like the Lord
1Samuel2:3 Talk no more so very proudly; let not arrogance go forth from your mouth

1Samuel2:5 Those who were full have hired themselves out for bread
1Samuel2:6 The Lord slays and makes alive; He brings down to Sheol and raises up.
1Samuel2:7 The Lord makes poor and makes rich; He brings low and He lifts up.
1Samuel2:8 He raises up the poor out of the dust and lifts up the needy from the ash heap

1Samuel2:10 The adversaries of the Lord shall be broken to pieces; against them will He thunder in heaven. The Lord will judge [all peoples] to the ends of the earth; and He will give strength to His king (King) and exalt the power of His anointed (Anointed His Christ). [Luke 1:46.]

2Samuel2:14 And Abner said to Joab
2Samuel2:15 Then there arose and went over by number–twelve of Benjamin who were with Ish-bosheth son of Saul
2Samuel2:16 And each caught his opponent by the head and thrust his sword into his side; so they all fell together. Therefore that place was called the Field of Sharp Knives
2Samuel2:17 A very fierce battle followed
2Samuel2:18 Three sons of Zeruiah [the half sister of David] were there: Joab

2Samuel2:21 Abner said to him
2Samuel2:22 And Abner said again to Asahel

2Samuel2:24 But Joab and Abishai [his brothers] pursued Abner; the sun was going down as they came to the hill of Ammah
2Samuel2:25 And the Benjamites gathered together behind Abner and became one troop and took their stand on the top of a hill.
2Samuel2:26 Then Abner called to Joab
2Samuel2:27 Joab said
2Samuel2:28 So Joab blew a trumpet
2Samuel2:29 Abner and his men went all night through the Arabah [plain]
2Samuel2:30 Joab returned from pursuing Abner
2Samuel2:31 But the servants of David had slain of Benjamin 360 of Abner's men.
2Samuel2:32 And they took up Asahel and buried him in the tomb of his father at Bethlehem. And Joab and his men walked all night and came to Hebron at daybreak.
2Samuel3:1 THERE WAS a long war between the house of Saul and the house of David. But David grew stronger and stronger
2Samuel3:2 Sons were born to David in Hebron: his firstborn was Amnon
2Samuel3:3 His second
2Samuel3:4 The fourth
2Samuel3:5 And the sixth
2Samuel3:6 While there was war between the houses of Saul and David
2Samuel3:7 Now Saul had a concubine whose name was Rizpah daughter of Aiah. And Ish-bosheth said to Abner
2Samuel3:8 Then Abner was very angry at the words of Ish-bosheth and said
2Samuel3:9 May God do so to Abner
2Samuel3:10 To transfer the kingdom from the house of Saul and set the throne of David over Israel and Judah from Dan to Beersheba.
2Samuel3:11 And Ish-bosheth could not answer Abner a word
2Samuel3:12 And Abner sent messengers to David where he was [at Hebron]
2Samuel3:13 And David said

2Samuel3:17 Abner talked with the seniors of Israel
2Samuel3:18 Now then

2Samuel3:20 So Abner came to David at Hebron
2Samuel3:21 Abner said to David
2Samuel3:22 Then the servants of David came with Joab from pursuing a troop and brought much spoil with them. But Abner was not with David in Hebron
2Samuel3:23 When Joab and all the army with him had come
2Samuel3:24 Then Joab came to the king and said
2Samuel3:25 You know that Abner son of Ner came to deceive you and to know your going out and coming in and all you are doing.
2Samuel3:26 When Joab came from seeing David
2Samuel3:27 And when Abner returned to Hebron
2Samuel3:28 When David heard of it
2Samuel3:29 Let it fall on the head of Joab and on all his father's house; and let the house of Joab never be without one who has a discharge or is a leper or walks with a crutch or is a distaff holder [unfit for war] or who falls by the sword or lacks food!
2Samuel3:30 So Joab and Abishai his brother slew Abner because he had slain their brother Asahel at Gibeon in the battle.
2Samuel3:31 And David said to Joab and to all the people with him
2Samuel3:32 They buried Abner in Hebron. And the king lifted up his voice and wept at the grave of Abner
2Samuel3:33 And the king lamented over Abner and said
2Samuel3:34 Your hands were not bound or your feet put into fetters; as a man falls before wicked men
2Samuel3:35 All the people came to urge David to eat food while it was yet day; but David took an oath
2Samuel3:36 And all the people took notice of it
2Samuel3:37 For all the people and all Israel understood that day that it was not the king's will to slay Abner son of Ner.
2Samuel3:38 King David said to his servants
2Samuel3:39 And I am this day weak

2Samuel6:9 David was afraid of the Lord that day and said

2Samuel7:9 And I was with you wherever you went
2Samuel7:10 And I will appoint a place for My people Israel and will plant them
2Samuel7:11 And as from the time that I appointed judges over My people Israel; and I will cause you to rest from all your enemies. Also the Lord declares to you that He will make for you a house:
2Samuel7:12 And when your days are fulfilled and you sleep with your fathers
2Samuel7:13 He shall build a house for My Name [and My Presence]
2Samuel7:14 I will be his Father
2Samuel7:15 But My mercy and loving-kindness shall not depart from him
2Samuel7:16 And your house and your kingdom shall be made sure forever before you; your throne shall be established forever.
2Samuel7:17 In accordance with all these words and all this vision Nathan spoke to David.

2Samuel7:23 What [other] one nation on earth is like Your people Israel

2Samuel7:25 Now
2Samuel7:26 And Your name [and presence] shall be magnified forever

2Samuel22:1 DAVID SPOKE to the Lord the words of this song on the day when the Lord delivered him from the hands of all his enemies and from the hand of Saul.
2Samuel22:2 He said: The Lord is my Rock [of escape from Saul] and my Fortress [in the wilderness] and my Deliverer; [I Sam. 23:14
2Samuel22:3 My God
2Samuel22:4 I call on the Lord
2Samuel22:5 For the waves of death enveloped me; the torrents of destruction made me afraid.
2Samuel22:6 The cords of Sheol were entangling me; I encountered the snares of death.
2Samuel22:7 In my distress I called upon the Lord; I cried to my God
2Samuel22:8 Then the earth reeled and quaked
2Samuel22:9 Smoke went up from His nostrils
2Samuel22:10 He bowed the heavens and came down; thick darkness was under His feet.
2Samuel22:11 He rode on a cherub and flew; He was seen upon the wings of the wind.
2Samuel22:12 He made darkness His canopy around Him
2Samuel22:13 Out of the brightness before Him coals of fire flamed forth.
2Samuel22:14 The Lord thundered from heaven
2Samuel22:15 He sent out arrows and scattered them; lightning confused and troubled them.
2Samuel22:16 The channels of the sea were visible
2Samuel22:17 He sent from above
2Samuel22:18 He delivered me from my strong enemy
2Samuel22:19 They came upon me in the day of my calamity
2Samuel22:20 He brought me forth into a large place; He delivered me because He delighted in me.
2Samuel22:21 The Lord rewarded me according to my uprightness with Him; He compensated and benefited me according to the cleanness of my hands.
2Samuel22:22 For I have kept the ways of the Lord
2Samuel22:23 For all His ordinances were before me; and from His statutes I did not turn aside.
2Samuel22:24 I was also blameless before Him and kept myself from guilt and iniquity.
2Samuel22:25 Therefore the Lord has recompensed me according to my righteousness
2Samuel22:26 Toward the loving and loyal You will show Yourself loving and loyal
2Samuel22:27 To the pure You will show Yourself pure
2Samuel22:28 And the afflicted people You will deliver
2Samuel22:29 For You
2Samuel22:30 For by You I run through a troop; by my God I leap over a wall.
2Samuel22:31 As for God
2Samuel22:32 For who is God but the Lord? And who is a Rock except our God?
2Samuel22:33 God is my strong Fortress; He guides the blameless in His way and sets him free.
2Samuel22:34 He makes my feet like the hinds' [firm and able]; He sets me secure and confident upon the heights.
2Samuel22:35 He trains my hands for war
2Samuel22:36 You have also given me the shield of Your salvation; and Your condescension and gentleness have made me great.
2Samuel22:37 You have enlarged my steps under me
2Samuel22:38 I have pursued my enemies and destroyed them; and I did not turn back until they were consumed.
2Samuel22:39 I consumed them and thrust them through
2Samuel22:40 For You girded me with strength for the battle; those who rose up against me You subdued under me.
2Samuel22:41 You have made my enemies turn their backs to me
2Samuel22:42 They looked
2Samuel22:43 Then I beat them small as the dust of the earth; I crushed them as the mire of the street and scattered them abroad.
2Samuel22:44 You also have delivered me from strife with my people; You kept me as the head of the nations. People whom I had not known served me.
2Samuel22:45 Foreigners yielded feigned obedience to me; as soon as they heard of me
2Samuel22:46 Foreigners faded away; they came limping and trembling from their strongholds.
2Samuel22:47 The Lord lives; blessed be my Rock
2Samuel22:48 It is God Who executes vengeance for me and Who brought down [and disciplined] the peoples under me
2Samuel22:49 Who brought me out from my enemies. You also lifted me up above those who rose up against me; You delivered me from the violent man.
2Samuel22:50 For this I will give thanks and extol You
2Samuel22:51 He is a Tower of salvation and great deliverance to His king
