1Kings3:9 So give Your servant an understanding mind and a hearing heart to judge Your people
1Kings3:10 It pleased the Lord that Solomon had asked this.
1Kings3:11 God said to him
1Kings3:12 Behold
1Kings3:13 I have also given you what you have not asked
1Kings3:14 And if you will go My way
1Kings3:15 Solomon awoke
1Kings3:16 Then two women who had become mothers out of wedlock came and stood before the king.
1Kings3:17 And one woman said
1Kings3:18 And the third day after I was delivered
1Kings3:19 And this woman's child died in the night because she lay on him.
1Kings3:20 And she arose at midnight and took my son from beside me while your handmaid slept and laid him in her bosom and laid her dead child in my bosom.
1Kings3:21 And when I rose to nurse my child
1Kings3:22 But the other woman said
1Kings3:23 The king said
1Kings3:24 And the king said
1Kings3:25 And the king said
1Kings3:26 Then the mother of the living child said to the king
1Kings3:27 Then the king said
1Kings3:28 And all Israel heard of the judgment which the king had made
1Kings4:1 KING SOLOMON was king over all Israel.
1Kings4:2 These were his chief officials: Azariah son of Zadok was the [high] priest;
1Kings4:3 Elihoreph and Ahijah
1Kings4:4 Benaiah son of Jehoiada commanded the army; Zadok and Abiathar were priests;
1Kings4:5 Azariah son of Nathan was over the officers; Zabud son of Nathan was priest and the king's friend and private advisor;
1Kings4:6 Ahishar was in charge of the palace; and Adoniram son of Abda was in charge of the forced labor.
1Kings4:7 Solomon had twelve officers over all Israel
1Kings4:8 These were their names: Ben-hur
1Kings4:9 Ben-deker
1Kings4:10 Ben-hesed
1Kings4:11 Ben-abinadab
1Kings4:12 Baana son of Ahilud
1Kings4:13 Ben-geber
1Kings4:14 Ahinadab son of Iddo
1Kings4:15 Ahimaaz
1Kings4:16 Baana son of Hushai
1Kings4:17 Jehoshaphat son of Paruah
1Kings4:18 Shimei son of Ela
1Kings4:19 Geber son of Uri
1Kings4:20 Judah and Israel were many
1Kings4:21 Solomon reigned over all the kingdoms from the [Euphrates] River to the land of the Philistines and to the border of Egypt; they brought tribute and served Solomon all the days of his life.
1Kings4:22 Solomon's provision for one day was thirty measures of fine flour
1Kings4:23 Ten fat oxen
1Kings4:24 For he had dominion over all the region west of the [Euphrates] River
1Kings4:25 Judah and Israel dwelt safely
1Kings4:26 Solomon also had 40
1Kings4:27 And those officers provided food for King Solomon and for all who came to his table
1Kings4:28 Barley also and straw for the horses and swift steeds they brought to the place where it was needed
1Kings4:29 And God gave Solomon exceptionally much wisdom and understanding
1Kings4:30 Solomon's wisdom excelled the wisdom of all the people of the East and all the wisdom of Egypt.
1Kings4:31 For he was wiser than all other men–than Ethan the Ezrahite
1Kings4:32 He also originated 3
1Kings4:33 He spoke of trees
1Kings4:34 Men came from all peoples to hear the wisdom of Solomon
1Kings5:1 HIRAM KING of Tyre sent his servants to Solomon
1Kings5:2 And Solomon sent to Hiram
1Kings5:3 You know how David my father could not build a house to the Name of the Lord his God because wars were about him on every side
1Kings5:4 But now the Lord my God has given me rest on every side
1Kings5:5 And I purpose to build a house to the Name of the Lord my God
1Kings5:6 So
1Kings5:7 When Hiram heard the words of Solomon
1Kings5:8 And Hiram sent to Solomon
1Kings5:9 My servants shall bring the logs down from Lebanon to the sea
1Kings5:10 So Hiram gave Solomon all the cedar and cypress trees he desired
1Kings5:11 And Solomon gave Hiram 20
1Kings5:12 The Lord gave Solomon wisdom
1Kings5:13 King Solomon raised a levy [of forced labor] out of all Israel; and the levy was 30
1Kings5:14 He sent them to Lebanon
1Kings5:15 And Solomon had 70
1Kings5:16 Besides Solomon's 3
1Kings5:17 The king commanded
1Kings5:18 Solomon's builders and Hiram's builders and the men of Gebal did the hewing and prepared the timber and stones to build the house.
1Kings6:1 AND 480 years after the Israelites came out of the land of Egypt
1Kings6:2 The length of the house Solomon built for the Lord was sixty cubits
1Kings6:3 The length of the vestibule in front of the temple was twenty cubits
1Kings6:4 For the house he made narrow [latticed] windows.
1Kings6:5 Against the wall of the house he built chambers running round the walls of the house both of the Holy Place and of the Holy of Holies; and he made side chambers all around.
1Kings6:6 The first story's side chambers were five cubits wide
1Kings6:7 When the house was being built
1Kings6:8 The entrance to the lowest side chamber was on the right [or south] side of the house; and one went up winding stairs into the middle chamber and from the middle into the third.
1Kings6:9 So Solomon built the temple building and finished it
1Kings6:10 Then he built the stories of chambers [the lean-to] against all the house
1Kings6:11 Now the word of the Lord came to Solomon
1Kings6:12 Concerning this house which you are building
1Kings6:13 And I will dwell among the Israelites and will not forsake My people Israel.
1Kings6:14 So Solomon built the house and finished it.
1Kings6:15 He built the walls of the house (the Holy Place and the Holy of Holies) within with boards of cedar
1Kings6:16 He built twenty cubits of the rear of the house with boards of cedar from the floor to the rafters; he built it within for the sanctuary
1Kings6:17 The [rest of the] house
1Kings6:18 The cedar on the house within was carved with gourds and open flowers. All was cedar; no stone was visible.
1Kings6:19 And he prepared the Holy of Holies in the inner room in which to set the ark of the covenant of the Lord.
1Kings6:20 The Holy of Holies was twenty cubits in length
1Kings6:21 Solomon overlaid the house within with pure gold
1Kings6:22 And the whole house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:23 Within the Holy of Holies he made two cherubim of olive wood
1Kings6:24 Five cubits was the length of one wing of the cherub and five cubits its other wing; from the tip of one wing to the tip of the other was ten cubits.
1Kings6:25 The wings of the other cherub were also ten cubits. Both cherubim were the same
1Kings6:26 The height of one cherub ten cubits
1Kings6:27 He put the cherubim within the inner sanctuary. Their wings were stretched out
1Kings6:28 Solomon overlaid the cherubim with gold.
1Kings6:29 He carved all the walls of the house (these two holy rooms) round about with figures of cherubim
1Kings6:30 The floor of the house he overlaid with gold
1Kings6:31 For the Holy of Holies he made [folding] doors of olive wood; their entire width was one-fifth that of the wall.
1Kings6:32 On the two doors of olive wood he carved cherubim
1Kings6:33 Also he made for the door of the Holy Place four-sided posts of olive wood.
1Kings6:34 The two doors were of cypress wood; the two leaves of each door were folding.
1Kings6:35 He carved on them cherubim
1Kings6:36 He built the inner court with three rows of hewn stone and a row of cedar beams.
1Kings6:37 In the fourth year the foundation of the Lord's house was laid
1Kings6:38 In the eleventh year
1Kings7:1 SOLOMON WAS building his own house thirteen years
1Kings7:2 He built also the Forest of Lebanon House; its length was a hundred cubits
1Kings7:3 And it was covered with cedar above the side chambers that were upon the forty-five pillars
1Kings7:4 There were window frames in three rows
1Kings7:5 All the doorways and windows were square cut
1Kings7:6 He also made the Hall of Pillars; its length was fifty cubits and its breadth thirty cubits. There was a porch in front
1Kings7:7 He made the porch for the throne where he was to judge
1Kings7:8 His house where he was to dwell had another court behind the Porch of Judgment of similar work. Solomon also made a house like this porch for Pharaoh's daughter
1Kings7:9 All were of costly stones hewn according to measure
1Kings7:10 The foundation was of costly stones
1Kings7:11 And above were costly stones hewn according to measure
1Kings7:12 Also the great encircling court had three courses of hewn stone and a course of cedar beams
1Kings7:13 King Solomon brought Hiram from Tyre.
1Kings7:14 He was the son of a widow of the tribe of Naphtali
1Kings7:15 He fashioned the two pillars of bronze
1Kings7:16 He made two capitals of molten bronze to set upon the tops of the pillars; the height of each capital was five cubits.
1Kings7:17 Nets of checkerwork and wreaths of chainwork for the capitals were on the tops of the pillars
1Kings7:18 So Hiram made the pillars. There were two rows of pomegranates encircling each network to cover the capitals that were upon the top.
1Kings7:19 The capitals that were upon the top of the pillars in the porch were of lily work [design]
1Kings7:20 The capitals were upon the two pillars and also above the rounded projection beside the network. There were 200 pomegranates in two rows round about
1Kings7:21 Hiram set up the pillars of the porch of the temple; he set up the right pillar and called its name Jachin [he will establish]
1Kings7:22 On the tops of the pillars was lily work [design]. So the work of the pillars was finished.
1Kings7:23 He made a round molten Sea
1Kings7:24 Under its brim were gourds encircling the Sea
1Kings7:25 It stood upon twelve oxen
1Kings7:26 It was a handbreadth thick
1Kings7:27 Hiram made ten bronze bases [for the lavers]; their length and breadth were four cubits
1Kings7:28 This is the way the bases were made: they had panels between the ledges.
1Kings7:29 On the panels between the ledges were lions
1Kings7:30 And every base had four bronze wheels and axles of bronze
1Kings7:31 Its mouth within the capital projected upward a cubit
1Kings7:32 Under the borders were four wheels
1Kings7:33 The wheels were made like a chariot wheel: their axles
1Kings7:34 There were four supports to the four corners of each base; the supports were part of the base itself.
1Kings7:35 On the top of the base there was a circular elevation half a cubit high
1Kings7:36 And on the surface of its stays and its panels Hiram carved cherubim
1Kings7:37 Thus he made the ten bases. They all had one casting
1Kings7:38 Then he made ten lavers of bronze; each laver held forty baths and measured four cubits
1Kings7:39 He put the bases five on the south side of the house and five on the north side; and he set the Sea at the southeast corner of the house.
1Kings7:40 Hiram made the lavers
1Kings7:41 The two pillars; and the two bowls of the capitals that were on the tops of the two pillars; and the two networks to cover the two bowls;
1Kings7:42 And the 400 pomegranates for the two networks
1Kings7:43 The ten bases and the ten lavers on the bases;
1Kings7:44 One Sea
1Kings7:45 The pots
1Kings7:46 In the Jordan plain the king cast them
1Kings7:47 Solomon left all the vessels unweighed
1Kings7:48 Solomon made all the other vessels of the Lord's house: the [incense] altar of gold; the table of gold for the showbread;
1Kings7:49 The lampstands of pure gold
1Kings7:50 The cups
1Kings7:51 So all the work that King Solomon did on the house of the Lord was completed. Solomon brought in the things which David his father had dedicated–the silver
1Kings8:1 THEN SOLOMON assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes
1Kings8:2 All the men of Israel assembled themselves before King Solomon at the feast in the seventh month
1Kings8:3 All the elders of Israel came
1Kings8:4 And they brought up the ark of the Lord
1Kings8:5 King Solomon and all the congregation of Israel who had assembled before him were with him before the ark
1Kings8:6 And the priests brought the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place in the Holy of Holies of the house
1Kings8:7 For the cherubim spread forth their two wings over the place of the ark
1Kings8:8 The poles were so long that the ends of them were seen from the Holy Place before the Holy of Holies
1Kings8:9 There was nothing in the ark except the two tables of stone which Moses put there at Horeb
1Kings8:10 When the priests had come out of the Holy Place
1Kings8:11 So the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud
1Kings8:12 Then Solomon said
1Kings8:13 I have surely built You a house of habitation
1Kings8:14 And the king turned his face about and blessed all the assembly of Israel
1Kings8:15 He said
1Kings8:16 Since the day that I brought forth My people Israel out of Egypt
1Kings8:17 Now it was in the heart of David my father to build a house for the Name [the Presence] of the Lord
1Kings8:18 And the Lord said to David my father
1Kings8:19 Yet you shall not build the house
1Kings8:20 And the Lord has fulfilled His promise which He made: I have risen up in the place of David my father
1Kings8:21 And I have made there a place for the ark [the token of His presence]
1Kings8:22 Then Solomon stood [in the court] before the Lord's burnt offering altar in the presence of all the assembly of Israel
1Kings8:23 And he said
1Kings8:24 You have kept what You promised Your servant David my father. You also spoke with Your mouth and have fulfilled it with Your hand
1Kings8:25 Therefore now
1Kings8:26 Now
1Kings8:27 But will God indeed dwell with men on the earth? Behold
1Kings8:28 Yet graciously consider the prayer and supplication of Your servant
1Kings8:29 That Your eyes may be open toward this house night and day
1Kings8:30 Hearken to the prayer of Your servant and of Your people Israel when they pray in or toward this place. Hear in heaven
1Kings8:31 Whenever a man sins against his neighbor and is made to take an oath and comes and swears the oath before Your altar in this house
1Kings8:32 Then hear in heaven and do and judge Your servants
1Kings8:33 When Your people Israel are struck down before the enemy because they have sinned against You
1Kings8:34 Then hear in heaven and forgive the sin of Your people Israel and return them to the land You gave to their fathers.
1Kings8:35 When heaven is shut up and no rain falls because they have sinned against You
1Kings8:36 Then hear in heaven and forgive the sin of Your servants
1Kings8:37 If there is famine in the land or pestilence
1Kings8:38 Whatever prayer or supplication is made by any or all of Your people Israel–each man knowing the affliction of his own heart
1Kings8:39 Then hear in heaven
1Kings8:40 That they may fear and revere You all the days that they live in the land which You gave to our fathers.
1Kings8:41 Moreover
1Kings8:42 For they will hear of Your great name (Your revelation of Yourself)
1Kings8:43 Hear in heaven
1Kings8:44 If Your people go out to battle against their enemy
1Kings8:45 Then hear in heaven their prayer and supplication
1Kings8:46 If they sin against You–for there is no man who does not sin–and You are angry with them and deliver them to the enemy
1Kings8:47 Yet if they think and consider in the land where they were carried captive
1Kings8:48 If they repent and turn to You with all their mind and with all their heart in the land of their enemies who took them captive
1Kings8:49 Then hear their prayer and their supplication in heaven
1Kings8:50 And forgive Your people
1Kings8:51 For they are Your people and Your heritage
1Kings8:52 Let Your eyes be open to the supplication of Your servant and of Your people Israel
1Kings8:53 For You separated them from among all the peoples of the earth to be Your heritage
1Kings8:54 When Solomon finished offering all this prayer and supplication to the Lord
1Kings8:55 And he stood and blessed all the assembly of Israel with a loud voice
1Kings8:56 Blessed be the Lord
1Kings8:57 May the Lord our God be with us as He was with our fathers; may He not leave us or forsake us
1Kings8:58 That He may incline our hearts to Him
1Kings8:59 Let these my words
1Kings8:60 That all the earth's people may know that the Lord is God and that there is no other.
1Kings8:61 Let your hearts therefore be blameless and wholly true to the Lord our God
1Kings8:62 And the king and all Israel with him offered sacrifice before the Lord.
1Kings8:63 Solomon offered as peace offerings to the Lord: 22
1Kings8:64 On that same day the king consecrated the middle of the court that was before the Lord's house; there he offered burnt offerings
1Kings8:65 So at that time Solomon held the feast
1Kings8:66 On the eighth day he sent the people away; they blessed the king and went to their tents with greatest joy and gratitude for all the goodness the Lord had shown to David His servant and Israel His people.

1Kings9:2 The Lord appeared to Solomon the second time
1Kings9:3 The Lord told him
1Kings9:4 And if you will walk before Me
1Kings9:5 Then I will establish your royal throne over Israel forever
1Kings9:6 But if you turn away from following Me
1Kings9:7 Then I will cut off Israel from the land I have given them
1Kings9:8 This house shall become a heap of ruins; every passerby shall be astonished and shall hiss [with surprise] and say
1Kings9:9 Then they will answer
1Kings9:10 At the end of twenty years
1Kings9:11 For which Hiram king of Tyre had furnished Solomon with as much cedar and cypress timber and gold as he desired
1Kings9:12 And Hiram came from Tyre to see the cities which Solomon had given him
1Kings9:13 He said
1Kings9:14 And Hiram sent to the king 120 talents of gold.
1Kings9:15 This is the account of the levy [of forced labor] which King Solomon raised to build the house of the Lord

1Kings9:17 So Solomon rebuilt Gezer and Lower Beth-horon
1Kings9:18 Baalath and Tamar (Tadmor) in the wilderness
1Kings9:19 And all the store cities which Solomon had and cities for his chariots and cities for his horsemen
1Kings9:20 As for all the people who were left of the Amorites
1Kings9:21 Their children who were left after them in the land
1Kings9:22 But Solomon made no slaves of the Israelites; they were the soldiers
1Kings9:23 These were the chief officers over Solomon's work
1Kings9:24 But Pharaoh's daughter came up out of the City of David to her house which Solomon had built for her; then he built the Millo.
1Kings9:25 Three times a year Solomon offered burnt offerings and peace offerings on the altar he built to the Lord
1Kings9:26 And King Solomon made a fleet of ships in Ezion-geber
1Kings9:27 And Hiram sent with the fleet his servants
1Kings9:28 They came to Ophir and got 420 talents of gold and brought it to King Solomon.
1Kings10:1 WHEN THE queen of Sheba heard of [the constant connection of] the fame of Solomon with the name of the Lord
1Kings10:2 She came to Jerusalem with a very great train
1Kings10:3 Solomon answered all her questions; there was nothing hidden from the king which he failed to explain to her.
1Kings10:4 When the queen of Sheba had seen all Solomon's wisdom and skill
1Kings10:5 The food of his table
1Kings10:6 She said to the king
1Kings10:7 I did not believe it until I came and my eyes had seen. Behold
1Kings10:8 Happy are your men! Happy are these your servants who stand continually before you
1Kings10:9 Blessed be the Lord your God
1Kings10:10 And she gave the king 120 talents of gold and of spices a very great store and precious stones. Never again came such abundance of spices as these the queen of Sheba gave King Solomon.
1Kings10:11 The navy also of Hiram brought from Ophir gold and a great plenty of almug (algum) wood and precious stones.
1Kings10:12 Of the almug wood the king made pillars for the house of the Lord and for the king's house
1Kings10:13 King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all she wanted
1Kings10:14 Now the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one [particular] year was 666 talents of gold
1Kings10:15 Besides what the traders brought and the traffic of the merchants and from all the [tributary] kings and governors of the land of Arabia.
1Kings10:16 King Solomon made 200 large shields of beaten gold; 600 shekels of gold went into each shield.
1Kings10:17 And he made 300 shields of beaten gold; three minas of gold went into each shield. The king put them in the House of the Forest of Lebanon.
1Kings10:18 Also the king made a great throne of ivory and overlaid it with the finest gold.
1Kings10:19 The throne had six steps
1Kings10:20 Twelve lions stood there
1Kings10:21 All King Solomon's drinking vessels were of gold
1Kings10:22 For the king had a fleet of ships of Tarshish at sea with the fleet of Hiram. Once every three years the fleet of ships of Tarshish came bringing gold
1Kings10:23 So King Solomon exceeded all the kings of the earth in riches and in wisdom (skill).
1Kings10:24 And all the earth sought the presence of Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put in his mind.
1Kings10:25 Every man brought tribute: vessels of silver and gold
1Kings10:26 Solomon collected chariots and horsemen; he had 1
1Kings10:27 The king made silver as common in Jerusalem as stones
1Kings10:28 Solomon's horses were brought out of Egypt
1Kings10:29 A chariot could be brought out of Egypt for 600 shekels of silver

1Kings11:4 For when Solomon was old
1Kings11:5 For Solomon went after Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians
1Kings11:6 Solomon did evil in the sight of the Lord

1Kings11:8 And he did so for all of his foreign wives
1Kings11:9 And the Lord was angry with Solomon because his heart was turned from the Lord
1Kings11:10 And had commanded him concerning this thing
1Kings11:11 Therefore the Lord said to Solomon
1Kings11:12 However
1Kings11:13 However
1Kings11:14 The Lord stirred up an adversary against Solomon
1Kings11:15 For when David was in Edom
1Kings11:16 For Joab and all Israel remained there for six months
1Kings11:17 But Hadad fled
1Kings11:18 They set out from Midian and came to Paran
1Kings11:19 Hadad found great favor with Pharaoh
1Kings11:20 The sister of Tahpenes bore Hadad Genubath his son
1Kings11:21 But when Hadad heard in Egypt that David slept with his fathers and that Joab the commander of Israel's army was dead
1Kings11:22 Then Pharaoh said to him
1Kings11:23 God raised up for [Hadad] another adversary
1Kings11:24 Rezon gathered men about him and became leader of a marauding band after the slaughter by David. They went to Damascus and dwelt and made [Rezon] king in Damascus.
1Kings11:25 And Rezon was an adversary to Israel all the days of Solomon
1Kings11:26 Jeroboam son of Nebat
1Kings11:27 And for this reason: Solomon built the Millo and repaired the breaches of the city of David his father.
1Kings11:28 The man Jeroboam was a mighty man of courage. Solomon
1Kings11:29 At that time
1Kings11:30 Ahijah caught the new garment he wore and tore it into twelve pieces.
1Kings11:31 He said to Jeroboam
1Kings11:32 But he shall have one tribe
1Kings11:33 Because they have forsaken Me and have worshiped Ashtoreth the goddess of the Sidonians
1Kings11:34 However
1Kings11:35 But I will take the kingdom out of his son's hand and give it to you
1Kings11:36 Yet to his son I will give one tribe
1Kings11:37 And I will take you
1Kings11:38 And if you will hearken to all I command you and will walk in My ways and do right in My sight
1Kings11:39 And I will for this afflict the descendants of David
1Kings11:40 Solomon sought therefore to kill Jeroboam. But Jeroboam arose and fled into Egypt
1Kings11:41 The rest of the acts of Solomon–and all that he did
1Kings11:42 The time Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel was forty years.
1Kings11:43 And Solomon slept with his fathers and was buried in the city of David his father. Rehoboam his son reigned in his stead.
1Kings12:1 REHOBOAM WENT to Shechem
1Kings12:2 And when Jeroboam son of Nebat heard of it–for he still dwelt in Egypt
1Kings12:3 And they sent and called him
1Kings12:4 Your father made our yoke heavy; now therefore lighten the hard service and the heavy yoke your father put upon us
1Kings12:5 He replied
1Kings12:6 And King Rehoboam consulted with the old men who stood before Solomon his father while he yet lived and said
1Kings12:7 And they said to him
1Kings12:8 But he forsook the counsel the old men gave him and consulted the young men who grew up with him and stood before him.
1Kings12:9 He said to them
1Kings12:10 The young men who grew up with him answered
1Kings12:11 And now whereas my father loaded you with a heavy yoke
1Kings12:12 So Jeroboam and all the people came to Rehoboam on the third day
1Kings12:13 And the king answered the people roughly and forsook the counsel the old men had given him
1Kings12:14 And spoke to them after the counsel of the young men
1Kings12:15 So the king did not hearken to the people
1Kings12:16 So when all Israel saw that the king did not heed them
1Kings12:17 But Rehoboam reigned over the Israelites who dwelt in the cities of Judah.
1Kings12:18 Then King Rehoboam sent Adoram
1Kings12:19 So Israel has rebelled against the house of David to this day.
1Kings12:20 When all Israel heard that Jeroboam had returned
1Kings12:21 And when Rehoboam had come to Jerusalem
1Kings12:22 But the word of God came to Shemaiah the man of God
1Kings12:23 Tell Rehoboam son of Solomon king of Judah and all the house of Judah and Benjamin and the remnant of the people
1Kings12:24 Thus says the Lord
1Kings12:25 Then Jeroboam built Shechem in the hill country of Ephraim and lived there. He went out from there and built Penuel.
1Kings12:26 Jeroboam said in his heart
1Kings12:27 If this people goes up to the house of the Lord at Jerusalem to sacrifice
1Kings12:28 So the king took counsel and made two calves of gold. And he said to the people

1Kings12:30 And this thing became a sin; for the people went to worship each of them even as far as Dan.
1Kings12:31 Jeroboam also made houses on high places and made priests of people who were not Levites.
1Kings12:32 And Jeroboam appointed a feast on the fifteenth day of the eighth month
1Kings12:33 So he offered upon the altar he had made in Bethel on the fifteenth day of the eighth month
1Kings13:1 AND BEHOLD
1Kings13:2 The man cried against the altar by the word of the Lord
1Kings13:3 And he gave a sign the same day
1Kings13:4 When King Jeroboam heard the words the man of God cried against the altar in Bethel
1Kings13:5 The altar also was split and the ashes poured out from the altar according to the sign which the man of God had given by the word of the Lord.
1Kings13:6 And the king said to the man of God
1Kings13:7 And the king said to the man of God
1Kings13:8 And the man of God said to the king
1Kings13:9 For I was commanded by the word of the Lord
1Kings13:10 So he went another way and did not return by the way that he came to Bethel.
1Kings13:11 Now there dwelt an old prophet in Bethel; and his sons came and told him all that the man of God had done that day in Bethel; the words which he had spoken to the king they told also to their father.
1Kings13:12 Their father asked them
1Kings13:13 He said to his sons
1Kings13:14 And went after the man of God. And he found him sitting under an oak

1Kings14:27 King Rehoboam made in their stead bronze shields and committed them to the hands of the captains of the guard who kept the door of the king's house.

1Kings15:1 IN THE eighteenth year of King Jeroboam son of Nebat
1Kings15:2 He reigned three years in Jerusalem. His mother was Maacah (Micaiah) daughter [granddaughter] of Abishalom (Absalom).
1Kings15:3 He walked in all the sins of his father [Rehoboam] before him; and his heart was not blameless with the Lord his God
1Kings15:4 Nevertheless
1Kings15:5 Because David did what was right in the eyes of the Lord and turned not aside from anything that He commanded him all the days of his life

1Kings15:7 The rest of the acts of Abijam
1Kings15:8 Abijam slept with his fathers and they buried him in the City of David. Asa his son reigned in his stead.
1Kings15:9 In the twentieth year of Jeroboam king of Israel

1Kings15:15 He brought the things which his father had dedicated and the things which he himself had dedicated into the house of the Lord–silver
1Kings15:16 There was war between Asa and Baasha king of Israel all their days.
1Kings15:17 Baasha king of Israel went up against Judah and built up Ramah
1Kings15:18 Then Asa took all the silver and gold left in the treasuries of the house of the Lord and of the king's house and delivered them into the hands of his servants. And King Asa sent them to Ben-hadad son of Tabrimmon

1Kings15:20 So Ben-hadad hearkened to king Asa and sent the commanders of his armies against the cities of Israel
1Kings15:21 When Baasha heard of it
1Kings15:22 Then King Asa made a proclamation to all Judah–none was exempted. They carried away the stones of Ramah and its timber with which Baasha had been building. And King Asa built up with them Geba of Benjamin
1Kings15:23 The rest of all the acts of Asa
1Kings15:24 Asa slept with his fathers and was buried with them in the city of David his father. Jehoshaphat his son reigned in his stead.

1Kings15:26 He did evil in the sight of the Lord and walked in the way of his father and in his sin

2Kings15:25 But Pekah son of Remaliah
2Kings15:26 The rest of the acts of Pekahiah
2Kings15:27 In the fifty-second year of Azariah king of Judah
2Kings15:28 He did evil in the Lord's sight; he did not depart from the sins of Jeroboam [I] son of Nebat
2Kings15:29 In the days of Pekah king of Israel
2Kings15:30 Hoshea son of Elah conspired against Pekah son of Remaliah [of Israel]; he smote and killed him

2Kings15:32 In the second year of Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel
2Kings15:33 When he was twenty-five years old
2Kings15:34 He did right in the Lord's sight
2Kings15:35 Yet the high places were not removed; the people sacrificed and burned incense still on the high places. He built the Upper Gate of the house of the Lord.
2Kings15:36 The rest of the acts of Jotham
2Kings15:37 In those days the Lord began sending Rezin king of Syria and Pekah son of Remaliah against Judah.
2Kings15:38 Jotham slept with his fathers and was buried [with them] in the city of David his [forefather]. Ahaz his son succeeded him.
2Kings16:1 IN THE seventeenth year of Pekah son of Remaliah
2Kings16:2 Ahaz was twenty years old when he began his sixteen-year reign in Jerusalem. He did not do right in the sight of the Lord his God
2Kings16:3 But he walked in the ways of Israel's kings
2Kings16:4 He sacrificed and burned incense in the high places
2Kings16:5 Then Rezin king of Syria and Pekah son of Remaliah king of Israel came up to Jerusalem to wage war; they besieged Ahaz
2Kings16:6 At that time
2Kings16:7 So Ahaz sent messengers to Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria
2Kings16:8 And Ahaz took the silver and gold in the house of the Lord and in the treasuries of the king's house and sent a present to the king of Assyria.
2Kings16:9 Assyria's king hearkened to him; he went up against Damascus
2Kings16:10 King Ahaz went to Damascus to meet Tiglath-pileser king of Assyria
2Kings16:11 So Urijah the priest built an altar according to all that King Ahaz had sent from Damascus
2Kings16:12 When the king came from Damascus
2Kings16:13 King Ahaz burned his burnt offering and his cereal offering
2Kings16:14 The bronze altar which was before the Lord he removed from the front of the house
2Kings16:15 And King Ahaz commanded Urijah the priest: Upon the principal (the new) altar
2Kings16:16 Urijah the priest did all this as King Ahaz commanded.
2Kings16:17 [To keep Assyria's king from getting them] King Ahaz cut off the panels of the bases [of the ten lavers] and removed the laver from each of them; and he took down the Sea from off the bronze oxen that were under it and put it upon stone supports.
2Kings16:18 And the covered way for the Sabbath that they had built in the temple court
2Kings16:19 The rest of the acts of Ahaz
2Kings16:20 Ahaz slept with his fathers and was buried [with them] in the City of David. Hezekiah his son reigned in his stead.
2Kings17:1 IN THE twelfth year of Ahaz king of Judah
2Kings17:2 He did evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings17:3 Against him came up Shalmaneser king of Assyria
2Kings17:4 But the king of Assyria found treachery in Hoshea
2Kings17:5 Then the king of Assyria invaded all the land and went up to Samaria and besieged it for three years.
2Kings17:6 In the ninth year of Hoshea
2Kings17:7 This was so because the Israelites had sinned against the Lord their God
2Kings17:8 And walked in the customs of the [heathen] nations whom the Lord drove out before the Israelites
2Kings17:9 The Israelites did secretly against the Lord their God things not right. They built for themselves high places in all their towns
2Kings17:10 They set up for themselves pillars and Asherim [symbols of the goddess Asherah] on every high hill and under every green tree.
2Kings17:11 There they burned incense on all the high places
2Kings17:12 And they served idols
2Kings17:13 Yet the Lord warned Israel and Judah through all the prophets and all the seers
2Kings17:14 Yet they would not hear
2Kings17:15 They despised and rejected His statutes and His covenant which He made with their fathers and His warnings to them
2Kings17:16 And they forsook all the commandments of the Lord their God and made for themselves molten images
2Kings17:17 They caused their sons and their daughters to pass through the fire and used divination and enchantments and sold themselves to do evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings17:18 Therefore the Lord was very angry with Israel and removed them out of His sight. None was left but the tribe of Judah.
2Kings17:19 Judah also did not keep the commandments of the Lord their God
2Kings17:20 The Lord rejected all the descendants of Israel and afflicted them and delivered them into the hands of spoilers
2Kings17:21 For He tore Israel from the house of David; and they made Jeroboam son of Nebat king. And Jeroboam drew and drove Israel away from following the Lord and made them sin a great sin.
2Kings17:22 For the Israelites walked in all the sins Jeroboam committed; they departed not from them
2Kings17:23 Until the Lord removed Israel from His sight
2Kings17:24 The king of Assyria brought men from Babylon
2Kings17:25 At the beginning of their dwelling there
2Kings17:26 So the king of Assyria was told: The nations you removed and placed in the cities of Samaria do not know the manner in which the God of the land requires their worship. Therefore He has sent lions among them
2Kings17:27 Then the king of Assyria commanded
2Kings17:28 So one of the priests whom they had carried away from Samaria came and dwelt in Bethel and taught them how they should fear and revere the Lord.
2Kings17:29 But every nationality still made gods of their own and put them in the shrines of the high places which the Samaritans had made
2Kings17:30 The men of Babylon made [and worshiped their deity] Succoth-benoth
2Kings17:31 The Avvites made Nibhaz and Tartak
2Kings17:32 So they feared the Lord
2Kings17:33 They feared the Lord
2Kings17:34 Unto this day they do after their former custom: they do not fear the Lord [as God sees it]
2Kings17:35 With whom the Lord had made a covenant and commanded them
2Kings17:36 But you shall [reverently] fear
2Kings17:37 And the statutes
2Kings17:38 And the covenant that I have made with you you shall not forget; you shall not fear other gods.
2Kings17:39 But the Lord your God you shall [reverently] fear; then He will deliver you out of the hands of all your enemies.
2Kings17:40 However
2Kings17:41 So these nations [vainly] feared the Lord and also served their graven images

2Kings21:1 MANASSEH WAS twelve years old when he began his fifty-five-year [wicked] reign in Jerusalem. His mother's name was Hephzibah.
2Kings21:2 He [Hezekiah's son] did evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings21:3 For he built up again the high places which Hezekiah his father had destroyed; and he reared up altars for Baal and made an Asherah
2Kings21:4 And he built [heathen] altars in the house of the Lord
2Kings21:5 And he [good Hezekiah's son] built altars for all the hosts of the heavens in the two courts of the house of the Lord!
2Kings21:6 And he made his son pass through the fire and burned him as an offering [to Molech]; he practiced soothsaying and augury
2Kings21:7 He made a graven image of [the goddess] Asherah and set it in the house
2Kings21:8 And I will not cause the feet of Israel to wander any more out of the land which I gave their fathers
2Kings21:9 But they would not listen; and Manasseh seduced them to do more evil than the nations did whom the Lord destroyed before the Israelites!
2Kings21:10 And the Lord said through His servants the prophets:
2Kings21:11 Because Manasseh king of Judah has committed these abominations
2Kings21:12 Therefore thus says the Lord
2Kings21:13 And I will stretch over Jerusalem the measuring line of Samaria and the plummet of the house of Ahab; and I will wipe Jerusalem as one wipes a dish
2Kings21:14 And I will cast off the rest of My inheritance and deliver them into the hands of their enemies; and they shall become a prey and a spoil to all their enemies
2Kings21:15 For they have done evil in My sight and have provoked Me to anger since their fathers came out of Egypt to this day.
2Kings21:16 Moreover
2Kings21:17 The rest of the acts of Manasseh
2Kings21:18 Manasseh slept with his fathers and was buried in the garden of his own house
2Kings21:19 Amon was twenty-two years old when he began his two-year reign in Jerusalem. His mother was Meshullemeth daughter of Haruz of Jotbah.
2Kings21:20 [But] he also did evil in the sight of the Lord
2Kings21:21 He walked in all the ways of his father; and he served the idols that his father served
2Kings21:22 He forsook the Lord
2Kings21:23 The servants of Amon conspired against him and killed the king in his own house.
2Kings21:24 But the people of the land killed all those who had conspired against King Amon
2Kings21:25 The rest of the acts of Amon
2Kings21:26 He was buried in his tomb in the garden of Uzza. Josiah his son succeeded him.

2Kings23:1 KING JOSIAH sent and gathered to him all the elders of Judah and of Jerusalem.

2Kings23:5 He put away the idolatrous priests whom the kings of Judah had ordained to burn incense in the high places in Judah's cities and round about Jerusalem–also those who burned incense to Baal

2Kings25:1 IN THE ninth year of Zedekiah's reign
2Kings25:2 The city was besieged [nearly two years] until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah.
2Kings25:3 On the ninth day of the fourth month the famine was complete in the city; there was no food for the people of the land.
2Kings25:4 Then the city was broken through; the king and all the warriors fled by night by way of the gate between the two walls by the king's garden
2Kings25:5 The Chaldean army pursued the king and overtook him in the plains of Jericho. All his army was scattered from him.
2Kings25:6 So they captured Zedekiah and brought him to the king of Babylon at Riblah
2Kings25:7 And they slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes and put out the eyes of Zedekiah and bound him in double fetters [hands and feet] and carried him to Babylon. [Foretold in Jer. 34:3; Ezek. 12:13.]

2Kings25:10 All the army of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the [Babylonian] guard broke down the walls around Jerusalem.
2Kings25:11 Now the rest of the people left in the city and the deserters who fell away to the king of Babylon
2Kings25:12 But the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and soil tillers.

2Kings25:16 The two pillars
2Kings25:17 The height of the one pillar was eighteen cubits
2Kings25:18 The captain of the guard took Seraiah the chief priest
2Kings25:19 And out of the city he took an officer who was in command of the men of war and five men of the king's personal advisors
2Kings25:20 Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took these and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
2Kings25:21 The king of Babylon smote and killed them at Riblah in the land of Hamath [north of Damascus]. So Judah was taken into exile.

1Chronicles10:1 NOW THE Philistines fought against Israel; and the men of Israel fled from before them and fell slain on Mount Gilboa.
1Chronicles10:2 And the Philistines followed close after Saul and his sons and overtook them
1Chronicles10:3 And the battle raged about Saul
1Chronicles10:4 Then Saul said to his armor-bearer
1Chronicles10:5 When his armor-bearer saw that Saul was dead
1Chronicles10:6 So Saul died; he and his three sons and all his house died together.
1Chronicles10:7 And when all the men of Israel who were in the valley saw that the army had fled and that Saul and his sons were dead
1Chronicles10:8 The next day
1Chronicles10:9 They stripped [Saul] and took his head and his armor
1Chronicles10:10 And they put [Saul's] armor in the house of their gods and fastened his head in the temple of Dagon.
1Chronicles10:11 When all Jabesh-gilead heard all that the Philistines had done to Saul
1Chronicles10:12 All the brave men arose
1Chronicles10:13 So Saul died for his trespass against the Lord [in sparing Amalek]
1Chronicles10:14 And inquired not so of the Lord [in earnest penitence]. Therefore the Lord slew him and turned the kingdom over to David son of Jesse. [I Sam. 28:6.]

1Chronicles26:1 FOR THE divisions of the gatekeepers: Of the Korahites was: Meshelemiah son of Kore

1Chronicles26:3 Elam the fifth
1Chronicles26:4 Obed-edom had sons: Shemaiah the firstborn
1Chronicles26:5 Ammiel the sixth
1Chronicles26:6 Also to Shemaiah his son were sons born
1Chronicles26:7 The sons of Shemaiah: Othni
1Chronicles26:8 All these were sons of Obed-edom [in whose house the ark was kept]
1Chronicles26:9 Meshelemiah had sons and brethren
1Chronicles26:10 Also Hosah
1Chronicles26:11 Hilkiah the second
1Chronicles26:12 Of these were the divisions of the gatekeepers

1Chronicles26:14 The lot for the east fell to Shelemiah. They cast lots also for Zechariah his son
1Chronicles26:15 To Obed-edom it came out for the south
1Chronicles26:16 To Shuppim and Hosah the lot fell for the west
1Chronicles26:17 On the east were six Levites
1Chronicles26:18 At the colonnade on the west side [of the outer court of the temple]
1Chronicles26:19 These were the divisions of the gatekeepers among the Korahites and the sons of Merari.

1Chronicles26:28 Also all that Samuel the seer
1Chronicles26:29 Of the Izharites: Chenaniah and his sons were appointed to outside duties for Israel

1Chronicles26:31 Of the Hebronites: Jerijah was the chief
1Chronicles26:32 Jerijah's kinsmen
1Chronicles27:1 THIS IS the list of the Israelites
1Chronicles27:2 Over the first division for the first month was Jashobeam son of Zabdiel. In his division were 24
1Chronicles27:3 He was descended from Perez and was chief of all the commanders of the army for the first month.
1Chronicles27:4 Over the division for the second month was Dodai the Ahohite; and of his division Mikloth was the chief officer. In his division were 24
1Chronicles27:5 The third commander of the army for the third month was Benaiah son of Jehoiada the priest
1Chronicles27:6 This is the Benaiah who was a mighty man of the Thirty and over the Thirty; and in his division was Ammizabad his son.
1Chronicles27:7 The fourth
1Chronicles27:8 The fifth
1Chronicles27:9 The sixth
1Chronicles27:10 The seventh
1Chronicles27:11 The eighth
1Chronicles27:12 The ninth
1Chronicles27:13 The tenth
1Chronicles27:14 The eleventh
1Chronicles27:15 The twelfth
1Chronicles27:16 Also over the tribes of Israel: of the Reubenites: Eliezer son of Zichri was chief officer; of the Simeonites: Shephatiah son of Maachah;
1Chronicles27:17 Of Levi: Hashabiah son of Kemuel; of Aaron: Zadok;
1Chronicles27:18 Of Judah: Elihu
1Chronicles27:19 Of Zebulun: Ishmaiah son of Obadiah; of Naphtali: Jerimoth son of Azriel;
1Chronicles27:20 Of the Ephraimites: Hoshea son of Azaziah; of the half-tribe of Manasseh: Joel son of Pedaiah;
1Chronicles27:21 Of the half-tribe of Manasseh in Gilead: Iddo son of Zechariah; of Benjamin: Jaasiel son of Abner;
1Chronicles27:22 Of Dan: Azarel son of Jeroham. These were the leaders of the tribes of Israel.
1Chronicles27:23 But David did not number those under twenty years of age
1Chronicles27:24 Joab son of Zeruiah began a census but did not finish
1Chronicles27:25 Over the king's treasuries was Azmaveth son of Adiel; and over the treasuries in the country
1Chronicles27:26 Over those who did the work of the field of tilling the soil was Ezri son of Chelub;
1Chronicles27:27 Over the vineyards was Shimei the Ramathite; over the produce of the vineyards for the wine cellars
1Chronicles27:28 Over the olive and sycamore trees in the low plains
1Chronicles27:29 Over the herds pasturing in Sharon
1Chronicles27:30 Over the camels
1Chronicles27:31 And over the flocks
1Chronicles27:32 Also Jonathan
1Chronicles27:33 Ahithophel was the king's counselor; Hushai the Archite was the king's companion and friend.
1Chronicles27:34 Ahithophel was succeeded by Jehoiada son of Benaiah and by Abiathar. Joab was the commander of the king's army.

1Chronicles28:9 And you
1Chronicles28:10 Take heed now

1Chronicles28:13 The plan for the divisions of the priests and the Levites
1Chronicles28:14 The weight of gold and silver for all the gold and silver articles of every kind of service–
1Chronicles28:15 The weight of the golden lampstands and their lamps

1Chronicles28:19 All this the Lord made me understand by the writing by His hand upon me
1Chronicles28:20 Also David told Solomon his son

1Chronicles29:8 And whoever had precious stones gave them to the treasury of the house of the Lord in the care of Jehiel the Gershonite.

1Chronicles29:22 They ate and drank before the Lord on that day with great rejoicing. They made Solomon son of David king a second time
1Chronicles29:23 Then Solomon sat on the throne of the Lord as king instead of David his father; and he prospered
1Chronicles29:24 All the leaders and mighty men
1Chronicles29:25 And the Lord magnified Solomon exceedingly in the sight of all Israel and bestowed upon him such royal majesty as had not been on any king before him in Israel.
1Chronicles29:26 Thus David son of Jesse reigned over all Israel.
1Chronicles29:27 The time he reigned over Israel was forty years–he reigned seven years in Hebron and thirty-three years in Jerusalem.
1Chronicles29:28 He died in a good old age [his seventy-first year]

2Chronicles1:1 SOLOMON SON of David was strengthened in his kingdom
2Chronicles1:2 Solomon spoke to all Israel
2Chronicles1:3 And Solomon and all the assembly [a united nation] with him went to the high place that was at Gibeon
2Chronicles1:4 But David had brought up the ark of God from Kiriath-jearim to the place which David had prepared for it
2Chronicles1:5 Moreover
2Chronicles1:6 Solomon went up there to the bronze altar before the Lord at the Tent of Meeting and offered 1
2Chronicles1:7 That night God appeared to Solomon and said to him
2Chronicles1:8 And Solomon said to God
2Chronicles1:9 Now
2Chronicles1:10 Give me now wisdom and knowledge to go out and come in before this people
2Chronicles1:11 God replied to Solomon
2Chronicles1:12 Wisdom and knowledge are granted you. And I will give you riches
2Chronicles1:13 Then Solomon came from the high place at Gibeon
2Chronicles1:14 Solomon gathered chariots and horsemen; he had 1
2Chronicles1:15 And the king made silver and gold in Jerusalem as common as stones
2Chronicles1:16 Solomon's horses were brought out of Egypt; the king's merchants received them in droves
2Chronicles1:17 They imported from Egypt a chariot for 600 shekels of silver
2Chronicles2:1 SOLOMON DETERMINED to build a temple for the Name of the Lord and a royal capitol.
2Chronicles2:2 And Solomon counted out 70
2Chronicles2:3 And Solomon sent to Hiram king of Tyre
2Chronicles2:4 Behold
2Chronicles2:5 The house which I am to build is great
2Chronicles2:6 But who is able to build Him a house
2Chronicles2:7 Now therefore
2Chronicles2:8 Send me also from Lebanon cedar
2Chronicles2:9 To prepare for me timber in abundance
2Chronicles2:10 And I will give to your servants who cut timber 20
2Chronicles2:11 Then Hiram king of Tyre replied in writing sent to Solomon
2Chronicles2:12 Hiram said also
2Chronicles2:13 Now I have sent a skilled man
2Chronicles2:14 The son of a woman of the daughters of Dan; his father was a man of Tyre. He is a trained worker in gold
2Chronicles2:15 Now therefore
2Chronicles2:16 And we will cut whatever timber you need from Lebanon and bring it to you in rafts by sea to Joppa
2Chronicles2:17 Then Solomon took a census of all the aliens in the land of Israel
2Chronicles2:18 And he assigned 70
2Chronicles3:1 THEN SOLOMON began to build the house of the Lord at Jerusalem on Mount Moriah
2Chronicles3:2 And Solomon began to build on the second day of the second month in the fourth year of his reign.
2Chronicles3:3 Now these are the measurements for the foundations which Solomon laid for the house of God. The length in cubits by the former measure was sixty cubits
2Chronicles3:4 The porch or vestibule across the front of the house was the same length as the house's breadth
2Chronicles3:5 And the greater house (the Holy Place) he lined with cypress and overlaid it with fine gold and made palm trees and chains on it.
2Chronicles3:6 And he adorned the house with precious stones for beauty; and the gold was gold of Parvaim.
2Chronicles3:7 He lined the house (the Holy Place)
2Chronicles3:8 He made the Most Holy Place
2Chronicles3:9 The weight of the nails was fifty shekels of gold. And he lined the upper chambers with gold.
2Chronicles3:10 And in the Most Holy Place he made two cherubim of image work
2Chronicles3:11 And the wings of the cherubim [combined] extended twenty cubits: one wing of one cherub was five cubits
2Chronicles3:12 And of the other cherub one wing of five cubits touched the wall of the house
2Chronicles3:13 The wings of these cherubim extended twenty cubits; the cherubim stood on their feet
2Chronicles3:14 And he made the veil [between the Holy Place and the Most Holy Place] of blue
2Chronicles3:15 Before the house he made two pillars
2Chronicles3:16 He made chains like a necklace and put them on the heads of the pillars
2Chronicles3:17 He erected the pillars before the temple
2Chronicles4:1 ALSO SOLOMON made an altar of bronze
2Chronicles4:2 Also he made a round Sea of molten metal
2Chronicles4:3 Under it were figures of oxen encircling it
2Chronicles4:4 It stood upon twelve oxen
2Chronicles4:5 Its thickness was a handbreadth; its brim was like the brim of a cup
2Chronicles4:6 He made also ten lavers in which to wash and put five on the right (south) side and five on the left (north). Such things as they offered for the burnt offering they washed in them
2Chronicles4:7 And he made ten golden lampstands as directed and set them in the temple
2Chronicles4:8 He made also ten tables and placed them in the temple
2Chronicles4:9 Moreover
2Chronicles4:10 And he set the Sea at the southeast corner of the house.
2Chronicles4:11 And Huram made the pots
2Chronicles4:12 The two pillars; the bowls; the capitals on top of the two pillars; and the two networks to cover the two bowls of the capitals on top of the pillars;
2Chronicles4:13 And 400 pomegranates for the two networks
2Chronicles4:14 He made also bases or stands and lavers upon the bases;
2Chronicles4:15 One Sea and the twelve oxen under it;
2Chronicles4:16 The pots
2Chronicles4:17 In the plain of the Jordan the king cast them
2Chronicles4:18 Solomon made all these things in such great numbers that the weight of the bronze was not computed.
2Chronicles4:19 And Solomon made all the vessels for the house of God: the golden altar also; and the tables for the showbread (the bread of the Presence);
2Chronicles4:20 And the lampstands with their lamps of pure gold
2Chronicles4:21 The flowers
2Chronicles4:22 The snuffers
2Chronicles5:1 THUS ALL the work that Solomon did for the house of the Lord was finished. He brought in all the things that David his father had dedicated
2Chronicles5:2 Then Solomon assembled the elders of Israel and all the heads of the tribes
2Chronicles5:3 All the men of Israel gathered to the king at the feast in the seventh month.
2Chronicles5:4 And all the elders of Israel came
2Chronicles5:5 And the priests and Levites brought up the ark
2Chronicles5:6 Also King Solomon and all the assembly of Israel who were gathered to him before the ark sacrificed sheep and oxen so numerous that they could not be counted or reported.
2Chronicles5:7 And the priests brought the ark of the covenant of the Lord to its place
2Chronicles5:8 For the cherubim spread out their wings over the place of the ark
2Chronicles5:9 And they drew out the poles of the ark
2Chronicles5:10 There was nothing in the ark except the two tables [the Ten Commandments] which Moses put in it at Mount Horeb
2Chronicles5:11 And when the priests had come out of the Holy Place–for all the priests present had sanctified themselves
2Chronicles5:12 And all the Levites who were singers–all of those of Asaph
2Chronicles5:13 And when the trumpeters and singers were joined in unison
2Chronicles5:14 So that the priests could not stand to minister because of the cloud
2Chronicles6:1 THEN SOLOMON said
2Chronicles6:2 I have built You a house
2Chronicles6:3 And the king turned his face and blessed all the assembly of Israel
2Chronicles6:4 And he said
2Chronicles6:5 Since the day that I brought My people out of the land of Egypt
2Chronicles6:6 But I have chosen Jerusalem
2Chronicles6:7 Now it was in the heart of David my father to build a house for the Name and renown of the Lord
2Chronicles6:8 But the Lord said to David my father
2Chronicles6:9 Yet you shall not build the house
2Chronicles6:10 The Lord therefore has performed His word that He has spoken
2Chronicles6:11 In it have I put the ark [the symbol of His presence]
2Chronicles6:12 And Solomon stood before the altar of the Lord in the presence of all the assembly of Israel and spread forth his hands.
2Chronicles6:13 For he had made a bronze scaffold
2Chronicles6:14 And said
2Chronicles6:15 You Who have kept Your promises to my father David and fulfilled with Your hand what You spoke with Your mouth
2Chronicles6:16 Now therefore
2Chronicles6:17 Now then
2Chronicles6:18 But will God actually dwell with men on the earth? Behold
2Chronicles6:19 Yet have respect for the prayer of Your servant and for his supplication
2Chronicles6:20 That Your eyes may be open upon this house day and night
2Chronicles6:21 So listen to and heed the requests of Your servant and Your people Israel which they shall make facing this place. Hear from Your dwelling place
2Chronicles6:22 If a man sins against his neighbor
2Chronicles6:23 Then hear from heaven and do; and judge Your servants
2Chronicles6:24 If Your people Israel have been defeated before the enemy because they have sinned against You
2Chronicles6:25 Then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your people Israel and bring them again to the land which You gave to them and their fathers.
2Chronicles6:26 When the heavens are shut up and there is no rain because Your people have sinned against You
2Chronicles6:27 Then hear from heaven and forgive the sin of Your servants
2Chronicles6:28 If there is famine in the land
2Chronicles6:29 Then whatever prayer or supplication any man or all of Your people Israel shall make–each knowing his own affliction and his own sorrow and stretching out his hands toward this house–
2Chronicles6:30 Then hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:31 That they may fear You and walk in Your ways as long as they live in the land which You gave to our fathers.
2Chronicles6:32 Also concerning the stranger who is not of Your people Israel but has come from a far country for Your great name's sake and Your mighty power and Your outstretched arm–if he comes and prays toward this house
2Chronicles6:33 Hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:34 If Your people go out to war against their enemies by the way that You send them
2Chronicles6:35 Then hear from heaven their prayer and supplication
2Chronicles6:36 If they sin against You–for there is no man who does not sin–and You are angry with them and give them to enemies who take them captive to a land far or near;
2Chronicles6:37 Yet if they repent in the land to which they have been carried captive
2Chronicles6:38 If they return to You with all their heart and soul in the land of their captivity
2Chronicles6:39 Then hear from heaven
2Chronicles6:40 Now
2Chronicles6:41 So now arise
2Chronicles6:42 O Lord God
2Chronicles7:1 WHEN SOLOMON had finished praying
2Chronicles7:2 The priests could not enter the house of the Lord
2Chronicles7:3 And when all the people of Israel saw how the fire came down and the glory of the Lord upon the house
2Chronicles7:4 Then the king and all the people offered sacrifices before the Lord.
2Chronicles7:5 King Solomon offered a sacrifice of 22
2Chronicles7:6 The priests stood at their posts
2Chronicles7:7 Moreover
2Chronicles7:8 At that time Solomon held the feast for seven days
2Chronicles7:9 The eighth day they made a solemn assembly
2Chronicles7:10 And on the twenty-third day of the seventh month he sent the people away to their homes
2Chronicles7:11 Thus Solomon finished the Lord's house and the king's house; all that [he] had planned to do in the Lord's house and his own house he accomplished successfully.
2Chronicles7:12 And the Lord appeared to Solomon by night and said to him: I have heard your prayer and have chosen this place for Myself as a house of sacrifice.
2Chronicles7:13 If I shut up heaven so no rain falls
2Chronicles7:14 If My people
2Chronicles7:15 Now My eyes will be open and My ears attentive to prayer offered in this place.
2Chronicles7:16 For I have chosen and sanctified (set apart for holy use) this house
2Chronicles7:17 As for you [Solomon]
2Chronicles7:18 Then I will establish the throne of your kingdom
2Chronicles7:19 But if you [people] turn away and forsake My statutes and My commandments which I have set before you and go and serve other gods and worship them
2Chronicles7:20 Then will I pluck [Israel] up by the roots out of My land which I have given [them]; and this house which I have hallowed for My Name will I cast out of My sight
2Chronicles7:21 And this house
2Chronicles7:22 Then men will say
2Chronicles8:1 AT THE end of twenty years
2Chronicles8:2 The cities which Huram had given to [him] Solomon rebuilt and fortified
2Chronicles8:3 And Solomon took Hamath-zobah.
2Chronicles8:4 He built Tadmor in the wilderness and all his store cities in Hamath.
2Chronicles8:5 Also he built Upper Beth-horon and Lower Beth-horon
2Chronicles8:6 And Baalath and all the store cities [he] had
2Chronicles8:7 All the people who were left of the Hittites
2Chronicles8:8 But descendants of those who were left in the land
2Chronicles8:9 But of the Israelites Solomon made no slaves for his work; but they were men of war
2Chronicles8:10 These were the chiefs of King Solomon's officers
2Chronicles8:11 Solomon brought the daughter of Pharaoh out of the City of David into the house he had built for her
2Chronicles8:12 Then Solomon offered burnt offerings to the Lord on the Lord's altar which he had built before the [temple] porch or vestibule
2Chronicles8:13 A certain number every day
2Chronicles8:14 And he appointed
2Chronicles8:15 And they did not turn from the command of the king to the priests and Levites in any respect or concerning the treasuries.
2Chronicles8:16 Thus all the work of Solomon was prepared from the day the foundation of the Lord's house was laid until it was finished. So the house of the Lord was completed.
2Chronicles8:17 Then Solomon went to Ezion-geber and to Eloth on the shore of the [Red] Sea in the land of Edom.
2Chronicles8:18 And Huram sent him by his servants ships and servants familiar with the sea; and they went with the servants of Solomon to Ophir and took from there 450 talents of gold and brought them to King Solomon.
2Chronicles9:1 WHEN THE queen of Sheba heard of the fame of Solomon
2Chronicles9:2 And Solomon answered all her questions; there was nothing hidden from [him] which he was unable to make clear to her.
2Chronicles9:3 And when the queen of Sheba had seen Solomon's wisdom
2Chronicles9:4 The food of his table
2Chronicles9:5 She said to the king
2Chronicles9:6 But I did not believe their words until I came and my eyes had seen it. Behold
2Chronicles9:7 Happy are your wives and men
2Chronicles9:8 Blessed be the Lord your God
2Chronicles9:9 She gave the king 120 talents of gold
2Chronicles9:10 The servants of Huram and [those] of Solomon
2Chronicles9:11 The king made of the algum trees terraces or walks to the house of the Lord and to the king's palace
2Chronicles9:12 And King Solomon gave to the queen of Sheba all her desire
2Chronicles9:13 Now the weight of gold that came to Solomon in one year was 666 talents
2Chronicles9:14 Besides what traders and merchants brought; and all the kings of Arabia and governors of the country brought gold and silver to Solomon.
2Chronicles9:15 And King Solomon made 200 large shields or bucklers of beaten gold; 600 shekels of beaten gold went into each shield.
2Chronicles9:16 And he made 300 shields of beaten gold
2Chronicles9:17 Moreover
2Chronicles9:18 There were six steps to the throne and a gold footstool attached to the throne
2Chronicles9:19 And twelve lions stood there one on either end of each of the six steps. The like of it was never made in any kingdom before.
2Chronicles9:20 King Solomon's drinking vessels were all of gold
2Chronicles9:21 For the king's ships went to Tarshish with Huram's servants; once every three years the ships of Tarshish came bringing gold
2Chronicles9:22 King Solomon surpassed all the kings of the earth in riches and wisdom.
2Chronicles9:23 And all the kings of the earth sought the presence of Solomon to hear his wisdom which God had put into his mind.
2Chronicles9:24 And every man brought his tribute: silver and gold articles
2Chronicles9:25 Solomon had 4
2Chronicles9:26 And he ruled over all the kings from the [Euphrates] River to the land of Philistia and to the frontier of Egypt.
2Chronicles9:27 The king made silver in Jerusalem as common as stones
2Chronicles9:28 And they imported horses for Solomon from Egypt and from all lands.
2Chronicles9:29 Now the rest of the acts of Solomon
2Chronicles9:30 Solomon reigned in Jerusalem over all Israel forty years.
2Chronicles9:31 Then Solomon slept with his fathers; he was buried in the city of David his father. Rehoboam his son reigned in his stead.
2Chronicles10:1 REHOBOAM WENT to Shechem
2Chronicles10:2 Jeroboam the son of Nebat was in Egypt
2Chronicles10:3 And the people sent for him. So Jeroboam and all Israel came to Rehoboam
2Chronicles10:4 Your father [King Solomon] made our yoke grievous. So now make lighter the grievous service of your father and his heavy yoke that he put upon us
2Chronicles10:5 Rehoboam replied
2Chronicles10:6 King Rehoboam took counsel with the old men who stood before Solomon his father while he was alive
2Chronicles10:7 And they answered him
2Chronicles10:8 But the king forsook the counsel which the old men gave him and took counsel with the young men who were brought up with him and stood before him.
2Chronicles10:9 And he said to them
2Chronicles10:10 The young men who were brought up with him said to him
2Chronicles10:11 For whereas my father put a heavy yoke upon you
2Chronicles10:12 The third day Jeroboam and all the people returned to Rehoboam as he had said.
2Chronicles10:13 And the king answered them harshly
2Chronicles10:14 And answered them after the advice of the young men
2Chronicles10:15 So the king did not heed the people
2Chronicles10:16 And when all Israel saw that the king would not listen to and heed them
2Chronicles10:17 But as for the Israelites who dwelt in Judah's cities
2Chronicles10:18 Then King Rehoboam sent Hadoram
2Chronicles10:19 And Israel has rebelled against the house of David to this day.
2Chronicles11:1 AND WHEN Rehoboam came to Jerusalem
2Chronicles11:2 But the word of the Lord came to Shemaiah the man of God
2Chronicles11:3 Say to Rehoboam son of Solomon king of Judah and to all Israel in Judah and Benjamin
2Chronicles11:4 Thus says the Lord: You shall not go up or fight against your brethren. Return every man to his house
2Chronicles11:5 Rehoboam dwelt in Jerusalem and built cities for defense in Judah.
2Chronicles11:6 He built Bethlehem
2Chronicles11:7 Beth-zur
2Chronicles11:8 Gath
2Chronicles11:9 Adoraim
2Chronicles11:10 Zorah
2Chronicles11:11 He fortified the strongholds and put captains in them
2Chronicles11:12 And in each city he put shields and spears
2Chronicles11:13 And the priests and the Levites who were in all Israel came over to Rehoboam from wherever they lived.
2Chronicles11:14 For the Levites left their suburbs and their possessions and came to Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles11:15 And he appointed his own priests for the high places and for the [idols of demon] he-goats
2Chronicles11:16 And after them out of all the tribes of Israel there came to Jerusalem those who set their hearts to seek and inquire of the Lord
2Chronicles11:17 So they strengthened the kingdom of Judah and upheld Rehoboam son of Solomon for three years; for they walked in the ways of David and Solomon for three years.

2Chronicles28:7 And Zichri
2Chronicles28:8 And the Israelites carried away captive 200
2Chronicles28:9 But a prophet of the Lord was there whose name was Oded
2Chronicles28:10 And now you intend to suppress the people of Judah and Jerusalem
2Chronicles28:11 Now hear me therefore
2Chronicles28:12 Then certain of the heads of the Ephraimites [Israel]–Azariah son of Johanan
2Chronicles28:13 And said
2Chronicles28:14 So the armed men [of Israel] left the captives and the spoil [of Judah] before the princes and all the assembly.
2Chronicles28:15 And the men who have been mentioned by name rose up and took the captives
2Chronicles28:16 At that time King Ahaz sent to the king of Assyria to help him.
2Chronicles28:17 For again the Edomites had come and smitten Judah and carried away captives.
2Chronicles28:18 The Philistines had invaded the cities of the low country and of the South (the Negeb) of Judah
2Chronicles28:19 For the Lord brought Judah low because of Ahaz king of Israel
2Chronicles28:20 So Tilgath-pilneser king of Assyria came to him and distressed him without strengthening him.
2Chronicles28:21 For Ahaz took [treasure] from the house of the Lord and out of the house of the king and from the princes and gave it as tribute to the king of Assyria
2Chronicles28:22 In the time of his distress he became still more unfaithful to the Lord–this same King Ahaz.
2Chronicles28:23 For he sacrificed to the gods of Damascus
2Chronicles28:24 And Ahaz collected the utensils of the house of God and cut them in pieces; and he shut up the doors of the Lord's temple [the Holy Place and the Holy of Holies] and made himself altars in every corner of Jerusalem.

2Chronicles28:26 Now the rest of his acts and of all his ways

2Chronicles30:2 For the king and his princes and all the assembly in Jerusalem took counsel to keep the Passover in the second month. [Num. 9:10
2Chronicles30:3 For they could not keep it at the set time because not enough priests had sanctified themselves
2Chronicles30:4 The new time pleased the king and all the assembly.
2Chronicles30:5 So they decreed to make a proclamation throughout all Israel
2Chronicles30:6 So the posts went with the letters from the king and his princes throughout all Israel and Judah

2Chronicles30:14 They rose up and took away the altars [to idols] that were in Jerusalem
2Chronicles30:15 Then they killed the Passover lamb on the fourteenth day of the second month. And the priests and the Levites were ashamed and sanctified themselves and brought burnt offerings to the Lord's house.
2Chronicles30:16 They stood in their accustomed places
2Chronicles30:17 For many were in the assembly who had not sanctified themselves [become clean and free from all sin]. So the Levites had to kill the Passover lambs for all who were not clean
2Chronicles30:18 For a multitude of the people

2Chronicles30:20 And the Lord hearkened to Hezekiah and healed the people.
2Chronicles30:21 And the Israelites who were in Jerusalem kept the Feast of Unleavened Bread for seven days with great joy. The Levites and priests praised the Lord day by day
2Chronicles30:22 Hezekiah spoke encouragingly to all the Levites who had good understanding in the Lord's work. So the people ate the seven-day appointed feast
2Chronicles30:23 And the whole assembly took counsel to prolong the feast another seven days; and they kept it another seven days with joy.

2Chronicles30:25 All the assembly of Judah
2Chronicles30:26 So there was great joy in Jerusalem

2Chronicles32:1 AFTER THESE things and this loyalty
2Chronicles32:2 When Hezekiah saw that Sennacherib had come and intended to fight against Jerusalem
2Chronicles32:3 He decided with his officers and his mighty men to stop up the waters of the fountains which were outside the city [by enclosing them with masonry and concealing them]
2Chronicles32:4 So many people gathered
2Chronicles32:5 Also Hezekiah took courage and built up all the wall that was broken
2Chronicles32:6 And he set captains of war over the people and gathered them together to him in the street of the gate of the city and spoke encouragingly to them
2Chronicles32:7 Be strong and courageous. Be not afraid or dismayed before the king of Assyria and all the horde that is with him
2Chronicles32:8 With him is an arm of flesh
2Chronicles32:9 And this Sennacherib king of Assyria
2Chronicles32:10 Thus says Sennacherib king of Assyria: On what do you trust
2Chronicles32:11 Is not Hezekiah leading you on in order to let you die by famine and thirst
2Chronicles32:12 Has not the same Hezekiah taken away his high places and his altars
2Chronicles32:13 Do you not know what I and my fathers have done to all the peoples of other lands? Were the gods of the nations of those lands in any way able to deliver their lands out of my hand?
2Chronicles32:14 Who among all the gods of those nations that my fathers utterly destroyed was able to deliver his people out of my hand
2Chronicles32:15 So now
2Chronicles32:16 And his servants said still more against the Lord God and against His servant Hezekiah.
2Chronicles32:17 The Assyrian king also wrote letters insulting the Lord
2Chronicles32:18 And they shouted it loudly in the Jewish language to the people of Jerusalem who were on the wall
2Chronicles32:19 And they spoke of the God of Jerusalem as they spoke of the gods of the peoples of the earth
2Chronicles32:20 For this cause Hezekiah the king and the prophet Isaiah son of Amoz prayed and cried to heaven.
2Chronicles32:21 And the Lord sent an angel
2Chronicles32:22 Thus the Lord saved Hezekiah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem from the hand of Sennacherib the king of Assyria and from the hand of all his enemies
2Chronicles32:23 And many brought gifts to Jerusalem to the Lord and presents to Hezekiah king of Judah; so from then on he was magnified in the sight of all nations.
2Chronicles32:24 In those days Hezekiah was sick to the point of death; and he prayed to the Lord and He answered him and gave him a sign.
2Chronicles32:25 But Hezekiah did not make return [to the Lord] according to the benefit done to him
2Chronicles32:26 But Hezekiah humbled himself for the pride of his heart
2Chronicles32:27 And Hezekiah had very great wealth and honor
2Chronicles32:28 Storehouses also for the increase of grain
2Chronicles32:29 Moreover
2Chronicles32:30 This same Hezekiah also closed the upper springs of Gihon and directed the waters down to the west side of the City of David. And Hezekiah prospered in all his works.
2Chronicles32:31 And so in the matter of the ambassadors of the princes of Babylon who were sent to him to inquire about the wonder that was done in the land
2Chronicles32:32 Now the rest of the acts of Hezekiah and his good deeds
2Chronicles32:33 And Hezekiah slept with his fathers and was buried in the ascent of the tombs of the descendants of David; and all Judah and the inhabitants of Jerusalem did him honor at his death. Manasseh his son reigned in his stead.

2Chronicles34:1 JOSIAH WAS eight years old when he began his thirty-one-year reign in Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:2 He did right in the sight of the Lord and walked in the ways of David his father [forefather] and turned aside neither to the right hand nor to the left.
2Chronicles34:3 For in the eighth year of his reign
2Chronicles34:4 They broke down the altars of the Baals in his presence; the sun-images that were high above them he hewed down; the Asherim and the graven images and the molten images he broke in pieces and made dust of them and strewed it upon the graves of those who sacrificed to them.
2Chronicles34:5 Josiah burned the bones of the [idolatrous] priests upon their altars
2Chronicles34:6 So he did in the cities of Manasseh
2Chronicles34:7 He broke down the altars and the Asherim and beat the graven images into powder and hewed down all the sun-images throughout all the land of Israel. Then he returned to Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:8 In the eighteenth year of Josiah's reign
2Chronicles34:9 When they came to Hilkiah the high priest
2Chronicles34:10 They delivered it to the workmen who had oversight of the Lord's house
2Chronicles34:11 To the carpenters and builders to buy hewn stone
2Chronicles34:12 The men did the work faithfully. Their overseers were Jahath and Obadiah
2Chronicles34:13 Also had oversight of the burden bearers and all who did work in any kind of service; and some of the Levites were scribes

2Chronicles34:18 Then Shaphan the scribe said to the king
2Chronicles34:19 When King Josiah had heard the words of the Law

2Chronicles34:21 Go

2Chronicles34:24 Thus says the Lord: Behold
2Chronicles34:25 Because they have forsaken Me and have burned incense to other gods
2Chronicles34:26 But say to King Josiah of Judah
2Chronicles34:27 Because your heart was tender and penitent and you humbled yourself before God when you heard His words against this place and its inhabitants
2Chronicles34:28 Behold
2Chronicles34:29 Then King Josiah sent and gathered all the elders of Judah and Jerusalem.
2Chronicles34:30 And [he] went up into the house of the Lord
2Chronicles34:31 Then the king stood in his place and made a covenant before the Lord–to walk after the Lord and to keep His commandments
2Chronicles34:32 And he caused all who were present in Jerusalem and Benjamin to stand in confirmation of it. And the inhabitants of Jerusalem did according to the covenant of God

Ezra4:2 They came to Zerubbabel [now governor] and to the heads of the fathers' houses and said
Ezra4:3 But Zerubbabel and Jeshua and the rest of the heads of fathers' houses of Israel said to them
Ezra4:4 Then [the Samaritans] the people of the land [continually] weakened the hands of the people of Judah and troubled and terrified them in building
Ezra4:5 And hired counselors against them to frustrate their purpose and plans all the days of Cyrus king of Persia
Ezra4:6 And in the reign of Ahasuerus [or Xerxes]
Ezra4:7 Later
Ezra4:8 Rehum the [Persian] commander [of the Samaritans] and Shimshai the scribe wrote a letter against Jerusalem to Artaxerxes the king of this sort–
Ezra4:9 Then wrote Rehum the [Persian] commander
Ezra4:10 And the rest of the nations whom the great and noble Osnappar deported and settled in the city of Samaria and the rest of the country beyond [west of] the Euphrates River
Ezra4:11 This is a copy of the letter which they sent to King Artaxerxes: Your servants
Ezra4:12 Be it known to the king that the Jews who came up from you to us have come to Jerusalem. This rebellious and bad city they are rebuilding
Ezra4:13 Be it known now to the king that if this city is rebuilt and the walls finished
Ezra4:14 Now because we eat the salt of the king's palace and it is not proper for us to witness the king's discredit
Ezra4:15 In order that a search may be made in the book of the records of your fathers
Ezra4:16 We declare to the king that if this city is rebuilt and its walls finished
Ezra4:17 Then the king sent an answer: To Rehum the [Persian] official
Ezra4:18 The letter which you sent to us has been plainly read before me.
Ezra4:19 I commanded and search has been made
Ezra4:20 There have been mighty kings also over Jerusalem who have ruled over all countries beyond [west of] the [Euphrates] River
Ezra4:21 Therefore give a decree to make these men stop
Ezra4:22 Be sure that you do this. Why should damage grow
Ezra4:23 When the copy of King Artaxerxes' letter was read before Rehum
Ezra4:24 Then the work on the house of God in Jerusalem stopped. It stopped until the second year of Darius [I] king of Persia.
Ezra5:1 NOW THE prophets

Ezra6:1 THEN KING Darius [I] decreed
Ezra6:2 And at Ecbatana in the capital in the province of Media
Ezra6:3 In the first year of King Cyrus
Ezra6:4 With three courses of great stones and one course of new timber. Let the cost be paid from the royal treasury.
Ezra6:5 Also let the gold and silver vessels of the house of God
Ezra6:6 Now therefore
Ezra6:7 Leave the work on this house of God alone; let the governor and the elders of the Jews build this house of God on its site.
Ezra6:8 Moreover
Ezra6:9 And all they need
Ezra6:10 That they may offer pleasing sacrifices to the God of heaven and pray for the life of the king and his sons.
Ezra6:11 Also I make a decree that whoever shall change or infringe on this order
Ezra6:12 May the God Who has caused His Name to dwell there overthrow all kings and peoples who put forth their hands to alter this or to destroy this house of God in Jerusalem. I Darius make a decree; let it be executed speedily and exactly.
Ezra6:13 Then Tattenai
Ezra6:14 And the elders of the Jews built and prospered through the prophesying of Haggai the prophet and Zechariah son of Iddo. They finished their building as commanded by the God of Israel and by decree of Cyrus and Darius and Artaxerxes king of Persia.
Ezra6:15 And this house was finished on the third day of the month of Adar
Ezra6:16 And the Israelites–the priests
Ezra6:17 They offered at the dedication of this house of God 100 young bulls
Ezra6:18 And they set the priests in their divisions and the Levites in their courses for the service of God at Jerusalem
Ezra6:19 The returned exiles kept the Passover on the fourteenth day of the first month.
Ezra6:20 For the priests and the Levites had purified themselves together; all of them were clean. So they killed the Passover lamb for all the returned exiles
Ezra6:21 It was eaten by the Israelites who had returned from exile and by all who had joined them and separated themselves from the pollutions of the peoples of the land to seek the Lord
Ezra6:22 They kept the Feast of Unleavened Bread for seven days with joy

Ezra7:18 And whatever shall seem good to you and to your brethren to do with the rest of the silver and the gold

Ezra7:25 You
Ezra7:26 And whoever will not do the law of your God and the law of the king
Ezra7:27 Blessed be the Lord
Ezra7:28 And Who has extended His mercy and steadfast love to me before the king

Ezra8:30 So the priests and the Levites received the weight of the silver

Ezra9:1 AFTERWARD
Ezra9:2 For they have taken as wives some of their daughters for themselves and for their sons
Ezra9:3 When I heard this
Ezra9:4 Then all those who trembled at the words of the God of Israel because of the offensive violation of His will by the returned exiles gathered around me as I sat astounded until the evening sacrifice.
Ezra9:5 At the evening sacrifice I arose from my depression
Ezra9:6 Saying
