Ezra9:8 And now
Ezra9:9 For we are bondmen; yet our God has not forsaken us in our bondage
Ezra9:10 Now
Ezra9:11 Which You have commanded by Your servants the prophets
Ezra9:12 Therefore
Ezra9:13 And after all that has come upon us for our evil deeds and for our great guilt
Ezra9:14 Shall we break Your commandments again and intermarry with the peoples who practice these abominations? Would You not be angry with us till You had consumed us

Ezra10:12 Then all the assembly answered with a loud voice

Ezra10:15 Only Jonathan son of Asahel and Jahzeiah son of Tikvah opposed this
Ezra10:16 Then the returned exiles did so. Ezra the priest and certain heads of fathers' houses were selected
Ezra10:17 And by the first day of the first month they had come to the end of the cases of the men married to foreign wives.

Nehemiah1:5 And I said

Nehemiah1:10 Now these are Your servants and Your people

Nehemiah3:2 And next to him [Eliashib] the men of Jericho built. Next to [them] Zaccur son of Imri built.

Nehemiah3:16 After him Nehemiah [III] son of Azbuk
Nehemiah3:17 After him the Levites: Rehum son of Bani. Next to him repaired Hashabiah
Nehemiah3:18 After him repaired their brethren under Bavvai son of Henadad
Nehemiah3:19 Next to him repaired Ezer son of Jeshua
Nehemiah3:20 After him Baruch son of Zabbai (Zaccai) earnestly repaired another portion [toward the hill] from the angular turning of the wall to the door of the house of Eliashib the high priest.
Nehemiah3:21 After him Meremoth son of Uriah
Nehemiah3:22 After him the priests
Nehemiah3:23 After them Benjamin and Hasshub repaired opposite their house. After them repaired Azariah son of Maaseiah
Nehemiah3:24 After him Binnui son of Henadad repaired another section [of the wall]
Nehemiah3:25 Palal son of Uzai repaired opposite the angular turn of the wall and the tower which stands out from the upper house of the king by the court of the guard. After him Pedaiah son of Parosh
Nehemiah3:26 And the servants of the priests dwelling on Ophel [the hill south of the temple] repaired to opposite the Water Gate on the east and the projecting tower.
Nehemiah3:27 After them the Tekoites repaired another portion opposite the great projecting tower to the wall of Ophel.
Nehemiah3:28 Above the Horse Gate the priests repaired
Nehemiah3:29 After them repaired Zadok son of Immer opposite his house. Then Shemaiah son of Shecaniah
Nehemiah3:30 After him Hananiah son of Shelemiah
Nehemiah3:31 After him Malchijah
Nehemiah3:32 And from the ascent and upper room of the corner to the Sheep Gate the goldsmiths and merchants repaired.

Nehemiah4:11 And our enemies said
Nehemiah4:12 And when the Jews who lived near them came
Nehemiah4:13 So I set [armed men] behind the wall in places where it was least protected; I even thus used the people as families with their swords

Nehemiah4:15 And when our enemies heard that their plot was known to us and that God had frustrated their purpose
Nehemiah4:16 And from that time forth

Nehemiah4:19 And I said to the nobles and officials and the rest of the people
Nehemiah4:20 In whatever place you hear the sound of the trumpet
Nehemiah4:21 So we labored at the work while half of them held the spears from dawn until the stars came out.

Nehemiah4:23 So none of us–I

Nehemiah9:1 NOW ON the twenty-fourth day of this month
Nehemiah9:2 And the Israelites separated themselves from all foreigners and stood and confessed their sins and the iniquities of their fathers.
Nehemiah9:3 And they stood in their place and read from the Book of the Law of the Lord their God for a fourth of the day
Nehemiah9:4 On the stairs of the Levites stood Jeshua
Nehemiah9:5 Then the Levites–Jeshua
Nehemiah9:6 [And Ezra said]
Nehemiah9:7 You are the Lord
Nehemiah9:8 You found his heart faithful before You
Nehemiah9:9 You saw our fathers' affliction in Egypt
Nehemiah9:10 You performed signs and wonders against Pharaoh and all his servants and all the people of his land
Nehemiah9:11 You divided the sea before them
Nehemiah9:12 Moreover
Nehemiah9:13 You came down also upon Mount Sinai and spoke with them from Heaven and gave them right ordinances and true laws
Nehemiah9:14 And You made known to them Your holy Sabbath and gave them commandments
Nehemiah9:15 You gave them bread from heaven for their hunger and brought water for them out of the rock for their thirst; and You told them to go in and possess the land You had sworn to give them. [John 6:31-34.]
Nehemiah9:16 But they and our fathers acted presumptuously and stiffened their necks
Nehemiah9:17 They refused to obey
Nehemiah9:18 Even when they had made for themselves a molten calf and said
Nehemiah9:19 You in Your great mercy forsook them not in the wilderness; the pillar of the cloud departed not from them by day to lead them in the way
Nehemiah9:20 You also gave Your good Spirit to instruct them
Nehemiah9:21 Forty years You sustained them in the wilderness; they lacked nothing
Nehemiah9:22 Also You gave them kingdoms and peoples and allotted to them every corner. So they possessed the land of Sihon king of Heshbon and the land of Og king of Bashan.
Nehemiah9:23 Their children You also multiplied as the stars of heaven and brought them into the land which You told their fathers they should go in and possess.
Nehemiah9:24 So the descendants went in and possessed the land; and You subdued before them the inhabitants of the land
Nehemiah9:25 And they captured fortified cities and a rich land and took possession of houses full of all good things
Nehemiah9:26 Yet they were disobedient and rebelled against You and cast Your law behind their back and killed Your prophets who accused and warned them to turn to You again; and they committed great and contemptible blasphemies.
Nehemiah9:27 Therefore You delivered them into the hand of their enemies
Nehemiah9:28 But after they had rest
Nehemiah9:29 And reproved and warned them
Nehemiah9:30 Yet You bore with them many years more and reproved and warned them by Your Spirit through Your prophets; still they would not listen. Therefore You gave them into the power of the peoples of the lands.
Nehemiah9:31 Yet in Your great mercies You did not utterly consume them or forsake them
Nehemiah9:32 Now therefore
Nehemiah9:33 However
Nehemiah9:34 Our kings
Nehemiah9:35 They did not serve You in their kingdom
Nehemiah9:36 Behold
Nehemiah9:37 And its rich yield goes to the kings whom You have set over us because of our sins; they have power also over our bodies and over our livestock at their pleasure. And we are in great distress.
Nehemiah9:38 Because of all this

Esther1:1 IT WAS in the days of Ahasuerus [Xerxes]
Esther1:2 In those days when King Ahasuerus sat on his royal throne which was in Shushan or Susa [the capital of the Persian Empire] in the palace or castle
Esther1:3 In the third year of his reign he made a feast for all his princes and his courtiers. The chief officers of the Persian and Median army and the nobles and governors of the provinces were there before him
Esther1:4 While he showed the riches of his glorious kingdom and the splendor and excellence of his majesty for many days
Esther1:5 And when these days were completed
Esther1:6 There were hangings of fine white cloth
Esther1:7 Drinks were served in different kinds of golden goblets
Esther1:8 And drinking was according to the law; no one was compelled to drink
Esther1:9 Also Queen Vashti gave a banquet for the women in the royal house which belonged to King Ahasuerus.
Esther1:10 On the seventh day
Esther1:11 To bring Queen Vashti before the king
Esther1:12 But Queen Vashti refused to come at the king's command conveyed by the eunuchs. Therefore the king was enraged
Esther1:13 Then the king spoke to the wise men who knew the times–for this was the king's procedure toward all who were familiar with law and judgment–
Esther1:14 Those next to him being Carshena
Esther1:15 [He said] According to the law
Esther1:16 And Memucan answered before the king and the princes
Esther1:17 For this deed of the queen will become known to all women
Esther1:18 This very day the ladies of Persia and Media who have heard of the queen's behavior will be telling it to all the king's princes. So contempt and wrath in plenty will arise.
Esther1:19 If it pleases the king
Esther1:20 So when the king's decree is made and proclaimed throughout all his kingdom
Esther1:21 This advice pleased the king and the princes
Esther1:22 He sent letters to all the royal provinces
Esther2:1 AFTER THESE things
Esther2:2 Then the king's servants who ministered to him said
Esther2:3 And let the king appoint officers in all the provinces of his kingdom to gather all the beautiful young virgins to the capital in Shushan
Esther2:4 And let the maiden who pleases the king be queen instead of Vashti. This pleased the king
Esther2:5 There was a certain Jew in the capital in Shushan whose name was Mordecai son of Jair
Esther2:6 Who had been carried away from Jerusalem with the captives taken away with Jeconiah king of Judah
Esther2:7 He had brought up Hadassah
Esther2:8 So when the king's command and his decree were proclaimed and when many maidens were gathered in Shushan the capital under the custody of Hegai
Esther2:9 And the maiden pleased [Hegai] and obtained his favor. And he speedily gave her the things for her purification and her portion of food and the seven chosen maids to be given her from the king's palace; and he removed her and her maids to the best [apartment] in the harem.
Esther2:10 Esther had not made known her nationality or her kindred
Esther2:11 And Mordecai [who was an attendant in the king's court] walked every day before the court of the harem to learn how Esther was and what would become of her.
Esther2:12 Now when the turn of each maiden came to go in to King Ahasuerus
Esther2:13 Then in this way the maiden came to the king: whatever she desired was given her to take with her from the harem into the king's palace.
Esther2:14 In the evening she went and next day she returned into the second harem in the custody of Shaashgaz
Esther2:15 Now when the turn for Esther the daughter of Abihail
Esther2:16 So Esther was taken to King Ahasuerus into his royal palace in the tenth month
Esther2:17 And the king loved Esther more than all the women

Esther2:20 Now Esther had not yet revealed her nationality or her people

Job1:1 THERE WAS a man in the land of Uz whose name was Job; and that man was blameless and upright
Job1:2 And there were born to him seven sons and three daughters.
Job1:3 He possessed 7
Job1:4 His sons used to go and feast in the house of each on his day (birthday) in turn
Job1:5 And when the days of their feasting were over
Job1:6 Now there was a day when the sons (the angels) of God came to present themselves before the Lord
Job1:7 And the Lord said to Satan
Job1:8 And the Lord said to Satan
Job1:9 Then Satan answered the Lord
Job1:10 Have You not put a hedge about him and his house and all that he has
Job1:11 But put forth Your hand now and touch all that he has
Job1:12 And the Lord said to Satan (the adversary and the accuser)
Job1:13 And there was a day when [Job's] sons and his daughters were eating and drinking wine in their eldest brother's house [on his birthday]
Job1:14 And there came a messenger to Job and said
Job1:15 And the Sabeans swooped down upon them and took away [the animals]. Indeed
Job1:16 While he was yet speaking
Job1:17 While he was yet speaking
Job1:18 While he was yet speaking
Job1:19 And behold
Job1:20 Then Job arose and rent his robe and shaved his head and fell down upon the ground and worshiped
Job1:21 And said
Job1:22 In all this Job sinned not nor charged God foolishly.

Job2:3 And the Lord said to Satan
Job2:4 Then Satan answered the Lord
Job2:5 But put forth Your hand now
Job2:6 And the Lord said to Satan
Job2:7 So Satan went forth from the presence of the Lord and smote Job with loathsome and painful sores from the sole of his foot to the crown of his head.
Job2:8 And he took a piece of broken pottery with which to scrape himself
Job2:9 Then his wife said to him
Job2:10 But he said to her
Job2:11 Now when Job's three friends heard of all this evil that was come upon him
Job2:12 And when they looked from afar off and saw him [disfigured] beyond recognition
Job2:13 So they sat down with [Job] on the ground for seven days and seven nights
Job3:1 AFTER THIS
Job3:2 And Job said
Job3:3 Let the day perish wherein I was born
Job3:4 Let that day be darkness! May not God above regard it
Job3:5 Let gloom and deep darkness claim it for their own; let a cloud dwell upon it; let all that blackens the day terrify it (the day that I was born).
Job3:6 As for that night
Job3:7 Yes
Job3:8 Let those curse it who curse the day
Job3:9 Let the stars of the early dawn of that day be dark; let [the morning] look in vain for the light
Job3:10 Because it shut not the doors of my mother's womb nor hid sorrow and trouble from my eyes.
Job3:11 Why was I not stillborn? Why did I not give up the ghost when my mother bore me?
Job3:12 Why did the knees receive me? Or why the breasts
Job3:13 For then would I have lain down and been quiet; I would have slept; then would I have been at rest [in death]
Job3:14 With kings and counselors of the earth
Job3:15 Or with princes who had gold
Job3:16 Or [why] was I not a miscarriage
Job3:17 There [in death] the wicked cease from troubling
Job3:18 There the [captive] prisoners rest together; they hear not the taskmaster's voice.
Job3:19 The small and the great are there
Job3:20 Why is light [of life] given to him who is in misery
Job3:21 Who long and wait for death
Job3:22 Who rejoice exceedingly and are elated when they find the grave?
Job3:23 [Why is the light of day given] to a man whose way is hidden
Job3:24 For my sighing comes before my food
Job3:25 For the thing which I greatly fear comes upon me
Job3:26 I was not or am not at ease
Job4:1 THEN ELIPHAZ the Temanite answered and said
Job4:2 If we venture to converse with you
Job4:3 Behold
Job4:4 Your words have held firm him who was falling
Job4:5 But now it is come upon you
Job4:6 Is not your [reverent] fear of God your confidence and the integrity and uprightness of your ways your hope?
Job4:7 Think [earnestly]
Job4:8 As I myself have seen
Job4:9 By the breath of God they perish
Job4:10 The roaring of the lion and the voice of the fierce lion
Job4:11 The old and strong lion perishes for lack of prey
Job4:12 Now a thing was secretly brought to me
Job4:13 In thoughts from the visions of the night
Job4:14 Fear came upon me and trembling
Job4:15 Then a spirit passed before my face; the hair of my flesh stood up!
Job4:16 [The spirit] stood still
Job4:17 Can mortal man be just before God
Job4:18 Even in His [heavenly] servants He puts no trust or confidence
Job4:19 How much more those who dwell in houses (bodies) of clay
Job4:20 Between morning and evening they are destroyed; without anyone noticing it they perish forever.
Job4:21 Is not their tent cord plucked up within them [so that the tent falls]? Do they not die
Job5:1 CALL NOW–is there any who will answer you? And to which of the holy [angels] will you turn?
Job5:2 For vexation and rage kill the foolish man; jealousy and indignation slay the simple.
Job5:3 I have seen the foolish taking root [and outwardly prospering]
Job5:4 His children are far from safety; [involved in their father's ruin] they are crushed in the [court of justice in the city's] gate
Job5:5 His harvest the hungry eat and take it even [when it grows] among the thorns; the snare opens for [his] wealth.
Job5:6 For affliction comes not forth from the dust
Job5:7 But man is born to trouble as the sparks and the flames fly upward.
Job5:8 As for me
Job5:9 Who does great things and unsearchable
Job5:10 Who gives rain upon the earth and sends waters upon the fields
Job5:11 So that He sets on high those who are lowly
Job5:12 He frustrates the devices of the crafty
Job5:13 He catches the [so-called] wise in their own trickiness
Job5:14 In the daytime they meet in darkness
Job5:15 But [God] saves [the fatherless] from the sword of their mouth
Job5:16 So the poor have hope
Job5:17 Happy and fortunate is the man whom God reproves; so do not despise or reject the correction of the Almighty [subjecting you to trial and suffering].
Job5:18 For He wounds
Job5:19 He will rescue you in six troubles; in seven nothing that is evil [for you] will touch you.
Job5:20 In famine He will redeem you from death
Job5:21 You shall be hidden from the scourge of the tongue
Job5:22 At destruction and famine you shall laugh
Job5:23 For you shall be in league with the stones of the field
Job5:24 And you shall know that your tent shall be in peace
Job5:25 You shall know also that your children shall be many
Job5:26 You shall come to your grave in ripe old age
Job5:27 This is what we have searched out; it is true. Hear and heed it and know for yourself [for your good].
Job6:1 THEN JOB answered
Job6:2 Oh
Job6:3 For now it would be heavier than the sand of the sea; therefore my words have been rash and wild
Job6:4 [But it is] because the arrows of the Almighty are within me
Job6:5 Does the wild ass bray when it has grass? Or does the ox low over its fodder?
Job6:6 Can that which has no taste to it be eaten without salt? Or is there any flavor in the white of an egg?
Job6:7 [These afflictions] my soul refuses to touch! Such things are like diseased food to me [sickening and repugnant]!
Job6:8 Oh
Job6:9 I even wish that it would please God to crush me
Job6:10 Then would I still have consolation–yes
Job6:11 What strength have I left
Job6:12 Is my strength and endurance that of stones? Or is my flesh made of bronze?
Job6:13 Is it not that I have no help in myself
Job6:14 To him who is about to faint and despair
Job6:15 [You] my brethren have dealt deceitfully as a brook
Job6:16 Which are black and turbid by reason of the ice
Job6:17 When they get warm
Job6:18 The caravans which travel by way of them turn aside; they go into the waste places and perish. [Such is my disappointment in you
Job6:19 The caravans of Tema looked [for water]
Job6:20 They were confounded because they had hoped [to find water]; they came there and were bitterly disappointed.
Job6:21 Now to me you are [like a dried-up brook]; you see my dismay and terror
Job6:22 Did I ever say
Job6:23 To deliver me from the adversary's hand
Job6:24 Teach me
Job6:25 How forcible are words of straightforward speech! But what does your arguing argue and prove or your reproof reprove?
Job6:26 Do you imagine your words to be an argument
Job6:27 Yes
Job6:28 Now be pleased to look upon me
Job6:29 Return [from your suspicion]
Job6:30 Is there wrong on my tongue? Cannot my taste discern what is destructive?
Job7:1 IS THERE not an [appointed] warfare and hard labor to man upon earth? And are not his days like the days of a hireling?
Job7:2 As a servant earnestly longs for the shade and the evening shadows
Job7:3 So am I allotted months of futile [suffering]
Job7:4 When I lie down I say
Job7:5 My flesh is clothed with worms and clods of dust; my skin is broken and has become loathsome
Job7:6 My days are swifter than a weaver's shuttle
Job7:7 Oh
Job7:8 The eye of him who sees me shall see me no more; while your eyes are upon me
Job7:9 As the cloud is consumed and vanishes away
Job7:10 He shall return no more to his house
Job7:11 Therefore I will not restrain my mouth; I will speak in the anguish of my spirit
Job7:12 Am I the sea
Job7:13 When I say
Job7:14 Then You scare me with dreams and terrify me through visions
Job7:15 So that I would choose strangling and death rather than these my bones.
Job7:16 I loathe my life; I would not live forever. Let me alone
Job7:17 What is man that You should magnify him and think him important? And that You should set Your mind upon him? [Ps. 8:4.]
Job7:18 And that You should visit him every morning and try him every moment?
Job7:19 How long will Your [plaguing] glance not look away from me
Job7:20 If I have sinned
Job7:21 And why do You not pardon my transgression and take away my iniquity? For now shall I lie down in the dust; and [even if] You will seek me diligently
Job8:1 THEN ANSWERED Bildad the Shuhite
Job8:2 How long will you say these things [Job]? And how long shall the words of your mouth be as a mighty wind?
Job8:3 Does God pervert justice? Or does the Almighty pervert righteousness?
Job8:4 If your children have sinned against Him
Job8:5 If you will seek God diligently and make your supplication to the Almighty
Job8:6 Then
Job8:7 And though your beginning was small
Job8:8 For inquire
Job8:9 For we are but of yesterday and know nothing
Job8:10 Shall not [the forefathers] teach you and tell you and utter words out of their hearts (the deepest part of their nature)?
Job8:11 Can the rush or papyrus grow up without marsh? Can the flag or reed grass grow without water?
Job8:12 While it is yet green
Job8:13 So are the ways of all who forget God; and the hope of the godless shall perish.
Job8:14 For his confidence breaks
Job8:15 He shall lean upon his house
Job8:16 He is green before the sun
Job8:17 [Godless] his roots are wrapped about the [stone] heap

Job8:19 See
Job8:20 Behold
Job8:21 He will yet fill your mouth with laughter [Job] and your lips with joyful shouting.

Job9:1 THEN JOB answered and said
Job9:2 Yes
Job9:3 If one should want to contend with Him
Job9:4 [God] is wise in heart and mighty in strength; who has [ever] hardened himself against Him and prospered or even been safe?
Job9:5 [God] Who removes the mountains
Job9:6 Who shakes the earth out of its place
Job9:7 Who commands the sun
Job9:8 Who alone stretches out the heavens and treads upon the waves and high places of the sea;
Job9:9 Who made [the constellations] the Bear
Job9:10 Who does great things past finding out
Job9:11 Behold
Job9:12 Behold
Job9:13 God will not withdraw His anger; the [proud] helpers of Rahab [arrogant monster of the sea] bow under Him.
Job9:14 How much less shall I answer Him
Job9:15 Whom
Job9:16 If I called and He answered me
Job9:17 For He overwhelms and breaks me with a tempest and multiplies my wounds without cause.
Job9:18 He will not allow me to catch my breath
Job9:19 If I speak of strength
Job9:20 Though I am innocent and in the right
Job9:21 Though I am blameless
Job9:22 It is all one; therefore I say
Job9:23 When [His] scourge slays suddenly
Job9:24 The earth is given into the hands of the wicked; He covers the faces of its judges [so that they are blinded to justice]. If it is not [God]
Job9:25 Now my days are swifter than a runner; they flee away
Job9:26 They are passed away like the swift rowboats made of reeds
Job9:27 If I say
Job9:28 I become afraid of all my pains and sorrows [yet to come]
Job9:29 I shall be held guilty and be condemned; why then should I labor in vain [to appear innocent]?
Job9:30 If I wash myself with snow and cleanse my hands with lye
Job9:31 Yet You will plunge me into the ditch
Job9:32 For [God] is not a [mere] man
Job9:33 There is no umpire between us
Job9:34 That He might take His rod away from [threatening] me
Job9:35 [Then] would I speak and not fear Him
Job10:1 I AM weary of my life and loathe it! I will give free expression to my complaint; I will speak in the bitterness of my soul.
Job10:2 I will say to God
Job10:3 Does it seem good to You that You should oppress
Job10:4 Have You eyes of flesh? Do You see as man sees?
Job10:5 Are Your days as the days of man
Job10:6 That You inquire after my iniquity and search for my sin–
Job10:7 Although You know that I am not wicked or guilty and that there is none who can deliver me out of Your hand?
Job10:8 Your hands have formed me and made me. Would You turn around and destroy me?
Job10:9 Remember [earnestly]
Job10:10 Have You not poured me out like milk and curdled me like cheese?
Job10:11 You have clothed me with skin and flesh and have knit me together with bones and sinews.
Job10:12 You have granted me life and favor
Job10:13 Yet these [the present evils] have You hid in Your heart [for me since my creation]; I know that this was with You [in Your purpose and thought].
Job10:14 If I sin
Job10:15 If I am wicked
Job10:16 If I lift myself up
Job10:17 You renew Your witnesses against me and increase Your indignation toward me; I am as if attacked by a troop time after time.
Job10:18 Why then did You bring me forth out of the womb? Would that I had perished and no eye had seen me!
Job10:19 I should have been as though I had not existed; I should have been carried from the womb to the grave.
Job10:20 Are not my days few? Cease then and let me alone
Job10:21 Before I go whence I shall not return
Job10:22 The land of sunless gloom as intense darkness
Job11:1 THEN ZOPHAR the Naamathite replied
Job11:2 Should not the multitude of words be answered? And should a man full of talk [and making such great professions] be pronounced free from guilt or blame?
Job11:3 Should your boastings and babble make men keep silent? And when you mock and scoff
Job11:4 For you have said
Job11:5 But oh
Job11:6 And that He would show you the secrets of wisdom! For He is manifold in understanding! Know therefore that God exacts of you less than your guilt and iniquity [deserve].
Job11:7 Can you find out the deep things of God
Job11:8 His wisdom is as high as the heights of heaven! What can you do? It is deeper than Sheol (the place of the dead)! What can you know?
Job11:9 Longer in measure [and scope] is it than the earth
Job11:10 If [God] sweeps in and arrests and calls into judgment
Job11:11 For He recognizes and knows hollow
Job11:12 But a stupid man will get wisdom [only] when a wild donkey's colt is born a man [as when he thinks himself free because he is lifted up in pride].
Job11:13 If you set your heart aright and stretch out your hands to [God]
Job11:14 If you put sin out of your hand and far away from you and let not evil dwell in your tents;
Job11:15 Then can you lift up your face to Him without stain [of sin
Job11:16 For you shall forget your misery; you shall remember it as waters that pass away.
Job11:17 And [your] life shall be clearer than the noonday and rise above it; though there be darkness
Job11:18 And you shall be secure and feel confident because there is hope; yes
Job11:19 You shall lie down
Job11:20 But the eyes of the wicked shall look [for relief] in vain
Job12:1 THEN JOB answered
Job12:2 No doubt you are the [only wise] people [in the world]
Job12:3 But I have understanding as well as you; I am not inferior to you. Who does not know such things as these [of God's wisdom and might]?
Job12:4 I am become one who is a laughingstock to his friend; I
Job12:5 In the thought of him who is at ease there is contempt for misfortune–but it is ready for those whose feet slip.
Job12:6 The dwellings of robbers prosper; those who provoke God are [apparently] secure; God supplies them abundantly [who have no god but their own hands and power].
Job12:7 For ask now the animals
Job12:8 Or speak to the earth [with its other forms of life]
Job12:9 Who [is so blind as] not to recognize in all these [that good and evil are promiscuously scattered throughout nature and human life] that it is God's hand which does it [and God's way]?
Job12:10 In His hand is the life of every living thing and the breath of all mankind.
Job12:11 Is it not the task of the ear to discriminate between [wise and unwise] words
Job12:12 With the aged [you say] is wisdom
Job12:13 But [only] with [God] are [perfect] wisdom and might; He [alone] has [true] counsel and understanding.
Job12:14 Behold
Job12:15 He withholds the waters
Job12:16 With Him are might and wisdom; the deceived and the deceiver are His [and in His power].
Job12:17 He leads [great and scheming] counselors away stripped and barefoot and makes the judges fools [in human estimation
Job12:18 He looses the fetters [ordered] by kings and has [the] waistcloth [of a slave] bound about their [own] loins.
Job12:19 He leads away priests as spoil
Job12:20 He deprives of speech those who are trusted and takes away the discernment and discretion of the aged.
Job12:21 He pours contempt on princes and loosens the belt of the strong [disabling them
Job12:22 He uncovers deep things out of darkness and brings into light black gloom and the shadow of death.
Job12:23 He makes nations great
Job12:24 He takes away understanding from the leaders of the people of the land and of the earth
Job12:25 They grope in the dark without light
Job13:1 [JOB CONTINUED:] Behold
Job13:2 What you know
Job13:3 Surely I wish to speak to the Almighty
Job13:4 But you are forgers of lies [you defame my character most untruthfully]; you are all physicians of no value and have no remedy to offer.
Job13:5 Oh
Job13:6 Hear now my reasoning
Job13:7 Will you speak unrighteously for God and talk deceitfully for Him?
Job13:8 Will you show partiality to Him [be unjust to me in order to gain favor with Him]? Will you act as special pleaders for God?
Job13:9 Would it be profitable for you if He should investigate your tactics [with me]? Or as one deceives and mocks a man
Job13:10 He will surely reprove you if you do secretly show partiality.
Job13:11 Shall not His majesty make you afraid
Job13:12 Your memorable sayings are proverbs of ashes [valueless]; your defenses are defenses of clay [and will crumble].
Job13:13 Hold your peace! Let me alone
Job13:14 Why should I take my flesh in my teeth and put my life in my hands [incurring the danger of God's wrath]?
Job13:15 [I do it because
Job13:16 This will be my salvation
Job13:17 Listen diligently to my speech
Job13:18 Behold now
Job13:19 Who is he who will argue against and refute me? For then I would hold my peace and expire.
Job13:20 Only [O Lord] grant two conditions to me
Job13:21 Withdraw Your hand and take this bodily suffering far from me; and let not my [reverent] dread of You terrify me.
Job13:22 Then [Lord] call and I will answer
Job13:23 How many are my iniquities and sins [that so much sorrow should come to me]? Make me recognize and know my transgression and my sin. [Rom. 8:1.]
Job13:24 Why do You hide Your face [as if offended] and alienate me as if I were Your enemy?
Job13:25 Will You harass and frighten a [poor
Job13:26 For You write bitter things against me [in Your bill of indictment] and make me inherit and be accountable now for the iniquities of my youth.
Job13:27 You put my feet also in the stocks and observe critically all my paths; You set a circle and limit around the soles of my feet [which I must not overstep].
Job13:28 And he wastes away as a rotten thing
Job14:1 MAN WHO is born of a woman is of few days and full of trouble.
Job14:2 He comes forth like a flower and withers; he flees also like a shadow and continues not.
Job14:3 And [Lord] do You open Your eyes upon such a one
Job14:4 Who can bring a clean thing out of an unclean? No one! [Isa. 1:18; I John 1:7.]
Job14:5 Since a man's days are already determined
Job14:6 [O God] turn from him [and cease to watch him so pitilessly]; let him rest until he has accomplished as does a hireling the appointed time for his day.
Job14:7 For there is hope for a tree if it is cut down
Job14:8 Though its roots grow old in the earth and its stock dies in the ground
Job14:9 Yet through the scent [and breathing] of water [the stump of the tree] will bud and bring forth boughs like a young plant.
Job14:10 But [the brave
Job14:11 As waters evaporate from the lake
Job14:12 So man lies down and does not rise [to his former state]. Till the heavens are no more

Job14:18 But as a mountain

Job15:1 THEN ELIPHAZ the Temanite answered [Job]
Job15:2 Should a wise man utter such windy knowledge [as we have just heard] and fill himself with the east wind [of withering
Job15:3 Should he reason with unprofitable talk? Or with speeches with which he can do no good?
Job15:4 Indeed
Job15:5 For your iniquity teaches your mouth
Job15:6 Your own mouth condemns you
Job15:7 Are you the first man that was born [the original wise man]? Or were you created before the hills?
Job15:8 Were you present to hear the secret counsel of God? And do you limit [the possession of] wisdom to yourself?
Job15:9 What do you know that we know not? What do you understand that is not equally clear to us?
Job15:10 Among us are both the gray-haired and the aged
Job15:11 Are God's consolations [as we have interpreted them to you] too trivial for you? Is there any secret thing (any bosom sin) which you have not given up? [Or] were we too gentle [in our first speech] toward you to be effective?
Job15:12 Why does your heart carry you away [why allow yourself to be controlled by feeling]? And why do your eyes flash [in anger or contempt]
Job15:13 That you turn your spirit against God and let [such] words [as you have spoken] go out of your mouth?
Job15:14 What is man
Job15:15 Behold
Job15:16 How much less that which is abominable and corrupt
Job15:17 I will show you
Job15:18 What wise men have not hid but have freely communicated; it was told to them by their fathers
Job15:19 Unto whom alone the land was given
Job15:20 The wicked man suffers with [self-inflicted] torment all his days
Job15:21 A [dreadful] sound of terrors is in his ears; in prosperity the destroyer shall come upon him [the dwellings of robbers are not at peace].
Job15:22 He believes that he will not return out of darkness
Job15:23 He wanders abroad for food
Job15:24 Distress and anguish terrify him; [he knows] they shall prevail against him
Job15:25 Because he has stretched out his hand against God and bids defiance and behaves himself proudly against the Almighty
Job15:26 Running stubbornly against Him with a thickly ornamented shield;
Job15:27 Because he has covered his face with his fat
Job15:28 And has lived in desolate [God-forsaken] cities and in houses which no man should inhabit
Job15:29 He shall not be rich
Job15:30 He shall not depart out of darkness [and escape from calamity; the wrath of God] shall consume him as flame consumes a dry tree
Job15:31 Let him not deceive himself and trust in vanity (emptiness
Job15:32 It shall be accomplished and paid in full while he still lives
Job15:33 He shall fail to bring his grapes to maturity [leaving them to wither unnourished] on the vine and shall cast off blossoms [and fail to bring forth fruit] like the olive tree.
Job15:34 For the company of the godless shall be barren
Job15:35 They conceive mischief and bring forth iniquity
Job16:1 THEN JOB answered
Job16:2 I have heard many such things; wearisome and miserable comforters are you all!
Job16:3 Will your futile words of wind have no end? Or what makes you so bold to answer [me like this]?
Job16:4 I also could speak as you do
Job16:5 [But] I would strengthen and encourage you with [the words of] my mouth
Job16:6 If I speak [to you miserable comforters]
Job16:7 But now [God] has taken away my strength. You [O Lord] have made desolate all my family and associates.
Job16:8 You have laid firm hold on me and have shriveled me up
Job16:9 [My adversary Satan] has torn [me] in his wrath and hated and persecuted me; he has gnashed upon me with his teeth; my adversary sharpens his eyes against me.
Job16:10 [The forces of evil] have gaped at me with their mouths; they have struck me upon the cheek insolently; they massed themselves together and conspired unanimously against me. [Ps. 22:13; 35:21.]
Job16:11 God has delivered me to the ungodly (to the evil one) and cast me [headlong] into the hands of the wicked (Satan's host).
Job16:12 I was living at ease
Job16:13 [Satan's] arrows whiz around me. He slashes open my vitals and does not spare; he pours out my gall on the ground.
Job16:14 [Satan] stabs me
Job16:15 I have sewed sackcloth over my skin [as a sign of mourning] and have defiled my horn (my insignia of strength) in the dust.
Job16:16 My face is red and swollen with weeping
Job16:17 Although there is no guilt or violence in my hands and my prayer is pure.
Job16:18 O earth
Job16:19 Even now
Job16:20 My friends scorn me
Job16:21 Oh
Job16:22 For when a few years are come
Job17:1 MY SPIRIT is broken
Job17:2 Surely there are mockers and mockery around me
Job17:3 Give me a pledge with Yourself [acknowledge my innocence before my death]; who is there that will give security for me?
Job17:4 But their hearts [Lord] You have closed to understanding; therefore You will not let them triumph [by giving them a verdict against me].
Job17:5 He who denounces his friends [in order to make them] a prey and get a share
Job17:6 But He has made me a byword among the people
Job17:7 My eye has grown dim because of grief
Job17:8 Upright men shall be astonished and appalled at this
Job17:9 Yet shall the righteous (those upright and in right standing with God) hold to their ways
Job17:10 But as for you
Job17:11 My days are past
Job17:12 These [thoughts] extend from the night into the day
Job17:13 But if I look to Sheol (the unseen state) as my abode
Job17:14 If I say to the grave and corruption
Job17:15 Where then is my hope? And if I have hope
Job17:16 [My hope] shall go down to the bars of Sheol (the unseen state) when once there is rest in the dust.
Job18:1 THEN BILDAD the Shuhite answered
Job18:2 How long will you lay snares for words and have to hunt for your argument? Do some clear thinking
Job18:3 Why are we counted as beasts [as if we had no sense]? Why are we unclean in your sight?
Job18:4 You who tear yourself in your anger
Job18:5 Yes
Job18:6 The light shall be dark in his dwelling
Job18:7 The steps of his strength shall be shortened
Job18:8 For the wicked is cast into a net by his own feet
Job18:9 A trap will catch him by the heel
Job18:10 A noose is hidden for him on the ground and a trap for him in the way.
Job18:11 Terrors shall make him afraid on every side and shall chase him at his heels.
Job18:12 The strength [of the wicked] shall be hunger-bitten
Job18:13 By disease his strength and his skin shall be devoured; the firstborn of death [the worst of diseases] shall consume his limbs.
Job18:14 He shall be rooted out of his dwelling place in which he trusted
Job18:15 There shall dwell in his tent that which is none of his [family]; sulphur shall be scattered over his dwelling [to purify it after his going].
Job18:16 The roots [of the wicked] shall be dried up beneath
Job18:17 His remembrance shall perish from the earth
Job18:18 He shall be thrust from light into darkness and driven out of the world.
Job18:19 He shall neither have son nor grandson among his people
Job18:20 They [of the west] that come after [the wicked man] shall be astonished and appalled at his day
Job18:21 Surely such are the dwellings of the ungodly
Job19:1 THEN JOB answered:
Job19:2 How long will you vex and torment me and break me in pieces with words?
Job19:3 These ten times you have reproached me; you are not ashamed that you make yourselves strange [harden yourselves against me and deal severely with me].
Job19:4 And if it were true that I have erred
Job19:5 If indeed you magnify yourselves against me and plead against me my reproach and humiliation
Job19:6 Know that God has overthrown and put me in the wrong and has closed His net about me.
Job19:7 Behold
Job19:8 He has walled up my way so that I cannot pass
Job19:9 He has stripped me of my glory and taken the crown from my head.
Job19:10 He has broken me down on every side
Job19:11 He has also kindled His wrath against me
Job19:12 His troops come together and cast up their way and siege works against me and encamp round about my tent.
Job19:13 He has put my brethren far from me
Job19:14 My kinsfolk have failed me
Job19:15 Those who live temporarily in my house and my maids count me as a stranger; I am an alien in their sight.
Job19:16 I call to my servant
Job19:17 I am repulsive to my wife and loathsome to the children of my own mother.
Job19:18 Even young children despise me; when I get up
Job19:19 All the men of my council and my familiar friends abhor me; those whom I loved are turned against me.
Job19:20 My bone clings to my skin and to my flesh
Job19:21 Have pity on me! Have pity on me
Job19:22 Why do you
Job19:23 Oh
Job19:24 That with an iron pen and [molten] lead they were graven in the rock forever!
Job19:25 For I know that my Redeemer and Vindicator lives
Job19:26 And after my skin
Job19:27 Whom I
Job19:28 If you say
Job19:29 Then beware and be afraid of the sword [of divine vengeance]
Job20:1 THEN ZOPHAR the Naamathite answered
Job20:2 Therefore do my thoughts give me an answer
Job20:3 I have heard the reproof which puts me to shame
Job20:4 Do you not know from of old
Job20:5 That the triumphing of the wicked is short
Job20:6 Though his [proud] height mounts up to the heavens and his head reaches to the clouds
Job20:7 Yet he will perish forever like his own dung; those who have seen him will say
Job20:8 He will fly away like a dream and will not be found; yes
Job20:9 The eye which saw him will see him no more
Job20:10 The poor will oppress his children
Job20:11 His bones are full of youthful energy
Job20:12 Though wickedness is sweet in his mouth
Job20:13 Though he is loath to let it go but keeps it still within his mouth
Job20:14 Yet his food turns [to poison] in his stomach; it is the venom of asps within him.
Job20:15 He has swallowed down [his ill-gotten] riches
Job20:16 He shall suck the poison of asps [which ill-gotten wealth contains]; the viper's tongue shall slay him.
Job20:17 He shall not look upon the rivers
Job20:18 That which he labored for shall he give back and shall not swallow it down [to enjoy it]; according to his wealth shall the restitution be
Job20:19 For he has oppressed and forsaken the poor; he has violently taken away a house which he did not build.
Job20:20 Because his desire and greed knew no quietness within him
Job20:21 There was nothing left that he did not devour; therefore his prosperity will not endure.
Job20:22 In the fullness of his sufficiency [in the time of his great abundance] he shall be poor and in straits; every hand of everyone who is in misery shall come upon him [he is but a wretch on every side].
Job20:23 When he is about to fill his belly [as in the wilderness when God sent the quails]
Job20:24 He will flee from the iron weapon
Job20:25 [The arrow] is drawn forth and it comes out after passing through his body; yes
Job20:26 Every misfortune is laid up for his treasures. A fire not blown by man shall devour him; it shall consume what is left in his tent [and it shall go ill with him who remains there].
Job20:27 The heavens shall reveal his iniquity
Job20:28 The produce and increase of his house will go into exile [with the victors]
Job20:29 This is the wicked man's portion from God
Job21:1 THEN JOB answered
Job21:2 Hear diligently my speech
Job21:3 Allow me
Job21:4 As for me
Job21:5 Look at me and be astonished (appalled); and lay your hand upon your mouth.
Job21:6 Even when I remember
Job21:7 Why do the wicked live
Job21:8 Their children are established with them in their sight
Job21:9 Their houses are safe and in peace
Job21:10 Their bull breeds and fails not; their cows calve and do not miscarry.
Job21:11 They send forth their little ones like a flock
Job21:12 They themselves lift up their voices and sing to the tambourine and the lyre and rejoice to the sound of the pipe.
Job21:13 They spend their days in prosperity and go down to Sheol (the unseen state) in a moment and peacefully.
Job21:14 Yet they say to God
Job21:15 Who is the Almighty
Job21:16 But notice
Job21:17 How often [then] is it that the lamp of the wicked is put out? That their calamity comes upon them? That God distributes pains and sorrows to them in His anger? [Luke 12:46.]
Job21:18 That they are like stubble before the wind and like chaff that the storm steals and carries away?
Job21:19 You say
Job21:20 Let his own eyes see his destruction
Job21:21 For what pleasure or interest has a man in his house and family after he is dead
Job21:22 Shall any teach God knowledge
Job21:23 One dies in his full strength
Job21:24 His pails are full of milk [his veins are filled with nourishment]
Job21:25 Whereas another man dies in bitterness of soul and never tastes of pleasure or good fortune.
Job21:26 They lie down alike in the dust
Job21:27 Behold
Job21:28 For you say
Job21:29 Have you not asked those who travel this way
Job21:30 That the evil man is [now] spared in the day of calamity and destruction
Job21:31 But who declares [a man's] way [and rebukes] him to his face? And who pays him back for what he has done?
Job21:32 When he is borne to the grave
Job21:33 The clods of the valley are sweet to him
Job21:34 How then can you comfort me with empty and futile words
Job22:1 THEN ELIPHAZ the Temanite answered [Job]
Job22:2 Can a man be profitable to God? Surely he that is wise is profitable to himself. [Ps. 16:2; Luke 17:10.]
Job22:3 Is it any pleasure or advantage to the Almighty that you are righteous (upright and in right standing with Him)? Or is it gain to Him that you make your ways perfect? [Isa. 62:3; Zech. 2:8; Mal. 3:17; Acts 20:28.]
Job22:4 Is it for your [reverential] fear of Him that He [thus] reproves you
Job22:5 Is not your wickedness great? There is no end to your iniquities.
Job22:6 For you have taken pledges of your brother for nothing
Job22:7 You have not given water to the weary to drink
Job22:8 But [you
Job22:9 You have sent widows away empty-handed
Job22:10 Therefore snares are round about you
Job22:11 Your light is darkened
Job22:12 Is not God in the height of heaven? And behold the height of the stars
Job22:13 Therefore you say
Job22:14 Thick clouds are a covering to Him
Job22:15 Will you pay attention and keep to the old way that wicked men trod [in Noah's time]
Job22:16 Men who were snatched away before their time
Job22:17 They said to God
Job22:18 Yet He filled their houses with good [things]. But the counsel of the ungodly is far from me.
Job22:19 The righteous see it and are glad; and the innocent laugh them to scorn [saying]
Job22:20 Surely those who rose up against us are cut off
Job22:21 Acquaint now yourself with Him [agree with God and show yourself to be conformed to His will] and be at peace; by that [you shall prosper and great] good shall come to you.
Job22:22 Receive
Job22:23 If you return to the Almighty [and submit and humble yourself before Him]
Job22:24 If you lay gold in the dust
Job22:25 And make the Almighty your gold and [the Lord] your precious silver treasure
Job22:26 Then you will have delight in the Almighty
Job22:27 You will make your prayer to Him
Job22:28 You shall also decide and decree a thing
Job22:29 When they make [you] low
Job22:30 He will even deliver the one [for whom you intercede] who is not innocent; yes
Job23:1 THEN JOB answered
Job23:2 Even today is my complaint rebellious and bitter; my stroke is heavier than my groaning.
Job23:3 Oh
Job23:4 I would lay my cause before Him and fill my mouth with arguments.
Job23:5 I would learn what He would answer me
Job23:6 Would He plead against me with His great power? No
Job23:7 There the righteous [one who is upright and in right standing with God] could reason with Him; so I should be acquitted by my Judge forever.
Job23:8 Behold
Job23:9 On the left hand [and to the north] where He works [I seek Him]
Job23:10 But He knows the way that I take [He has concern for it
Job23:11 My foot has held fast to His steps; His ways have I kept and not turned aside.
Job23:12 I have not gone back from the commandment of His lips; I have esteemed and treasured the words of His mouth more than my necessary food.
Job23:13 But He is unchangeable
Job23:14 For He performs [that which He has] planned for me
Job23:15 Therefore am I troubled and terrified at His presence; when I consider
Job23:16 For God has made my heart faint
Job23:17 Because I was not cut off before the darkness [of these woes befell me]
Job24:1 WHY [seeing times are not hidden from the Almighty] does He not set seasons for judgment? Why do those who know Him see not His days [for punishment of the wicked]? [Acts 1:7.]
Job24:2 Some remove the landmarks; they violently take away flocks and pasture them [appropriating land and flocks openly].
Job24:3 They drive away the donkey of the fatherless; they take the widow's ox for a pledge.
Job24:4 They crowd the poor and needy off the road; the poor and meek of the earth all hide themselves.
Job24:5 Behold
Job24:6 They reap each one his fodder in a field [that is not his own]
Job24:7 They lie all night naked
Job24:8 They are wet with the showers of the mountains and cling to the rock for want of shelter.
Job24:9 [The violent men whose wickedness seems unnoticed] pluck the fatherless infants from the breast [to sell or make them slaves]
Job24:10 So that the needy go about naked for lack of clothing
Job24:11 Among the olive rows [of the wicked
Job24:12 From out of the populous city men groan
Job24:13 These wrongdoers are of those who rebel against the light; they know not its ways nor stay in its paths.
Job24:14 The murderer rises with the light; he kills the poor and the needy
Job24:15 The eye also of the adulterer waits for the twilight
Job24:16 In the dark
Job24:17 For midnight is morning to all of them; for they are familiar with the terrors of deep darkness.
Job24:18 [You say] Swiftly such men pass away on the face of the waters; their portion is cursed in the earth; [no treader] turns into their vineyards.
Job24:19 Drought and heat consume the snow waters; so does Sheol (the place of the dead) those who have sinned.
Job24:20 The womb shall forget him
Job24:21 [The evil man] preys upon the barren
Job24:22 Yet [God] prolongs the life of the [wicked] mighty by His power; they rise up when they had despaired of life.
Job24:23 God gives them security
Job24:24 They are exalted for a little while
Job24:25 And if this is not so

Job25:2 Dominion and fear are with [God]; He makes peace in His high places.
Job25:3 Is there any number to His armies? And upon whom does not His light arise?
Job25:4 How then can man be justified and righteous before God? Or how can he who is born of a woman be pure and clean? [Ps. 130:3; 143:2.]
Job25:5 Behold
Job25:6 How much less man
Job26:1 BUT JOB answered
Job26:2 How you have helped him who is without power! How you have sustained the arm that is without strength!
Job26:3 How you have counseled him who has no wisdom! And how plentifully you have declared to him sound knowledge!
Job26:4 With whose assistance have you uttered these words? And whose spirit [inspired what] came forth from you?
Job26:5 The shades of the dead tremble underneath the waters and their inhabitants.
Job26:6 Sheol (the place of the dead) is naked before God
Job26:7 He it is Who spreads out the northern skies over emptiness and hangs the earth upon or over nothing.
Job26:8 He holds the waters bound in His clouds [which otherwise would spill on earth all at once]
Job26:9 He covers the face of His throne and spreads over it His cloud.
Job26:10 He has placed an enclosing limit [the horizon] upon the waters at the boundary between light and darkness.
Job26:11 The pillars of the heavens tremble and are astonished at His rebuke.
Job26:12 He stills or stirs up the sea by His power
Job26:13 By His breath the heavens are garnished; His hand pierced the [swiftly] fleeing serpent. [Ps. 33:6.]
Job26:14 Yet these are but [a small part of His doings] the outskirts of His ways or the mere fringes of His force
Job27:1 JOB AGAIN took up his discourse and said
Job27:2 As God lives
Job27:3 As long as my life is still whole within me
Job27:4 My lips shall not speak untruth
Job27:5 God forbid that I should justify you–saying you are right [in your accusations against me]; till I die
Job27:6 My uprightness and my right standing with God I hold fast and will not let them go; my heart does not reproach me for any of my days and it shall not reproach me as long as I live.
Job27:7 Let my enemy be as the wicked
Job27:8 For what is the hope of the godless and polluted
Job27:9 Will God hear his cry when trouble comes upon him?
Job27:10 Will he take delight in the Almighty? Will he call upon God at all times?
Job27:11 I will teach you regarding the hand and handiwork of God; that which is with the Almighty [God's actual treatment of the wicked man] will I not conceal.
Job27:12 Behold
Job27:13 This [which I am about to tell] is the portion of a wicked man with God
Job27:14 If his children are multiplied
Job27:15 Those who survive him
Job27:16 Though he heaps up silver like dust and piles up clothing like clay
Job27:17 He may prepare it
Job27:18 He builds his house like a moth or a spider
Job27:19 [The wicked] will lie down rich
Job27:20 Terrors overtake him like a [suddenly loosened] flood; a windstorm steals him away in the night.
Job27:21 The east wind lifts him up
Job27:22 For [God and the storm] hurl at him without pity and unsparingly [their thunderbolts of wrath]; he flees in haste before His power.
Job27:23 [God causes] men to clap their hands at him [in malignant joy] and hiss him out of his place.
Job28:1 SURELY THERE is a mine for silver
Job28:2 Iron is taken out of the earth
Job28:3 Man sets an end to darkness
Job28:4 Men break open shafts away from where people sojourn
Job28:5 As for the earth
Job28:6 Its stones are the bed of sapphires; it holds dust of gold [which he wins].
Job28:7 That path no bird of prey knows
Job28:8 The proud beasts [and their young] have not trodden it
Job28:9 Man puts forth his hand upon the flinty rock; he overturns the mountains by the roots.
Job28:10 He cuts out channels and passages among the rocks; and his eye sees every precious thing.
Job28:11 [Man] binds the streams so that they do not trickle [into the mine]
Job28:12 But where shall Wisdom be found? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:13 Man knows not the price of it; neither is it found in the land of the living.
Job28:14 The deep says
Job28:15 It cannot be gotten for gold
Job28:16 It cannot be valued in [terms of] the gold of Ophir
Job28:17 Gold and glass cannot equal [Wisdom]
Job28:18 No mention shall be made of coral or of crystal; for the possession of Wisdom is even above rubies or pearls.
Job28:19 The topaz of Ethiopia cannot compare with it
Job28:20 From where then does Wisdom come? And where is the place of understanding?
Job28:21 It is hidden from the eyes of all living
Job28:22 Abaddon (the place of destruction) and Death say
Job28:23 God understands the way [to Wisdom] and He knows the place of it [Wisdom is with God alone].
Job28:24 For He looks to the ends of the earth and sees everything under the heavens.
Job28:25 When He gave to the wind weight or pressure and allotted the waters by measure
Job28:26 When He made a decree for the rain and a way for the lightning of the thunder
Job28:27 Then He saw [Wisdom] and declared it; He established it
Job28:28 But to man He said
Job29:1 AND JOB again took up his discussion and said
Job29:2 Oh
Job29:3 When His lamp shone above and upon my head and by His light I walked through darkness;
Job29:4 As I was in the [prime] ripeness of my days
Job29:5 When the Almighty was yet with me and my children were about me
Job29:6 When my steps [through rich pasturage] were washed with butter and the rock poured out for me streams of oil!
Job29:7 When I went out to the gate of the city
Job29:8 The young men saw me and hid themselves; the aged rose up and stood;
Job29:9 The princes refrained from talking and laid their hands on their mouths;
Job29:10 The voices of the nobles were hushed
Job29:11 For when the ear heard
Job29:12 Because I delivered the poor who cried
Job29:13 The blessing of him who was about to perish came upon me
Job29:14 I put on righteousness
Job29:15 I was eyes to the blind
Job29:16 I was a father to the poor and needy; the cause of him I did not know I searched out.
Job29:17 And I broke the jaws or the big teeth of the unrighteous and plucked the prey out of his teeth.
Job29:18 Then I said
Job29:19 My root is spread out and open to the waters
Job29:20 My glory and honor are fresh in me [being constantly renewed]
Job29:21 Men listened to me and waited and kept silence for my counsel.
Job29:22 After I spoke
Job29:23 And they waited for me as for the rain
Job29:24 I smiled on them when they had no confidence
Job29:25 I chose their way [for them] and sat as [their] chief
Job30:1 BUT NOW they who are younger than I have me in derision
Job30:2 Yes
Job30:3 They are gaunt with want and famine; they gnaw the dry and barren ground or flee into the wilderness
Job30:4 They pluck saltwort or mallows among the bushes
Job30:5 They are driven from among men
Job30:6 They must dwell in the clefts of frightful valleys (gullies made by torrents) and in holes of the earth and of the rocks.
Job30:7 Among the bushes they bray and howl [like wild animals]; beneath the prickly scrub they fling themselves and huddle together.
Job30:8 Sons of the worthless and nameless
Job30:9 And now I have become their song; yes
Job30:10 They abhor me
Job30:11 For God has loosed my bowstring and afflicted and humbled me; they have cast off the bridle [of restraint] before me.
Job30:12 On my right hand rises the rabble brood; they jostle me and push away my feet
Job30:13 They break up and clutter my path [embarrassing my plans]; they urge on my calamity
Job30:14 As through a wide breach they come in; amid the crash [of falling walls] they roll themselves upon me.
Job30:15 Terrors are turned upon me; my honor and reputation they chase away like the wind
Job30:16 And now my life is poured out within me; the days of affliction have gripped me.
Job30:17 My bones are pierced [with aching] in the night season
Job30:18 By the great force [of my disease] my garment is disguised and disfigured; it binds me about like the collar of my coat.
Job30:19 [God] has cast me into the mire
Job30:20 I cry to You
Job30:21 You have become harsh and cruel to me; with the might of Your hand You [keep me alive only to] persecute me.
Job30:22 You lift me up on the wind; You cause me to ride upon it
Job30:23 For I know that You will bring me to death and to the house [of meeting] appointed for all the living.
Job30:24 However
Job30:25 Did not I weep for him who was in trouble? Was not my heart grieved for the poor and needy?
Job30:26 But when I looked for good
Job30:27 My heart is troubled and does not rest; days of affliction come to meet me.
Job30:28 I go about blackened
Job30:29 I am a brother to jackals [which howl]
Job30:30 My skin falls from me in blackened flakes
Job30:31 Therefore my lyre is turned to mourning
Job31:1 I DICTATED a covenant (an agreement) to my eyes; how then could I look [lustfully] upon a girl?
Job31:2 For what portion should I have from God above [if I were lewd]
Job31:3 Does not calamity [justly] befall the unrighteous
Job31:4 Does not [God] see my ways and count all my steps?
Job31:5 If I have walked with falsehood or vanity
Job31:6 Oh
Job31:7 If my step has turned out of [God's] way
Job31:8 Then let me sow and let another eat; yes
Job31:9 If my heart has been deceived and I made a fool by a woman
Job31:10 Then let my wife grind [meal
Job31:11 For [adultery] is a heinous and chief crime
Job31:12 For [uncontrolled passion] is a fire which consumes to Abaddon (to destruction
Job31:13 If I have despised and rejected the cause of my manservant or my maidservant when they contended or brought a complaint against me
Job31:14 What then shall I do when God rises up [to judge]? When He visits [to inquire of me]
Job31:15 Did not He Who made me in the womb make [my servant]? And did not One fashion us both in the womb? [Prov. 14:31; 22:2; Mal. 2:10.]
Job31:16 If I have withheld from the poor and needy what they desired
Job31:17 Or have eaten my morsel alone and have not shared it with the fatherless–
Job31:18 No
Job31:19 If I have seen anyone perish for want of clothing
Job31:20 If his loins have not blessed me [for clothing them]
Job31:21 If I have lifted my hand against the fatherless when I saw [that the judges would be favorable and be] my help at the [council] gate
Job31:22 Then let my shoulder fall away from my shoulder blade
Job31:23 For calamity from God was a terror to me
Job31:24 If I have made gold my trust and hope or have said to fine gold
Job31:25 If I rejoiced because my wealth was great and because my [powerful] hand [alone] had gotten much
Job31:26 If I beheld [as an object of worship] the sunlight when it shone or the moon walking in its brightness
Job31:27 And my heart has been secretly enticed by them or my mouth has kissed my hand [in homage to them]
Job31:28 This also would have been [a heinous and principal] iniquity to demand the judges' action and punishment
Job31:29 If I rejoiced at the destruction of him who hated me or lifted myself up [in malicious triumph] when evil overtook him–
Job31:30 No
Job31:31 [Just ask] if the men of my tent will not say
Job31:32 The temporary resident has not lodged in the street
Job31:33 If like Adam or like [other] men I have concealed my transgressions
Job31:34 Because I feared the great multitude and the contempt of families terrified me so that I kept silence and did not go out of the door–
Job31:35 Oh
Job31:36 Surely I would [proudly] bear it on my shoulder and wind the scroll about my head as a diadem.
Job31:37 I would count out to Him the number of my steps [with every detail of my life]
Job31:38 For if my land has cried out against me and its furrows have complained together with tears [that I have no right to them]
Job31:39 If I have eaten its fruits without paying for them or have caused its [rightful] owners to breathe their last
Job31:40 Let thistles grow instead of wheat and cockleburs instead of barley. The [controversial] words of Job [with his friends] are ended.

Job32:5 But when Elihu saw that there was no answer in the mouths of these three men

Job32:8 But there is [a vital force] a spirit [of intelligence] in man
Job32:9 It is not the great [necessarily] who are wise
Job32:10 So I say
Job32:11 You see
Job32:12 Yes
Job32:13 Beware lest you say
Job32:14 Now [Job] has not directed his words against me [therefore I have no cause for irritation]

Job32:16 And shall I wait

Job33:1 BE THAT as it may
Job33:2 Behold
Job33:3 My words shall express the uprightness of my heart
Job33:4 [It is] the Spirit of God that made me [which has stirred me up]
Job33:5 Answer me now
Job33:6 Behold
Job33:7 See my terror [for I am only a fellow mortal
Job33:8 Surely you have spoken in my hearing
Job33:9 I am clean
Job33:10 But behold
Job33:11 He puts my feet in the stocks; He [untrustingly] watches all my paths [you say].
Job33:12 I reply to you
Job33:13 Why do you contend against Him? For He does not give account of any of His actions. [Sufficient for us it should be to know that it is He Who does them.]
Job33:14 For God [does reveal His will; He] speaks not only once
Job33:15 [One may hear God's voice] in a dream
Job33:16 Then He opens the ears of men and seals their instruction [terrifying them with warnings]
Job33:17 That He may withdraw man from his purpose and cut off pride from him [disgusting him with his own disappointing self-sufficiency].
Job33:18 He holds him back from the pit [of destruction]
Job33:19 [God's voice may be heard by man when] he is chastened with pain upon his bed and with continual strife in his bones or while all his bones are firmly set
Job33:20 So that his desire makes him loathe food
Job33:21 His flesh is so wasted away that it cannot be seen
Job33:22 Yes
Job33:23 [God's voice may be heard] if there is for the hearer a messenger or an angel
Job33:24 Then [God] is gracious to him and says
Job33:25 [Then the man's] flesh shall be restored; it becomes fresher and more tender than a child's; he returns to the days of his youth.
Job33:26 He prays to God
Job33:27 He looks upon other men or sings out to them
Job33:28 [God] has redeemed my life from going down to the pit [of destruction]
Job33:29 [Elihu comments] Behold
Job33:30 To bring back his life from the pit [of destruction]
Job33:31 Give heed
Job33:32 If you have anything to say
Job33:33 If [you do] not [have anything to say]
Job34:1 ELIHU ANSWERED (continued his discourse) and said
Job34:2 Hear my words
Job34:3 For the ear tries words as the palate tastes food.
Job34:4 Let us choose for ourselves that which is right; let us know among ourselves what is good.
Job34:5 For Job has said
Job34:6 Would I lie against my right? Yet
Job34:7 What man is like Job
Job34:8 Who goes in company with the workers of iniquity and walks with wicked men?
Job34:9 For he has said
Job34:10 Therefore hear me
Job34:11 For according to the deeds of a man God will [exactly] proportion his pay
Job34:12 Truly God will not do wickedly
Job34:13 Who put [God] in charge over the earth? Or who laid on Him the whole world?
Job34:14 If [God] should set His heart upon him [man] and withdraw His [life-giving] spirit and His breath [from man] to Himself
Job34:15 All flesh would perish together
Job34:16 If now you have understanding
Job34:17 Is it possible that an enemy of right should govern? And will you condemn Him Who is just and mighty?
Job34:18 [God] Who says to a king
Job34:19 [God] is not partial to princes
Job34:20 In a moment they die; even at midnight the people are shaken and pass away
Job34:21 For [God's] eyes are upon the ways of a man
Job34:22 There is no darkness nor thick gloom where the evildoers may hide themselves.
Job34:23 [God] sets before man no appointed time
Job34:24 He breaks in pieces mighty men without inquiry [before a jury] and in ways past finding out and sets others in their stead. [Dan. 2:21.]
Job34:25 Therefore He takes knowledge of their works
Job34:26 God strikes them down as wicked men in the open sight of beholders
Job34:27 Because they turned aside from Him and would not consider or show regard for any of His ways
Job34:28 So that they caused the cry of the poor to come to Him
Job34:29 When He gives quietness (peace and security from oppression)
Job34:30 That the godless man may not reign
Job34:31 For has anyone said to God
Job34:32 Teach me what I do not see [in regard to how I have sinned]; if I have done iniquity
Job34:33 Should [God's] recompense [for your sins] be as you will it
Job34:34 Men of understanding will tell me
Job34:35 That Job speaks without knowledge

Job36:1 ELIHU PROCEEDED and said

Job36:6 He does not prolong the life of the wicked
Job36:7 He withdraws not His eyes from the righteous (the upright in right standing with God); but He sets them forever with kings upon the throne
Job36:8 And if they are bound in fetters [of adversity] and held by cords of affliction

Job38:1 THEN THE Lord answered Job out of the whirlwind and said
Job38:2 Who is this that darkens counsel by words without knowledge? [Job 35:16.]
Job38:3 Gird up now your loins like a man
Job38:4 Where were you when I laid the foundation of the earth? Declare to Me
Job38:5 Who determined the measures of the earth
Job38:6 Upon what were the foundations of it fastened
Job38:7 When the morning stars sang together and all the sons of God shouted for joy?
Job38:8 Or who shut up the sea with doors when it broke forth and issued out of the womb?–
Job38:9 When I made the clouds the garment of it
Job38:10 And marked for it My appointed boundary and set bars and doors
Job38:11 And said
Job38:12 Have you commanded the morning since your days began and caused the dawn to know its place
Job38:13 So that [light] may get hold of the corners of the earth and shake the wickedness [of night] out of it?
Job38:14 It is changed like clay into which a seal is pressed; and things stand out like a many-colored garment.
Job38:15 From the wicked their light is withheld
Job38:16 Have you explored the springs of the sea? Or have you walked in the recesses of the deep?
Job38:17 Have the gates of death been revealed to you? Or have you seen the doors of deep darkness?
Job38:18 Have you comprehended the breadth of the earth? Tell Me
Job38:19 Where is the way where light dwells? And as for darkness
Job38:20 That you may conduct it to its home
Job38:21 You must know
Job38:22 Have you entered the treasuries of the snow
Job38:23 Which I have reserved for the time of trouble
Job38:24 By what way is the light distributed
Job38:25 Who has prepared a channel for the torrents of rain
Job38:26 To cause it to rain on the uninhabited land [and] on the desert where no man lives
Job38:27 To satisfy the waste and desolate ground and to cause the tender grass to spring forth?
Job38:28 Has the rain a father? Or who has begotten the drops of dew?
Job38:29 Out of whose womb came the ice? And the hoary frost of heaven
Job38:30 The waters are congealed like stone
Job38:31 Can you bind the chains of [the cluster of stars called] Pleiades
Job38:32 Can you lead forth the signs of the zodiac in their season? Or can you guide [the stars of] the Bear with her young?
Job38:33 Do you know the ordinances of the heavens? Can you establish their rule upon the earth?
Job38:34 Can you lift up your voice to the clouds
Job38:35 Can you send lightnings
Job38:36 Who has put wisdom in the inward parts [or in the dark clouds]? Or who has given understanding to the mind [or to the meteor]?
Job38:37 Who can number the clouds by wisdom? Or who can pour out the [water] bottles of the heavens
Job38:38 When [heat has caused] the dust to run into a mass and the clods to cleave fast together?
Job38:39 Can you [Job] hunt the prey for the lion? Or satisfy the appetite of the young lions
Job38:40 When they couch in their dens or lie in wait in their hiding place?
Job38:41 Who provides for the raven its prey when its young ones cry to God and wander about for lack of food?

Job39:2 Can you number the months that they carry their offspring? Or do you know the time when they are delivered
Job39:3 When they bow themselves
Job39:4 Their young ones become strong

Job39:6 Whose home I have made the wilderness

Job39:12 Will you depend upon him to bring home your seed and gather the grain of your threshing floor? [Who

Job39:14 The ostrich leaves her eggs on the ground and warms them in the dust
Job39:15 Forgetting that a foot may crush them or that the wild beast may trample them.
Job39:16 She is hardened against her young ones
Job39:17 For God has deprived her of wisdom
Job39:18 Yet when she lifts herself up in flight

Job39:20 Was it you [Job] who made him to leap like a locust? The majesty of his [snorting] nostrils is terrible.
Job39:21 He paws in the valley and exults in his strength; he goes out to meet the weapons [of armed men].
Job39:22 He mocks at fear and is not dismayed or terrified; neither does he turn back [in battle] from the sword.
Job39:23 The quiver rattles upon him
Job39:24 [He seems in running to] devour the ground with fierceness and rage; neither can he stand still at the sound of the [war] trumpet.
Job39:25 As often as the trumpet sounds he says
Job39:26 Is it by your wisdom [Job] that the hawk soars and stretches her wings toward the south [as winter approaches]?

Job39:28 On the cliff he dwells and remains securely
Job39:29 From there he spies out the prey; and his eyes see it afar off.
Job39:30 His young ones suck up blood

Job41:1 CAN YOU draw out the leviathan (the crocodile) with a fishhook? Or press down his tongue with a cord?
Job41:2 Can you put a rope into his nose? Or pierce his jaw through with a hook or a spike?
Job41:3 Will he make many supplications to you [begging to be spared]? Will he speak soft words to you [to coax you to treat him kindly]?
Job41:4 Will he make a covenant with you to take him for your servant forever?
Job41:5 Will you play with [the crocodile] as with a bird? Or will you put him on a leash for your maidens?
Job41:6 Will traders bargain over him? Will they divide him up among the merchants?
Job41:7 Can you fill his skin with harpoons? Or his head with fishing spears?
Job41:8 Lay your hand upon him! Remember your battle with him; you will not do [such an ill-advised thing] again!
Job41:9 Behold

Job41:11 Who has first given to Me
Job41:12 I will not keep silence concerning his limbs
Job41:13 Who can strip off [the crocodile's] outer garment? [Who can penetrate his double coat of mail?] Who shall come within his jaws?
Job41:14 Who can open the doors of his [lipless] mouth? His [extended jaws and bare] teeth are terrible round about.
Job41:15 His scales are [the crocodile's] pride
Job41:16 One is so near to another that no air can come between them.
Job41:17 They are joined one to another; they stick together so that they cannot be separated.
Job41:18 His sneezings flash forth light
Job41:19 Out of his mouth go burning torches
Job41:20 Out of his nostrils goes forth smoke
Job41:21 His breath kindles coals
Job41:22 In [the crocodile's] neck abides strength
Job41:23 The folds of his flesh cleave together; they are firm upon him
Job41:24 His heart is as firm as a stone
Job41:25 When [the crocodile] raises himself up
Job41:26 Even if one strikes at him with the sword
Job41:27 He counts iron as straw and bronze as rotten wood.
Job41:28 The arrow cannot make [the crocodile] flee; slingstones are treated by him as stubble.
Job41:29 Clubs [also] are counted as stubble; he laughs at the rushing and the rattling of the javelin.
Job41:30 His underparts are like sharp pieces of broken pottery; he spreads [grooves like] a threshing sledge upon the mire.
Job41:31 He makes the deep boil like a pot; he makes the sea like a [foaming] pot of ointment.
Job41:32 [His swift darting] makes a shining track behind him; one would think the deep to be hoary [with foam].
Job41:33 Upon earth there is not [the crocodile's] equal
Job41:34 He looks all mighty [beasts of prey] in the face [without terror]; he is monarch over all the sons of pride. [And now
Job42:1 THEN JOB said to the Lord

Job42:3 [You said to me] Who is this that darkens and obscures counsel [by words] without knowledge? Therefore [I now see] I have [rashly] uttered what I did not understand

Job42:5 I had heard of You [only] by the hearing of the ear
Job42:6 Therefore I loathe [my words] and abhor myself and repent in dust and ashes.
Job42:7 After the Lord had spoken the previous words to Job
Job42:8 Now therefore take seven bullocks and seven rams and go to My servant Job and offer up for yourselves a burnt offering; and My servant Job shall pray for you
Job42:9 So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did as the Lord commanded them; and the Lord accepted [Job's prayer].
Job42:10 And the Lord turned the captivity of Job and restored his fortunes
Job42:11 Then there came to him all his brothers and sisters and all who had known him before
Job42:12 And the Lord blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning; for he had 14
Job42:13 He had also seven sons and three daughters.
Job42:14 And he called the name of the first Jemimah
Job42:15 And in all the land there were no women so fair as the daughters of Job
Job42:16 After this
Job42:17 So Job died

Psalms18:6 In my distress [when seemingly closed in] I called upon the Lord and cried to my God; He heard my voice out of His temple (heavenly dwelling place)
Psalms18:7 Then the earth quaked and rocked
Psalms18:8 There went up smoke from His nostrils; and lightning out of His mouth devoured; coals were kindled by it.
Psalms18:9 He bowed the heavens also and came down; and thick darkness was under His feet.
Psalms18:10 And He rode upon a cherub [a storm] and flew [swiftly]; yes
Psalms18:11 He made darkness His secret hiding place; as His pavilion (His canopy) round about Him were dark waters and thick clouds of the skies.
Psalms18:12 Out of the brightness before Him there broke forth through His thick clouds hailstones and coals of fire.
Psalms18:13 The Lord also thundered from the heavens
Psalms18:14 And He sent out His arrows and scattered them; and He flashed forth lightnings and put them to rout.
Psalms18:15 Then the beds of the sea appeared and the foundations of the world were laid bare at Your rebuke
Psalms18:16 He reached from on high
Psalms18:17 He delivered me from my strong enemy and from those who hated and abhorred me
Psalms18:18 They confronted and came upon me in the day of my calamity
Psalms18:19 He brought me forth also into a large place; He was delivering me because He was pleased with me and delighted in me.
Psalms18:20 The Lord rewarded me according to my righteousness (my conscious integrity and sincerity with Him); according to the cleanness of my hands has He recompensed me.
Psalms18:21 For I have kept the ways of the Lord and have not wickedly departed from my God.
Psalms18:22 For all His ordinances were before me
Psalms18:23 I was upright before Him and blameless with Him
Psalms18:24 Therefore has the Lord recompensed me according to my righteousness (my uprightness and right standing with Him)

Psalms18:26 With the pure You will show Yourself pure
Psalms18:27 For You deliver an afflicted and humble people but will bring down those with haughty looks.

Psalms18:35 You have also given me the shield of Your salvation
Psalms18:36 You have given plenty of room for my steps under me
Psalms18:37 I pursued my enemies and overtook them; neither did I turn again till they were consumed.
Psalms18:38 I smote them so that they were not able to rise; they fell wounded under my feet.
Psalms18:39 For You have girded me with strength for the battle; You have subdued under me and caused to bow down those who rose up against me.

Proverbs1:8 My son
Proverbs1:9 For they are a [victor's] chaplet (garland) of grace upon your head and chains and pendants [of gold worn by kings] for your neck.

Proverbs1:16 For their feet run to evil
Proverbs1:17 For in vain is the net spread in the sight of any bird!
Proverbs1:18 But [when these men set a trap for others] they are lying in wait for their own blood; they set an ambush for their own lives.
Proverbs1:19 So are the ways of everyone who is greedy of gain; such [greed for plunder] takes away the lives of its possessors. [Prov. 15:27; I Tim. 6:10.]

Proverbs1:27 When your panic comes as a storm and desolation and your calamity comes on as a whirlwind

Proverbs1:30 Would accept none of my counsel
Proverbs1:31 Therefore shall they eat of the fruit of their own way and be satiated with their own devices.
Proverbs1:32 For the backsliding of the simple shall slay them
Proverbs1:33 But whoso hearkens to me [Wisdom] shall dwell securely and in confident trust and shall be quiet
