Ecclesiastes1:1 THE WORDS of the Preacher
Ecclesiastes1:2 Vapor of vapors and futility of futilities
Ecclesiastes1:3 What profit does man have left from all his toil at which he toils under the sun? [Is life worth living?]
Ecclesiastes1:4 One generation goes and another generation comes
Ecclesiastes1:5 The sun also rises and the sun goes down
Ecclesiastes1:6 The wind goes to the south and circles about to the north; it circles and circles about continually
Ecclesiastes1:7 All the rivers run into the sea
Ecclesiastes1:8 All things are weary with toil and all words are feeble; man cannot utter it. The eye is not satisfied with seeing
Ecclesiastes1:9 The thing that has been–it is what will be again
Ecclesiastes1:10 Is there a thing of which it may be said
Ecclesiastes1:11 There is no remembrance of former happenings or men
Ecclesiastes1:12 I
Ecclesiastes1:13 And I applied myself by heart and mind to seek and search out by [human] wisdom all human activity under heaven. It is a miserable business which God has given to the sons of man with which to busy themselves.
Ecclesiastes1:14 I have seen all the works that are done under the sun
Ecclesiastes1:15 What is crooked cannot be made straight
Ecclesiastes1:16 I entered into counsel with my own mind
Ecclesiastes1:17 And I gave my mind to know [practical] wisdom and to discern [the character of] madness and folly [in which men seem to find satisfaction]; I perceived that this also is a searching after wind and a feeding on it. [I Thess. 5:21.]
Ecclesiastes1:18 For in much [human] wisdom is much vexation
Ecclesiastes2:1 I SAID in my mind
Ecclesiastes2:2 I said of laughter
Ecclesiastes2:3 I searched in my mind how to cheer my body with wine–yet at the same time having my mind hold its course and guide me with [human] wisdom–and how to lay hold of folly
Ecclesiastes2:4 I made great works; I built myself houses
Ecclesiastes2:5 I made for myself gardens and orchards and I planted in them all kinds of fruit trees.
Ecclesiastes2:6 I made for myself pools of water from which to water the forest and make the trees bud.
Ecclesiastes2:7 I bought menservants and maidservants and had servants born in my house. Also I had great possessions of herds and flocks
Ecclesiastes2:8 I also gathered for myself silver and gold and the treasure of kings and of the provinces. I got for myself men singers and women singers
Ecclesiastes2:9 So I became great and increased more than all who were before me in Jerusalem. Also my wisdom remained with me and stood by me.
Ecclesiastes2:10 And whatever my eyes desired I kept not from them; I withheld not my heart from any pleasure
Ecclesiastes2:11 Then I looked on all that my hands had done and the labor I had spent in doing it
Ecclesiastes2:12 So I turned to consider [human] wisdom and madness and folly; for what can the man do who succeeds the king? Nothing but what has been done already.
Ecclesiastes2:13 Then I saw that even [human] wisdom [that brings sorrow] is better than [the pleasures of] folly as far as light is better than darkness.
Ecclesiastes2:14 The wise man's eyes are in his head
Ecclesiastes2:15 Then said I in my heart
Ecclesiastes2:16 For of the wise man
Ecclesiastes2:17 So I hated life
Ecclesiastes2:18 And I hated all my labor in which I had toiled under the sun
Ecclesiastes2:19 And who knows whether he will be a wise man or a fool? Yet he will have dominion over all my labor in which I have toiled and in which I have shown myself wise under the sun. This is also vanity (emptiness
Ecclesiastes2:20 So I turned around and gave my heart up to despair over all the labor of my efforts under the sun.
Ecclesiastes2:21 For here is a man whose labor is with wisdom and knowledge and skill; yet to a man who has not toiled for it he must leave it all as his portion. This also is vanity (emptiness
Ecclesiastes2:22 For what has a man left from all his labor and from the striving and vexation of his heart in which he has toiled under the sun?
Ecclesiastes2:23 For all his days are but pain and sorrow
Ecclesiastes2:24 There is nothing better for a man than that he should eat and drink and make himself enjoy good in his labor. Even this
Ecclesiastes2:25 For who can eat or who can have enjoyment any more than I can– apart from Him?
Ecclesiastes2:26 For to the person who pleases Him God gives wisdom and knowledge and joy; but to the sinner He gives the work of gathering and heaping up
Ecclesiastes3:1 TO EVERYTHING there is a season
Ecclesiastes3:2 A time to be born and a time to die
Ecclesiastes3:3 A time to kill and a time to heal
Ecclesiastes3:4 A time to weep and a time to laugh
Ecclesiastes3:5 A time to cast away stones and a time to gather stones together
Ecclesiastes3:6 A time to get and a time to lose
Ecclesiastes3:7 A time to rend and a time to sew
Ecclesiastes3:8 A time to love and a time to hate
Ecclesiastes3:9 What profit remains for the worker from his toil?
Ecclesiastes3:10 I have seen the painful labor and exertion and miserable business which God has given to the sons of men with which to exercise and busy themselves.
Ecclesiastes3:11 He has made everything beautiful in its time. He also has planted eternity in men's hearts and minds [a divinely implanted sense of a purpose working through the ages which nothing under the sun but God alone can satisfy]
Ecclesiastes3:12 I know that there is nothing better for them than to be glad and to get and do good as long as they live;
Ecclesiastes3:13 And also that every man should eat and drink and enjoy the good of all his labor–it is the gift of God.
Ecclesiastes3:14 I know that whatever God does
Ecclesiastes3:15 That which is now already has been
Ecclesiastes3:16 Moreover
Ecclesiastes3:17 I said in my heart
Ecclesiastes3:18 I said in my heart regarding the subject of the sons of men
Ecclesiastes3:19 For that which befalls the sons of men befalls beasts; even [in the end] one thing befalls them both. As the one dies
Ecclesiastes3:20 All go to one place; all are of the dust
Ecclesiastes3:21 Who knows the spirit of man
Ecclesiastes3:22 So I saw that there is nothing better than that a man should rejoice in his own works
Ecclesiastes4:1 THEN I returned and considered all the oppressions that are practiced under the sun: And I beheld the tears of the oppressed
Ecclesiastes4:2 So I praised and thought more fortunate those who have been long dead than the living
Ecclesiastes4:3 But better than them both [I thought] is he who has not yet been born
Ecclesiastes4:4 Then I saw that all painful effort in labor and all skill in work comes from man's rivalry with his neighbor. This is also vanity
Ecclesiastes4:5 The fool folds his hands together and eats his own flesh [destroying himself by indolence].
Ecclesiastes4:6 Better is a handful with quietness than both hands full with painful effort
Ecclesiastes4:7 Then I returned
Ecclesiastes4:8 Here is one alone–no one with him; he neither has child nor brother. Yet there is no end to all his labor
Ecclesiastes4:9 Two are better than one
Ecclesiastes4:10 For if they fall
Ecclesiastes4:11 Again
Ecclesiastes4:12 And though a man might prevail against him who is alone
Ecclesiastes4:13 Better is a poor and wise youth than an old and foolish king who no longer knows how to receive counsel (friendly reproof and warning)–
Ecclesiastes4:14 Even though [the youth] comes out of prison to reign
Ecclesiastes4:15 I saw all the living who walk under the sun with the youth who was to stand up in the king's stead.
Ecclesiastes4:16 There was no end to all the people; he was over all of them. Yet those who come later will not rejoice in him. Surely this also is vanity (emptiness
Ecclesiastes5:1 KEEP YOUR foot [give your mind to what you are doing] when you go [as Jacob to sacred Bethel] to the house of God. For to draw near to hear and obey is better than to give the sacrifice of fools [carelessly
Ecclesiastes5:2 Be not rash with your mouth
Ecclesiastes5:3 For a dream comes with much business and painful effort
Ecclesiastes5:4 When you vow a vow or make a pledge to God
Ecclesiastes5:5 It is better that you should not vow than that you should vow and not pay. [Prov. 20:25; Acts 5:4.]
Ecclesiastes5:6 Do not allow your mouth to cause your body to sin
Ecclesiastes5:7 For in a multitude of dreams there is futility and worthlessness
Ecclesiastes5:8 If you see the oppression of the poor and the violent taking away of justice and righteousness in the state or province
Ecclesiastes5:9 Moreover

Ecclesiastes6:5 Moreover

Ecclesiastes7:19 [True] wisdom is a strength to the wise man more than ten rulers or valiant generals who are in the city. [Ps. 127:1; II Tim. 3:15.]
Ecclesiastes7:20 Surely there is not a righteous man upon earth who does good and never sins. [Isa. 53:6; Rom. 3:23.]

Ecclesiastes7:22 For often your own heart knows that you have likewise cursed others.

Ecclesiastes9:4 [There is no exemption] but he who is joined to all the living has hope–for a living dog is better than a dead lion.
Ecclesiastes9:5 For the living know that they will die
Ecclesiastes9:6 Their love and their hatred and their envy have already perished; neither have they any more a share in anything that is done under the sun.

Isaiah62:3 You shall also be [so beautiful and prosperous as to be thought of as] a crown of glory and honor in the hand of the Lord
Isaiah62:4 You [Judah] shall no more be termed Forsaken
Isaiah62:5 For as a young man marries a virgin [O Jerusalem]
Isaiah62:6 I have set watchmen upon your walls
Isaiah62:7 And give Him no rest until He establishes Jerusalem and makes her a praise in the earth.
Isaiah62:8 The Lord has sworn by His right hand and by His mighty arm: Surely I will not again give your grain as food for your enemies
Isaiah62:9 But they who have gathered it shall eat it and praise the Lord
Isaiah62:10 Go through
Isaiah62:11 Behold
Isaiah62:12 And they shall call them the Holy People
Isaiah63:1 WHO IS this Who comes from Edom
Isaiah63:2 Why is Your apparel splashed with red
Isaiah63:3 I have trodden the winepress alone
Isaiah63:4 For the day of vengeance was in My heart
Isaiah63:5 And I looked
Isaiah63:6 I trod down the peoples in My anger and made them drink of the cup of My wrath until they were intoxicated
Isaiah63:7 I will recount the loving-kindnesses of the Lord and the praiseworthy deeds of the Lord
Isaiah63:8 For He said
Isaiah63:9 In all their affliction He was afflicted
Isaiah63:10 But they rebelled and grieved His Holy Spirit; therefore He turned to become their enemy and Himself fought against them.
Isaiah63:11 Then His people [seriously] remembered the days of old
Isaiah63:12 Who caused His glorious arm to go at the right hand of Moses
Isaiah63:13 Who led them through the depths
Isaiah63:14 Like the cattle that go down into the valley [to find better pasturage
Isaiah63:15 Look down from heaven and see from the dwelling place of Your holiness and Your glory. Where are Your zeal and Your jealousy and Your mighty acts [which you formerly did for Your people]? Your yearning pity and the [multitude of] compassions of Your heart are restrained and withheld from me.
Isaiah63:16 For [surely] You are our Father
Isaiah63:17 O Lord
Isaiah63:18 Your holy people possessed Your sanctuary but a little while; our adversaries have trodden it down.
Isaiah63:19 We have become [to You] like those over whom You never exercised rule
Isaiah64:1 OH
Isaiah64:2 As when fire kindles the brushwood and the fire causes the waters to boil–to make Your name known to Your adversaries
Isaiah64:3 When You did terrible things which we did not expect
Isaiah64:4 For from of old no one has heard nor perceived by the ear
Isaiah64:5 You meet and spare him who joyfully works righteousness (uprightness and justice)
Isaiah64:6 For we have all become like one who is unclean [ceremonially
Isaiah64:7 And no one calls on Your name and awakens and bestirs himself to take and keep hold of You; for You have hidden Your face from us and have delivered us into the [consuming] power of our iniquities. [Rom. 1:21-24.]
Isaiah64:8 Yet
Isaiah64:9 Do not be exceedingly angry
Isaiah64:10 Your holy cities have become a wilderness; Zion has become a wilderness
Isaiah64:11 Our holy and our beautiful house

Isaiah65:1 I WAS [ready to be] inquired of by those who asked not; I was [ready to be] found by those who sought Me not. I said
Isaiah65:2 I have spread out My hands all the day long to a rebellious people
Isaiah65:3 A people who provoke Me to My face continually
Isaiah65:4 Who sit among the graves [trying to talk with the dead] and lodge among the secret places [or caves where familiar spirits were thought to dwell]; who eat swine's flesh
Isaiah65:5 Who say
Isaiah65:6 Behold
Isaiah65:7 Both your own iniquities and the iniquities of your fathers
Isaiah65:8 Thus says the Lord: As the juice [of the grape] is found in the cluster
Isaiah65:9 And I will bring forth an offspring from Jacob
Isaiah65:10 And [the plain of] Sharon shall be a pasture and fold for flocks
Isaiah65:11 But you who forsake the Lord
Isaiah65:12 I will destine you [says the Lord] for the sword
Isaiah65:13 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Isaiah65:14 Behold
Isaiah65:15 And you will leave your name to My chosen [to those who will use it] for a curse; and the Lord God will slay you
Isaiah65:16 So [it shall be] that he who invokes a blessing on himself in the land shall do so by saying
Isaiah65:17 For behold
Isaiah65:18 But be glad and rejoice forever in that which I create; for behold
Isaiah65:19 And I will rejoice in Jerusalem and be glad in My people; and the sound of weeping will no more be heard in it
Isaiah65:20 There shall no more be in it an infant who lives but a few days
Isaiah65:21 They shall build houses and inhabit them
Isaiah65:22 They shall not build and another inhabit; they shall not plant and another eat [the fruit]. For as the days of a tree
Isaiah65:23 They shall not labor in vain or bring forth [children] for sudden terror or calamity; for they shall be the descendants of the blessed of the Lord
Isaiah65:24 And it shall be that before they call I will answer; and while they are yet speaking I will hear. [Isa. 30:19; 58:9; Matt. 6:8.]
Isaiah65:25 The wolf and the lamb shall feed together
Isaiah66:1 THUS SAYS the Lord: Heaven is My throne
Isaiah66:2 For all these things My hand has made
Isaiah66:3 [The acts of the hypocrite's worship are as abominable to God as if they were offered to idols.] He who kills an ox [then] will be as guilty as if he slew and sacrificed a man; he who sacrifices a lamb or a kid
Isaiah66:4 So I also will choose their delusions and mockings
Isaiah66:5 Hear the word of the Lord
Isaiah66:6 [Hark!] An uproar from the city! A voice from the temple! The voice of the Lord
Isaiah66:7 Before [Zion] travailed
Isaiah66:8 Who has heard of such a thing? Who has seen such things? Shall a land be born in one day? Or shall a nation be brought forth in a moment? For as soon as Zion was in labor
Isaiah66:9 Shall I bring to the [moment of] birth and not cause to bring forth? says the Lord. Shall I Who causes to bring forth shut the womb? says your God.
Isaiah66:10 Rejoice with Jerusalem and be glad for her
Isaiah66:11 That you may nurse and be satisfied from her consoling breasts
Isaiah66:12 For thus says the Lord: Behold
Isaiah66:13 As one whom his mother comforts
Isaiah66:14 When you see this
Isaiah66:15 For behold
Isaiah66:16 For by fire and by His sword will the Lord execute judgment upon all flesh
Isaiah66:17 Those who [attempt to] sanctify themselves and cleanse themselves to enter [and sacrifice to idols] in the gardens
Isaiah66:18 For I know their works and their thoughts. And the time is coming when I will gather all nations and tongues
Isaiah66:19 And I will set up a [miraculous] sign among them
Isaiah66:20 And they shall bring all your brethren from all the nations as an offering to the Lord–upon horses and in chariots and in litters and upon mules and upon camels–to My holy mountain Jerusalem
Isaiah66:21 And I will also take some of them for priests and for Levites
Isaiah66:22 For as the new heavens and the new earth which I make shall remain before Me
Isaiah66:23 And it shall be that from one New Moon to another New Moon and from one Sabbath to another Sabbath
Isaiah66:24 And they shall go forth and gaze upon the dead bodies of the [rebellious] men who have stepped over against Me; for their worm shall not die
Jeremiah1:1 THE WORDS of Jeremiah son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah1:2 To whom the word of the Lord came in the days of Josiah son of Amon king of Judah in the thirteenth year of his reign.
Jeremiah1:3 It came also in the days of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah until the end of the eleventh year of Zedekiah son of Josiah king of Judah
Jeremiah1:4 Then the word of the Lord came to me [Jeremiah]
Jeremiah1:5 Before I formed you in the womb I knew [and] approved of you [as My chosen instrument]
Jeremiah1:6 Then said I
Jeremiah1:7 But the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:8 Be not afraid of them [their faces]
Jeremiah1:9 Then the Lord put forth His hand and touched my mouth. And the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:10 See
Jeremiah1:11 Moreover
Jeremiah1:12 Then said the Lord to me
Jeremiah1:13 And the word of the Lord came to me the second time
Jeremiah1:14 Then the Lord said to me
Jeremiah1:15 For
Jeremiah1:16 And I will utter My judgments against them for all the wickedness of those who have forsaken Me
Jeremiah1:17 But you [Jeremiah]
Jeremiah1:18 For I
Jeremiah1:19 And they shall fight against you
Jeremiah2:1 AND THE word of the Lord came to me [Jeremiah]
Jeremiah2:2 Go and cry in the ears of Jerusalem
Jeremiah2:3 Israel was holiness [something set apart from ordinary purposes
Jeremiah2:4 Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah2:5 Thus says the Lord: What unrighteousness did your fathers find in Me
Jeremiah2:6 Nor did they say
Jeremiah2:7 And I brought you into a plentiful land to enjoy its fruits and good things. But when you entered
Jeremiah2:8 [Even] the priests did not say
Jeremiah2:9 Therefore I will still contend with you [by inflicting further judgments on you]
Jeremiah2:10 For cross over to the coasts of Cyprus [to the west] and see
Jeremiah2:11 Has a nation [ever] changed its gods
Jeremiah2:12 Be astonished and appalled
Jeremiah2:13 For My people have committed two evils: they have forsaken Me
Jeremiah2:14 Is Israel a servant? Is he a homeborn slave? Why has he become a captive and a prey?
Jeremiah2:15 The young lions have roared over him and made their voices heard. And they have made his land a waste; his cities are burned ruins without inhabitant.
Jeremiah2:16 Moreover
Jeremiah2:17 Have you not brought this upon yourself by forsaking the Lord your God when He led you in the way?
Jeremiah2:18 And now what have you to gain by allying yourself with Egypt and going her way
Jeremiah2:19 Your own wickedness shall chasten and correct you
Jeremiah2:20 For long ago [in Egypt] I broke your yoke and burst your bonds [not that you might be free
Jeremiah2:21 Yet I had planted you [O house of Israel] a choice vine
Jeremiah2:22 For though you wash yourself with lye and use much soap
Jeremiah2:23 How can you say
Jeremiah2:24 Or [you have the untamed and reckless nature of] a wild donkey used to the desert
Jeremiah2:25 [Cease from your mad running after idols
Jeremiah2:26 As the thief is brought to shame when he is caught
Jeremiah2:27 [Inasmuch as] they say to a tree
Jeremiah2:28 But where are your gods that you made for yourself? Let them arise if they can save you in the time of your trouble! For [as many as] the number of your cities are your gods
Jeremiah2:29 Why do you complain and remonstrate against My wrath? You all have rebelled and revolted against Me
Jeremiah2:30 In vain have I stricken your children (your people); they received no discipline (no correction). Your own sword devoured your prophets like a destroying lion.
Jeremiah2:31 O generation [that you are]! Behold
Jeremiah2:32 Can a maid forget and neglect [to wear] her ornaments
Jeremiah2:33 How you deck yourself and direct your way to procure [adulterous] love! Because of it even wicked women have learned [indecent] ways from you.
Jeremiah2:34 Also on your skirts is found the lifeblood of the persons of the innocent poor; you did not find them housebreaking
Jeremiah2:35 Yet you keep saying
Jeremiah2:36 Why do you gad or wander about so much to change your way? You shall be put to shame by Egypt as you were put to shame by Assyria.
Jeremiah2:37 From [Egypt] also you will come away with your hands upon your head

Jeremiah10:1 HEAR THE word which the Lord speaks to you
Jeremiah10:2 Thus says the Lord: Learn not the way of the [heathen] nations and be not dismayed at the signs of the heavens
Jeremiah10:3 For the customs and ordinances of the peoples are false
Jeremiah10:4 They deck [the idol] with silver and with gold; they fasten it with nails and with hammers so it will not fall apart or move around.
Jeremiah10:5 [Their idols] are like pillars of turned work [as upright and stationary and immobile as a palm tree]

Jeremiah25:24 All the kings of Arabia and all the kings of the mixed foreign people who dwell in the desert;
Jeremiah25:25 All the kings of Zimri
Jeremiah25:26 All the kings of the north
Jeremiah25:27 Then you shall say to them
Jeremiah25:28 And if they refuse to take the cup from your hand to drink
Jeremiah25:29 For behold
Jeremiah25:30 Therefore prophesy against them all these words and say to them: The Lord shall roar from on high and utter His voice from His holy habitation; He shall roar mightily against His fold and pasture. He shall give a shout like those who tread grapes [in the winepress
Jeremiah25:31 A noise will come even to the ends of the earth
Jeremiah25:32 Thus says the Lord of hosts: Behold
Jeremiah25:33 And those slain by the Lord shall be at that day from one end of the earth even to the other end of the earth. They shall not be lamented or gathered or buried; their [dead bodies] shall be dung upon the ground. [Jer. 8:2; 16:4.]
Jeremiah25:34 Wail
Jeremiah25:35 And the shepherds shall have no way to flee
Jeremiah25:36 A voice! The cry of the shepherds and the wailing of the principal ones of the flock! For the Lord is laying waste and destroying their pasture.
Jeremiah25:37 And the peaceable folds are devastated and brought to silence because of the fierce anger of the Lord.
Jeremiah25:38 He has left His shelter like the lion; for their land has become a waste and an astonishment because of the fierceness of the oppressor and because of [the Lord's] fierce anger.
Jeremiah26:1 IN THE beginning of the reign of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah came this word from the Lord:
Jeremiah26:2 Thus says the Lord: Stand in the court of the Lord's house [Jeremiah] and speak to all [the people of] the cities of Judah who come to worship in the Lord's house all the words that I command you to speak to them; subtract not a word.
Jeremiah26:3 It may be that they will listen and turn every man from his evil way
Jeremiah26:4 And you will say to them
Jeremiah26:5 And to hear and obey the words of My servants the prophets
Jeremiah26:6 Then will I make this house [the temple] like Shiloh [the home of the Tent of Meeting
Jeremiah26:7 And the priests and the [false] prophets and all the people heard Jeremiah speaking these words in the house of the Lord.
Jeremiah26:8 Now when Jeremiah had finished speaking all that the Lord had commanded him to speak to all the people
Jeremiah26:9 Why have you prophesied in the name of the Lord
Jeremiah26:10 When the princes of Judah heard these things
Jeremiah26:11 Then the priests and the prophets said to the princes and to all the people
Jeremiah26:12 Then Jeremiah said to all the princes and to all the people: The Lord sent me to prophesy against this house and against this city all the words that you have heard.
Jeremiah26:13 Therefore now amend your ways and your doings and obey the voice of the Lord your God; then the Lord will relent and reverse the decision concerning the evil which He has pronounced against you.
Jeremiah26:14 As for me
Jeremiah26:15 But know for certain that if you put me to death
Jeremiah26:16 Then said the princes and all the people to the priests and to the prophets: This man is not deserving of death
Jeremiah26:17 Then certain of the elders of the land arose and said to all the assembly of the people
Jeremiah26:18 Micah of Moresheth prophesied in the days of Hezekiah king of Judah and said to all the people of Judah
Jeremiah26:19 Did Hezekiah king of Judah and all Judah put [Micah] to death? Did he not [reverently] fear the Lord and entreat the Lord? And did not the Lord relent and reverse the decision concerning the evil which He had pronounced against them? But [here] we are thinking of committing what will be a great evil against ourselves.
Jeremiah26:20 And there was also a man who prophesied in the name of the Lord
Jeremiah26:21 And when Jehoiakim the king
Jeremiah26:22 And Jehoiakim the king sent men into Egypt
Jeremiah26:23 And they fetched Uriah from Egypt and brought him to Jehoiakim the king
Jeremiah26:24 But the hand of Ahikam son of Shaphan was with Jeremiah
Jeremiah27:1 IN THE beginning of the reign of Zedekiah son of Josiah king of Judah
Jeremiah27:2 Thus says the Lord to me: Make for yourself thongs and yoke bars and put them on your neck
Jeremiah27:3 And send them to the king of Edom
Jeremiah27:4 And command them to say to their masters
Jeremiah27:5 I have made the earth
Jeremiah27:6 And now I have given all these lands into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon
Jeremiah27:7 And all nations shall serve him and his son and his grandson until the [God-appointed] time [of punishment] of his own land comes; and then many nations and great kings shall make him their slave.
Jeremiah27:8 But any nation or kingdom that will not serve this same Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and put its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon
Jeremiah27:9 So do not listen to your [false] prophets
Jeremiah27:10 For they prophesy a lie to you which will cause you to be removed far from your land; and I will drive you out
Jeremiah27:11 But any nation that brings its neck under the yoke of the king of Babylon and serves him
Jeremiah27:12 I spoke also to Zedekiah king of Judah in the same way: Bring your necks under the yoke of the king of Babylon
Jeremiah27:13 Why will you and your people die by the sword
Jeremiah27:14 Do not listen to and believe the words of the [false] prophets who are saying to you
Jeremiah27:15 For I have not sent them
Jeremiah27:16 Also I said to the priests and to all these people
Jeremiah27:17 Do not listen to them or heed them; serve the king of Babylon
Jeremiah27:18 But if they are true prophets and if the word of the Lord is really spoken by them
Jeremiah27:19 For thus says the Lord of hosts concerning the [bronze] pillars [each twenty-seven feet high]
Jeremiah27:20 Which Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon did not take when he carried into exile from Jerusalem to Babylon Jeconiah [also called Coniah and Jehoiachin] son of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Jeremiah27:21 Yes
Jeremiah27:22 They will be carried to Babylon and there will they be until the day that I visit them [with My favor]
Jeremiah28:1 IN THAT same year
Jeremiah28:2 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah28:3 Within two [full] years will I bring back into this place all the vessels of the Lord's house that Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon took away from this place and carried to Babylon.
Jeremiah28:4 And I will also bring back to this place Jeconiah [also called Coniah and Jehoiachin] son of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Jeremiah28:5 Then the prophet Jeremiah spoke to the prophet Hananiah in the presence of the priests and all the people who stood in the house of the Lord.
Jeremiah28:6 The prophet Jeremiah said
Jeremiah28:7 Nevertheless
Jeremiah28:8 The prophets who were before me and before you from of old prophesied against many countries and against great kingdoms
Jeremiah28:9 But as for the prophet who [on the contrary] prophesies of peace
Jeremiah28:10 Then Hananiah the prophet took the yoke bar off the prophet Jeremiah's neck and smashed it.
Jeremiah28:11 And Hananiah said in the presence of all the people
Jeremiah28:12 The word of the Lord came to Jeremiah the prophet [some time] after Hananiah the prophet had broken the yoke bar from the neck of the prophet Jeremiah:
Jeremiah28:13 Go
Jeremiah28:14 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah28:15 Then said the prophet Jeremiah to Hananiah the prophet
Jeremiah28:16 Therefore thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah28:17 So Hananiah the prophet died [two months later]
Jeremiah29:1 NOW THESE are the words of the letter that Jeremiah the prophet sent from Jerusalem to the rest of the elders in exile and to the priests
Jeremiah29:2 This was after King Jeconiah [also called Coniah and Jehoiachin] and the queen mother
Jeremiah29:3 [The letter was sent] by the hand of Elasah son of Shaphan and Gemariah son of Hilkiah
Jeremiah29:4 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:5 Build yourselves houses and dwell in them; plant gardens and eat the fruit of them.
Jeremiah29:6 Take wives and have sons and daughters; take wives for your sons and give your daughters in marriage
Jeremiah29:7 And seek (inquire for
Jeremiah29:8 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:9 For they prophesy falsely to you in My name. I have not sent them
Jeremiah29:10 For thus says the Lord
Jeremiah29:11 For I know the thoughts and plans that I have for you
Jeremiah29:12 Then you will call upon Me
Jeremiah29:13 Then you will seek Me
Jeremiah29:14 I will be found by you
Jeremiah29:15 [But as for those still in Jerusalem] because you have said
Jeremiah29:16 Thus says the Lord concerning the king who sits upon the throne of David and concerning all the people who dwell in this city
Jeremiah29:17 Thus says the Lord of hosts: Behold
Jeremiah29:18 And I will pursue them with the sword
Jeremiah29:19 Because they have not listened to and heeded My words
Jeremiah29:20 Hear therefore the word of the Lord
Jeremiah29:21 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:22 And because of them
Jeremiah29:23 Because they have committed folly in Israel and have committed adultery with their neighbors' wives and have spoken words in My name falsely
Jeremiah29:24 Also you shall say this concerning and to Shemaiah of Nehelam [among the exiles in Babylon]:
Jeremiah29:25 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah29:26 The Lord has made you [Zephaniah] priest instead of Jehoiada the [deputy] priest
Jeremiah29:27 Now therefore [continued the letter from Shemaiah in Babylon to Zephaniah in Jerusalem]
Jeremiah29:28 For he has sent to us in Babylon
Jeremiah29:29 And Zephaniah the priest read this letter in the hearing of Jeremiah the prophet.
Jeremiah29:30 Then came the word of the Lord to Jeremiah:
Jeremiah29:31 Send [this message] to all those in captivity
Jeremiah29:32 Therefore thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah30:1 THE WORD that came to Jeremiah from the Lord:
Jeremiah30:2 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah30:3 For
Jeremiah30:4 And these are the words the Lord spoke concerning Israel and Judah:
Jeremiah30:5 Thus says the Lord: We have heard a voice of trembling and panic–of terror
Jeremiah30:6 Ask now and see whether a man can give birth to a child? Why then do I see every man with his hands on his loins like a woman in labor? Why are all faces turned pale?
Jeremiah30:7 Alas! for that day will be great
Jeremiah30:8 For it will come to pass in that day
Jeremiah30:9 But they will serve the Lord their God and David's [descendant] their King
Jeremiah30:10 Therefore fear not
Jeremiah30:11 For I am with you
Jeremiah30:12 For thus says the Lord: Your hurt is incurable and your wound is grievous.
Jeremiah30:13 There is none to plead your cause; for [the pressing together of] your wound you have no healing [device]
Jeremiah30:14 All your lovers (allies) have forgotten you; they neither seek
Jeremiah30:15 Why do you cry out because of your hurt [the natural result of your sins]? Your pain is deadly (incurable). Because of the greatness of your perversity and guilt
Jeremiah30:16 Therefore all who devour you will be devoured; and all your adversaries
Jeremiah30:17 For I will restore health to you
Jeremiah30:18 Thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah30:19 Out of them [city and palace] will come songs of thanksgiving and the voices of those who make merry. And I will multiply them
Jeremiah30:20 Their children too shall be as in former times
Jeremiah30:21 And their prince will be one of them
Jeremiah30:22 Then you will be My people
Jeremiah30:23 Behold
Jeremiah30:24 The fierce anger and indignation of the Lord shall not turn back until He has executed and accomplished the thoughts and intents of His mind and heart. In the latter days you shall understand this.
Jeremiah31:1 AT THAT time
Jeremiah31:2 Thus says the Lord: The people who survived the sword found favor in the wilderness [place of exile]–when Israel sought to find rest.
Jeremiah31:3 The Lord appeared from of old to me [Israel]
Jeremiah31:4 Again I will build you and you will be built
Jeremiah31:5 Again you shall plant vineyards upon the mountains of Samaria; the planters shall plant and make the fruit common and enjoy it [undisturbed].
Jeremiah31:6 For there shall be a day when the watchmen on the hills of Ephraim shall cry out
Jeremiah31:7 For thus says the Lord: Sing aloud with gladness for Jacob
Jeremiah31:8 Behold
Jeremiah31:9 They will come with weeping [in penitence and for joy]
Jeremiah31:10 Hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah31:11 For the Lord has ransomed Jacob and has redeemed him from the hand of him who was too strong for him.
Jeremiah31:12 They shall come and sing aloud on the height of Zion and shall flow together and be radiant with joy over the goodness of the Lord–for the corn
Jeremiah31:13 Then will the maidens rejoice in the dance
Jeremiah31:14 I will satisfy fully the life of the priests with abundance [of offerings shared with them]
Jeremiah31:15 Thus says the Lord: A voice is heard in Ramah
Jeremiah31:16 Thus says the Lord: Restrain your voice from weeping and your eyes from tears
Jeremiah31:17 And there is hope for your future
Jeremiah31:18 I have surely heard Ephraim [Israel] moaning thus: You have chastised me
Jeremiah31:19 Surely after I [Ephraim] was turned [from You]
Jeremiah31:20 Is Ephraim My dear son? Is he a darling child and beloved? For as often as I speak against him
Jeremiah31:21 Set up for yourselves highway markers [back to Canaan]
Jeremiah31:22 How long will you waver and hesitate [to return]
Jeremiah31:23 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah31:24 And [the people of] Judah and all its cities shall dwell there together–[nomad] farmers and those who wander about with their flocks.
Jeremiah31:25 For I will [fully] satisfy the weary soul
Jeremiah31:26 Thereupon I [Jeremiah] awoke and looked
Jeremiah31:27 Behold
Jeremiah31:28 And it will be that as I have watched over them to pluck up and to break down
Jeremiah31:29 In those days they shall say no more
Jeremiah31:30 But everyone shall die for his own iniquity [only]; every man who eats sour grapes–his [own] teeth shall be set on edge.
Jeremiah31:31 Behold
Jeremiah31:32 Not according to the covenant which I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to bring them out of the land of Egypt
Jeremiah31:33 But this is the covenant which I will make with the house of Israel: After those days
Jeremiah31:34 And they will no more teach each man his neighbor and each man his brother
Jeremiah31:35 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah31:36 If these ordinances [of fixed order] depart from before Me
Jeremiah31:37 Thus says the Lord: If the heavens above can be measured and the foundations of the earth searched out beneath
Jeremiah31:38 Behold
Jeremiah31:39 And the measuring line shall go out farther straight onward to the hill Gareb and shall then turn to Goah [exact location unknown].
Jeremiah31:40 And the whole valley [Hinnom] of the dead bodies and [the hill] of the ashes [long dumped there from the temple sacrifices]
Jeremiah32:1 THE WORD that came to Jeremiah from the Lord in the tenth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah32:2 For the king of Babylon's army was then besieging Jerusalem
Jeremiah32:3 For Zedekiah king of Judah had locked him up
Jeremiah32:4 And Zedekiah king of Judah shall not escape out of the hands of the Chaldeans but shall surely be delivered into the hand of the king of Babylon
Jeremiah32:5 And he shall lead Zedekiah to Babylon
Jeremiah32:6 And Jeremiah said
Jeremiah32:7 Behold
Jeremiah32:8 So Hanamel my uncle's son came to me in the court of the guard in accordance with the word of the Lord
Jeremiah32:9 And I bought the field that was in Anathoth from Hanamel my uncle's son and weighed out for him the money–seventeen shekels of silver.
Jeremiah32:10 And I signed the deed and sealed it
Jeremiah32:11 So I took the deed of the purchase–both that which was sealed
Jeremiah32:12 And I gave the purchase deed to Baruch son of Neriah
Jeremiah32:13 And I charged Baruch before them
Jeremiah32:14 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah32:15 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah32:16 Now when I had delivered the purchase deed to Baruch son of Neriah
Jeremiah32:17 Alas
Jeremiah32:18 You Who show loving-kindness to thousands but recompense the iniquity of the fathers into the bosoms of their children after them. The great
Jeremiah32:19 Great [are You] in counsel and mighty in deeds
Jeremiah32:20 Who wrought signs and wonders in the land of Egypt
Jeremiah32:21 And You brought forth Your people Israel out of the land of Egypt with signs and wonders
Jeremiah32:22 And You gave them this land which You swore to their fathers to give them
Jeremiah32:23 And they entered and took possession of it
Jeremiah32:24 See the siege mounds [of earth which the foe has heaped against the walls]; they have come up to the city to take it. And the city is given into the hand of the Chaldeans who fight against it
Jeremiah32:25 Yet
Jeremiah32:26 Then came the word of the Lord to Jeremiah
Jeremiah32:27 Behold
Jeremiah32:28 Therefore thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah32:29 And the Chaldeans who are fighting against this city shall come in and set this city on fire and burn it
Jeremiah32:30 For the children of Israel and the children of Judah have done only evil before Me from their youth; for the children of Israel have only provoked Me to anger with the work of their hands [the idols]
Jeremiah32:31 For this city has been to Me a [such a] provocation of My anger and My wrath from the day that they [finished] building it [in the time of Solomon
Jeremiah32:32 Because of all the evil of the children of Israel and of the children of Judah which they have done to provoke Me to anger–they
Jeremiah32:33 And they have turned their backs to Me and not their faces; though I taught them persistently
Jeremiah32:34 But they set their abominations [of idol worship] in the house which is called by My Name to defile it.
Jeremiah32:35 And they built the high places [for worship] of Baal in the Valley of Ben-hinnom [son of Hinnom] to cause their sons and their daughters to pass through the fire [in worship also of and] to Molech–which I did not command them
Jeremiah32:36 And now therefore thus says the Lord
Jeremiah32:37 Behold
Jeremiah32:38 And they will be My people
Jeremiah32:39 And I will give them one heart and one way
Jeremiah32:40 And I will make an everlasting covenant with them: I will not turn away from following them to do them good
Jeremiah32:41 Yes
Jeremiah32:42 For thus says the Lord: As I have brought all this great evil upon this people
Jeremiah32:43 And fields shall be bought in this land of which you say
Jeremiah32:44 Men shall buy fields for money and shall sign deeds
Jeremiah33:1 MOREOVER
Jeremiah33:2 Thus says the Lord Who made [the earth]
Jeremiah33:3 Call to Me and I will answer you and show you great and mighty things
Jeremiah33:4 For thus says the Lord
Jeremiah33:5 They [the besieged Jews] are coming in to fight against the Chaldeans
Jeremiah33:6 Behold
Jeremiah33:7 And I will cause the captivity of Judah and the captivity of Israel to be reversed and will rebuild them as they were at first.
Jeremiah33:8 And I will cleanse them from all the guilt and iniquity by which they have sinned against Me
Jeremiah33:9 And [Jerusalem] shall be to Me a name of joy
Jeremiah33:10 Thus says the Lord: Yet again there shall be heard in this place of which you say
Jeremiah33:11 [There shall be heard again] the voice of joy and the voice of gladness
Jeremiah33:12 Thus says the Lord of hosts: In this place which is desolate
Jeremiah33:13 In the cities of the hill country
Jeremiah33:14 Behold
Jeremiah33:15 In those days and at that time will I cause a righteous Branch [the Messiah] to grow up to David; and He shall execute justice and righteousness in the land. [Isa. 4:2; Jer. 23:5; Zech. 3:8; 6:12.]
Jeremiah33:16 In those days Judah shall be saved and Jerusalem shall dwell safely. And this is the name by which it will be called
Jeremiah33:17 For thus says the Lord: David shall never fail [to have] a man [descendant] to sit on the throne of the house of Israel
Jeremiah33:18 Nor shall the Levitical priests fail [to have] a man [descendant] to offer burnt offerings before Me and to burn cereal offerings and to make sacrifices continually (all day long).
Jeremiah33:19 And the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah
Jeremiah33:20 Thus says the Lord: If you can break My covenant with the day
Jeremiah33:21 Then can also My covenant be broken with David My servant
Jeremiah33:22 As the host of [the stars of] the heavens cannot be numbered nor the sand of the sea be measured
Jeremiah33:23 Moreover
Jeremiah33:24 Have you not noticed that these people [the Jews] are saying
Jeremiah33:25 Thus says the Lord: If My covenant with day and night does not stand
Jeremiah33:26 Then will I also cast away the descendants of Jacob and David My servant and will not choose one of his offspring to be ruler over the descendants of Abraham
Jeremiah34:1 THE WORD that came to Jeremiah from the Lord when Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and all his army and all the kingdoms of the earth under his dominion and all the people were fighting against Jerusalem and all of its cities:
Jeremiah34:2 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah34:3 And you will not escape out of his hand but will surely be taken and delivered into his hand; you will see the king of Babylon eye to eye
Jeremiah34:4 Yet hear the word of the Lord
Jeremiah34:5 But you shall die in peace. And as with the burnings of [spices and perfumes on wood that were granted as suitable for and in honor of] your fathers
Jeremiah34:6 Then Jeremiah the prophet spoke all these words to Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah34:7 When the army of the king of Babylon was fighting against Jerusalem and against all the cities of Judah that were left
Jeremiah34:8 [This is] the word that came to Jeremiah from the Lord after King Zedekiah had made a covenant with all the people who were at Jerusalem to proclaim liberty to them:
Jeremiah34:9 Every man should let his Hebrew slaves
Jeremiah34:10 And all the princes and all the people obeyed
Jeremiah34:11 But afterward they turned around and caused the servants and the handmaids whom they had let go free to return [to their former masters] and brought them into subjection for servants and for handmaids.
Jeremiah34:12 Therefore the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah from the Lord
Jeremiah34:13 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah34:14 At the end of seven years you shall let every man his brother who is a Hebrew go free who has sold himself or has been sold to you and has served you six years; but your fathers did not listen to and obey Me or incline their ear [submitting and consenting to Me]. [Deut. 15:12.]
Jeremiah34:15 And you recently turned around and repented
Jeremiah34:16 But then you turned around and defiled My name; each of you caused to return to you your servants
Jeremiah34:17 Therefore thus says the Lord: You have not listened to Me and obeyed Me in proclaiming liberty each one to his brother and neighbor. Behold
Jeremiah34:18 And the men who have transgressed My covenant
Jeremiah34:19 The princes of Judah
Jeremiah34:20 I will give them into the hands of their enemies and into the hands of those who seek their life. And their dead bodies will be food for the birds of the heavens and the beasts of the earth.
Jeremiah34:21 And Zedekiah king of Judah and his princes will I give into the hands of their enemies and into the hands of those who seek their life
Jeremiah34:22 Behold
Jeremiah35:1 THE WORD that came to Jeremiah from the Lord in the days of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah:
Jeremiah35:2 Go to the house of the Rechabites and speak to them and bring them into the house of the Lord
Jeremiah35:3 So I took Jaazaniah son of Jeremiah
Jeremiah35:4 And I brought them into the house of the Lord
Jeremiah35:5 And I set before the sons of the house of the Rechabites pitchers full of wine
Jeremiah35:6 But they said
Jeremiah35:7 Neither shall you build a house or sow seed or plant a vineyard or have them; but you shall dwell all your days in tents
Jeremiah35:8 And we have obeyed the voice of Jonadab son of Rechab
Jeremiah35:9 And not to build ourselves houses to live in; nor do we have vineyard or field or seed.
Jeremiah35:10 But we have dwelt in tents and have obeyed and done according to all that Jonadab our ancestor commanded us.
Jeremiah35:11 But when Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon came up against the land
Jeremiah35:12 Then came the word of the Lord to Jeremiah:
Jeremiah35:13 Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah35:14 The command which Jonadab son of Rechab gave to his sons not to drink wine
Jeremiah35:15 I have sent also to you all My servants the prophets earnestly and persistently
Jeremiah35:16 Since the sons of Jonadab son of Rechab have fulfilled and established the command of their father which he commanded them
Jeremiah35:17 Therefore thus says the Lord God of hosts
Jeremiah35:18 And Jeremiah said to the house of the Rechabites
Jeremiah35:19 Therefore thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah36:1 IN THE fourth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah
Jeremiah36:2 Take a scroll [of parchment] for a book and write on it all the words I have spoken to you against Israel and Judah and all the nations from the day I spoke to you in the days of [King] Josiah until this day.
Jeremiah36:3 It may be that the house of Judah will hear all the evil which I purpose to do to them
Jeremiah36:4 Then Jeremiah called Baruch son of Neriah
Jeremiah36:5 And Jeremiah commanded Baruch
Jeremiah36:6 Therefore you go
Jeremiah36:7 It may be that they will make their supplication [for mercy] before the Lord
Jeremiah36:8 And Baruch son of Neriah did according to all that Jeremiah the prophet commanded him
Jeremiah36:9 And in the fifth year of Jehoiakim son of Josiah king of Judah
Jeremiah36:10 Then Baruch read in the hearing of all the people the words of Jeremiah from the scroll of the book in the house of the Lord
Jeremiah36:11 When Micaiah son of Gemariah
Jeremiah36:12 He went down to the king's house into the scribe's chamber
Jeremiah36:13 Then Micaiah declared to them all the words that he had heard when Baruch read the book in the hearing of the people.
Jeremiah36:14 Therefore all the princes sent Jehudi son of Nethaniah
Jeremiah36:15 And they said to him
Jeremiah36:16 Now when they had heard all the words
Jeremiah36:17 And they asked Baruch
Jeremiah36:18 Then Baruch answered them
Jeremiah36:19 Then the princes said to Baruch
Jeremiah36:20 Then they went into the court to the king
Jeremiah36:21 So the king sent Jehudi to get the scroll
Jeremiah36:22 Now it was the ninth month
Jeremiah36:23 And [each time] when Jehudi had read three or four columns [of the scroll]
Jeremiah36:24 Yet they were not afraid
Jeremiah36:25 Even though Elnathan and Delaiah and Gemariah tried to persuade the king not to burn the scroll
Jeremiah36:26 And the king commanded Jerahmeel the king's son and Seraiah son of Azriel and Shelemiah son of Abdeel to seize Baruch the scribe and Jeremiah the prophet
Jeremiah36:27 Now the word of the Lord came to Jeremiah after the king had burned the scroll with the words which Baruch wrote at the dictation of Jeremiah
Jeremiah36:28 Take another scroll and write on it all the former words that were on the first scroll
Jeremiah36:29 And concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah you shall say
Jeremiah36:30 Therefore thus says the Lord concerning Jehoiakim king of Judah: He shall have no [heir] to sit upon the throne of David
Jeremiah36:31 And I will punish him and his offspring and his servants for their iniquity; and I will bring upon them and the inhabitants of Jerusalem and the men of Judah all the evil that I have pronounced against them–but they would not hear.
Jeremiah36:32 Then Jeremiah took another scroll and gave it to Baruch the scribe
Jeremiah37:1 AND ZEDEKIAH son of Josiah
Jeremiah37:2 But neither he nor his servants nor the people of the land listened to and obeyed the words of the Lord which He spoke through the prophet Jeremiah.
Jeremiah37:3 Zedekiah the king sent Jehucal son of Shelemiah with Zephaniah son of Maaseiah
Jeremiah37:4 Now Jeremiah was coming in and going out among the people
Jeremiah37:5 And Pharaoh's army had come forth out of Egypt
Jeremiah37:6 Then came the word of the Lord to the prophet Jeremiah:
Jeremiah37:7 Thus says the Lord
Jeremiah37:8 And the Chaldeans shall come again and fight against this city
Jeremiah37:9 Thus says the Lord: Do not deceive yourselves
Jeremiah37:10 For though you should defeat the whole army of the Chaldeans who fight against you

Jeremiah37:14 Then said Jeremiah

Jeremiah37:16 When Jeremiah had come into the cells in the dungeon and had remained there many days
Jeremiah37:17 Zedekiah the king sent and brought him out; and the king asked him secretly in his house
Jeremiah37:18 Moreover

Jeremiah37:20 Therefore hear now

Jeremiah38:4 Therefore the princes said to the king
Jeremiah38:5 Then Zedekiah the king said

Jeremiah38:7 Now when Ebed-melech the Ethiopian [a Cushite]
Jeremiah38:8 Ebed-melech went out of the king's house and spoke to the king
Jeremiah38:9 My lord the king
Jeremiah38:10 Then the king commanded Ebed-melech the Ethiopian

Jeremiah38:12 And Ebed-melech the Ethiopian said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:13 So they drew up Jeremiah with the ropes and took him up out of the dungeon or cistern pit; and Jeremiah remained in the court of the guard.
Jeremiah38:14 Then Zedekiah the king sent and brought Jeremiah the prophet to him into the third entrance that is in the house of the Lord. And the king said to Jeremiah
Jeremiah38:15 Then Jeremiah said to Zedekiah

Jeremiah38:19 And Zedekiah the king said to Jeremiah

Jeremiah38:25 But if the princes hear that I have talked with you
Jeremiah38:26 Then you shall say to them
Jeremiah38:27 Then came all the princes to Jeremiah and asked him [just what King Zedekiah had anticipated they would ask]
Jeremiah38:28 So Jeremiah remained in the court of the guard until the day that Jerusalem was taken [by the Chaldeans].
Jeremiah39:1 IN THE ninth year of Zedekiah king of Judah
Jeremiah39:2 And in the eleventh year of Zedekiah

Jeremiah39:4 And when Zedekiah king of Judah and all the men of war saw them
Jeremiah39:5 But the Chaldean army pursued them and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho. And when they had taken him
Jeremiah39:6 Then the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah at Riblah before his eyes; also the king of Babylon slew all the nobles of Judah.
Jeremiah39:7 Moreover
Jeremiah39:8 And the Chaldeans burned the king's house and the houses of the people and broke down the walls of Jerusalem.

Jeremiah39:10 But Nebuzaradan the [Babylonian] captain of the guard left in the land of Judah some of the poor of the people who had nothing

Jeremiah39:12 Take him and look after him well; do him no harm but deal with him as he may ask of you.

Jeremiah41:1 NOW IN the seventh month [of that year] Ishmael son of Nethaniah

Jeremiah41:3 Ishmael [manipulated by the Ammonites] also slew all the Jews who were with Gedaliah at Mizpah
Jeremiah41:4 And the second day after the slaying of Gedaliah

Jeremiah41:6 And Ishmael son of Nethaniah went out from Mizpah to meet them

Ishmael son of Nethaniah filled it with those who were slain.

Jeremiah41:15 But Ishmael son of Nethaniah escaped from Johanan with eight men and went to the Ammonites.
Jeremiah41:16 Then Johanan son of Kareah and all the captains of the forces that were with him took from Mizpah all the remainder of the people whom he had recovered from Ishmael son of Nethaniah after he had slain Gedaliah son of Ahikam: [they were] the soldiers
Jeremiah41:17 And they departed and stayed at the lodging place of Chimham

Jeremiah48:1 CONCERNING MOAB: Thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah48:2 The glory of Moab is no more; in Heshbon [a border town between Reuben and Gad

Jeremiah48:4 Moab is destroyed; her little ones have caused a cry to be heard [as far as Zoar].
Jeremiah48:5 For the ascent of Luhith will be climbed [by successive bands of fugitives] with continual weeping; for on the descent of Horonaim they have heard the distress of the cry of destruction.
Jeremiah48:6 Flee! Save your lives! But they shall be like a destitute and forsaken person in the wilderness.
Jeremiah48:7 For because you have trusted in your works [your bungling idol images] and in your treasures [instead of in God]

Jeremiah48:22 And upon Dibon and Nebo and Beth-diblathaim
Jeremiah48:23 And upon Kiriathaim and Beth-gamul and Beth-meon
Jeremiah48:24 And upon Kerioth and Bozrah–and all the cities of the land of Moab

Jeremiah48:26 Make him drunk

Jeremiah48:28 O you inhabitants of Moab

Jeremiah48:34 From the cry of Heshbon even to Elealeh even to Jahaz have they uttered their voice
Jeremiah48:35 Moreover
Jeremiah48:36 Therefore My heart moans and sighs for Moab like flutes
Jeremiah48:37 For every head is shaven bald and every beard cut off: upon all the hands are cuts (slashes) and upon the loins is sackcloth [all to express mourning]. [Isa. 15:2
Jeremiah48:38 On all the housetops of Moab and in its streets there is lamentation everywhere
Jeremiah48:39 How it is broken down! How they wail! How Moab has turned his back in shame! So Moab has become [an object of] a derision and a [horrifying] terror to all who are round about him.
Jeremiah48:40 For thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah48:41 Kerioth [and the cities] shall be taken and the strongholds seized; and the hearts of the mighty warriors of Moab in that day shall be as the heart of a woman in her pangs [in childbirth].

Jeremiah48:46 Woe to you
