Jeremiah50:1 THE WORD that the Lord spoke concerning and against Babylon and concerning and against the land of the Chaldeans through Jeremiah the prophet: [Isa. 13:1-14:23; 47; Hab. 1
Jeremiah50:2 Declare it among the nations and publish it and set up a signal [to spread the news]–publish and conceal it not; say
Jeremiah50:3 For out of the north there has come up a nation [Media] against her which will make her land desolate
Jeremiah50:4 In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:5 They shall ask the way to Zion
Jeremiah50:6 My people have been lost sheep; their shepherds have led them astray [to favorite places of idolatry] on mountains [that seduce]. They have gone from [one sin to another] mountain to hill; they have forgotten their [own] resting-place. [Isa. 53:6; I Pet. 2:25.]
Jeremiah50:7 All who found them devoured them; and their adversaries said
Jeremiah50:8 Flee out of the midst of Babylon
Jeremiah50:9 For behold
Jeremiah50:10 And Chaldea shall become plunder; all who plunder her shall be satisfied
Jeremiah50:11 Though you are glad
Jeremiah50:12 Your mother [Babylon] shall be put to great shame; she who bore you shall blush and be disgraced. Behold
Jeremiah50:13 Because of the wrath of the Lord she shall not be inhabited but shall be wholly desolate; everyone who goes by Babylon shall be appalled and hiss and mock at all her wounds and plagues.
Jeremiah50:14 Set yourselves in array against Babylon round about
Jeremiah50:15 Raise the battle cry against her round about! She gives her hand [in agreement] and surrenders; her supports and battlements fall
Jeremiah50:16 Exterminate the sower from Babylon
Jeremiah50:17 Israel is a hunted and scattered sheep [driven hither and thither and preyed upon by savage beasts]; the lions have chased him. First the king of Assyria devoured him
Jeremiah50:18 Therefore thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah50:19 And I will bring Israel [home] again to his fold and pasturage
Jeremiah50:20 In those days and at that time
Jeremiah50:21 Go up against [Babylon] the land of Merathaim [two rebellions
Jeremiah50:22 The cry and noise of battle is in the land
Jeremiah50:23 How the hammer of the whole earth is crushed and broken! How Babylon has become a horror of desolation among the nations!
Jeremiah50:24 I set a trap for you
Jeremiah50:25 The Lord has opened His armory and has brought forth [the nations who unknowingly are] the weapons of His indignation and wrath
Jeremiah50:26 Come against her from every quarter and from the utmost border. Open her granaries and storehouses; pile up [their contents] like heaps of rubbish. Burn and destroy her utterly; let nothing be left of her.
Jeremiah50:27 Slay all her bullocks (her choice youths
Jeremiah50:28 Listen! The voice of those [Jews] who flee and escape out of the land of Babylon proclaiming in Zion the vengeance of the Lord our God
Jeremiah50:29 Call together [many] archers against Babylon
Jeremiah50:30 Therefore shall her young men fall in her streets and squares
Jeremiah50:31 Behold
Jeremiah50:32 And Pride (the arrogant one) shall stumble (totter) and fall
Jeremiah50:33 Thus says the Lord of hosts: The children of Israel and the children of Judah are oppressed together; all who took them captive have held them fast; they refuse to let them go.
Jeremiah50:34 Their Redeemer is strong; the Lord of hosts is His name. He will surely and thoroughly plead their case and defend their cause
Jeremiah50:35 A sword upon the Chaldeans
Jeremiah50:36 A sword upon the babbling liars (the diviners)
Jeremiah50:37 A sword upon their horses and upon their chariots and upon all the mixed foreign troops that are in the midst of her
Jeremiah50:38 A sword and a drought upon her waters
Jeremiah50:39 Therefore wild beasts of the desert shall dwell [in Babylon] with the jackals
Jeremiah50:40 As when God overthrew Sodom and Gomorrah and their neighboring cities
Jeremiah50:41 Behold
Jeremiah50:42 They lay hold of bow
Jeremiah50:43 The king of Babylon has heard the news about them
Jeremiah50:44 See
Jeremiah50:45 Therefore hear the plan of the Lord which He has made against Babylon
Jeremiah50:46 At the cry
Jeremiah51:1 THUS SAYS the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah51:2 And I will send to Babylon strangers or winnowers who will winnow her and will empty her land; for in the day of calamity they will be against her on every side.
Jeremiah51:3 Against him who bends let the archer bend his bow
Jeremiah51:4 Thus they shall fall down slain in the land of the Chaldeans
Jeremiah51:5 For Israel has not been widowed and forsaken
Jeremiah51:6 Flee out of the midst of Babylon! Let every man save his life! Let not destruction come upon you through her [punishment for] sin and guilt. For it is the time of the Lord's vengeance; He will render to her a recompense. [Jer. 50:28; II Cor. 6:17; Rev. 18:4.]
Jeremiah51:7 Babylon was a golden cup in the Lord's hand
Jeremiah51:8 Babylon has suddenly fallen and is shattered (destroyed)! Wail for her [if you care to]! Get balm for her [incurable] pain; if [you do] so she may [possibly] be healed! [Jer. 25:15; Rev. 14:8-10; 16:19; 18:2
Jeremiah51:9 We would have healed Babylon
Jeremiah51:10 The Lord has brought forth and made known the righteousness [of our cause]; come and let us declare in Zion the work of the Lord our God.
Jeremiah51:11 Make clean and sharp the arrows
Jeremiah51:12 Set up a standard or signal [to spread the news] upon the walls of Babylon! Make the watch and blockade strong
Jeremiah51:13 O [Babylon] you who dwell by many waters
Jeremiah51:14 The Lord of hosts has sworn by Himself
Jeremiah51:15 He made the earth by His power; He established the world by His wisdom and stretched out the heavens by His understanding.
Jeremiah51:16 When He utters His voice
Jeremiah51:17 Every man has become stupid and brutelike
Jeremiah51:18 They are worthless (emptiness
Jeremiah51:19 Not like these [gods] is He Who is the Portion of Jacob [the true God on Whom Israel has a claim]
Jeremiah51:20 You [Cyrus of Persia
Jeremiah51:21 With you I break in pieces the horse and his rider
Jeremiah51:22 With you I break in pieces man and woman
Jeremiah51:23 With you I break in pieces the shepherd and his flock
Jeremiah51:24 And I will [completely] repay Babylon and all the inhabitants of Chaldea for all the evil that they have done in Zion–before your very eyes [I will do it]
Jeremiah51:25 Behold
Jeremiah51:26 And [O Babylon] they shall not take your cracked stones for a cornerstone
Jeremiah51:27 Set up a standard or signal in the land [to spread the news]! Blow the trumpet among the nations! Prepare and dedicate the nations for war against her; call against her the kingdoms of Ararat
Jeremiah51:28 Prepare and dedicate the nations for war against her–the kings of Media
Jeremiah51:29 [I foresee this:] The land trembles and writhes in pain and sorrow
Jeremiah51:30 The mighty warriors of Babylon have ceased to fight; they have remained in their holds. Their might has failed; they have become [weak and helpless] like women. Her dwelling places are burned up; her bars [and defenses generally] are broken.
Jeremiah51:31 One post shall run to meet another and one messenger to meet another to show the king of Babylon that his city is taken on every side and to its farthest end
Jeremiah51:32 And that the passages [or ferries across the Euphrates] are stopped
Jeremiah51:33 For thus says the Lord of hosts
Jeremiah51:34 [The inhabitants of Zion say] Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon has devoured us
Jeremiah51:35 May the violence done to me and to my flesh and blood be upon Babylon
Jeremiah51:36 Therefore thus says the Lord: Behold
Jeremiah51:37 And Babylon shall become heaps [of ruins]
Jeremiah51:38 They [the Chaldean lords] shall be roaring together [before their sudden capture] like young lions [over their prey]
Jeremiah51:39 When the revelers are inflamed [with wine and lust during their drinking bouts]
Jeremiah51:40 I will bring them down like lambs to the slaughter
Jeremiah51:41 How Sheshach [Babylon] is taken! And the praise of the whole earth is surprised and seized! How Babylon has become an astonishing desolation and a horror among the nations!
Jeremiah51:42 The sea has come up upon Babylon; she is covered with the tumult and multitude of its waves.
Jeremiah51:43 Her cities have become a desolation and a horror
Jeremiah51:44 And I will punish and execute judgment upon Bel [the god] in Babylon and take out of his mouth what he has swallowed up [the sacred vessels and the people of Judah and elsewhere who were taken captive]. The nations will not flow any more to him. Yes
Jeremiah51:45 My people
Jeremiah51:46 And beware
Jeremiah51:47 Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:48 Then heaven and earth and all that is in them shall sing for joy over Babylon
Jeremiah51:49 As Babylon caused the slain of Israel to fall
Jeremiah51:50 You who have escaped the sword
Jeremiah51:51 We are confounded and ashamed
Jeremiah51:52 Therefore behold
Jeremiah51:53 Though Babylon should mount up to heaven
Jeremiah51:54 The sound of a cry [comes] from Babylon
Jeremiah51:55 For the Lord is destroying Babylon and laying her waste and stilling her great voice [the hum of the city's life]. And the waves [of her conquerors] roar like great waters
Jeremiah51:56 For the destroyer is coming upon her
Jeremiah51:57 And I will make drunk her princes and her wise men
Jeremiah51:58 Thus says the Lord of hosts: The broad walls of Babylon shall be utterly overthrown and [the foundations] made bare
Jeremiah51:59 The word that Jeremiah the prophet commanded Seraiah son of Neriah
Jeremiah51:60 So Jeremiah wrote in a book all the evil that would come upon Babylon–even all these words that are written against Babylon.
Jeremiah51:61 And Jeremiah said to Seraiah
Jeremiah51:62 Then say
Jeremiah51:63 And it shall be that when you have finished reading this book
Jeremiah51:64 Then say
Jeremiah52:1 ZEDEKIAH WAS twenty-one years old when he began to reign
Jeremiah52:2 And he did that which was evil in the sight of the Lord
Jeremiah52:3 For all this came to pass in Jerusalem and Judah because of the anger of the Lord
Jeremiah52:4 And in the ninth year of his reign
Jeremiah52:5 So the city was besieged until the eleventh year of King Zedekiah. [II Chron. 36:11-13.]
Jeremiah52:6 And in the fourth month
Jeremiah52:7 Then the city [wall] was broken through
Jeremiah52:8 But the army of the Chaldeans pursued the king and overtook Zedekiah in the plains of Jericho; and all his army was scattered from him.
Jeremiah52:9 Then they seized the king and brought him up to the king of Babylon at Riblah in the [Syrian] land of Hamath [on the northern border of Israel]
Jeremiah52:10 And the king of Babylon slew the sons of Zedekiah before his eyes; he slew also all the princes of Judah at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:11 Then he put out the eyes of Zedekiah; and the king of Babylon bound him with shackles and carried him to Babylon and put him in prison [mill] till the day of his death. [Ezek. 12:13.]
Jeremiah52:12 Now in the fifth month
Jeremiah52:13 And he burned the house of the Lord and the king's house and all the houses of Jerusalem; every great house he consumed with fire.
Jeremiah52:14 And all the army of the Chaldeans who were with the captain of the guard broke down all the walls round about Jerusalem.
Jeremiah52:15 Then Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard carried away captive some of the poorest of the people and those who were left in the city [at the time it was captured]
Jeremiah52:16 But Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard left some of the poorest of the land to be vinedressers and tillers of the soil.
Jeremiah52:17 Also the pillars of bronze that belonged to the house of the Lord
Jeremiah52:18 The pots [for carrying away ashes] also and the shovels and the snuffers and the bowls and the spoons and all the vessels of bronze used in the temple service they took away.
Jeremiah52:19 Also the small bowls and the firepans and the basins and the pots and the lampstands and the incense cups and the bowls for the drink offerings–whatever was of gold the captain of the guard took away as gold
Jeremiah52:20 The two pillars
Jeremiah52:21 Concerning the pillars
Jeremiah52:22 An upper part or capital of bronze was on top of it. The height of one capital was five cubits (seven and one-half feet)
Jeremiah52:23 And there were ninety-six pomegranates on the sides; and all the pomegranates upon the network were a hundred round about.
Jeremiah52:24 And the captain of the guard took [as prisoners] Seraiah the chief priest and Zephaniah the second priest and the three keepers of the door.
Jeremiah52:25 He took also out of the city a court officer who had been overseer of the soldiers
Jeremiah52:26 And Nebuzaradan the captain of the guard took them and brought them to the king of Babylon at Riblah.
Jeremiah52:27 And the king of Babylon smote them and put them to death at Riblah in the land of Hamath. Thus Judah was carried away captive out of his own land.
Jeremiah52:28 This is the number of people whom Nebuchadrezzar carried away captive: in the seventh year
Jeremiah52:29 In the eighteenth year of Nebuchadrezzar
Jeremiah52:30 In the twenty-third year of Nebuchadrezzar
Jeremiah52:31 And in the thirty-seventh year of the captivity of Jehoiachin [also called Coniah and Jeconiah] king of Judah
Jeremiah52:32 He spoke kindly to him and gave him a seat above the seats of the kings who were [captives] with him in Babylon
Jeremiah52:33 Jehoiachin put off his prison garments
Jeremiah52:34 And his allowance
Lamentations1:1 HOW SOLITARY and lonely sits the city [Jerusalem] that was [once] full of people! How like a widow has she become! She who was great among the nations and princess among the provinces has become a tributary [in servitude]!
Lamentations1:2 She weeps bitterly in the night
Lamentations1:3 Judah has gone into exile [to escape] from the affliction and laborious servitude [of the homeland]. She dwells among the [heathen] nations
Lamentations1:4 The roads to Zion mourn
Lamentations1:5 Her adversaries have become the head; her enemies prosper. For the Lord has afflicted her for the multitude of her transgressions; her young children have gone into captivity before the enemy. [Jer. 30:14
Lamentations1:6 From the Daughter of Zion all her beauty and majesty have departed. Her princes have become like harts that find no pasture; they have fled without strength before the pursuer.
Lamentations1:7 Jerusalem [earnestly] remembers in the days of her affliction
Lamentations1:8 Jerusalem has grievously sinned; therefore she has become an unclean thing and has been removed. All who honored her despise her
Lamentations1:9 Her filthiness was in and on her skirts; she did not [seriously and earnestly] consider her final end. Therefore she has come down [from throne to slavery] singularly and astonishingly; she has no comforter. O Lord [cries Jerusalem]
Lamentations1:10 The adversary has spread out his hand upon all her precious and desirable things; for she has seen the nations enter her sanctuary [of the temple]–when You commanded that they should not even enter Your congregation [in the outer courts]. [Deut. 23:3; Jer. 51:51; Ezek. 44:7
Lamentations1:11 All her people groan and sigh
Lamentations1:12 Is it nothing to you
Lamentations1:13 From above He has sent fire into my bones
Lamentations1:14 The yoke of my transgressions is bound by His hand; they were twined together; they were set upon my neck. He has made my strength fail and [me to] stumble; the Lord has delivered me into the hands of those I am unable to resist or withstand. [Deut. 28:48.]
Lamentations1:15 The Lord has made of no account all my [Jerusalem's] mighty men in the midst of me; He has proclaimed a set time against me to crush my young men. The Lord has trodden as in a winepress the Virgin Daughter of Judah.
Lamentations1:16 For these things I weep; my eyes overflow with tears
Lamentations1:17 Zion stretches forth her hands
Lamentations1:18 The Lord is righteous (just and in the right); for I have rebelled against His commandment (His word). Hear
Lamentations1:19 I [Jerusalem] called to my lovers [allies]
Lamentations1:20 Behold
Lamentations1:21 [My foes] have heard that I [Jerusalem] sigh and groan
Lamentations1:22 Let all their wickedness come before You; and deal with them as You have dealt with me because of all my transgressions; for my sighs and groans are many and my heart is faint.
Lamentations2:1 HOW THE Lord has covered the Daughter of Zion with a cloud in His anger! He has cast down from heaven to the earth the beauty and splendor of Israel and has not [earnestly] remembered His footstool in the day of His anger!
Lamentations2:2 The Lord has swallowed up all the country places and habitations of Jacob and has spared not nor pitied; He has demolished in His wrath the strongholds of the Daughter of Judah. He has cast down to the ground the kingdom and its rulers
Lamentations2:3 He has broken off in His fierce anger every horn (means of defense) of Israel. He has drawn back His right hand from before the enemy. And He has burned amidst Jacob like a flaming fire consuming all around.
Lamentations2:4 He has bent His bow like an enemy; He has stood with His right hand set like a foe and has slain all the delights and pride of the eye; on and in the tent of the Daughter of Zion He has poured out His wrath like fire.
Lamentations2:5 The Lord has become like an enemy; He has destroyed Israel. He has destroyed all its palaces
Lamentations2:6 And He has violently broken down His temple like a booth or hedge of a garden; He has destroyed the place of His appointed assembly. The Lord has caused the solemn appointed feasts and Sabbaths to be forgotten in Zion and has spurned and rejected in the indignation of His anger the king and the priest.
Lamentations2:7 The Lord has scorned
Lamentations2:8 The Lord purposed to lay in ruins the [city] wall of the Daughter of Zion. He marked it off by measuring line; He restrained not His hand from destroying. He made rampart and wall lament; they languished together.
Lamentations2:9 Her gates have sunk into the ground; He has destroyed and broken her bars. Her king and her princes are [exiled] among the nations; the law is no more; her prophets also obtain no vision from the Lord.
Lamentations2:10 The elders of the Daughter of Zion sit on the ground keeping silent; they have cast dust on their heads
Lamentations2:11 My eyes fail from weeping
Lamentations2:12 They keep crying to their mothers
Lamentations2:13 What [example of suffering in the past] is sufficient for me to remind you for your [comfort]? To what shall I liken you
Lamentations2:14 Your prophets have predicted for you falsehood and delusion and foolish things; and they have not exposed your iniquity and guilt to avert your captivity [by causing you to repent]. But they have divined and declared to you false and deceptive prophecies
Lamentations2:15 All who pass by clap their hands at you; they hiss and wag their heads at the Daughter of Jerusalem
Lamentations2:16 All your enemies have opened wide their mouths against you; they scornfully hiss and gnash their teeth. They cry
Lamentations2:17 The Lord has done what He planned; He has carried out and finished His word which He threatened and decreed in the days of old. He has demolished without pity; He has made the enemy rejoice over you and has exalted the might of your foes. [Lev. 26:14-39; Deut. 28:15-68.]
Lamentations2:18 The hearts [of the inhabitants of Jerusalem] cried to the Lord. [Then to the congregation
Lamentations2:19 Arise [from your bed]
Lamentations2:20 Behold
Lamentations2:21 The young and the old lie on the ground in the streets; my maidens and my young men have fallen by the sword. You have slain them in the day of Your anger
Lamentations2:22 You [Lord] called together
Lamentations3:1 I AM [Jeremiah] the man who has seen affliction under the rod of His wrath.
Lamentations3:2 He has led me and brought me into darkness and not light.
Lamentations3:3 Surely He has turned away from me; His hand is against me all the day.
Lamentations3:4 My flesh and my skin has He worn out and made old; He has shattered my bones.
Lamentations3:5 He has built up [siege mounds] against me and surrounded me with bitterness
Lamentations3:6 He has caused me to dwell in dark places like those long dead.
Lamentations3:7 He walled me in so that I cannot get out; He has weighted down my chain.
Lamentations3:8 Even when I cry and shout for help
Lamentations3:9 He has enclosed my ways with hewn stone; He has made my paths crooked.
Lamentations3:10 He is to me like a bear lying in wait
Lamentations3:11 He has turned me off my ways and pulled me in pieces; He has made me desolate.
Lamentations3:12 He has bent His bow and set me as a mark for the arrow.
Lamentations3:13 He has caused the arrows of His quiver to enter into my heart [the seat of my affections and desires].
Lamentations3:14 I have become a derision to all my people
Lamentations3:15 He has filled me with bitterness; He has made me drink to excess and until drunken with wormwood [bitterness].
Lamentations3:16 He has also broken my teeth with gravel (stones); He has covered me with ashes.
Lamentations3:17 And You have bereaved my soul and cast it off far from peace; I have forgotten what good and happiness are.
Lamentations3:18 And I say
Lamentations3:19 [O Lord] remember [earnestly] my affliction and my misery
Lamentations3:20 My soul has them continually in remembrance and is bowed down within me.
Lamentations3:21 But this I recall and therefore have I hope and expectation:
Lamentations3:22 It is because of the Lord's mercy and loving-kindness that we are not consumed
Lamentations3:23 They are new every morning; great and abundant is Your stability and faithfulness. [Isa. 33:2.]
Lamentations3:24 The Lord is my portion or share
Lamentations3:25 The Lord is good to those who wait hopefully and expectantly for Him
Lamentations3:26 It is good that one should hope in and wait quietly for the salvation (the safety and ease) of the Lord.
Lamentations3:27 It is good for a man that he should bear the yoke [of divine disciplinary dealings] in his youth.
Lamentations3:28 Let him sit alone uncomplaining and keeping silent [in hope]
Lamentations3:29 Let him put his mouth in the dust [in abject recognition of his unworthiness]–there may yet be hope. [Mic. 7:17.]
Lamentations3:30 Let him give his cheek to the One Who smites him [even through His human agents]; let him be filled [full] with [men's] reproach [in meekness].
Lamentations3:31 For the Lord will not cast off forever! [Ps. 94:14.]
Lamentations3:32 But though He causes grief
Lamentations3:33 For He does not willingly and from His heart afflict or grieve the children of men. [Ezek. 18:23
Lamentations3:34 To trample and crush underfoot all the prisoners of the earth
Lamentations3:35 To turn aside and deprive a man of his rights before the face of the Most High or a superior [acting as God's representative]
Lamentations3:36 To subvert a man in his cause–[of these things] the Lord does not approve.
Lamentations3:37 Who is he who speaks and it comes to pass
Lamentations3:38 Is it not out of the mouth of the Most High that evil and good both proceed [adversity and prosperity
Lamentations3:39 Why does a living man sigh [one who is still in this life's school of discipline]? [And why does] a man complain for the punishment of his sins?
Lamentations3:40 Let us test and examine our ways
Lamentations3:41 Let us lift up our hearts and our hands [and then with them mount up in prayer] to God in heaven:
Lamentations3:42 We have transgressed and rebelled and You have not pardoned.
Lamentations3:43 You have covered Yourself with wrath and pursued and afflicted us; You have slain without pity.
Lamentations3:44 You have covered Yourself with a cloud so that no prayer can pass through.
Lamentations3:45 You have made us offscouring and refuse among the nations.
Lamentations3:46 All our enemies have gaped at us and railed against us.
Lamentations3:47 Fear and pitfall have come upon us
Lamentations3:48 My eyes overflow with streams of tears because of the destruction of the daughter of my people.
Lamentations3:49 My eyes overflow continually and will not cease
Lamentations3:50 Until the Lord looks down and sees from heaven.
Lamentations3:51 My eyes cause me grief at the fate of all the maidens [and the daughter-towns] of my city [Jerusalem].
Lamentations3:52 I have been hunted down like a bird by those who were my enemies without cause.
Lamentations3:53 They [thought they had] destroyed my life in the dungeon (pit) and cast a stone [over it] above me. [Jer. 38.]
Lamentations3:54 The waters ran down on my head; I said
Lamentations3:55 I called upon Your name
Lamentations3:56 You heard my voice [then]: [Oh] hide not Your ear [now] at my prayer for relief.
Lamentations3:57 You drew near on the day I called to You; You said
Lamentations3:58 O Lord
Lamentations3:59 O Lord
Lamentations3:60 You have seen all their vengeance
Lamentations3:61 You have heard their reproach and revilings
Lamentations3:62 The lips and thoughts of my assailants are against me all day long.
Lamentations3:63 Look at their sitting down and their rising up [their movements
Lamentations3:64 Render to them a recompense
Lamentations3:65 You will give them hardness and blindness of heart; Your curse will be upon them.
Lamentations3:66 You will pursue and afflict them in anger and destroy them from under Your heavens
Lamentations4:1 HOW THE gold has become dim! How the most pure gold has changed! The hallowed stones [of the temple] are poured out at the head of every street.
Lamentations4:2 The noble and precious sons of Zion
Lamentations4:3 Even the jackals draw out the breast
Lamentations4:4 The tongue of the nursing babe cleaves to the roof of its mouth because of thirst; the young children beg for food
Lamentations4:5 Those who feasted on dainties are perishing in the streets; those who were brought up in purple lie cleaving to refuse and ash heaps.
Lamentations4:6 For the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the punishment of the sin of Sodom
Lamentations4:7 [In physical appearance] her princes were purer than snow
Lamentations4:8 [Prolonged famine has made] them look blacker than soot and darkness; they are not recognized in the streets. Their skin clings to their bones; it is withered and it has become [dry] like a stick.
Lamentations4:9 Those who are slain with the sword are more fortunate than those who are the victims of hunger [slain by the famine]; for they [the hungry] pine and ebb away
Lamentations4:10 The hands of [heretofore] compassionate women have boiled their own children; they were their food during the destruction of the daughter of my people [Judah].
Lamentations4:11 The Lord has fulfilled His wrath; He has poured out His fierce anger and has kindled a fire in Zion that has consumed her foundations.
Lamentations4:12 The kings of the earth did not believe
Lamentations4:13 [But this happened] because of the sins of her [false] prophets and the iniquities of her priests
Lamentations4:14 [The false prophets and priests] wandered [staggering] in the streets as if blind; they had so polluted themselves with blood it was not [lawful] for men to touch their garments.
Lamentations4:15 [People] cried to them
Lamentations4:16 The anger of the Lord has scattered [and divided them among the nations]; He will no longer look after them. They did not respect the persons of the priests; they did not favor the elders.
Lamentations4:17 As for us
Lamentations4:18 [The missiles of the enemy] dog our steps
Lamentations4:19 Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles of the sky; they pursued us on the mountains
Lamentations4:20 The breath of our nostrils
Lamentations4:21 Rejoice and be glad
Lamentations4:22 The punishment of your iniquity will be accomplished and completed
Lamentations5:1 O
Lamentations5:2 Our inheritance has fallen over to strangers
Lamentations5:3 We have become orphans and fatherless; our mothers are like widows.
Lamentations5:4 We have had to pay money to drink the water that belongs to us; our [own] wood is sold to us.
Lamentations5:5 Our pursuers are upon our necks [like a yoke]; we are weary and are allowed no rest.
Lamentations5:6 We have given the hand [as a pledge of fidelity and submission] to the Egyptians and to the Assyrians [merely] to get food to satisfy [our hunger].
Lamentations5:7 Our fathers sinned and are no more
Lamentations5:8 Servants and slaves rule over us; there is none to deliver us out of their hands. [Neh. 5:15.]
Lamentations5:9 We get our bread at the peril of our lives because of the sword of the wilderness [the wild Arabs
Lamentations5:10 Our skin glows and is parched as from [the heat of] an oven because of the burning heat of [the fever of] famine.
Lamentations5:11 They ravished the women in Zion
Lamentations5:12 They hung princes by their hands; the persons of elders were not respected.
Lamentations5:13 Young men carried millstones
Lamentations5:14 The elders have ceased from [congregating at] the city's gate
Lamentations5:15 Ceased is the joy of our hearts; our dancing has turned into mourning.
Lamentations5:16 The crown has fallen from our head [our honor is brought to the dust]! Woe to us
Lamentations5:17 Because of this our hearts are faint and sick; because of these things our eyes are dim and see darkly.
Lamentations5:18 As for Mount Zion
Lamentations5:19 But You
Lamentations5:20 Why do You forget us forever? Why do You forsake us so long?
Lamentations5:21 Turn us to Yourself
Lamentations5:22 Or have You utterly rejected us? Or are You exceedingly angry with us [still]?
Ezekiel1:1 NOW [when I was] in [my] thirtieth year
Ezekiel1:2 On the fifth day of the month
Ezekiel1:3 The word of the Lord came expressly to Ezekiel the priest
Ezekiel1:4 As I looked
Ezekiel1:5 And out of the midst of it came the likeness of four living creatures [or cherubim]. And this was their appearance: they had the likeness of a man
Ezekiel1:6 But each one had four faces and each one had four wings.
Ezekiel1:7 And their legs were straight legs
Ezekiel1:8 And they had the hands of a man under their wings on their four sides. And the four of them had their faces and their wings thus:
Ezekiel1:9 Their wings touched one another; they turned not when they went but went every one straight forward.
Ezekiel1:10 As for the likeness of their faces
Ezekiel1:11 Such were their faces. And their wings were stretched out upward [each creature had four wings]; two wings of each one were touching the [adjacent] wing of the creatures on either side of it
Ezekiel1:12 And they went every one straight forward; wherever the spirit would go
Ezekiel1:13 In the midst of the living creatures there was what looked like burning coals of fire
Ezekiel1:14 And the living creatures darted back and forth like a flash of lightning.
Ezekiel1:15 Now as I was still looking at the living creatures
Ezekiel1:16 As to the appearance of the wheels and their construction: in appearance they gleamed like chrysolite; and the four were formed alike
Ezekiel1:17 When they went
Ezekiel1:18 As for their rims
Ezekiel1:19 And when the living creatures went
Ezekiel1:20 Wherever the spirit went
Ezekiel1:21 When those went
Ezekiel1:22 Over the head of the [combined] living creature there was the likeness of a firmament
Ezekiel1:23 And under the firmament their wings were stretched out straight
Ezekiel1:24 And when they went
Ezekiel1:25 And there was a voice above the firmament that was over their heads; when they stood
Ezekiel1:26 And above the firmament that was over their heads was the likeness of a throne in appearance like a sapphire stone
Ezekiel1:27 From what had the appearance of His waist upward
Ezekiel1:28 Like the appearance of the bow that is in the cloud on the day of rain
Ezekiel2:1 AND HE said to me [Ezekiel]
Ezekiel2:2 And the Spirit entered into me when He spoke to me and set me upon my feet

Ezekiel2:4 And the children are impudent and hard of heart. I send you to them and you shall say to them

Ezekiel16:11 I decked you also with ornaments and I put bracelets on your wrists and a chain on your neck.

Ezekiel16:14 And your renown went forth among the nations for your beauty

Ezekiel16:17 You did also take your fair jewels and beautiful vessels of My gold and My silver which I had given you and made for yourself images of men
Ezekiel16:18 And you took your embroidered garments and covered them and set My oil and My incense before them.

Ezekiel16:23 And after all your wickedness–Woe

Ezekiel16:26 You have also played the harlot with the Egyptians
Ezekiel16:27 Behold therefore

Ezekiel16:29 Moreover
Ezekiel16:30 How weak and spent with longing and lust is your heart and mind

Ezekiel16:32 Rather
Ezekiel16:33 Men give gifts to all harlots
Ezekiel16:34 And you are different [the reverse] from other women in your harlotries
Ezekiel16:35 Therefore

Ezekiel16:37 Therefore behold
Ezekiel16:38 And I the Lord will judge you as women who break wedlock and shed blood are judged
Ezekiel16:39 And I will also give you into the hand of those [your enemies]
Ezekiel16:40 They shall also bring up a company against you
Ezekiel16:41 And they shall burn your houses with fire and execute judgments upon you before the eyes of many women spectators [the nations]. And I will cause you to cease playing the harlot
Ezekiel16:42 So will I make My wrath toward you to rest and My jealousy shall depart from you [My adulterous wife]
Ezekiel16:43 Because you have not [earnestly] remembered the days of your youth but have enraged Me with all these things

Ezekiel16:45 You are your [spiritual] mother's daughter who loathed her husband and her children
Ezekiel16:46 And your elder sister is Samaria
Ezekiel16:47 Yet you were not satisfied to walk after their ways or to do after their abominations
Ezekiel16:48 As I live
Ezekiel16:49 Behold

Ezekiel18:22 None of his transgressions which he has committed shall be remembered against him; for his righteousness which he has executed [for his moral and spiritual rectitude in every area and relation]
Ezekiel18:23 Have I any pleasure in the death of the wicked? says the Lord
Ezekiel18:24 But if the righteous man turns away from his righteousness and commits iniquity and does according to all the abominations that the wicked man does
Ezekiel18:25 Yet you say
Ezekiel18:26 When a righteous man turns away from his righteousness and commits iniquity and dies in his sins
Ezekiel18:27 Again
Ezekiel18:28 Because he considers and turns away from all his transgressions which he has committed
Ezekiel18:29 Yet says the house of Israel
Ezekiel18:30 Therefore I will judge you
Ezekiel18:31 Cast away from you all your transgressions by which you have transgressed against Me
Ezekiel18:32 For I have no pleasure in the death of him who dies
Ezekiel19:1 MOREOVER

Ezekiel19:4 The nations also heard of him; he was taken in their pit
Ezekiel19:5 Now when she had waited
Ezekiel19:6 And he [Jehoiachin] went up and down among the lions; he became a young lion and learned to catch prey
Ezekiel19:7 And he knew and ravaged their strongholds and he laid waste their cities
Ezekiel19:8 Then the nations set against [the king] on every side from the provinces
Ezekiel19:9 With hooks they put him in a cage and brought him to the king of Babylon; they brought him into custody and put him in strongholds
Ezekiel19:10 Your mother [the mother-city Jerusalem] was like a vine [like you
Ezekiel19:11 And it had strong rods for the scepters of those who bore rule and its height was exalted among the thick branches and into the clouds

Ezekiel19:13 And now it is transplanted in the wilderness
Ezekiel19:14 And fire went out of a rod [Zedekiah] of its branches which has consumed the vine's fruit
Ezekiel20:1 IN THE seventh year
Ezekiel20:2 Then came the word of the Lord to me

Ezekiel20:7 Then said I to them
Ezekiel20:8 But they rebelled against Me and would not listen to Me; they did not every man cast away the abominable things on which they feasted their eyes
Ezekiel20:9 But I acted for My name's sake

Ezekiel20:22 Yet I withheld My hand and acted for My name's sake
Ezekiel20:23 Moreover
Ezekiel20:24 Because they had not executed My ordinances but had despised and rejected My statutes and had profaned My Sabbaths
Ezekiel20:25 Wherefore also I gave them [over to] statutes that were not good and ordinances whereby they should not live and could not have life
Ezekiel20:26 And I [let them] pollute and make themselves unclean in their own offerings [to their idols]
Ezekiel20:27 Therefore
Ezekiel20:28 For when I had brought them into the land which I lifted up My hand and swore to give to them

Ezekiel20:37 And I will cause you to pass under the rod [as the shepherd does his sheep when he counts them

Ezekiel20:39 As for you

Ezekiel21:32 You shall be for fuel to the fire; your blood shall be in the midst of the land; you shall be no more remembered
Ezekiel22:1 MOREOVER
Ezekiel22:2 And you son of man [Ezekiel]
Ezekiel22:3 And say
Ezekiel22:4 In your blood which you have shed you have become guilty
Ezekiel22:5 Those who are near and those who are far from you will mock you
Ezekiel22:6 Behold
Ezekiel22:7 In you have they treated father and mother lightly; in the midst of you they have dealt unjustly and by oppression in relation to the stranger; in you they have wronged the fatherless and the widow.
Ezekiel22:8 You have despised and scorned My sacred things and have profaned My Sabbaths.
Ezekiel22:9 In you are slanderous men who arouse suspicions to shed blood
Ezekiel22:10 In you men have uncovered their fathers' nakedness [the nakedness of mother or stepmother]; in you they have humbled women who are [ceremonially] unclean [during their periods or because of childbirth].
Ezekiel22:11 And one has committed abomination with his neighbor's wife
Ezekiel22:12 In you they have accepted bribes to shed blood; you have taken [forbidden] interest and [percentage of] increase
Ezekiel22:13 Behold therefore
Ezekiel22:14 Can your heart and courage endure or can your hands be strong in the days that I shall deal with you? I the Lord have spoken it
Ezekiel22:15 And I will scatter you among the nations and disperse you through the countries
Ezekiel22:16 And you shall be dishonored and profane yourself in the sight of the nations
Ezekiel22:17 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel22:18 Son of man
Ezekiel22:19 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have all become scum and waste matter
Ezekiel22:20 As they gather silver and bronze and iron and lead and tin into the midst of the furnace
Ezekiel22:21 Yes
Ezekiel22:22 As silver is melted in the midst of the furnace
Ezekiel22:23 And the word of the Lord came to me
Ezekiel22:24 Son of man
Ezekiel22:25 There is a conspiracy of [Israel's false] prophets in the midst of her
Ezekiel22:26 Her priests have done violence to My law and have profaned My holy things. They have made no distinction between the sacred and the secular
Ezekiel22:27 Her princes in the midst of her are like wolves rending and devouring the prey
Ezekiel22:28 And her prophets have daubed them over with whitewash
Ezekiel22:29 The people of the land have used oppression and extortion and have committed robbery; yes
Ezekiel22:30 And I sought a man among them who should build up the wall and stand in the gap before Me for the land
Ezekiel22:31 Therefore have I poured out My indignation upon them; I have consumed them with the fire of My wrath; their own way have I repaid [by bringing it] upon their own heads

Ezekiel23:5 And Aholah played the harlot when she was Mine
Ezekiel23:6 Who were clothed with blue
Ezekiel23:7 And she bestowed her harlotries upon them

Ezekiel23:11 And her sister Aholibah saw this; yet she was more corrupt in her foolish fondness than she
Ezekiel23:12 She doted upon the Assyrians–governors and deputies
Ezekiel23:13 And I saw that she was defiled
Ezekiel23:14 But [Aholibah] carried her harlotries further
Ezekiel23:15 Girded with girdles on their loins
Ezekiel23:16 Then as soon as she saw [the sketches of] them
Ezekiel23:17 And the Babylonians came to her into the bed of love
Ezekiel23:18 So she flaunted her harlotries and exposed her nakedness
Ezekiel23:19 Yet she multiplied her harlotries
Ezekiel23:20 For she doted upon her paramours there
Ezekiel23:21 Thus you yearned for the lewdness of your youth
Ezekiel23:22 Therefore
Ezekiel23:23 The Babylonians and all the Chaldeans
Ezekiel23:24 And they shall come against you with weapons
Ezekiel23:25 And I will set My jealous indignation against you
Ezekiel23:26 They shall also strip you [Judah] of your clothes and take away your fine jewels.
Ezekiel23:27 Thus I will put an end to your lewdness and your harlotry brought from the land of Egypt
Ezekiel23:28 For thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel23:29 They shall deal with you in hatred and shall take away all [the earnings of] your labor and shall leave you naked and bare
Ezekiel23:30 These things shall be done to you
Ezekiel23:31 You have walked in the way of your sister [Samaria
Ezekiel23:32 Thus says the Lord God: You shall drink of your sister's cup which is deep and wide and brimful; you shall be laughed to scorn and held in derision
Ezekiel23:33 You shall be filled with drunkenness and sorrow
Ezekiel23:34 You shall drink it and drain it out
Ezekiel23:35 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have forgotten Me [your divine Husband] and cast Me behind your back
Ezekiel23:36 The Lord said
Ezekiel23:37 For they have committed adultery and blood is on their hands
Ezekiel23:38 Moreover
Ezekiel23:39 For when they had slain their children [as offerings] to their idols
Ezekiel23:40 And furthermore
Ezekiel23:41 And you sat upon a stately couch with a table spread before it upon which you set My incense and My oil.
Ezekiel23:42 And the sound of a careless crowd was with her
Ezekiel23:43 Then I said of the one [Aholah] worn out with adulteries
Ezekiel23:44 Yet they went in to her as they go in to a woman who plays the harlot; so they went in to Aholah and to Aholibah [Israel and Judah]
Ezekiel23:45 And the righteous men
Ezekiel23:46 For thus says the Lord God: I will bring up a host upon them and will give them over to be tossed to and fro and robbed.
Ezekiel23:47 And the host shall stone them with stones and cut them down with their swords; they shall slay their sons and their daughters and burn up their houses with fire.
Ezekiel23:48 Thus will I cause lewdness to cease out of the land
Ezekiel23:49 Thus your lewdness shall be recompensed upon you and you shall suffer the penalty for your sinful idolatry; and you shall know (understand and realize) that I am the Lord God.

Ezekiel24:8 That it may cause wrath to come up to take vengeance

Ezekiel24:11 Then set [the pot Jerusalem] back empty upon the coals

Ezekiel26:6 And Tyre's daughters [her towns and villages on the mainland] in the level place shall be slain by the sword

Ezekiel26:8 He shall slay with the sword your daughters [the towns and villages] in the level area [on the mainland]

Ezekiel26:10 Because of the great number of [Nebuchadrezzar's] horses

Ezekiel26:14 And I will make you [Tyre] a bare rock; you shall be a place upon which to spread nets; you shall never be rebuilt

Ezekiel26:16 Then all the princes of the sea shall come down from their thrones and lay aside their robes and strip off their embroidered garments; they shall clothe themselves with tremblings; they shall sit upon the ground and shall tremble every moment and be astonished at you and appalled.

Ezekiel26:18 Now the isles and coastlands tremble in the day of your fall; yes
Ezekiel26:19 For thus says the Lord God: When I make you a desolate city like the cities that are not inhabited
Ezekiel26:20 Then I will thrust you down with those who descend into the pit (the place of the dead) to the people of olden times
Ezekiel26:21 I will make you a terror [bring you to a dreadful end] and you shall be no more. Though you be sought
Ezekiel27:1 THE WORD of the Lord came again to me
Ezekiel27:2 Now you
Ezekiel27:3 And say to Tyre

Ezekiel27:8 The inhabitants of Sidon and [the island] of Arvad were your oarsmen; your skilled and wise men

Ezekiel27:10 Persia and Lud and Put were in your army as your men of war; they hung the shield and helmet in you; they gave you beauty and splendor.
Ezekiel27:11 The men of Arvad with your army were upon your walls round about and valorous men [of Gamad] were in your towers; they hung their shields upon your walls round about; they have perfected your beauty and splendor.
Ezekiel27:12 Tarshish [in Spain] carried on traffic with you because of the abundance of your riches of all kinds; with silver
Ezekiel27:13 Javan (Greece)
Ezekiel27:14 They of the house of Togarmah (Armenia) traded for your wares with [chariot] horses
Ezekiel27:15 The men of Dedan [in Arabia] traded with you; many islands and coastlands were your own markets; they brought you in payment or as presents ivory tusks and ebony.
Ezekiel27:16 Aram (Syria or Mesopotamia) and Edom traded with you because of the multitude of the wares of your making. They exchanged for your merchandise emeralds
Ezekiel27:17 Judah and the land of Israel
Ezekiel27:18 Damascus traded with you because of the abundance of supplies of your handiworks and the immense wealth of every kind
Ezekiel27:19 Vedan also and [Arabic] Javan traded with yarn from Uzal [in Arabia] for your wares; wrought iron
Ezekiel27:20 Dedan supplied you with precious [saddle] cloths for riding.
Ezekiel27:21 Arabia and all the princes of Kedar
Ezekiel27:22 The merchants of Sheba and Raamah [in Arabia] traded with you; they exchanged for your wares the choicest of all kinds of spices and all precious stones and gold.
Ezekiel27:23 Haran and Canneh and Eden [in Mesopotamia]
Ezekiel27:24 These traded with you in choice fabrics
Ezekiel27:25 The ships of Tarshish were your caravans for your merchandise
Ezekiel27:26 Your rowers have brought you out into great and deep waters; the east wind has broken and wrecked you in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel27:27 Your riches
Ezekiel27:28 The waves and the countryside shake at the [piercing] sound of the [hopeless
Ezekiel27:29 And down from their ships come all who handle the oar. The mariners and all the pilots of the sea stand upon the shore
Ezekiel27:30 And are heard wailing loudly over you
Ezekiel27:31 And they make themselves [utterly] bald for you and gird themselves with sackcloth
Ezekiel27:32 And in their wailing they take up a lamentation for you and lament over you
Ezekiel27:33 When your wares came forth from the seas
Ezekiel27:34 Now you are shattered by the seas in the depths of the waters; your merchandise and all your crew have gone down with you.
Ezekiel27:35 All the inhabitants of the isles and coastlands are astonished and appalled at you
Ezekiel27:36 The merchants among the people hiss over you [with malicious joy]; you have become a horror and a source of terrors. You shall be no more [forever].
Ezekiel28:1 THE WORD of the Lord came again to me
Ezekiel28:2 Son of man
Ezekiel28:3 Indeed
Ezekiel28:4 With your own wisdom and with your own understanding you have gotten you riches and power and have brought gold and silver into your treasuries;
Ezekiel28:5 By your great wisdom and by your traffic you have increased your riches and power
Ezekiel28:6 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Because you have imagined your mind as the mind of God [having thoughts and purposes suitable only to God Himself]
Ezekiel28:7 Behold therefore
Ezekiel28:8 They shall bring you down to the pit [of destruction] and you shall die the [many] deaths of all the Tyrians that are slain in the heart of the seas.
Ezekiel28:9 Will you still say
Ezekiel28:10 You shall die the death of the uncircumcised by the hand of strangers
Ezekiel28:11 Moreover
Ezekiel28:12 Son of man
Ezekiel28:13 You were in Eden
Ezekiel28:14 You were the anointed cherub that covers with overshadowing [wings]
Ezekiel28:15 You were blameless in your ways from the day you were created until iniquity and guilt were found in you.
Ezekiel28:16 Through the abundance of your commerce you were filled with lawlessness and violence
Ezekiel28:17 Your heart was proud and lifted up because of your beauty; you corrupted your wisdom for the sake of your splendor. I cast you to the ground; I lay you before kings
Ezekiel28:18 You have profaned your sanctuaries by the multitude of your iniquities and the enormity of your guilt
Ezekiel28:19 All who know you among the people are astonished and appalled at you; you have come to a horrible end and shall never return to being. [Isa. 23; Joel 3:4-8; Amos 1:9

Ezekiel28:21 Son of man

Ezekiel28:25 Thus says the Lord God: When I gather the house of Israel from the peoples among whom they are scattered
Ezekiel28:26 And they shall dwell safely in it and shall build houses and plant vineyards; yes

Ezekiel29:6 And all the inhabitants of Egypt shall know (understand and realize) that I am the Lord [the Sovereign Ruler
Ezekiel29:7 When they grasped you with the hand and leaned upon you
Ezekiel29:8 Therefore thus says the Lord God: Behold
Ezekiel29:9 And the land of Egypt shall be a desolation and a waste. And they shall know (understand and realize) that I am the Lord [the Sovereign Ruler
Ezekiel29:10 Behold therefore
Ezekiel29:11 No foot of man shall pass through it [in travel]
Ezekiel29:12 And I will make the land of Egypt a desolation [plundered and reduced to subjection] in the midst of desolated (plundered and reduced to subjection) countries

Ezekiel29:15 It shall be the lowliest of the kingdoms
Ezekiel29:16 And never again shall Egypt have the confidence and be the reliance of the house of Israel; their iniquity will be brought to remembrance whenever [Israel] looks toward them [for help]. They shall know (understand and realize) that I am the Lord God [Who demands loyalty and obedient service].
Ezekiel29:17 In the twenty-seventh year [after King Jehoiachin was taken to Babylon]
Ezekiel29:18 Son of man

Ezekiel30:19 Thus will I execute judgments and punishments upon Egypt. Then shall they know (understand and realize) that I am the Lord [the Sovereign Ruler

Ezekiel31:1 AND IN the eleventh year [after King Jehoiachin was taken captive to Babylon]
Ezekiel31:2 Son of man
Ezekiel31:3 Behold
Ezekiel31:4 The waters nourished it; the deep made it grow tall; its rivers ran round about its planting
Ezekiel31:5 Therefore it towered higher than all the trees of the forest; its boughs were multiplied and its branches became long
Ezekiel31:6 All the birds of the heavens made their nests in its boughs
Ezekiel31:7 Thus was it beautiful in its greatness
Ezekiel31:8 The cedars in the garden of God could not hide or rival it; the cypress trees did not have boughs like it and the plane trees did not have branches like it
Ezekiel31:9 I made it beautiful with the multitude of its branches
Ezekiel31:10 Therefore thus said the Lord God: Because it is exalted in stature and has set its top among the thick boughs and the clouds
Ezekiel31:11 I will even deliver it into the hand of a mighty one of the nations; he shall surely deal with it. I have driven it out for its wickedness and lawlessness.
Ezekiel31:12 And strangers
Ezekiel31:13 Upon its ruins all the birds of the heavens will dwell
Ezekiel31:14 All this is so that none of the trees by the waters may exalt themselves because of their height or shoot up their top among the thick boughs and the clouds
Ezekiel31:15 Thus says the Lord God: When [Assyria] goes down to Sheol (the place of the dead)
Ezekiel31:16 I will make the nations quake at the sound of its fall when I cast it down to Sheol with those who descend into the pit
Ezekiel31:17 They also shall go down into Sheol with it to those who were slain by the sword–yes
Ezekiel31:18 To whom [O Egypt] among the trees of Eden are you thus like in glory and in greatness? Yet you [also] shall be brought down with the trees of Eden to the netherworld. You shall lie among the uncircumcised heathen with those who are slain by the sword. This is how it shall be with Pharaoh and all the multitude of his strength

Ezekiel40:2 In the visions of God He brought me into the land of Israel and set me down upon a very high mountain
Ezekiel40:3 He brought me there
Ezekiel40:4 And the man said to me
Ezekiel40:5 And behold
Ezekiel40:6 Then he came to the gate which faced the east and went up its [seven] steps and measured the threshold of the gateway
Ezekiel40:7 And every room for the guards was one reed long and one reed broad
Ezekiel40:8 He measured also the porch or vestibule of the gate toward the house [of the Lord]
Ezekiel40:9 Then he measured the porch or vestibule of the gateway
Ezekiel40:10 And the guardrooms or lodges of the east gateway were three on this side and three on that side; the three were the same size
Ezekiel40:11 And he measured the breadth of the opening of the gateway
Ezekiel40:12 And a border or barrier before the guardrooms was one cubit on this side
Ezekiel40:13 And the man [an angel] measured the gate from the outer wall of one chamber or guardroom to the outer wall of another–a breadth of twenty-five cubits from door to door.
Ezekiel40:14 And the open part of the porch or vestibule of the gateway on the outside was twenty cubits
Ezekiel40:15 And including this porch or vestibule of the gate on the outside and the porch or vestibule on the inside
Ezekiel40:16 And there were closed windows to the guardrooms or chambers and to their posts or pillars within the gate round about
Ezekiel40:17 Then he brought me into the outward court
Ezekiel40:18 And the pavement was along by the side of the gates
Ezekiel40:19 Then the man measured the distance from the inner front before the lower gate to the outer front of the inner court
Ezekiel40:20 And the gate of the outward court which faced the north
Ezekiel40:21 And its guardrooms or lodges
Ezekiel40:22 And its windows and its archway or vestibule and its palm trees were of the same size as those of the gate that faces toward the east. It was reached by going up seven steps
Ezekiel40:23 Opposite the gate on the north and on the east was a gate to the inner court
Ezekiel40:24 After that the man brought me toward the south
Ezekiel40:25 And there were windows round about in it and in its archway or vestibule
Ezekiel40:26 And there were seven steps going up to the gate
Ezekiel40:27 And there was a gate to the inner court on the south
Ezekiel40:28 And the man [an angel] brought me into the inner court by the south gate

Ezekiel40:31 And its [arched] vestibule faced the outer court; and palm trees were carved upon its posts or pillars
Ezekiel40:32 And he brought me into the inner court toward the east and he measured the gate; it measured the same as the others.

Ezekiel40:34 And its [arched] vestibule faced the outer court; and palm trees were carved upon its posts or pillars on either side
Ezekiel40:35 And the man [an angel] brought me to the north gate and measured it; the measurements were the same as those of the other gates.

Ezekiel40:37 And its posts or pillars were toward the outer court

Ezekiel42:11 With a passage before them that gave the appearance of the attached chambers on the north
Ezekiel42:12 And like the doors of the chambers that were toward the south there was an entrance at the head of the way

Ezekiel43:1 AFTERWARD the man [an angel] brought me to the gate
Ezekiel43:2 And behold
Ezekiel43:3 And the vision which I saw was like the vision I had seen when I came to foretell the destruction of the city and like the vision I had seen beside the river Chebar [near Babylon]; and I fell on my face. [Ezek. 1:4; 3:23; 10:15
Ezekiel43:4 And the glory of the Lord entered the temple by the gate facing east.
Ezekiel43:5 Then the Spirit caught me up and brought me into the inner court
Ezekiel43:6 And I heard One speaking to me out of the temple
Ezekiel43:7 And He [the Lord] said to me
Ezekiel43:8 Nor by setting their threshold by My thresholds and their doorposts by My doorposts
Ezekiel43:9 Now let them put away their [idolatrous] harlotry and the dead bodies and monuments of their kings far from Me
Ezekiel43:10 Son of man
Ezekiel43:11 And if they are ashamed of all that they have done
Ezekiel43:12 This is the law of the house [of the Lord]: The whole area round about on the top of the mountain [Mount Moriah] shall be most holy
Ezekiel43:13 And these are the measurements of the altar [of burnt offering] in cubits. The cubit is a royal cubit [the length of a forearm and a palm of the hand]; the bottom or gutter shall be a cubit deep and a cubit wide
Ezekiel43:14 From the bottom or gutter on the ground to the lower ledge or brim shall be two cubits
Ezekiel43:15 And the altar hearth shall be four cubits high
Ezekiel43:16 And the altar hearth shall be square–twelve cubits long
Ezekiel43:17 And the ledge shall be fourteen cubits long and fourteen cubits broad on its four sides
Ezekiel43:18 And [the Lord] said to me
Ezekiel43:19 You shall give to the priests
Ezekiel43:20 And you shall take of its blood and put it on the four horns of [the altar of burnt offering] and on the four corners of the ledge and on the rim or border round about. Thus shall you cleanse and make atonement for [the altar].
Ezekiel43:21 You shall also take the bullock of the sin offering
Ezekiel43:22 And on the second day you shall offer a male goat without blemish for a sin offering. Thus the altar shall be cleansed
Ezekiel43:23 When you have finished cleansing it
Ezekiel43:24 And you shall bring them near before the Lord
Ezekiel43:25 Seven days you shall prepare every day a goat for a sin offering; also a young bull and a ram out of the flock
Ezekiel43:26 For seven days shall they make atonement for the altar and purify it; so the priests shall consecrate
Ezekiel43:27 And when these days have been accomplished
Ezekiel44:1 THEN the man [an angel] brought me back the way of the outer gate of the sanctuary which faces the east
Ezekiel44:2 Then the Lord said to me
Ezekiel44:3 As for the prince
Ezekiel44:4 Then he brought me by way of the north gate to the front of the temple; I looked
Ezekiel44:5 And the Lord said to me
Ezekiel44:6 And you shall say to the rebellious
Ezekiel44:7 You have brought into My sanctuary aliens
Ezekiel44:8 And you have not kept charge of My holy things
Ezekiel44:9 Therefore thus says the Lord God: No foreigner uncircumcised in heart and flesh shall enter into My sanctuary [where no one but the priests might enter]
Ezekiel44:10 But the Levites who went far away from Me when Israel went astray
Ezekiel44:11 They shall minister in My sanctuary
Ezekiel44:12 Because [the priests] ministered to [the people] before their idols and became a stumbling block of iniquity and guilt to the house of Israel
Ezekiel44:13 And they shall not come near to Me to do the office of a priest to Me
Ezekiel44:14 Yet I will appoint them as caretakers to have charge of the temple
Ezekiel44:15 But the Levitical priests
Ezekiel44:16 They shall enter into My sanctuary; and they shall come near to My table to minister to Me
Ezekiel44:17 When they enter the gates of the inner court
Ezekiel44:18 They shall have linen turbans on their heads and linen breeches upon their loins; they shall not gird themselves with anything that causes [them to] sweat.
Ezekiel44:19 And when they go out into the outer court to the people
Ezekiel44:20 Neither shall they shave their heads or allow their locks to grow long; they shall only cut short or trim the hair of their heads.
Ezekiel44:21 Neither shall any priest drink wine when he enters the inner court.
Ezekiel44:22 Neither shall they take for their wives a widow or a woman separated or divorced from her husband; but they shall marry maidens [who are virgins] of the offspring of the house of Israel or a widow previously married to a priest.
Ezekiel44:23 The priests shall teach My people the difference between the holy and the common or profane
Ezekiel44:24 And in a controversy they shall act as judges
Ezekiel44:25 And they shall go near to no dead person to defile themselves
Ezekiel44:26 And after he is cleansed [from the defilement of a dead body] they shall reckon to him seven days more before returning to the temple.
Ezekiel44:27 And on the day that he goes into the sanctuary
Ezekiel44:28 This [their ministry to Me] shall be to them as an inheritance
Ezekiel44:29 They shall eat the meal offering and the sin offering and the trespass offering
Ezekiel44:30 And the first of all the firstfruits of all kinds
Ezekiel44:31 The priests shall not eat of anything that has died of itself or is torn

Daniel1:1 IN THE third year of the reign of Jehoiakim king of Judah
Daniel1:2 And the Lord gave Jehoiakim king of Judah into his hand
Daniel1:3 And the [Babylonian] king told Ashpenaz
Daniel1:4 Youths without blemish
Daniel1:5 And the king assigned for them a daily portion of his own rich and dainty food and of the wine which he drank. They were to be so educated and so nourished for three years that at the end of that time they might stand before the king.
Daniel1:6 Among these were of the children of Judah: Daniel
Daniel1:7 The chief of the eunuchs gave them names: Daniel he called Belteshazzar [the king's attendant]
Daniel1:8 But Daniel determined in his heart that he would not defile himself by [eating his portion of] the king's rich and dainty food or by [drinking] the wine which he drank; therefore he requested of the chief of the eunuchs that he might [be allowed] not to defile himself. [Num. 6:1-4; I Cor. 10:21.]
Daniel1:9 Now God made Daniel to find favor
Daniel1:10 And the chief of the eunuchs said to Daniel
Daniel1:11 Then said Daniel to the steward whom the chief of the eunuchs had set over Daniel
Daniel1:12 Prove your servants
Daniel1:13 Then let our appearance and the appearance of the youths who eat of the king's [rich] dainties be observed and compared by you
Daniel1:14 So [the man] consented to them in this matter and proved them ten days.
Daniel1:15 And at the end of ten days it was seen that they were looking better and had taken on more flesh than all the youths who ate of the king's rich dainties.
Daniel1:16 So the steward took away their [rich] dainties and the wine they were to drink and gave them vegetables.
Daniel1:17 As for these four youths
Daniel1:18 Now at the end of the time which the king had set for bringing [all the young men in]
Daniel1:19 And the king conversed with them
Daniel1:20 And in all matters of wisdom and understanding concerning which the king asked them
Daniel1:21 And Daniel continued there even to the first year of King Cyrus [at the close of the seventy years' exile of Judah in Babylonia

Daniel2:2 Then the king commanded to call the magicians
Daniel2:3 And the king said to them
Daniel2:4 Then said the Chaldeans [diviners] to the king in Aramaic [the Syrian language]
Daniel2:5 The king answered the Chaldeans
Daniel2:6 But if you show the dream and its interpretation
Daniel2:7 They answered again
Daniel2:8 The king answered
Daniel2:9 If you will not make known to me the dream
Daniel2:10 The Chaldeans [diviners] answered before the king and said
Daniel2:11 A rare and weighty thing indeed the king requires! None except the gods can reveal it to the king
Daniel2:12 For this cause the king was angry and very furious and commanded that all the wise men of Babylon be destroyed.
Daniel2:13 So the decree went forth that the wise men were to be killed
Daniel2:14 Then Daniel returned an answer which was full of prudence and wisdom to Arioch the captain or executioner of the king's guard
Daniel2:15 He said to Arioch
Daniel2:16 And Daniel went in and desired of the king that he would set a date and give him time
Daniel2:17 Then Daniel went to his house and made the thing known to Hananiah
Daniel2:18 So that they would desire and request mercy of the God of heaven concerning this secret
Daniel2:19 Then the secret was revealed to Daniel in a vision of the night
Daniel2:20 Daniel answered
Daniel2:21 He changes the times and the seasons; He removes kings and sets up kings. He gives wisdom to the wise and knowledge to those who have understanding! [Dan. 4:35.]
Daniel2:22 He reveals the deep and secret things; He knows what is in the darkness
Daniel2:23 I thank You and praise You
Daniel2:24 Therefore Daniel went to Arioch
Daniel2:25 Then Arioch brought in Daniel before the king in haste and said thus to him: I have found a man of the captives of Judah who will make known to the king the interpretation [of his dream].
Daniel2:26 The king said to Daniel
Daniel2:27 Daniel answered the king
Daniel2:28 But there is a God in heaven Who reveals secrets
Daniel2:29 As for you
Daniel2:30 But as for me
Daniel2:31 You
Daniel2:32 As for this image
Daniel2:33 Its legs of iron
Daniel2:34 As you looked
Daniel2:35 Then the iron
Daniel2:36 This was the dream
Daniel2:37 You
Daniel2:38 And wherever the children of men dwell
Daniel2:39 And after you shall arise another kingdom [the Medo-Persian]
Daniel2:40 And the fourth kingdom [Rome] shall be strong as iron
Daniel2:41 And as you saw the feet and toes
Daniel2:42 And as the toes of the feet were partly of iron and partly of [baked] clay [of the potter]
Daniel2:43 And as you saw the iron mixed with miry and earthen clay
Daniel2:44 And in the days of these [final ten] kings shall the God of heaven set up a kingdom which shall never be destroyed
Daniel2:45 Just as you saw that the Stone was cut out of the mountain without hands and that it broke in pieces the iron
Daniel2:46 Then King Nebuchadnezzar fell on his face and paid homage to Daniel [as a great prophet of the highest God] and ordered that an offering and incense should be offered up to him [in honor of his God].
Daniel2:47 The king answered Daniel
Daniel2:48 Then the king made Daniel great and gave him many great gifts

Daniel4:1 NEBUCHADNEZZAR THE king
Daniel4:2 It seemed good to me to show the signs and wonders that the Most High God has performed toward me.
Daniel4:3 How great are His signs! And how mighty His wonders! His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom
Daniel4:4 I
Daniel4:5 I had a dream which made me afraid
Daniel4:6 Therefore I made a decree to bring in all the wise men of Babylon before me
Daniel4:7 Then the magicians
Daniel4:8 But at last Daniel came in before me–he who was named Belteshazzar
Daniel4:9 O Belteshazzar
Daniel4:10 The visions of my head [as I lay] on my bed were these: I saw
Daniel4:11 The tree grew and was strong and its height reached to the heavens
Daniel4:12 Its leaves were fair and its fruit abundant
Daniel4:13 I saw in the visions of my head [as I lay] on my bed
Daniel4:14 He cried aloud [with might] and said
Daniel4:15 Nevertheless leave the stump of its roots in the earth
Daniel4:16 Let his nature and understanding be changed from a man's and let a beast's nature and understanding be given him
Daniel4:17 This sentence is by the decree of the [heavenly] watchers and the decision is by the word of the holy ones
Daniel4:18 This dream I
Daniel4:19 Then Daniel
Daniel4:20 The tree that you saw
Daniel4:21 Whose foliage was beautiful and its fruit abundant
Daniel4:22 It is you
Daniel4:23 And whereas the king saw a watcher
Daniel4:24 This is the interpretation
Daniel4:25 You shall be driven from among men and your dwelling shall be with the beasts of the field; you shall be made to eat grass as do the oxen and you shall be wet with the dew of the heavens; and seven times [or years] shall pass over you until you learn and know and recognize that the Most High [God] rules the kingdom of mankind and gives it to whomever He will.
Daniel4:26 And in that it was commanded to leave the stump of the roots of the tree
Daniel4:27 Therefore
Daniel4:28 All this was fulfilled and came upon King Nebuchadnezzar.
Daniel4:29 At the end of twelve months he was walking in the royal palace of Babylon.
Daniel4:30 The king said
Daniel4:31 While the words were still in the king's mouth
Daniel4:32 And you shall be driven from among men and your dwelling will be with the living creatures of the field. You will be made to eat grass like the oxen
Daniel4:33 That very hour the thing was [in process of] being fulfilled upon Nebuchadnezzar. He was driven from among men and did eat grass like oxen [as Daniel had said he would]
Daniel4:34 And at the end of the days [seven years]
Daniel4:35 And all the inhabitants of the earth are accounted as nothing. And He does according to His will in the host of heaven and among the inhabitants of the earth
Daniel4:36 Now at the same time my reason and understanding returned to me; and for the glory of my kingdom
Daniel4:37 Now I

Daniel6:23 Then the king was exceedingly glad and commanded that Daniel should be taken up out of the den. So Daniel was taken up out of the den

Daniel6:25 Then King Darius wrote to all peoples
Daniel6:26 I make a decree that in all my royal dominion men must tremble and fear before the God of Daniel
Daniel6:27 He is a Savior and Deliverer
Daniel6:28 So this [man] Daniel prospered in the reign of Darius and in the reign of Cyrus the Persian.
Daniel7:1 IN THE first year of Belshazzar king of Babylon Daniel had a dream and visions in his head as he was lying upon his bed. Then he wrote down the dream and told the gist of the matter.
Daniel7:2 Daniel said
Daniel7:3 And four great beasts came up out of the sea in succession
Daniel7:4 The first [the Babylonian empire under Nebuchadnezzar] was like a lion and had eagle's wings. I looked till the wings of it were plucked
Daniel7:5 And behold another beast
Daniel7:6 After this I looked
Daniel7:7 After this I saw in the night visions
Daniel7:8 I considered the horns
Daniel7:9 I kept looking until thrones were placed [for the assessors with the Judge]
Daniel7:10 A stream of fire came forth from before Him; a thousand thousands ministered to Him and ten thousand times ten thousand rose up and stood before Him; the Judge was seated [the court was in session] and the books were opened.
Daniel7:11 I looked then because of the sound of the great words which the horn was speaking. I watched until the beast was slain and its body destroyed and given over to be burned with fire.
Daniel7:12 And as for the rest of the beasts
Daniel7:13 I saw in the night visions
Daniel7:14 And there was given Him [the Messiah] dominion and glory and kingdom
Daniel7:15 As for me
Daniel7:16 I came near to one of those who stood there and asked him the truth of all this. So he told me and made known to me the interpretation of the things.
Daniel7:17 These four great beasts are four kings who shall arise out of the earth.
Daniel7:18 But the saints of the Most High [God] shall receive the kingdom and possess the kingdom forever
Daniel7:19 Then I wished to know the truth about the fourth beast–which was different from all the others
Daniel7:20 And about the ten horns [representing kings] that were on its head
Daniel7:21 As I looked
Daniel7:22 Until the Ancient of Days came
Daniel7:23 Thus [the angel] said
Daniel7:24 And as for the ten horns
Daniel7:25 And he shall speak words against the Most High [God] and shall wear out the saints of the Most High and think to change the time [of sacred feasts and holy days] and the law; and the saints shall be given into his hand for a time
Daniel7:26 But the judgment shall be set [by the court of the Most High]
Daniel7:27 And the kingdom and the dominion and the greatness of the kingdom under the whole heavens shall be given to the people of the saints of the Most High; His kingdom is an everlasting kingdom
Daniel7:28 Here is the end of the matter. As for me

Daniel11:19 Then he shall turn his face back toward the fortresses of his own land [of Syria]

Hosea13:13 The pains of a woman in childbirth are coming on for him [to be born]; but he is an unwise son
Hosea13:14 Should I ransom them from the power of Sheol (the place of the dead)? Should I redeem them from death? O death
Hosea13:15 For though among his brethren [his fellow tribes] he may be fruitful

Amos2:4 Thus says the Lord: For three transgressions of Judah and for four [for multiplied delinquencies]

Amos2:8 And they lay themselves down beside every [pagan] altar upon clothes they have taken in pledge [for indebtedness]

Amos2:10 Also I brought you up out of the land of Egypt and led you forty years through the wilderness to possess the land of the Amorite.

Amos3:1 HEAR THIS word that the Lord has spoken against you
Amos3:2 You only have I known (chosen
Amos3:3 Do two walk together except they make an appointment and have agreed?
Amos3:4 Will a lion roar in the forest when he has no prey? Will a young lion cry out of his den if he has taken nothing?
Amos3:5 Can a bird fall in a snare upon the earth where there is no trap for him? Does a trap spring up from the ground when nothing at all has sprung it?
Amos3:6 Shall a trumpet be blown in the city and the people not be alarmed and afraid? Shall misfortune or evil occur [as punishment] and the Lord has not caused it?
Amos3:7 Surely the Lord God will do nothing without revealing His secret to His servants the prophets. [Rev. 10:7.]

Amos4:8 So [the people of] two or three cities wandered and staggered into one city to drink water

Amos5:2 The Virgin of Israel has fallen; she shall no more rise; she lies cast down and forsaken on her land; there is no one to raise her up.

Amos5:6 Seek the Lord [inquire for and of Him and require Him] and you shall live

Amos5:8 Seek Him Who made the [cluster of stars called] Pleiades and [the constellation] Orion
Amos5:9 Who causes sudden destruction to flash forth upon the strong so that destruction comes upon the fortress.
Amos5:10 They hate him who reproves in the [city] gate [holding him as an abomination and rejecting his rebuke]
Amos5:11 Therefore because you tread upon the poor and take from him exactions of wheat
Amos5:12 For I know how manifold are your transgressions and how mighty are your sins–you who afflict the [uncompromisingly] righteous
Amos5:13 Therefore he who is prudent will keep silence in such a time

Amos5:16 Therefore thus says the Lord

Amos5:18 Woe to you who desire the day of the Lord! Why would you want the day of the Lord? It is darkness and not light;
Amos5:19 It is as if a man fled from a lion and a bear met him
Amos5:20 Shall not the day of the Lord be darkness
Amos5:21 I hate
Amos5:22 Though you offer Me your burnt offerings and your cereal offerings
Amos5:23 Take away from Me the noise of your songs
Amos5:24 But let justice run down like waters and righteousness as a mighty and ever-flowing stream.
Amos5:25 Did you bring to Me sacrifices and cereal offerings during those forty years in the wilderness
Amos5:26 [No] but [instead of bringing Me the appointed sacrifices] you carried about the tent of your king Sakkuth and Kaiwan [names for the gods of the planet Saturn]

Jonah1:16 Then the men [reverently and worshipfully] feared the Lord exceedingly
Jonah1:17 Now the Lord had prepared and appointed a great fish to swallow up Jonah. And Jonah was in the belly of the fish three days and three nights. [Matt. 12:40.]

Micah1:6 Therefore I [the Lord] will make Samaria a heap in the open country
Micah1:7 And all her carved images shall be broken in pieces
Micah1:8 Therefore I [Micah] will lament and wail; I will go stripped and [virtually] naked; I will make a wailing like the jackals and a lamentation like the ostriches.
Micah1:9 For [Samaria's] wounds are incurable and they come even to Judah; He [the Lord] has reached to the gate of my people
Micah1:10 In Gath [a city in Philistia] announce it not; in Acco weep not at all
Micah1:11 Pass on your way [into exile]
Micah1:12 For the inhabitant of Maroth [bitterness] writhes in pain [at its losses] and waits anxiously for good
Micah1:13 Bind the chariot to the swift steed
Micah1:14 Therefore you must give parting gifts to Moresheth-gath [Micah's home town]; the houses of Achzib [place of deceit] shall be a deception to the kings of Israel.
Micah1:15 Yet will I bring a conqueror upon you
Micah1:16 Make yourself bald in mourning and cut off your hair for the children of your delight; enlarge your baldness as the eagle

Micah7:1 WOE IS me! For I am as when the summer fruits have been gathered

Micah7:11 In the day that your walls are to be built [a day for building]

Micah7:17 They shall lick the dust like a serpent; like crawling things of the earth they shall come trembling out of their strongholds and close places. They shall turn and come with fear and dread to the Lord our God and shall be afraid and stand in awe because of You [O Lord]. [Jer. 33:9.]

Nahum1:1 THE BURDEN or oracle (the thing to be lifted up) concerning Nineveh [the capital of Assyria]. The book of the vision of Nahum of Elkosh.
Nahum1:2 The Lord is a jealous God and avenging; the Lord avenges and He is full of wrath. The Lord takes vengeance on His adversaries and reserves wrath for His enemies. [Exod. 20:5.]
Nahum1:3 The Lord is slow to anger and great in power and will by no means clear the guilty. The Lord has His way in the whirlwind and in the storm
Nahum1:4 He rebukes and threatens the sea and makes it dry
Nahum1:5 The mountains tremble and quake before Him and the hills melt away
Nahum1:6 Who can stand before His indignation? And who can stand up and endure the fierceness of His anger? His wrath is poured out like fire
Nahum1:7 The Lord is good
Nahum1:8 But with an overrunning flood He will make a full end of [Nineveh's very] site and pursue His enemies into darkness.
Nahum1:9 What do you devise and [how mad is your attempt to] plot against the Lord? He will make a full end [of Nineveh]; affliction [which My people shall suffer from Assyria] shall not rise up the second time.
Nahum1:10 For [the Ninevites] are as bundles of thorn branches [for fuel]
Nahum1:11 There is one gone forth out of you [O Nineveh] who plots evil against the Lord
Nahum1:12 Thus says the Lord: Though they be in full strength and likewise many
Nahum1:13 For now will I break his yoke from off you and will burst your bonds asunder. [Isa. 14:25.]
Nahum1:14 And the Lord has given a commandment concerning you [evil Assyrian counselor]
Nahum1:15 Behold! upon the mountains the feet of him who brings good tidings [telling of the Assyrian's death]

Nahum2:5 [The Assyrian leader] remembers and summons his bravest men; they stumble in their march. They hasten to the city's wall
Nahum2:6 The gates or dams of the rivers [surrounding and guarding Nineveh] are opened and the [imperial] palace [of sun-dried brick] is dissolved [by the torrents] and is in dismay.

Habakkuk1:2 O Lord

Habakkuk1:5 Look around [you
Habakkuk1:6 For behold
Habakkuk1:7 [The Chaldeans] are terrible and dreadful; their justice and dignity proceed [only] from themselves.
Habakkuk1:8 Their horses also are swifter than leopards and are fiercer than the evening wolves
Habakkuk1:9 They all come for violence; their faces turn eagerly forward
Habakkuk1:10 They scoff at kings
Habakkuk1:11 Then they sweep by like a wind and pass on
Habakkuk1:12 Are not You from everlasting
Habakkuk1:13 You are of purer eyes than to behold evil and can not look [inactively] upon injustice. Why then do You look upon the plunderer? Why are you silent when the wicked one destroys him who is more righteous than [the Chaldean oppressor] is?
Habakkuk1:14 Why do You make men like the fish of the sea
Habakkuk1:15 [The Chaldean] brings all of them up with his hook; he catches and drags them out with his net
Habakkuk1:16 Therefore he sacrifices [offerings] to his net and burns incense to his dragnet
Habakkuk1:17 Shall he therefore continue to empty his net and mercilessly go on slaying the nations forever?

Habakkuk2:13 Behold

Habakkuk2:17 For the violence done to Lebanon will cover and overwhelm you; the destruction of the animals [which the violence frightened away] will terrify you on account of men's blood and the violence done to the land
