Habakkuk3:7 I [Habakkuk
Habakkuk3:8 Were You displeased with the rivers
Habakkuk3:9 Your bow was made quite bare; sworn to the tribes [of Israel] by Your sure word were the rods of chastisement
Habakkuk3:10 The mountains saw You; they trembled and writhed [as if in pain]. The overflowing of the water passed by [as at the deluge]; the deep uttered its voice and lifted its hands on high.
Habakkuk3:11 The sun and moon stood back [as before Joshua] in their habitation at the light of Your arrows as they sped
Habakkuk3:12 You marched through the land in indignation; You trampled and threshed the nations in anger.
Habakkuk3:13 You went forth and have come for the salvation of Your people
Habakkuk3:14 You pierced with his own arrows the head of [the enemy's] hordes; they came out as a whirlwind to scatter me [the people]
Habakkuk3:15 You have trodden the sea with Your horses
Habakkuk3:16 I heard and my [whole inner self] trembled; my lips quivered at the sound. Rottenness enters into my bones and under me [down to my feet]; I tremble. I will wait quietly for the day of trouble and distress when there shall come up against [my] people him who is about to invade and oppress them.
Habakkuk3:17 Though the fig tree does not blossom and there is no fruit on the vines
Habakkuk3:18 Yet I will rejoice in the Lord; I will exult in the [victorious] God of my salvation! [Rom. 8:37.]

Matthew24:8 All this is but the beginning [the early pains] of the birth pangs [of the intolerable anguish].
Matthew24:9 Then they will hand you over to suffer affliction and tribulation and put you to death
Matthew24:10 And then many will be offended and repelled and will begin to distrust and desert [Him Whom they ought to trust and obey] and will stumble and fall away and betray one another and pursue one another with hatred.
Matthew24:11 And many false prophets will rise up and deceive and lead many into error.
Matthew24:12 And the love of the great body of people will grow cold because of the multiplied lawlessness and iniquity
Matthew24:13 But he who endures to the end will be saved.
Matthew24:14 And this good news of the kingdom (the Gospel) will be preached throughout the whole world as a testimony to all the nations
Matthew24:15 So when you see the appalling sacrilege [the abomination that astonishes and makes desolate]

Matthew24:17 Let him who is on the housetop not come down and go into the house to take anything;
Matthew24:18 And let him who is in the field not turn back to get his overcoat.
Matthew24:19 And alas for the women who are pregnant and for those who have nursing babies in those days!
Matthew24:20 Pray that your flight may not be in winter or on a Sabbath.
Matthew24:21 For then there will be great tribulation (affliction
Matthew24:22 And if those days had not been shortened
Matthew24:23 If anyone says to you then
Matthew24:24 For false Christs and false prophets will arise

Matthew24:30 Then the sign of the Son of Man will appear in the sky
Matthew24:31 And He will send out His angels with a loud trumpet call

Matthew24:33 So also when you see these signs
Matthew24:34 Truly I tell you
Matthew24:35 Sky and earth will pass away
Matthew24:36 But of that [exact] day and hour no one knows

Matthew24:38 For just as in those days before the flood they were eating and drinking
Matthew24:39 And they did not know or understand until the flood came and swept them all away–so will be the coming of the Son of Man. [Gen. 6:5-8; 7:6-24.]

Mark1:1 THE BEGINNING [of the facts] of the good news (the Gospel) of Jesus Christ
Mark1:2 Just as it is written in the prophet Isaiah: Behold
Mark1:3 A voice of one crying in the wilderness [shouting in the desert]
Mark1:4 John the Baptist appeared in the wilderness (desert)
Mark1:5 And there kept going out to him [continuously] all the country of Judea and all the inhabitants of Jerusalem; and they were baptized by him in the river Jordan
Mark1:6 And John wore clothing woven of camel's hair and had a leather girdle around his loins and ate locusts and wild honey.
Mark1:7 And he preached
Mark1:8 I have baptized you with water
Mark1:9 In those days Jesus came from Nazareth of Galilee and was baptized by John in the Jordan.
Mark1:10 And when He came up out of the water
Mark1:11 And there came a voice out from within heaven
Mark1:12 Immediately the [Holy] Spirit [from within] drove Him out into the wilderness (desert)
Mark1:13 And He stayed in the wilderness (desert) forty days
Mark1:14 Now after John was arrested and put in prison
Mark1:15 And saying
Mark1:16 And passing along the shore of the Sea of Galilee
Mark1:17 And Jesus said to them
Mark1:18 And at once they left their nets and [yielding up all claim to them] followed [with] Him [joining Him as disciples and siding with His party].
Mark1:19 He went on a little farther and saw James the son of Zebedee
Mark1:20 And immediately He called out to them
Mark1:21 And they entered into Capernaum
Mark1:22 And they were completely astonished at His teaching
Mark1:23 Just at that time there was in their synagogue a man [who was in the power] of an unclean spirit; and now [immediately] he raised a deep and terrible cry from the depths of his throat
Mark1:24 What have You to do with us
Mark1:25 And Jesus rebuked him
Mark1:26 And the unclean spirit
Mark1:27 And they were all so amazed and almost terrified that they kept questioning and demanding one of another
Mark1:28 And immediately rumors concerning Him spread [everywhere] throughout all the region surrounding Galilee.
Mark1:29 And at once He left the synagogue and went into the house of Simon [Peter] and Andrew
Mark1:30 Now Simon's mother-in-law had for some time been lying sick with a fever
Mark1:31 And He went up to her and took her by the hand and raised her up; and the fever left her
Mark1:32 Now when it was evening
Mark1:33 Until the whole town was gathered together about the door.
Mark1:34 And He cured many who were afflicted with various diseases; and He drove out many demons
Mark1:35 And in the morning
Mark1:36 And Simon [Peter] and those who were with him followed Him [pursuing Him eagerly and hunting Him out]
Mark1:37 And they found Him and said to Him
Mark1:38 And He said to them
Mark1:39 [So] He went throughout the whole of Galilee

Philippians1:1 PAUL AND Timothy

Philippians1:3 I thank my God in all my remembrance of you.
Philippians1:4 In every prayer of mine I always make my entreaty and petition for you all with joy (delight).
Philippians1:5 [I thank my God] for your fellowship (your sympathetic cooperation and contributions and partnership) in advancing the good news (the Gospel) from the first day [you heard it] until now.
Philippians1:6 And I am convinced and sure of this very thing
Philippians1:7 It is right and appropriate for me to have this confidence and feel this way about you all
Philippians1:8 For God is my witness how I long for and pursue you all with love
Philippians1:9 And this I pray: that your love may abound yet more and more and extend to its fullest development in knowledge and all keen insight [that your love may display itself in greater depth of acquaintance and more comprehensive discernment]
Philippians1:10 So that you may surely learn to sense what is vital
Philippians1:11 May you abound in and be filled with the fruits of righteousness (of right standing with God and right doing) which come through Jesus Christ (the Anointed One)
Philippians1:12 Now I want you to know and continue to rest assured
Philippians1:13 So much is this a fact that throughout the whole imperial guard and to all the rest [here] my imprisonment has become generally known to be in Christ [that I am a prisoner in His service and for Him].
Philippians1:14 And [also] most of the brethren have derived fresh confidence in the Lord because of my chains and are much more bold to speak and publish fearlessly the Word of God [acting with more freedom and indifference to the consequences].
Philippians1:15 Some
Philippians1:16 The latter [proclaim Christ] out of love
Philippians1:17 But the former preach Christ out of a party spirit
Philippians1:18 But what does it matter
Philippians1:19 For I am well assured and indeed know that through your prayers and a bountiful supply of the Spirit of Jesus Christ (the Messiah) this will turn out for my preservation (for the spiritual health and welfare of my own soul) and avail toward the saving work of the Gospel.
Philippians1:20 This is in keeping with my own eager desire and persistent expectation and hope
Philippians1:21 For me to live is Christ [His life in me]
Philippians1:22 If
Philippians1:23 But I am hard pressed between the two. My yearning desire is to depart (to be free of this world

Philippians1:25 Since I am convinced of this
Philippians1:26 So that in me you may have abundant cause for exultation and glorying in Christ Jesus
Philippians1:27 Only be sure as citizens so to conduct yourselves [that] your manner of life [will be] worthy of the good news (the Gospel) of Christ
Philippians1:28 And do not [for a moment] be frightened or intimidated in anything by your opponents and adversaries
Philippians1:29 For you have been granted [the privilege] for Christ's sake not only to believe in (adhere to

Philippians2:22 But Timothy's tested worth you know
Philippians2:23 I hope therefore to send him promptly

Philippians2:25 However

Philippians2:30 For it was through working for Christ that he came so near death

Colossians1:14 In Whom we have our redemption through His blood
Colossians1:15 [Now] He is the exact likeness of the unseen God [the visible representation of the invisible]; He is the Firstborn of all creation.
Colossians1:16 For it was in Him that all things were created
Colossians1:17 And He Himself existed before all things
Colossians1:18 He also is the Head of [His] body

Colossians1:25 In it I became a minister in accordance with the divine stewardship which was entrusted to me for you [as its object and for your benefit]
Colossians1:26 The mystery of which was hidden for ages and generations [from angels and men]

2Thessalonians1:1 PAUL

2Thessalonians1:5 This is positive proof of the just and right judgment of God to the end that you may be deemed deserving of His kingdom [a plain token of His fair verdict which designs that you should be made and counted worthy of the kingdom of God]

Hebrews2:3 How shall we escape [appropriate retribution] if we neglect and refuse to pay attention to such a great salvation [as is now offered to us
Hebrews2:4 [Besides this evidence] it was also established and plainly endorsed by God
Hebrews2:5 For it was not to angels that God subjected the habitable world of the future
Hebrews2:6 It has been solemnly and earnestly said in a certain place
Hebrews2:7 For some little time You have ranked him lower than and inferior to the angels; You have crowned him with glory and honor and set him over the works of Your hands
Hebrews2:8 For You have put everything in subjection under his feet. Now in putting everything in subjection to man
Hebrews2:9 But we are able to see Jesus
Hebrews2:10 For it was an act worthy [of God] and fitting [to the divine nature] that He
Hebrews2:11 For both He Who sanctifies [making men holy] and those who are sanctified all have one [Father]. For this reason He is not ashamed to call them brethren;
Hebrews2:12 For He says
Hebrews2:13 And again He says
Hebrews2:14 Since
Hebrews2:15 And also that He might deliver and completely set free all those who through the [haunting] fear of death were held in bondage throughout the whole course of their lives.
Hebrews2:16 For
Hebrews2:17 So it is evident that it was essential that He be made like His brethren in every respect
Hebrews2:18 For because He Himself [in His humanity] has suffered in being tempted (tested and tried)
Hebrews3:1 SO THEN
Hebrews3:2 [See how] faithful He was to Him Who appointed Him [Apostle and High Priest]
Hebrews3:3 Yet Jesus has been considered worthy of much greater honor and glory than Moses
Hebrews3:4 For [of course] every house is built and furnished by someone
Hebrews3:5 And Moses certainly was faithful in the administration of all God's house [but it was only] as a ministering servant. [In his entire ministry he was but] a testimony to the things which were to be spoken [the revelations to be given afterward in Christ]. [Num. 12:7.]
Hebrews3:6 But Christ (the Messiah) was faithful over His [own Father's] house as a Son [and Master of it]. And it is we who are [now members] of this house
Hebrews3:7 Therefore
Hebrews3:8 Do not harden your hearts
Hebrews3:9 Where your fathers tried [My patience] and tested [My forbearance] and found I stood their test
Hebrews3:10 And so I was provoked (displeased and sorely grieved) with that generation
Hebrews3:11 Accordingly
Hebrews3:12 [Therefore beware] brethren
Hebrews3:13 But instead warn (admonish
Hebrews3:14 For we have become fellows with Christ (the Messiah) and share in all He has for us
Hebrews3:15 Then while it is [still] called Today
Hebrews3:16 For who were they who heard and yet were rebellious and provoked [Him]? Was it not all those who came out of Egypt led by Moses?
Hebrews3:17 And with whom was He irritated and provoked and grieved for forty years? Was it not with those who sinned
Hebrews3:18 And to whom did He swear that they should not enter His rest
Hebrews3:19 So we see that they were not able to enter [into His rest]
Hebrews4:1 THEREFORE
Hebrews4:2 For indeed we have had the glad tidings [Gospel of God] proclaimed to us just as truly as they [the Israelites of old did when the good news of deliverance from bondage came to them]; but the message they heard did not benefit them
Hebrews4:3 For we who have believed (adhered to and trusted in and relied on God) do enter that rest
Hebrews4:4 For in a certain place He has said this about the seventh day: And God rested on the seventh day from all His works. [Gen. 2:2.]
Hebrews4:5 And [they forfeited their part in it

Hebrews4:8 [This mention of a rest was not a reference to their entering into Canaan.] For if Joshua had given them rest
Hebrews4:9 So then
Hebrews4:10 For he who has once entered [God's] rest also has ceased from [the weariness and pain] of human labors
Hebrews4:11 Let us therefore be zealous and exert ourselves and strive diligently to enter that rest [of God

Hebrews4:13 And not a creature exists that is concealed from His sight
Hebrews4:14 Inasmuch then as we have a great High Priest Who has [already] ascended and passed through the heavens
Hebrews4:15 For we do not have a High Priest Who is unable to understand and sympathize and have a shared feeling with our weaknesses and infirmities and liability to the assaults of temptation
Hebrews4:16 Let us then fearlessly and confidently and boldly draw near to the throne of grace (the throne of God's unmerited favor to us sinners)

Hebrews6:2 With teachings about purifying
Hebrews6:3 If indeed God permits

Hebrews6:6 If they then deviate from the faith and turn away from their allegiance–[it is impossible] to bring them back to repentance
Hebrews6:7 For the soil which has drunk the rain that repeatedly falls upon it and produces vegetation useful to those for whose benefit it is cultivated partakes of a blessing from God.
Hebrews6:8 But if [that same soil] persistently bears thorns and thistles
Hebrews6:9 Even though we speak this way
Hebrews6:10 For God is not unrighteous to forget or overlook your labor and the love which you have shown for His name's sake in ministering to the needs of the saints (His own consecrated people)
Hebrews6:11 But we do [strongly and earnestly] desire for each of you to show the same diligence and sincerity [all the way through] in realizing and enjoying the full assurance and development of [your] hope until the end
Hebrews6:12 In order that you may not grow disinterested and become [spiritual] sluggards

Hebrews7:6 But this person who has not their Levitical ancestry received tithes from Abraham [himself] and blessed him who possessed the promises [of God].

Hebrews7:9 A person might even say that Levi [the father of the priestly tribe] himself
Hebrews7:10 For he was still in the loins of his forefather [Abraham] when Melchizedek met him [Abraham].

Hebrews7:14 For it is obvious that our Lord sprang from the tribe of Judah
Hebrews7:15 And this becomes more plainly evident when another Priest arises Who bears the likeness of Melchizedek
Hebrews7:16 Who has been constituted a Priest
Hebrews7:17 For it is witnessed of Him
Hebrews7:18 So a previous physical regulation and command is cancelled because of its weakness and ineffectiveness and uselessness–
Hebrews7:19 For the Law never made anything perfect–but instead a better hope is introduced through which we [now] come close to God.
Hebrews7:20 And it was not without the taking of an oath [that Christ was made Priest]
Hebrews7:21 For those who formerly became priests received their office without its being confirmed by the taking of an oath by God

Hebrews9:1 NOW EVEN the first covenant had its own rules and regulations for divine worship
Hebrews9:2 For a tabernacle (tent) was erected
Hebrews9:3 But [inside] beyond the second curtain or veil
Hebrews9:4 It had the golden altar of incense and the ark (chest) of the covenant
Hebrews9:5 Above [the ark] and overshadowing the mercy seat were the representations of the cherubim [winged creatures which were the symbols] of glory. We cannot now go into detail about these things.
Hebrews9:6 These arrangements having thus been made
Hebrews9:7 But into the second [division of the tabernacle] none but the high priest goes
Hebrews9:8 By this the Holy Spirit points out that the way into the [true Holy of] Holies is not yet thrown open as long as the former [the outer portion of the] tabernacle remains a recognized institution and is still standing
Hebrews9:9 Seeing that that first [outer portion of the] tabernacle was a parable (a visible symbol or type or picture of the present age). In it gifts and sacrifices are offered
Hebrews9:10 For [the ceremonies] deal only with clean and unclean meats and drinks and different washings
Hebrews9:11 But [that appointed time came] when Christ (the Messiah) appeared as a High Priest of the better things that have come and are to come. [Then] through the greater and more perfect tabernacle not made with [human] hands
Hebrews9:12 He went once for all into the [Holy of] Holies [of heaven]
Hebrews9:13 For if [the mere] sprinkling of unholy and defiled persons with blood of goats and bulls and with the ashes of a burnt heifer is sufficient for the purification of the body
Hebrews9:14 How much more surely shall the blood of Christ
Hebrews9:15 [Christ
Hebrews9:16 For where there is a [last] will and testament involved
Hebrews9:17 For a will and testament is valid and takes effect only at death
Hebrews9:18 So even the [old] first covenant (God's will) was not inaugurated and ratified and put in force without the shedding of blood.
Hebrews9:19 For when every command of the Law had been read out by Moses to all the people
Hebrews9:20 Saying these words: This is the blood that seals and ratifies the agreement (the testament
Hebrews9:21 And in the same way he sprinkled with the blood both the tabernacle and all the [sacred] vessels and appliances used in [divine] worship.
Hebrews9:22 [In fact] under the Law almost everything is purified by means of blood
Hebrews9:23 By such means
Hebrews9:24 For Christ (the Messiah) has not entered into a sanctuary made with [human] hands
Hebrews9:25 Nor did He [enter into the heavenly sanctuary to] offer Himself regularly again and again
Hebrews9:26 For then would He often have had to suffer [over and over again] since the foundation of the world. But as it now is
Hebrews9:27 And just as it is appointed for [all] men once to die

Hebrews10:2 For if it were otherwise
Hebrews10:3 But [as it is] these sacrifices annually bring a fresh remembrance of sins [to be atoned for]
Hebrews10:4 Because the blood of bulls and goats is powerless to take sins away.
Hebrews10:5 Hence
Hebrews10:6 In burnt offerings and sin offerings You have taken no delight.

Hebrews10:16 This is the agreement (testament
Hebrews10:17 He then goes on to say

Hebrews10:24 And let us consider and give attentive
Hebrews10:25 Not forsaking or neglecting to assemble together [as believers]

Hebrews10:27 [There is nothing left for us then] but a kind of awful and fearful prospect and expectation of divine judgment and the fury of burning wrath and indignation which will consume those who put themselves in opposition [to God]. [Isa. 26:11.]
Hebrews10:28 Any person who has violated and [thus] rejected and set at naught the Law of Moses is put to death without pity or mercy on the evidence of two or three witnesses. [Deut. 17:2-6.]
Hebrews10:29 How much worse (sterner and heavier) punishment do you suppose he will be judged to deserve who has spurned and [thus] trampled underfoot the Son of God
Hebrews10:30 For we know Him Who said

Hebrews10:39 But our way is not that of those who draw back to eternal misery (perdition) and are utterly destroyed

Hebrews11:2 For by [faith–trust and holy fervor born of faith] the men of old had divine testimony borne to them and obtained a good report.

Hebrews11:4 [Prompted
Hebrews11:5 Because of faith Enoch was caught up and transferred to heaven
Hebrews11:6 But without faith it is impossible to please and be satisfactory to Him. For whoever would come near to God must [necessarily] believe that God exists and that He is the rewarder of those who earnestly and diligently seek Him [out].
Hebrews11:7 [Prompted] by faith Noah
Hebrews11:8 [Urged on] by faith Abraham

Hebrews11:10 For he was [waiting expectantly and confidently] looking forward to the city which has fixed and firm foundations
Hebrews11:11 Because of faith also Sarah herself received physical power to conceive a child
Hebrews11:12 So from one man
Hebrews11:13 These people all died controlled and sustained by their faith
Hebrews11:14 Now those people who talk as they did show plainly that they are in search of a fatherland (their own country).
Hebrews11:15 If they had been thinking with [homesick] remembrance of that country from which they were emigrants
Hebrews11:16 But the truth is that they were yearning for and aspiring to a better and more desirable country
Hebrews11:17 By faith Abraham
Hebrews11:18 Of whom it was said
Hebrews11:19 For he reasoned that God was able to raise [him] up even from among the dead. Indeed in the sense that Isaac was figuratively dead [potentially sacrificed]

Hebrews11:21 [Prompted] by faith Jacob
Hebrews11:22 [Actuated] by faith Joseph
Hebrews11:23 [Prompted] by faith Moses
Hebrews11:24 [Aroused] by faith Moses
Hebrews11:25 Because he preferred to share the oppression [suffer the hardships] and bear the shame of the people of God rather than to have the fleeting enjoyment of a sinful life.
Hebrews11:26 He considered the contempt and abuse and shame [borne for] the Christ (the Messiah Who was to come) to be greater wealth than all the treasures of Egypt
Hebrews11:27 [Motivated] by faith he left Egypt behind him
Hebrews11:28 By faith (simple trust and confidence in God) he instituted and carried out the Passover and the sprinkling of the blood [on the doorposts]
Hebrews11:29 [Urged on] by faith the people crossed the Red Sea as [though] on dry land
Hebrews11:30 Because of faith the walls of Jericho fell down after they had been encompassed for seven days [by the Israelites]. [Josh. 6:12-21.]
Hebrews11:31 [Prompted] by faith Rahab the prostitute was not destroyed along with those who refused to believe and obey
Hebrews11:32 And what shall I say further? For time would fail me to tell of Gideon
Hebrews11:33 Who by [the help of] faith subdued kingdoms
Hebrews11:34 Extinguished the power of raging fire
Hebrews11:35 [Some] women received again their dead by a resurrection. Others were tortured to death with clubs
Hebrews11:36 Others had to suffer the trial of mocking and scourging and even chains and imprisonment.
Hebrews11:37 They were stoned to death; they were lured with tempting offers [to renounce their faith]; they were sawn asunder; they were slaughtered by the sword; [while they were alive] they had to go about wrapped in the skins of sheep and goats
Hebrews11:38 [Men] of whom the world was not worthy–roaming over the desolate places and the mountains
Hebrews11:39 And all of these
Hebrews11:40 Because God had us in mind and had something better and greater in view for us
Hebrews12:1 THEREFORE THEN
Hebrews12:2 Looking away [from all that will distract] to Jesus
Hebrews12:3 Just think of Him Who endured from sinners such grievous opposition and bitter hostility against Himself [reckon up and consider it all in comparison with your trials]
Hebrews12:4 You have not yet struggled and fought agonizingly against sin
Hebrews12:5 And have you [completely] forgotten the divine word of appeal and encouragement in which you are reasoned with and addressed as sons? My son
Hebrews12:6 For the Lord corrects and disciplines everyone whom He loves
Hebrews12:7 You must submit to and endure [correction] for discipline; God is dealing with you as with sons. For what son is there whom his father does not [thus] train and correct and discipline?
Hebrews12:8 Now if you are exempt from correction and left without discipline in which all [of God's children] share
Hebrews12:9 Moreover
Hebrews12:10 For [our earthly fathers] disciplined us for only a short period of time and chastised us as seemed proper and good to them; but He disciplines us for our certain good
Hebrews12:11 For the time being no discipline brings joy
Hebrews12:12 So then
Hebrews12:13 And cut through and make firm and plain and smooth
Hebrews12:14 Strive to live in peace with everybody and pursue that consecration and holiness without which no one will [ever] see the Lord.
Hebrews12:15 Exercise foresight and be on the watch to look [after one another]
Hebrews12:16 That no one may become guilty of sexual vice
Hebrews12:17 For you understand that later on
Hebrews12:18 For you have not come [as did the Israelites in the wilderness] to a [material] mountain that can be touched
Hebrews12:19 And to the blast of a trumpet and a voice whose words make the listeners beg that nothing more be said to them. [Exod. 19:12-22; 20:18-21; Deut. 4:11
Hebrews12:20 For they could not bear the command that was given: If even a wild animal touches the mountain
Hebrews12:21 In fact
Hebrews12:22 But rather
Hebrews12:23 And to the church (assembly) of the Firstborn who are registered [as citizens] in heaven
Hebrews12:24 And to Jesus
Hebrews12:25 So see to it that you do not reject Him or refuse to listen to and heed Him Who is speaking [to you now]. For if they [the Israelites] did not escape when they refused to listen and heed Him Who warned and divinely instructed them [here] on earth [revealing with heavenly warnings His will]

Hebrews13:8 Jesus Christ (the Messiah) is [always] the same
Hebrews13:9 Do not be carried about by different and varied and alien teachings; for it is good for the heart to be established and ennobled and strengthened by means of grace (God's favor and spiritual blessing) and not [to be devoted to] foods [rules of diet and ritualistic meals]

Hebrews13:11 For when the blood of animals is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest as a sacrifice for sin

Hebrews13:14 For here we have no permanent city
Hebrews13:15 Through Him
Hebrews13:16 Do not forget or neglect to do kindness and good
Hebrews13:17 Obey your spiritual leaders and submit to them [continually recognizing their authority over you]
Hebrews13:18 Keep praying for us
Hebrews13:19 And I beg of you [to pray for us] the more earnestly

Hebrews13:25 Grace (God's favor and spiritual blessing) be with you all. Amen (so be it).

James1:2 Consider it wholly joyful

James1:6 Only it must be in faith that he asks with no wavering (no hesitating

James1:18 And it was of His own [free] will that He gave us birth [as sons] by [His] Word of Truth

James1:20 For man's anger does not promote the righteousness God [wishes and requires].
James1:21 So get rid of all uncleanness and the rampant outgrowth of wickedness
James1:22 But be doers of the Word [obey the message]
James1:23 For if anyone only listens to the Word without obeying it and being a doer of it
James1:24 For he thoughtfully observes himself
James1:25 But he who looks carefully into the faultless law
James1:26 If anyone thinks himself to be religious (piously observant of the external duties of his faith) and does not bridle his tongue but deludes his own heart
James1:27 External religious worship [religion as it is expressed in outward acts] that is pure and unblemished in the sight of God the Father is this: to visit and help and care for the orphans and widows in their affliction and need

1John5:20 And we [have seen and] know [positively] that the Son of God has [actually] come to this world and has given us understanding and insight [progressively] to perceive (recognize) and come to know better and more clearly Him Who is true; and we are in Him Who is true–in His Son Jesus Christ (the Messiah). This [Man] is the true God and Life eternal.

2John1:11 For he who wishes him success [who encourages him

Revelation17:3 And [the angel] bore me away [rapt] in the Spirit into a desert (wilderness)
Revelation17:4 The woman was robed in purple and scarlet and bedecked with gold
Revelation17:5 And on her forehead there was inscribed a name of mystery [with a secret symbolic meaning]: Babylon the great
Revelation17:6 I also saw that the woman was drunk
Revelation17:7 But the angel said to me
Revelation17:8 The beast that you saw [once] was
Revelation17:9 This calls for a mind [to consider that is packed] with wisdom and intelligence [it is something for a particular mode of thinking and judging of thoughts
Revelation17:10 And they are also seven kings
Revelation17:11 And as for the beast that [once] was
Revelation17:12 Also the ten horns that you observed are ten rulers (kings) who have as yet received no royal dominion
Revelation17:13 These have one common policy (opinion
Revelation17:14 They will wage war against the Lamb
Revelation17:15 And [the angel further] said to me
Revelation17:16 And the ten horns that you saw
Revelation17:17 For God has put it into their hearts to carry out His own purpose by acting in harmony in surrendering their royal power and authority to the beast
Revelation17:18 And the woman that you saw is herself the great city which dominates and controls the rulers and the leaders of the earth.
Revelation18:1 THEN I saw another angel descending from heaven
Revelation18:2 And he shouted with a mighty voice
Revelation18:3 For all nations have drunk the wine of her passionate unchastity
Revelation18:4 I then heard another voice from heaven saying
Revelation18:5 For her iniquities (her crimes and transgressions) are piled up as high as heaven
Revelation18:6 Repay to her what she herself has paid [to others] and double [her doom] in accordance with what she has done. Mix a double portion for her in the cup she mixed [for others]. [Ps. 137:8.]
Revelation18:7 To the degree that she glorified herself and reveled in her wantonness [living deliciously and luxuriously]
Revelation18:8 So shall her plagues (afflictions
Revelation18:9 And the rulers and leaders of the earth who joined her in her immorality (idolatry) and luxuriated with her will weep and beat their breasts and lament over her when they see the smoke of her conflagration. [Ezek. 26:16
Revelation18:10 They will stand a long way off
Revelation18:11 And earth's businessmen will weep and grieve over her because no one buys their freight (cargo) any more. [Ezek. 27:36.]
Revelation18:12 Their merchandise is of gold
Revelation18:13 Of cinnamon
Revelation18:14 The ripe fruits and delicacies for which your soul longed have gone from you
Revelation18:15 The dealers who handled these articles
Revelation18:16 Alas
Revelation18:17 Because in one [single] hour all the vast wealth has been destroyed (wiped out). And all ship captains and pilots
Revelation18:18 And exclaimed as they watched the smoke of her burning
Revelation18:19 And they threw dust on their heads as they wept and grieved
Revelation18:20 Rejoice (celebrate) over her
Revelation18:21 Then a single powerful angel took up a boulder like a great millstone and flung it into the sea
Revelation18:22 And the sound of harpists and minstrels and flute players and trumpeters shall never again be heard in you
Revelation18:23 And never again shall the light of a lamp shine in you
Revelation18:24 And in her was found the blood of prophets and of saints

Revelation19:2 Because His judgments (His condemnation and punishment
Revelation19:3 And again they shouted

Revelation19:6 After that I heard what sounded like the shout of a vast throng

Revelation19:8 She has been permitted to dress in fine (radiant) linen
Revelation19:9 Then [the angel] said to me
Revelation19:10 Then I fell prostrate at his feet to worship (to pay divine honors) to him
Revelation19:11 After that I saw heaven opened
Revelation19:12 His eyes [blaze] like a flame of fire
Revelation19:13 He is dressed in a robe dyed by dipping in blood
Revelation19:14 And the troops of heaven

Revelation19:18 That you may feast on the flesh of rulers

Revelation19:20 And the beast was seized and overpowered

Revelation20:4 Then I saw thrones
Revelation20:5 The remainder of the dead were not restored to life again until the thousand years were completed. This is the first resurrection.
Revelation20:6 Blessed (happy
Revelation20:7 And when the thousand years are completed

Revelation20:9 And they swarmed up over the broad plain of the earth and encircled the fortress (camp) of God's people (the saints) and the beloved city; but fire descended from heaven and consumed them. [II Kings 1:10-12; Ezek. 38:2
Revelation20:10 Then the devil who had led them astray [deceiving and seducing them] was hurled into the fiery lake of burning brimstone
Revelation20:11 Then I saw a great white throne and the One Who was seated upon it
Revelation20:12 I [also] saw the dead
Revelation20:13 And the sea delivered up the dead who were in it

Revelation21:1 THEN I saw a new sky (heaven) and a new earth
Revelation21:2 And I saw the holy city
Revelation21:3 Then I heard a mighty voice from the throne and I perceived its distinct words
Revelation21:4 God will wipe away every tear from their eyes; and death shall be no more
Revelation21:5 And He Who is seated on the throne said
Revelation21:6 And He [further] said to me
Revelation21:7 He who is victorious shall inherit all these things
Revelation21:8 But as for the cowards and the ignoble and the contemptible and the cravenly lacking in courage and the cowardly submissive
Revelation21:9 Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls filled with the seven final plagues (afflictions
Revelation21:10 Then in the Spirit He conveyed me away to a vast and lofty mountain and exhibited to me the holy (hallowed
Revelation21:11 Clothed in God's glory [in all its splendor and radiance]. The luster of it resembled a rare and most precious jewel
Revelation21:12 It had a massive and high wall with twelve [large] gates
Revelation21:13 On the east side three gates
Revelation21:14 And the wall of the city had twelve foundation [stones]
Revelation21:15 And he who spoke to me had a golden measuring reed (rod) to measure the city and its gates and its wall. [Ezek. 40:5.]
Revelation21:16 The city lies in a square
Revelation21:17 He measured its wall also–144 cubits (about 72 yards) by a man's measure [of a cubit from his elbow to his third fingertip]
Revelation21:18 The wall was built of jasper
Revelation21:19 The foundation [stones] of the wall of the city were ornamented with all of the precious stones. The first foundation [stone] was jasper
Revelation21:20 The fifth onyx
Revelation21:21 And the twelve gates were twelve pearls
Revelation21:22 I saw no temple in the city
Revelation21:23 And the city has no need of the sun nor of the moon to give light to it
Revelation21:24 The nations shall walk by its light and the rulers and leaders of the earth shall bring into it their glory.
Revelation21:25 And its gates shall never be closed by day
Revelation21:26 They shall bring the glory (the splendor and majesty) and the honor of the nations into it.
Revelation21:27 But nothing that defiles or profanes or is unwashed shall ever enter it
